items.question: What is the primary purpose of the Public Service Rules (PSR) as stated in the foreword?
options:
To define the employment relationship between employees in the Public Service and the 
Government.
To serve as a guide for private sector employees.
To provide a historical overview of governance.a
items.explanation:
The Foreword explicitly states that "Public Service Rules (PSR) define the 
employment relationship between employees in the Public Service and the Government."

items.question: The 2021 Edition of the Public Service Rules is a review of which previous edition?
items.explanation:
The foreword mentions, "This edition of the Public Service Rules is therefore a 
review of the 2008 edition."

items.question: What emerging issues are reflected in the 2021 edition of the Public Service Rules?
options:
Performance Management System, Talent Sourcing, Volunteerism, Virtual 
Meetings/Engagements.
items.explanation:
The foreword highlights that "in the 2021 Edition of Public Service Rules (PSR), 
emerging issues such as performance management systems, talent sourcing, volunteerism, and 
virtual meetings/engagements, which are products of our recent experience in the management 
of the COVID-19 pandemic, and many others are reflected."

items.question:
Who is encouraged to study and imbibe these rules and their associated and complementary 
instructions, guidelines, and extant circulars?
options: Only new entrants into the Service.
Only the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.a
items.explanation:
The Preamble states, "Public Servants are, therefore, implored to study and 
imbibe these Rules and their associated and complementary Instructions, Guidelines, and 
extant Circulars..."

items.question: How often shall the Public Service Rules (PSR) be reviewed?
items.explanation:
The Preamble, under "Review of the Public Service Rules," specifies that "The 
Public Service Rules (PSR) shall be reviewed every five years..."

items.question:
What is the consequence of reproducing any part of the 2021 edition of the Public Service 
Rules without express approval or authorization?
options: It is encouraged for wider dissemination.
It is copyrighted, and reproduction is prohibited without express approval.
It requires only a verbal notification.

It is subject to a minor fine.
□
items.explanation:
The Preamble states, "The 2021 edition of the Public Service Rules is 
copyrighted. Accordingly, no part of this publication shall be reproduced without express 
approval or authorization by the Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation."
8

items.question: To whom do the provisions of the Public Service Rules (PSR) apply?
options: Only to the Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
Strictly to all ministries, extra-ministerial offices, and agencies.
Only to officers on grade level 17 and above.
To all employees in the private sector.a
items.explanation:
Rule 010101 states, "The provisions of the Public Service Rules (PSR) shall be 
strictly enforced by all Ministries, Extra-Ministerial Offices, and Agencies."

items.question:
The Public Service Rules apply to all officers in the Public Service except where they conflict 
with specific terms approved by the Federal Government and written into the contract of 
employment or letters of appointment. This indicates that:
options: Specific contract terms can override the PSR.
The PSR is a suggestion, not a rule.

Only the Head of the Civil Service can grant exceptions.
Q
items.explanation:
Rule 010101 states that the rules apply "except where they conflict with specific 
terms approved by the Federal Government and written into the contract of employment or 
letters of appointment."

items.question:
What is the duty of every officer regarding the Public Service Rules and other extant 
regulations and circulars?
options: To delegate the responsibility to subordinates.
To challenge them if they disagree.a
items.explanation:
Rule 010103 states, "It shall be the duty of every officer to acquaint himself with 
the Public Service Rules and other extant regulations and circulars."

items.question: How are the chapters, sections, and rules numbered in the PSR?
options:
Each rule has a six-digit number, with the first two indicating the chapter, the next two the 
section, and the last two the rule number.

items.question: What does "Accredited Representative of the Federal Republic of Nigeria" mean?
options:
The Diplomatic or Consular Representative of the Federal Republic of Nigeria within the 
area.
items.explanation:
Rule 010105 defines "Accredited Representative of the Federal Republic of 
Nigeria" as "the Diplomatic or Consular Representative of the Federal Republic of Nigeria within 
the area."

items.question: What does "Civil Service of the Federation" mean?
options:
Service of the Federation in the civil capacity as a staff of the Office of the President, Vice 
President, a Ministry, and Extra Ministerial Offices of the Government of the Federation.
items.explanation:
Rule 010105 defines "Civil Service of the Federation" as "Service of the 
Federation in the civil capacity as a staff of the Office of the President, Vice President, a 
Ministry, and Extra Ministerial Offices of the Government of the Federation assigned with the 
responsibility for any business of the Government of the Federation."

items.question: "Classified Correspondence" means correspondence that has been graded:
options: Restricted, Confidential, Secret, or Top Secret.
items.explanation:
Rule 010105 defines "Classified Correspondence" as "correspondence that has 
been graded Restricted, Confidential, Secret, or Top Secret."
10

items.question: What does "cut-off mark" mean in the context of the PSR?
options: The minimum score for passing any exam.

The score set by the Federal Civil Service Commission to determine eligible candidates for 
promotion among those who pass the examination as determined by declared vacancies.
The maximum score achievable in an examination.
items.explanation:
Rule 010105 defines "cut-off mark" as "the score set by the Federal Civil Service 
Commission to determine eligible candidates for promotion among those who pass the 
examination as determined by declared vacancies."

items.question: What is an "Established Post"?
options: A temporary position in the public service.
A post provided for under the Personnel Emoluments sub-head of the Estimates.
A position for contract staff only.

A new position created without approval.
Q
items.explanation:
Rule 010105 defines "Established Post" as "a post provided for under the 
Personnel Emoluments sub-head of the Estimates."

items.question: What does "Expatriate Officer" mean?
options:
A staff member who ranks as such for the purpose of pension in accordance with the Pension 
Reform Act 2004 as amended.
A foreign national working in Nigeria.
An Officer on secondment from another country.a
items.explanation:
Rule 010105 defines "Expatriate Officer" as "a staff member who ranks as such 
for the purpose of pension in accordance with the Pension Reform Act 2004 as amended."

items.question: What does "Gazette" mean in the Public Service Rules?
options: The Federal Republic of Nigeria Official Gazette.
items.explanation:
Rule 010105 defines "Gazette" as "the Federal Republic of Nigeria Official 
Gazette."
11

items.question: What is "interdiction"?
options:
The temporary removal of an officer from performing his normal duties when disciplinary 
proceedings for his dismissal are being undertaken.

A form of leave without pay.
□
items.explanation:
Rule 010105 defines "interdiction" as "the temporary removal of an officer from 
performing his normal duties when disciplinary proceedings for his dismissal are being 
undertaken or is about to be undertaken, and he shall be placed on half salary pending the 
determination of the case."

items.question: What is a "junior officer"?
options: A new entrant to the service.
A pensionable officer on GL. 06 and below and their equivalent.
items.explanation:
Rule 010105 defines "Junior Officer" as "a pensionable officer on GL. 06 and 
below and their equivalent."

items.question: What are "Junior Posts"?
options:
Posts in ministries/extra-ministerial offices attracting emoluments on grade level 06 and 
below and their equivalent.
items.explanation:
Rule 010105 defines "Junior Posts" as "posts in Ministries/Extra-Ministerial
Offices attracting emoluments on Grade Level 06 and below and their equivalent."

items.question: What does "monthly-rated" employment mean?
options: Employment paid on a daily basis.
Employment on monthly rates of pay.
items.explanation:
Rule 010106 defines "monthly-rated" as "employment on monthly rates of pay."
12

items.question: What is an "officer" when used without qualification?
options: Any person employed by the Federal Government.
Means staff in an established post, either on pensionable or contract term.
items.explanation:
Rule 010106 defines "officer" as "staff in an established post, either on 
pensionable or contract term."

items.question: What does "pass mark" mean?
options: The minimum score required to pass any examination.

The score below which a candidate is considered to have failed the promotion examination, 
which currently is 60 percent.
The highest score in an examination.

The average score of all candidates.
□
items.explanation:
Rule 010106 defines "Pass Mark" as "the score below which a candidate is 
considered to have failed the promotion examination, which currently is 60 percent."

items.question: What does "Permanent Secretary" mean?
options: Officer in charge of day-to-day administration in the Ministry/Extra-Ministerial Offices.
The Chief Executive Officer of a Parastatal.

The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
Q
items.explanation:
Rule 010106 defines "Permanent Secretary" as "Officer in charge of day-to-day 
administration in the Ministry/Extra-Ministerial Offices."

items.question: What does "Public Service of the Federation" mean?
options: The service of the Federal Government in any capacity.

The service of the Federation as defined in Part 4 Section 318 of 1999 Constitution (as 
amended).
items.explanation:
Rule 010106 defines "Public Service of the Federation" as "the service of the 
Federation as defined in Part 4 Section 318 of 1999 Constitution (as amended)."
13

items.question: What are "Public Service Rules"?
options:
Consists of policies and guidelines which define the employment relationship between Public 
Servants and the Government.
Laws enacted by the National Assembly.
items.explanation:
Rule 010106 defines "Public Service Rules" as "consisting of policies and 
guidelines that define the employment relationship between public servants and the 
government; they also spell out the conditions of service and human resources management 
procedures."

items.question: What does "Schemes of Service" refer to?
options:
A reference document in the public service that specifies the duties of each post, method of 
entry, including qualification requirements and the prerequisites for advancement within and 
beyond cadres.
items.explanation:
Rule 010106 defines "Schemes of Service" as "a reference document in the 
public service that specifies the duties of each post, method of entry, including qualification 
requirements, and the prerequisites for advancement within and beyond cadres."

items.question: What are "Senior Posts"?
options:
Posts attracting emoluments on Grade Level 07 and above and other posts attracting fixed 
emoluments of equivalent grade levels in ministries/extra-ministerial offices.
items.explanation:
Rule 010106 defines "Senior Posts" as "posts attracting emoluments on Grade 
Level 07 and above and other posts attracting fixed emoluments of equivalent Grade Levels in 
Ministries/Extra-MinisterialOffices."

items.question: What does "spouse of an officer" mean?
options: A wife/husband married under the Marriage Act, customary law, or Islamic law.
items.explanation:
Rule 010106 defines "Spouse of an Officer" as "a wife/husband married under the 
Marriage Act, Customary Law, or Islamic Law."

items.question: What does "staff" mean in the context of the PSR?
options: A person employed by the Federal Government.
items.explanation:
Rule 010106 defines "staff' as "a person employed by the Federal Government."
Appointment and Leaving the Service
Part 3: General

items.question:
According to the Public Service Rules, who is responsible for making appointments into 
posts on GL. 06 and below?
options: The Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation
items.explanation:
Rule 020103(a) states that each ministry/extra-ministerial office shall handle all 
appointments to junior posts on GL. 06 and below.

items.question:
What is the incremental date for an officer appointed or promoted to a post in the Federal 
Public Service?
options: The first day of the month of appointment

The date of assumption of duty
15
items.explanation:
Rule 020104 establishes that the incremental date of an officer is the 1st of 
January every year, provided the officer has spent at least six months on the grade with good 
performance before enjoying the increment.

items.question: Who determines the seniority of an officer?
options: The date of assumption of duty

The date of appointment or the date of assumption of duty, whichever is earlier and duly 
certified in the appropriate register.
items.explanation:
Rule 020105 states that seniority is determined by the date of assumption of duty 
certified by an authorized officer and/or date of present appointment as reflected in the 
appropriate register and career progression.

items.question: Who is responsible for keeping the up-to-date personal record of every officer?
options: The Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation
items.explanation:
Rule 020107 mandates that Permanent Secretaries/Heads of Extra-Ministerial 
Offices shall keep an up-to-date record of every officer under their control.

items.question:
What form must be used for an officer's staff record, and how many copies must be sent to 
the National Record Centre?
items.explanation:
According to Rule 020107(i), a Staff Record Form (Gen 60) shall be used, and 
one copy must be sent to the National Record Centre.

items.question: According to Rule 020102(i), where are advertisements for posts on GL 07-17 to be made?
items.explanation:
Rule 020102(i) specifies that appointments to posts on GL. 07-17 shall be made 
through advertisements in three national newspapers and the Commission's website.

items.question:
What is the maximum period within which applications for advertised posts must be 
submitted?
options: Four weeks from the date of advertisement
Six weeks from the date of advertisement
Two weeks from the date of advertisement

Eight weeks from the date of advertisement
□
items.explanation:
Rule 020102(i) stipulates that the deadline for submission of applications shall be 
six weeks from the date of placement of the advertisement.

items.question:
What action should be taken if an officer's date of birth recorded on appointment is changed 
without due authority?
options: It shall be regarded as an act of serious misconduct.
items.explanation:
Rule 020108 states that a change of date of birth without due authority is to be 
regarded as an act of serious misconduct, which may lead to dismissal.
Part 4: Recruitment

items.question: How do the Public Service Rules define "recruitment"?
options:
The filling of vacancies by means of appointment of persons not already in the Public Service 
of the Federal Republic of Nigeria

The process of appointing public servants
□ 17
items.explanation:
Rule 020201 defines recruitment as the "filling of vacancies by direct appointment 
of persons not already in any of the Public Services of the Federal Republic of Nigeria."

items.question:
Which of the following is NOT a type of appointment recognized in the Federal Public 
Service?
items.explanation:
Rule 020202 lists the categories of appointments, and "casual appointment" is not 
among them.

items.question: What is the quota reserved for recruitment of persons with disabilities?
items.explanation:
Rule 020202(b) reserves five percent (5%) of all recruitments for persons with 
disabilities.

items.question:
What is the minimum age requirement for eligibility for appointment into the Federal Public 
Service?
items.explanation:
Rule 020206(a) states that to be eligible for appointment, every applicant must be 
"not less than 18 years and not more than 50 years of age."

items.question:
An applicant for a public service position must possess a testimonial of good conduct from 
which of the following?
options: The last school or college attended
items.explanation:
Rule 020206(d) requires an applicant to "possess a testimonial of good conduct 
from the last employer or, if not previously employed, from the last school or college attended."
18

items.question:
What is required for a person who has previously been employed in public service and 
dismissed or called upon to resign?
options: They are eligible for reappointment immediately.
They must obtain prior approval from the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.

They must obtain the approval of the Commission's powers, and it should be obtained 
irrespective of any delegation of the Commission's powers.

They are not eligible for reappointment.
□
items.explanation:
Rule 020207(i)(b) requires that any such applicant must obtain approval from the 
Commission's powers, "irrespective of any delegation."

items.question: What document is required for appointment to senior posts?
options: Federal Civil Service Commission Application Form (FCSC Form 1)
Federal Civil Service Commission Application Form (FCSC Form 2)
items.explanation:
Rule 020208(a) specifies that all applicants for senior posts shall submit a 
completed "Federal Civil Service Commission Application Form (FCSC Form 2)".

items.question:
What form should an officer newly appointed to a pensionable establishment use to provide 
their personal particulars?
items.explanation:
Rule 020209 states that a newly appointed officer is required to enter his/her 
personal particulars on a Staff Record Form (Gen 60).

items.question: Every newly appointed officer or staff member is required to sign which of the following?
items.explanation:
Rule 020210 mandates that every newly appointed officer/staff member must sign 
an Oath of Secrecy on Security (Form 1).

items.question: What is the consequence for a public officer found to be a member of a secret society?
items.explanation:
Rule 020212 states that contravention of Rule 020211 (Prohibition of Membership 
of Secret Society) "shall be regarded as an act of serious misconduct which may include 
dismissal from the service."
Part 5: Rules for Appointment on Probation

items.question: What is the typical period of probation for an officer newly appointed to the service?
options: Not less than six months and not more than two years.
items.explanation:
Rule 020301 specifies that officers on probation are required to serve for two 
years, but this period can be reduced to not less than six months or extended up to two years.

items.question:
What happens to an officer's appointment if they do not pass their prescribed examination 
within the probationary period?
items.explanation:
Rule 020303 states that an officer on probation is required to pass the prescribed 
examination, and if they fail to do so, their appointment may be terminated at the end of the 
probationary period.

items.question:
Which body is responsible for making recommendations regarding the confirmation of 
officers holding senior posts?
options: The Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office

The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation
□ .
items.explanation:
Rule 020304 states that recommendations for the confirmation of officers holding 
senior posts shall be made by the Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office.

items.question:
Who are the members of the Junior Staff Committee (JSC) and Senior Staff Committee 
(SSC) in Parastatals?
20
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation and the Permanent Secretary

The representative of the Department of Human Resource Management and an observer 
from the Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation
The Chief Executive Officer and the Financial Officer
The Board of Directors of the Parastatala
items.explanation:
Rule 020305 states that the membership of the JSC and SSC in Parastatals 
includes a representative of the Department of Human Resource Management and an observer 
from the Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.

items.question:
A newly recruited officer who has spent six months and above shall be allowed to sit for 
compulsory examinations for confirmation in the service. Which rule specifies this?
items.explanation:
Rule 020306 specifies that a newly recruited officer who has spent six months 
and above shall be allowed to sit for compulsory examinations for confirmation.

items.question:
An officer wishing to transfer from one senior post to another within the Federal Public 
Service must have served for a minimum period of how many months?
items.explanation:
Rule 020307 states that an officer must have served for a minimum period of six 
months in his original post before seeking a transfer to another post.

items.question:
What is the consequence of failing to pass the compulsory confirmation examinations for 
clerical grade officers?
options: The officer will have their probationary period extended.
The officer may have their appointment terminated at the end of the probationary period.

The officer will be demoted.
a
items.explanation:
Rule 020303 specifies that an officer on probation is required to pass the 
prescribed examination, and failure to do so may lead to the termination of the appointment

items.question:
The probationary period for an officer holding a senior post may be extended up to a
21
maximum of two years. Which rule specifies this?
items.explanation:
Rule 020301 states that the probationary period may be extended to a maximum 
of two years28.

items.question:
An officer's probationary appointment is terminated or extended based on the 
recommendation of which authority?
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation
The authority empowered to appoint the officer

The Federal Civil Service Commission
Q
items.explanation:
Rule 020303 indicates that at the end of the probationary period, the officer will, 
unless their probationary appointment is terminated or extended, be confirmed in their 
appointment by the authority empowered to appoint them29.

items.question: Which rule outlines the conditions for transfer within the Federal Public Service?
items.explanation:
Rule 020307 details the procedure and conditions for transfer within the Federal 
Public Service, including the minimum service period and the application process30.
Part 6: Rules for Appointment on Short-Term Engagement

items.question: How is a "Contract Appointment" defined in the rules?
options:
A temporary appointment for which provision is made for the payment of a pension to a post 
at the level to which the appointment is made.
A temporary appointment for which no pension is paid.

A temporary appointment for which provision is not made for the payment of a pension to a 
post at the level to which appointment is made.
□
items.explanation:
Rule 020402 defines a contract appointment as a "temporary appointment for
22
which provision is not made for the payment of a pension to a post at the level to which the 
appointment is made."

items.question: What is the maximum duration of a contract appointment?
items.explanation:
Rule 020406(vi) specifies that the duration of a contract appointment must be one 
or two years and renewable but not more than four years in total

items.question:
62. When processing contract appointments, who is responsible for identifying the required 
skills?
options: The Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF)
items.explanation:
Rule 020405(i) states that Ministries, extra-ministerial departments, and Agencies 
(MDAs) are to "undertake identification of required skills and defend the skills at the OHCSF, 
whose responsibility shall be to work out and approve a waiver for the desired skills."

items.question: What is the process for terminating a contract appointment?
options: A letter of termination is issued directly by the Ministry.
A recommendation from the Federal Civil Service Commission is required.
The contract is terminated at the discretion of the Permanent Secretary.
A simple verbal notification is sufficient.3 23
items.explanation:
Rule 020407 states that an appointment on contract may be terminated by the 
government in accordance with the terms specified, and a recommendation is sent to the 
Federal Civil Service Commission35.

items.question:
An officer on a contract appointment who desires re-engagement must inform the 
government in writing not less than how many months before the expiration of his contract?
items.explanation:
Rule 020408 states that an officer on contract who desires re-engagement "shall 
so inform the government in writing through the Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial 
Office not less than four months before his leave is due."

items.question: A sabbatical appointment is applicable to which grade level?
options:
GL. 10 or equivalent and above
Q
items.explanation:
Rule 020413 states that sabbatical appointment applies to "the Service at GL. 15 
(or equivalent) and above."

items.question: What is the tenure for an intern or a volunteer in the public service?
options: Not more than 12 months in a full-time academic session
items.explanation:
Rule 020418 states that interns shall be engaged for 3 to 6 months on a part-time 
basis during an academic session and for a period of not more than 12 months on a full-time 
basis38.

items.question: What is the definition of "volunteerism" according to the rules?
options: Offering one's time or talent for a fee.

Offering one’s time or talent for charitable, educational, or other worthwhile activities, 
especially in one’s community, without remuneration

Offering services for a short-term contract.
24
Q
items.explanation:
Rule 020416 defines volunteerism as a "practice of offering one’s time or talent for 
charitable, educational, or other worthwhile activities, especially in one’s community."

items.question: What is the legal framework required for processing short-term engagements?
options: The negotiation, drafting, and execution of contracts of engagement by the parties.
A circular from the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.a
items.explanation:
Rule 020421 states that due processes are followed in the "negotiation, drafting, 
and execution of contracts of engagement by the parties on all the short-term appointments."
Part 7: Transfers and Secondments

items.question: How is "transfer" defined in the Public Service Rules?
options:
The temporary release of an officer to the service of another government or international 
body.

The permanent release of an officer from one scheduled Service to another or from one class 
to another within the same Service.
The movement of an officer from one ministry to another.

The movement of an officer to a new post on promotion.
□
items.explanation:
Rule 020501 defines transfer as "the permanent release of an officer from one 
scheduled service to another or from one class to another within the same service."

items.question:
The authority to process inter-service transfer and secondment for posts graded GL. 06 and 
below lies with which body?
options: The Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation
The appropriate Committees of the benefitting Ministries/Extra-Ministerial Offices.

The Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office
Q
items.explanation:
Rule 020502 states that applications for transfer/secondment to posts on GL. 06 
and below shall be considered by the appropriate committees of the benefitting 
ministries/extra-ministerial offices.

items.question:
An officer applying for an inter-service transfer or secondment to a post on GL. 07-10 is 
required to sit for which examination?
options: The Annual Competitive Civil Service Entry Examination
items.explanation:
Rule 020503(ii) states that officers seeking a transfer from scheduled 
establishments on GL. 07-10 are required to "sit for the Annual Competitive Civil Service Entry 
Examination.

items.question:
What is the maximum period an officer can be seconded to another government or 
recognized international organization?
options: Two years in the first instance, with extensions up to four years.
One year in the first instance, with extensions up to two years.

Four years with no extension.
a
items.explanation:
Rule 020503(v) states that secondment can be for a maximum period of two 
years in the first instance and, with extensions, not to exceed four years44.

items.question:
During a period of secondment, what happens to the officer's substantive post and 
promotion prospects?
options: The officer forfeits their substantive post and promotion.
The officer's substantive post is held in abeyance, and they are not entitled to promotion.

The officer's substantive post is held, and they are entitled to be treated as having been 
posted on special duty, with their promotion prospects maintained.

The officer is permanently transferred to the new organization.
Q
items.explanation:
Rule 020503(h) states that during secondment, the officer's substantive post 
"shall continue to be held, and he/she will be entitled to increment and promotion and will be 
treated as having been posted on special duty."

items.question: What is the entitlement of an officer on transfer on promotion?
options: The officer is entitled to the salary of the lower grade.
The officer is not entitled to any promotion benefits.
The officer is entitled to the salary of the new grade.

The officer is placed on a probationary period in the new grade.
□
items.explanation:
Rule 020506 specifies that an applicant shall be considered for 
transfer-on-promotion to any MDA, except in response to an advertised post46.
26

items.question: What is the condition for a transfer to be approved?
options: The applicant must have a clean record.
The applicant must have served for a minimum of two years.
The applicant must be confirmed in their present appointment.
items.explanation:
Rule 020507(i) states that the applicant must be confirmed in their present 
appointment47.

items.question:
What is the requirement for Permanent Secretaries/Heads of Extra-Ministerial Offices 
regarding transfers on junior posts?
options: They must notify the Federal Civil Service Commission immediately.

They must not accept any staff seconded without the endorsement of the Federal Civil 
Service Commission/Board.
They must obtain approval from the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.

They must conduct an interview for the staff
a .
items.explanation:
Rule 020507(j) states that "No Ministry shall accept any staff seconded to it 
without the endorsement of the Federal Civil Service Commission/Board."

items.question: Who is responsible for paying the salary of an officer on secondment?
options: The parent MDA that released the officer.
The MDA where the officer is seconded to.
The Office of the Head of Civil Service of the Federation.
items.explanation:
Rule 020504 states that the MDA with the higher salary shall be responsible for 
the payment of the salary and non-salary allowances should be paid by the receiving MDA49.

items.question: What is the requirement for MDAs regarding staff on transfer/secondment?
options:
They must make quarterly returns to the Federal Civil Service Commission and the Office of 
the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
They must submit a report every six months.
They must seek approval from the Federal Civil Service Commission for every transfer.

They must maintain a list of all seconded staff.
3
items.explanation:
Rule 020509 states that MDAs "shall make quarterly returns to the Federal Civil
27
Service Commission and the Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation on the 
number of staff on transfer/secondment."
Part 8: Posting of Officers

items.question: What is the definition of "posting" according to the Public Service Rules?
options: The temporary movement of an officer.
The initial assignment of an officer in a position/job schedule in an MDA.
The permanent transfer of an officer to a new location.

The movement of an officer on promotion.
□
items.explanation:
Rule 020601 defines posting as "the initial assignment of an officer in a 
position/job schedule in an MDA, or reassignment to another position/job schedule within or 
outside his current MDA/Department."

items.question: Who is responsible for posting and redeploying all senior staff?
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation

The Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office
a
items.explanation:
The rules state that all senior staff can be posted and/or redeployed to new posts 
in accordance with the approved Employee Mobility Policy52.

items.question:
For an officer posted outside their professional cadre, what is the mandatory period they 
should serve in such a post?
items.explanation:
Rule 020602(i)(b) stipulates that posting of officers to posts outside their 
professional cadres is prohibited53. However, rule 020602 (i) (c) says that posting of pool officers 
in the service shall be mandatory every four (4) years.

items.question: The principle of posting is intended to ensure that the system is not used as a tool for what?
items.explanation:
Rule 020603 emphasizes that posting is done to ensure fairness to all officers and 
that the system is not used as a tool for "coercion, punishment, or favoritism."

items.question:
Who is responsible for submitting a report on compliance with posting instructions to the 
relevant pool office within three weeks of the release of the instruction?
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation
items.explanation:
Rule 020605 states that the "Director of HR shall be expected to submit a report 
on compliance to posting instructions to the relevant pool office within three weeks of the 
release of the instruction."

items.question:
An officer who desires to live and work in the location where their spouse lives should be 
allowed to do so, provided what?
options: The spouse is also a public servant.
The officer can prove marriage with the spouse.
The branch of the MDA has an established relevant post.

The posting does not affect the officer's career progression.
□
items.explanation:
Rule 020608 states that an officer can be posted to their spouse's location 
"provided the branch of his MDA can be found in the spouse’s location and that there is a 
relevant established post at the branch."

items.question:
What is the primary responsibility of all employees of the Federal Government with regard to 
union activities?
options: To participate in all union meetings.

To discharge the duties assigned by their employer to the Nigerian public in a timely and 
efficient manner.
To ensure the union's executives are posted to suitable locations.

To report to the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
□
items.explanation:
Rule 020609 states that the primary responsibility of all employees is "to 
discharge the duties assigned by their employer to the Nigerian public in a timely and efficient 
manner."

items.question:
What is the tenure of trade union/staff association executives?
29
options: Two terms of two years each.
Two terms of four years each.
A single term of four years.

A single term of two years.
a
items.explanation:
Rule 020612 states that the tenure of Trade Unions/Staff Associations Executives 
is "two terms in their constitutions. The duration for each tenure shall not be more than 2 years."

items.question: What happens to labor union executives at the expiration of their tenure in office?
options: They shall not be posted out of their MDAs.

They are offered a new contract.
a
items.explanation:
Rule 020610 specifies that labor union executives shall not be posted out of their 
MDAs until the expiration of their tenure60.

items.question:
What is the consequence of posting an officer in a manner that results in the loss of 
institutional memory?
options: The posting is considered a bad practice and should be avoided.
The officer will be compensated for the loss.

The posting will be cancelled.
a
items.explanation:
Rule 020603(i) states that posting should not result in the "loss of institutional 
memory in any Department, Division, or Unit nor impair its ability to continue to provide quality 
service to both internal and external stakeholders."
Part 9: Acting Appointment

items.question: When can an acting appointment be made?
options: Only when a substantive post becomes vacant.

Where a particular duty post is not lower than SGL 14 and the substantive holder is on leave 
or otherwise not available for posting.
When an officer is being considered for promotion.

Only for a short period of time.
a
items.explanation:
Rule 020701 specifies that an acting appointment is made when a particular duty
30
post not lower than SGL 14 becomes vacant because the substantive holder is on leave, 
secondment, or other similar circumstances.

items.question: An acting appointment is NOT a form of trial for what purpose?
items.explanation:
Rule 020703 explicitly states, "Acting appointment is not intended as a means of 
testing the suitability of officers for promotion."

items.question: What is the maximum duration of an acting appointment?
items.explanation:
Rule 020705 states that an acting appointment shall not exceed one year, but in 
exceptional circumstances, it can be extended for another one year.

items.question: What is the effective date of an acting appointment?
options: The date of the gazette notice of the appointment.

The date on which the officer takes over substantively the duties and responsibilities of the 
post.
The date the FCSC is notified.

The first day of the month.
□
items.explanation:
Rule 020705 states that the date on which an acting appointment commences 
"will be indicated in the gazette notice authorizing the appointment" and takes effect when the 
officer takes over the duties and responsibilities65.

items.question: What happens to an acting appointment when the substantive holder returns to the station?
items.explanation:
Rule 020706 states that an acting appointment "ceases immediately an Officer
31
relinquishes the duties and responsibilities of the post" or when the substantive holder returns to 
the station.

items.question: What is the responsibility of ministries regarding acting appointments?
options: They must inform the Federal Civil Service Commission about the acting appointment.
They must obtain approval from the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
They must conduct a fresh promotion exercise.

They must give a written test to the officer.
□
items.explanation:
Rule 020707 specifies that ministries "shall be required to notify the Federal Civil 
Service Commission when officers revert from acting appointments."

items.question: What happens if an officer on an acting appointment proceeds on casual or special leave?
options:
The officer will not be regarded as relinquishing the duties and responsibilities of the acting 
appointment.
items.explanation:
Rule 020708 states that an acting officer "will not be regarded as relinquishing the 
duties and responsibilities of the acting appointment" when proceeding on a casual or special 
leave.

items.question: Who is responsible for making acting appointments for a duty post below SGL 14?
options: The Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office
The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation
This is not explicitly stated in the provided text.
items.explanation:
Rule 020701 only specifies that acting appointments are made for a duty post not 
lower than SGL

items.question: When does a cessation of an acting appointment not happen?
options: When the substantive holder of a duty post resumes.
When the acting officer takes up another post.
When the acting officer proceeds on casual or special leave.

When the acting officer is promoted.
Q 32
items.explanation:
Rule 020708 states that an acting officer will not be regarded as relinquishing the 
duties of the acting appointment when on casual or special leave70.

items.question: Which of the following is true about acting appointments?
options: They are made only when an officer is due for promotion.
They are only for a duration of six months.
They are not to be made unless absolutely necessary.

They are made for all grade levels.
Q
items.explanation:
Rule 020702 states that an acting appointment should "not be made unless it is 
necessary."
Part 10: Promotion

items.question:
What is the authority responsible for the promotion of all posts in the Federal Civil Service, 
except for Permanent Secretaries?
options: The Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation
items.explanation:
Rule 020801(a) states that promotion to all posts in the Federal Civil Service, 
other than those of Permanent Secretaries, is vested in the Federal Civil Service Commission.

items.question:
What is the minimum number of years an officer must spend on a post on GL. 06 and 
below before being considered for promotion?
items.explanation:
Rule 020802(c) states the minimum number of years an officer must spend on 
GL-06 and below is two years.

items.question: How is promotion to all posts made?
options:
Strictly on the basis of competitive merit from amongst all eligible candidates
□ 33
items.explanation:
Rule 020802(d) states that promotion shall be made "strictly on the basis of 
competitive merit from amongst all eligible candidates."

items.question: The promotion of officers on GL. 07-14 is subject to confirmation by which body?
options: The Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office
The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation

The promotion committee of the MDA.
□
items.explanation:
Rule 020802(e)(ii) states that promotion on GL 07-14 is subject to "confirmation 
by the Federal Civil Service Commission/Board.

items.question: What is the effective date of a promotion?
options: The date of the promotion exercise
The date determined by the Permanent Secretary

The 1st of January of the year in which the promotion exercise was conducted or as may be 
determined by the Federal Civil Service Commission.
The date the officer assumes duty in the new post a
items.explanation:
Rule 020806 states that the effective date of promotion "shall be 1st January of 
the year for which the promotion exercise was conducted or as may be determined by the 
Federal Civil Service Commission."

items.question:
Who is responsible for compiling a list of all eligible candidates before each promotion 
exercise?
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation
items.explanation:
Rule 020803 states that before each promotion exercise, the appropriate office 
"shall compile a list of all eligible candidates to be considered."

items.question: How is the promotion of a contract officer handled?
options: A contract officer is not eligible for promotion.
A contract officer can be promoted if they have served for a long time.

A contract officer should be considered for an enhanced appointment during the 
renegotiation of their contract.
A contract officer is promoted through a different process.a 34
items.explanation:
Rule 020809 states that a contract officer should be considered for an enhanced 
appointment during the "renegotiation of his contract."

items.question:
What is the minimum number of years of service required for an officer to be eligible for the 
post of Permanent Secretary?
options:
The document does not explicitly state a number of years.
a
items.explanation:
Rule 020811 outlines the eligibility criteria for Permanent Secretaries but does not 
specify a minimum number of years of service

items.question: Who serves as resource persons during the selection process for permanent secretaries?
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
The Civil/Public Service and Private institutions.

The Federal Civil Service Commission.
a
items.explanation:
Rule 020813 states that "Resource persons from the Civil/Public Service and 
Private Institutions may be involved in the selection process."
Part 11: Leaving the Service

items.question:
What happens to the appointment of an officer during their probationary period if the 
authority empowered to appoint them is not satisfied with their efficiency or conduct?
options:
The officer is given a warning.
□ 35
items.explanation:
Rule 020901 states that an appointment may be terminated if the authority 
empowered to appoint the officer "is not satisfied about the officer's efficiency or his conduct has 
not been good."

items.question: What is the period of notice for relinquishing an appointment during a probationary period?
options: A month's notice or payment of one month's salary in lieu of notice.
items.explanation:
Rule 020902 states that an officer may relinquish their appointment by giving "one 
month's notice, or by paying one month's salary in lieu of notice."

items.question: An officer may be dismissed from service for what reason?
items.explanation:
Rule 020903 defines "termination" but does not explicitly mention "dismissal" in the 
provided text. However, Rule 100407 defines dismissal and its effects. This question is a 
knowledge gap in the provided text. Rule 020212 mentions that an act of serious misconduct 
may include dismissal from the service. Rule 100401 defines gross misconduct. Therefore, the 
most appropriate answer is "Gross misconduct."

items.question: What is the procedure for an officer who wishes to resign from the service?
options: They must submit their resignation letter to the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
They must apply for a leave of absence.
They must give one month's notice of their intention to resign.
They must give three months' notice.a
items.explanation:
Rule 020904 states that an officer who wishes to resign must give "a month's 
notice."

items.question:
What happens to an officer who leaves the service on the grounds of failing a compulsory 
examination?
options: The officer is eligible to reapply immediately.
The officer will not be regarded as having been dismissed from the service.
The officer is given another chance to take the exam.
items.explanation:
Rule 020906 states that an officer who fails a compulsory examination "shall not 
be regarded as having been dismissed from the service."

items.question: What is the mandatory retirement age for all grades in the Service?
options: 60 years or 35 years of pensionable service, whichever is earlier
items.explanation:
Rule 020908 states that the mandatory retirement age for all grades in the 
Service is "60 years or 35 years of pensionable service, whichever is earlier."

items.question: Which of the following is a ground for leaving the service?
items.explanation: The rules in Section 9 cover all these topics as reasons for leaving the service89.

items.question: What is the tenure policy for a director or its equivalent?
options: They shall hold office for a term of eight years.
They shall retire after 35 years of service.
items.explanation:
Rule 020909 states that a director or its equivalent shall hold office for "a term of 
eight years and renewable for a further term of four years."

items.question:
An officer who has relinquished their appointment during their probationary period is 
required to refund the cost of training, if any, given to them. What is the period for this refund?
options: At the time of leaving the service

At the time of leaving the service, the officer is liable to refund the cost of any training given 
to them.
□ 37
items.explanation:
Rule 020902 states that if a training has been given, the officer is liable to "refund 
the cost of any training given to them."

items.question:
What is the consequence for an officer whose office is no longer required due to the 
abolition of office?
options: They will be redeployed to another office.
They will be compulsorily retired from the Service.
They will be offered a new appointment.

They will be placed on a leave of absence.
□
items.explanation:
Rule 020907 states that an officer whose service is no longer required due to the 
abolition of office "shall be required to leave the Service."
Part 12: Mandatory Contribution to Pension Scheme

items.question: What is the main purpose of Rule 021002?
options: To ensure officers receive their retirement benefits promptly.
To process the retirement of an officer.
To access contributions to the National Housing Fund.
To provide a schedule for non-pensionable appointments.3
items.explanation:
Rule 021002 states that "In order to ensure that officers receive their retirement 
benefits promptly, Departmental Pension Officers are required to forward to the Pension 
Commission (PENCOM) an up-to-date record of service."

items.question: Which of the following is NOT a requirement for pension payment?
options: Original and photocopy of the gazetted publication of the Letter of First Appointment.
Original and photocopy of Transfer and Acceptance of Service.
Original and photocopy of birth certificate.

Evidence of a valid national passport.
3
items.explanation:
Rule 021002 lists several documents required for pension payment, but a valid
38
national passport is not among them. However, it does require a National Identification Number 
(NIN).

items.question: What is the purpose of Rule 021004?
options: To provide a framework for pension contributions.
To outline the requirements for pension payments.

To enable officers who exit the Service to access their National Housing Funds (NHF) 
contributions.
items.explanation:
Rule 021004 states that officers who exit the service "shall access their National 
Housing Funds (NHF) contributions within one month of retirement."

items.question: How are the requirements for pension payments processed?
options: The officer must personally submit the documents to PENCOM.
The Departmental Pension Officer forwards the required documents to PENCOM.
The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation sends the documents.

The ministry/extra-ministerial office submits the documents.
a
items.explanation:
Rule 021002 states that Departmental Pension Officers are required to forward to 
the Pension Commission (PENCOM) an up-to-date record of service of these officers.

items.question:
What is the process for an officer to access their National Housing Fund (NHF) 
contributions?
options: The officer must apply directly to the National Housing Fund.
The officer must wait for a circular from the Head of Civil Service of the Federation.
The officer must provide a stamped death certificate.

The officer must exit the service and can access their contributions within one month of 
retirement.
a
items.explanation:
Rule 021004 states that officers who exit the Service "shall access their National 
Housing Funds (NHF) contributions within one month of retirement."

items.question:
The provision for mandatory contribution to the pension scheme is for which type of 
officers?
items.explanation:
Rule 021001 states that "All pensionable officers shall participate in the 
Contributory Pension Scheme."

items.question: The requirements for pension payment include evidence of which of the following?
options: Evidence of a personal bank account a
items.explanation:
Rule 021002(j) lists "Evidence of National Identification Number (NIN)" as one of 
the requirements for pension payment.

items.question:
Who is responsible for forwarding departmental files and other documents necessary for 
processing retirement to PENCOM?
options: The Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office
The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation
items.explanation:
Rule 021003 states that "Forwarding of Departmental files and other documents 
not expressly mentioned in Rule 021002 shall no longer be a condition for processing of 
retirement benefits." This implies that the Departmental Pension Officer is responsible for the 
overall processing. The previous rule, 021002, mentions that Departmental Pension Officers are 
required to forward up-to-date records.
Part 13: Certificate of Service

items.question: What is the purpose of issuing a Certificate of Service?
options: To provide a record of an officer's salary.
To provide a record of an officer's leave.

To be used as a reference, covering the holder's Public Service when seeking other 
employment.
40
To provide a summary of the officer's performance.3
items.explanation:
Rule 021103 states that the main purpose of issuing a Certificate of Service is that 
it "may be used as a reference, covering the holder's public service when seeking other 
employment."

items.question: To whom is the Certificate of Service issued?
options: All officers, including those on non-pensionable appointments, on leaving the service.
Only to officers on GL. 07 and above.

Only to officers on GL. 06 and below.
□
items.explanation:
Rule 021101 states that "all officers, including those of non-pensionable 
appointments, shall be issued, on leaving the Service, a certificate of service.

items.question: The Certificate of Service is issued to officers on GL. 07 and above using which form?

Who is responsible for the countersignature of the Certificate of Service for officers holding 
senior posts?
options:
The Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office and the Federal Civil Service 
Commission.
The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
items.explanation:
Rule 021102 states that the Certificate of Service is "to be issued to Officers 
holding senior posts on GL.07 and above and requires the counter-signature of the Permanent 
Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office and the Federal Civil Service Commission.

items.question: What is the number of the rule concerning the objective of issuing a Certificate of Service?
items.explanation: Rule 021103 is titled "Objective.

items.question: In how many duplicates is a Certificate of Service rendered?
items.explanation: Rule 021104 states that the Certificate of Service "shall be rendered in triplicate.

items.question: Which form is used for the Certificate of Service for officers on GL. 06 and below?
items.explanation:
Rule 021104 specifies that for officers on GL. 06 and below, the form is "Form 
Gen. 58A."

items.question:
An action to issue a Certificate of Service should be taken in time for the Certificate to be 
handed to the recipient before they proceed on leave prior to leaving the Service. Which rule 
states this?
items.explanation:
Rule 021105 states that the action to issue a Certificate of Service "should be 
taken in time for the Certificate to be handed to the recipient before he proceeds on leave prior 
to leaving the Service.

items.question: What is the rule that outlines the forms of a Certificate of Service?
items.explanation: Rule 021104 is titled "Forms of Certificate of Service" and lists the different forms.

items.question: Who is required to countersign the Certificate of Service for officers on GL. 06 and below?
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation
items.explanation:
Rule 021102 states that for officers on GL. 06 and below, the certificate "requires 
the counter-signature of the Permanent Secretary/Career Management Officer (CMO).
Part 14: Schedule for Non-Pensionable Appointment

items.question: The term of engagement for a non-pensionable appointment commences from when?
options: The date of the offer of appointment.
The date the officer signs the contract.
The date of assumption of duty.

The date the contract is approved.
Q
items.explanation:
Rule 021201 states that the term of engagement "is from the date of assumption 
of duty."

items.question: The duties of an officer on a non-pensionable appointment shall include what?
options: The usual duties of the office to which the officer is appointed.
Any other duties the government may call upon the officer to perform.

Only the usual duties of the office.
□
items.explanation:
Rule 021203 states that the duties of the person engaged "shall include the usual 
duties of the Office to which he is appointed and any other duties which the Government may 
call upon him to perform.

items.question: An officer on a non-pensionable appointment must not directly or indirectly receive what?
options:
Commission or profits of any kind, but will devote the whole of their time to the service of the 
Government.
items.explanation:
Rule 021204 states that the person engaged shall not "either directly or indirectly 
be engaged in or concerned with any other service or business whatsoever or receive 
commission or profits of any kind.

items.question:
What happens to the emoluments of a non-pensionable officer if they are deemed to have 
been unsatisfactory?
options:
The emoluments will not be granted if the efficiency conduct and diligence of the person 
engaged have been satisfactory.
items.explanation:
Rule 021205(ii) states that emoluments "shall not be granted unless the efficiency, 
conduct, and diligence of the person engaged have been satisfactory.

items.question:
What happens if a non-pensionable officer is compelled to resign due to ill health not 
caused by their own negligence?
options: The officer will not receive any salary.
The officer shall receive such salary as may be due up to the date of such resignation.
The officer will receive a full month's salary.

The officer will receive a severance allowance.
a
items.explanation:
Rule 021207 states that if an officer is compelled to resign due to ill health, "he 
shall pay such salary as may be due up to the date of such resignation or the date on which a 
certificate in that respect was issued.

items.question: What is the process for terminating a non-pensionable appointment?
options:
The engagement may be terminated by giving one month's notice or paying one month's 
salary in lieu of notice.
The engagement may be terminated without notice.
The engagement may be terminated at the discretion of the Permanent Secretary.

The engagement may be terminated by paying three months' salary.
a
items.explanation:
Rule 021210 states that the engagement "may at any time terminate the 
engagement of the person engaged by giving three months' notice in writing or by paying one 
month's salary in lieu of notice.

items.question: What is the consequence of an officer's failure to comply with an order, regulation, or duty?
options:
The officer will receive a warning.
44

The officer may be dismissed, and any rights and privileges accrued to him by his agreement 
shall cease.

The officer will be transferred.
a
items.explanation:
Rule 021209 states that in case of any failure to comply with any order, the 
person "shall be dismissed, and all rights and privileges accrued to him by this agreement shall 
cease.

items.question:
What is the timing for an officer on a non-pensionable appointment to express their desire 
for further employment?
options: One month before the expiration of their agreement.
Four months before the date of expiration of their agreement.
Three months before the date of expiration of their agreement.

Six months before the date of expiration of their agreement.
□
items.explanation:
Rule 021212 states that the person engaged shall give a written notice to the 
government stating their desire to remain in employment "four months before the date of 
expiration of agreement.

items.question: What is one of the conditions for a non-pensionable officer to be granted leave?
options: The rules do not provide for leave.
They are entitled to annual leave as per the rules.
They are entitled to leave in accordance with the provisions of Chapter Twelve of the Rules.

The leave is at the discretion of the Permanent Secretary.
Q
items.explanation:
Rule 021214 states that the person engaged "shall be entitled to leave in 
accordance with the provisions of Chapter Twelve.

items.question:
What is the legal requirement for a non-pensionable officer to make good any pecuniary 
damage arising from their negligence or failure to comply with an order?
options: The officer is liable to have a deduction from their salary to make good the damage.
The officer must pay the full amount upfront.
items.explanation:
Rule 021211 states that in the event of any pecuniary damage arising from the 
person engaged disregarding or failing to comply with an order, "he may be liable to a deduction 
from his salary to make good the damage or any part thereof.
45

items.question:
What is the consequence of failing to pass the compulsory confirmation examinations for 
clerical grade officers?
options: The officer will have their probationary period extended.
The officer may have their appointment terminated at the end of the probationary period.
items.explanation:
Rule 020303 specifies that an officer on probation is required to pass the 
prescribed examination, and failure to do so may lead to the termination of the appointment.
Prescribed Examination for Confirmation
Part 15: General

items.question:
According to the Public Service Rules, who is responsible for the conduct and supervision 
of compulsory confirmation examinations?
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation
The Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Department

The Federal Civil Service Commission
D, The Examination Boarda
items.explanation:
Rule 030102 states that the Permanent Secretary or Head of an Extra-Ministerial 
Department is responsible for the conduct and supervision of these examinations.

items.question:
What is the consequence for an officer who fails to pass the compulsory confirmation 
examination within the stipulated period?
options: The officer is granted an extension to retake the exam.
The officer is demoted to a lower grade level.

The officer is suspended without pay.
□
items.explanation:
Rule 030104 clearly states that failure to pass the examination within the 
specified time shall result in the termination of the officer's appointment.

items.question:
For a probationary officer, how often should the compulsory confirmation examinations be 
held?
options:
As stipulated by the Examination Board
Q 46
items.explanation:
Rule 030106 specifies that these examinations shall be held at intervals of not 
more than six months.

items.question:
Which of the following professional officers are exempted from passing the compulsory 
confirmation examination?
items.explanation:
Rule 030109 grants an exemption from the compulsory confirmation 
examination to law professionals.

items.question:
When is an officer who is a confirmed member of the Junior Staff Committee (JSC) or 
Senior Staff Committee (SSC) required to sit for the compulsory confirmation examination?
options: Only if they are a new recruit.
They are not required to sit for any compulsory confirmation examination.
They must sit for the examination regardless of their committee membership.

The Permanent Secretary may grant them a waiver.
Ei
items.explanation:
According to Rule 020306, officers who are confirmed members of JSC or SSC 
are exempted from the Compulsory Confirmation Examination.

items.question:
Which of the following materials are allowed to be used by candidates during the 
compulsory confirmation examinations?
options: All Public Service Rules and Financial Regulations.
Reference books specified by the Examination Board.
items.explanation:
Rule 030108 specifies that candidates may be allowed to use specified 
reference books during the examinations.

items.question:
The Chairman of the Examination Board for the confirmation examinations shall be of 
what rank?
items.explanation: Rule 030103 states that the Examination Board shall be headed by a Director

items.question:
What is the consequence for an officer who fails to present themselves for a compulsory 
confirmation examination without an acceptable reason?
options: The officer is given another chance to write the exam.
The officer is suspended for a month.
The officer's appointment is liable to be terminated.
items.explanation:
According to Rule 030105, an officer who fails to present himself/herself for the 
examination without an acceptable reason is liable to have their appointment terminated.
Part 16: Compulsory Confirmation Examinations for Senior Executive Officers, 
Administrative Officers and Professionals (COMPRO I & II)

items.question:
Which officers are required to pass the Compulsory Confirmation Examination (COMPRO I
& II) for senior executive, administrative, and professional roles?
options: All officers on GL 08 and above.
Only officers on GL 10 and above.
Officers on GL 08-14 who are on probation.
All officers on probation, regardless of grade level.3 .
items.explanation:
Rule 030201 specifies that officers on GL 08-14 who are on probation are 
required to pass the Compulsory Confirmation Examination (COMPRO I & II)12.

items.question:
The syllabus for the compulsory confirmation examination for senior officers is 
determined by which body?
options: The Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF)
The Permanent Secretary of the MDA
items.explanation:
Rule 030202 refers to the syllabus for the examination, which is prepared by the 
Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation13.

items.question: COMPRO I is for officers on what grade levels?
items.explanation:
The syllabus section of the document specifies that COMPRO I is for officers on 
grade levels 08 and 09, and COMPRO II is for officers on grade levels 10-14

items.question: An officer on GL 11 is required to sit for which compulsory confirmation examination?
options:
Neither, as they are senior officers.
□
items.explanation:
The rules state that COMPRO II is for officers on Grade Levels 10-14, which 
includes an officer on GL

items.question: 163. How many papers must an officer on GL 09 pass to be confirmed?
items.explanation:
The syllabus in Rule 030202 lists 5 papers for COMPRO I, which officers on GL 
08-09 must pass to be confirmed.

items.question: For the COMPRO examinations, what is the pass mark for each subject?
items.explanation:
The syllabus section for COMPRO examinations indicates a pass mark of 50% in 
each subject.

items.question:
An officer on GL 14 must sit for how many subjects in the compulsory confirmation 
examination?
items.explanation:
The syllabus for COMPRO II (for officers on GL 10-14) lists four subjects: Public 
Service Rules, Financial Regulations, General Knowledge, and Civil Service Reforms18.

items.question:
What is the maximum number of sittings an officer is allowed for the compulsory 
confirmation examination?
items.explanation:
The rules do not explicitly state this; however, it is a well-known provision of the
PSR
Part 17: Compulsory Confirmation/Promotion Examinations for Junior Officers 
(COMPRO II)

items.question:
What is the primary purpose of the Compulsory Confirmation/Promotion Examination for 
Junior Officers?
options: To prepare them for promotion to senior posts
To confirm their appointment and/or promote them
items.explanation:
The title of the section itself, "Compulsory Confirmation/Promotion 
Examinations," indicates its dual purpose20.

items.question:
Which of the following is NOT a subject for junior officers in the Compulsory 
Confirmation/Promotion Examinations?
items.explanation:
Rule 030301 lists the subjects for junior officers as Public Service Rules, 
Financial Regulations, and General Knowledge. Public Administration is not included.
50

items.question:
An officer on Gracie Level 05, who is a junior officer, needs to pass which examination to 
be confirmed in their appointment?
items.explanation:
Section 3 is titled "Compulsory Confirmation/Promotion Examinations for Junior 
Officers (COMPRO II)," which applies to officers on GL 06 and below.

items.question:
What is the implication of passing the Compulsory Confirmation Examination for Junior 
Officers at an accelerated level?
options: The officer is immediately promoted to a senior post.
The officer is exempted from future promotion examinations.
The officer is granted an accelerated promotion to the next grade Ievel23.

The officer receives a cash reward.
Q .
items.explanation:
Rule 030302 discusses a "Pass at Accelerated Level" for junior officers, which 
leads to accelerated promotion.

items.question: The number of papers a junior officer must pass to be confirmed is:
items.explanation:
Rule 030301 lists three subjects for the examination: Public Service Rules, 
Financial Regulations, and General Knowledge25.

items.question:
Which body is responsible for preparing the syllabus for the compulsory confirmation 
examinations for junior officers?
options: Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF)
items.explanation:
The syllabus for junior officers' examinations is prepared by the OHCSF, similar
51
to the one for senior officers.

items.question:
An officer on probation who fails to pass the Compulsory Confirmation/Promotion 
Examination for Junior Officers is liable to what consequence?
items.explanation:
Rule 030104, which is a general rule for confirmation examinations, applies to 
all officers, including junior officers, stating that failure to pass leads to termination of 
appointment

items.question:
What is the required pass mark for each subject in the Compulsory 
Confirmation/Promotion Examination for Junior Officers?
items.explanation:
The pass mark for each subject in the junior officers' examination is 50%, 
consistent with the senior officers' examination
Part 18: Police and Para-Military Services Confirmation/Promotion Examinations

items.question:
What is the primary requirement for officers in police and paramilitary services with 
regard to confirmation and promotion examinations?
options: They must pass the same examinations as civil servants.
They must pass the examinations prescribed by their respective service commissions.
They are exempted from all confirmation examinations.

They are required to take an oral interview instead of an examination.
Q
items.explanation:
Rule 030401 states that officers of police and paramilitary services are required 
to pass the examinations prescribed by their respective service commissions.

items.question:
The responsibility for the conduct and supervision of these examinations for police and 
paramilitary services lies with whom?
options: The Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation

The Permanent Secretary of their ministry
□
items.explanation:
The rule specifies that these examinations are "prescribed by their respective 
Service Commissions," implying that the commissions also conduct and supervise them.

items.question:
Can a police officer on probation be confirmed without passing the prescribed 
examination?
options: Yes, if they have an excellent service record.
Yes, if they have a recommendation from their superior officer.
No, it is a compulsory requirement for confirmation.

Yes, if they are promoted to a higher rank.
□
items.explanation:
The title of the section and the rule itself refer to "Confirmation/Promotion 
Examinations," making it a mandatory requirement for the relevant officers.

items.question:
What is the effect of failing the confirmation examination for a paramilitary officer on 
probation?
options: They are transferred to another unit.
items.explanation:
The general rule (030104) on failing confirmation examinations applies here, 
leading to termination of appointment

items.question: Which of the following bodies is not a "paramilitary service" in the context of this section?
items.explanation:
The Federal Ministry of Finance is a civil service ministry, not a paramilitary 
service.

items.question:
The examinations for police and paramilitary services are primarily designed to test an 
officer's knowledge of:
options: Laws and regulations governing their respective services.
items.explanation:
The purpose of the examinations is to ensure officers are competent in the rules 
and regulations specific to their services

items.question:
Are officers in the police and paramilitary services required to sit for the Compulsory 
Confirmation Examination (COMPRO) I or II for civil servants?
options: Yes, they must sit for both.
Yes, they must sit for COMPRO II.
No, they have their own prescribed examinations.
items.explanation:
Rule 030401 specifies that they must pass the examinations prescribed by their 
respective service commissions, not the civil service COMPRO exams

items.question:
What is the main objective of the compulsory examinations for police and paramilitary 
officers?
options: To ensure officers are conversant with their service's rules, regulations, and laws.
items.explanation:
This is the core purpose of all confirmation examinations in the PSR.
Part 19: Officers Who Are Required to Pass the Compulsory Confirmation Examinations

items.question: Who is required to pass the compulsory confirmation examinations under this section?
options: All officers in the Public Service.
Officers on pensionable appointment who are on probation.
Only officers on GL 06 and below.
Only officers on GL 07 and above.3
items.explanation:
Rule 030501 explicitly states that all officers on a pensionable appointment who 
are on probation are required to pass the examinations40.

items.question:
When can an officer's compulsory confirmation examination result be used for the 
purpose of promotion?
54
options: Never, as it is only for confirmation.
Only for promotion from junior to senior post.
Only for promotion to the next grade level.

Only when the officer has passed at an accelerated level.
□
items.explanation:
Rule 030502 states that the COMPRO result can be used for promotion from a 
junior to a senior post, subject to other conditions.

items.question:
What is the condition for using the COMPRO result for promotion from a junior to a senior 
post?
options: The officer must have passed the exam on their first attempt.
The officer must have served for a minimum of two years.
The officer must meet other eligibility criteria for the senior post.

The officer must be recommended by the Permanent Secretary.
□
items.explanation:
Rule 030502 outlines that the result is used as a prerequisite, but the officer 
must still meet other conditions for promotion.

items.question:
An officer who is on probation on a pensionable appointment but is a confirmed member 
of the JSC or SSC is required to sit for the confirmation examinations:
options: Yes, because it is a mandatory requirement.
No, they are exempted from the examination.
Yes, but they only need to pass a single paper.

The rules are silent on this matter.
□
items.explanation: Rule 020306, referenced in my analysis, states that such officers are exempted

items.question:
For an officer to be confirmed in their appointment, they must pass the prescribed 
examination within how many years?
options:
As specified by the Examination Board
E3
items.explanation:
Rule 020301 specifies that the probationary period for an officer is two years, 
within which they must pass the examination to be confirmed

items.question:
What is the implication of an officer on a pensionable appointment failing to take the
55
compulsory confirmation examination?
options: The officer is given a warning.
The officer is liable to have their appointment terminated.
items.explanation:
This is a direct application of Rule 030105, which states that a failure to take the 
exam without good reason makes the officer liable to termination47.

items.question:
If an officer successfully passes the compulsory confirmation examination, what is the 
next step towards confirmation?
options: The Permanent Secretary reviews their performance and recommends confirmation.
They are promoted to the next grade level.

They are given a special bonus.
□
items.explanation:
Rule 020303 states that an officer is confirmed at the end of their probationary 
period upon passing the prescribed examination and a recommendation from the Permanent 
Secretary

items.question:
Can a contract officer on a non-pensionable appointment be required to sit for the 
compulsory confirmation examination?
options: Yes, it is mandatory for all officers.
No, as the examination is for officers on pensionable appointments.
Yes, but only if they desire to convert to a pensionable appointment.

Yes, if specified in their contract.
□
items.explanation:
The rule (030501) specifies that the examination is for officers on a 
"pensionable appointment who are on probation," thus excluding non-pensionable contract 
staff.
Part 20: Fees for Invigilators and Examiners

items.question:
Who is authorized to approve the fees for invigilators and examiners for the compulsory 
confirmation examinations?
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation

The Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF)
56
Q
items.explanation:
Rule 030601 states that the examination fees shall be determined by the Office 
of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation

items.question: The payment of fees to invigilators and examiners is typically based on what?
options: The number of questions they set.
A flat rate for all examinations.
The number of candidates they supervise or papers they mark.

The grade level of the officers they are examining.
□
items.explanation:
While not explicitly detailed, examination fees are conventionally tied to the 
work performed, such as marking papers or invigilating candidates, which is a standard 
practice in similar contexts

items.question:
Is the payment of examination fees for invigilators and examiners a mandatory provision 
of the Public Service Rules?
options: No, it is at the discretion of the Head of Service.
Yes, the fees are a formal part of the rules.
No, it is paid from a separate departmental budget.

The rules do not address this.
□
items.explanation:
Section 6 is dedicated to this topic, indicating that it is a formal and mandatory 
provision of the rules

items.question: The fees paid to invigilators and examiners are typically separate from what other fees?
options: The transport allowances of the candidates.
The fees for setting the questions.
The fees paid by the candidates for the examination.

The invigilators' and examiners' regular salaries.
□
items.explanation:
Examination fees are a form of extra-duty allowance and are separate from an 
officer's regular salary

items.question: The payment of fees to invigilators and examiners aims to:
options: Encourage more people to become examiners.
Compensate them for the extra duties they perform.
Fund the operations of the Examination Board.

Reduce the cost of running examinations.
57
Q
items.explanation:
The fees are a form of remuneration for the additional work and time spent on 
the examinations

items.question:
What document should be used to guide the payment of fees to invigilators and 
examiners?
options: A circular issued by the OHCSF

The annual budget of the MDA
Q
items.explanation:
Rule 030601 indicates that the fees are determined by the OHCSF, which would 
then communicate the rates via circulars

items.question: The fees for invigilators and examiners are for:
options: All examinations conducted in the public service.
items.explanation:
This section is specifically titled "Fees for Invigilators and Examiners" under the
