chapter on "Prescribed Examination for Confirmation," indicating its scope.

items.question:
Is an officer who is a member of the Examination Board automatically eligible to receive 
invigilator or examiner fees?
options: No, only those who perform the duties of invigilation or examination are eligible.
Yes, as long as they are a director.
The rules are silent on this.a
items.explanation:
The fees are for the performance of the duties of invigilation and examining, not 
merely for being on the board.

items.question: The payment of fees for invigilators and examiners is to be processed and paid:
options: After the examination results are released.

As soon as the claim is submitted and approved.
58
Q
items.explanation:
The general principle of financial management in the public service is that 
payments are made upon approval of a verified claim.

items.question:
Who is ultimately responsible for ensuring that the approved fees are paid to the 
deserving invigilators and examiners?
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation
The Permanent Secretary of the MDA

The Director of Human Resources
Q
items.explanation:
The Permanent Secretary is responsible for the overall supervision of the 
examinations, including the payment of fees
59
Section B:
COMPUTER-BASED TEST (CBT) PROMOTION EXAMINATION FOR OFFICERS IN 
GRADES 15 TO 17 IN THE FEDERAL CIVIL SERVICE OF THE FEDERATION ON

Instruction: This examination consists of 100 multiple-choice questions designed to assess your 
readiness for promotion to the next level in the Federal Civil Service. You have exactly one 
hour (60 minutes) to complete all questions; manage your time wisely to ensure you attempt 
every question. Read each question carefully before selecting the best answer from the options.
Part 1: Employment Consideration for Applicants Above 50 Years in the Public Service

items.question:
A 51-year-old applicant applies for a permanent and pensionable post in your organization. 
What should be your first observation?
options: He can be appointed without restrictions.

This applicant is well above the mandatory age limit for permanent and pensionable 
appointment into the public service.
Age is not considered in civil service employment.

items.question: What is the official age range for eligibility into permanent civil service appointments?
options: A minimum of 18 years and a maximum of 50 years

items.question: What type of employment is the 51-year-old applicant most likely qualified for?
options: Contract appointment, if he is a Nigerian and has requested for it

items.question: If the 51-year-old applicant had retired previously, at what level can he be re-engaged?
options: A grade level below the one on which he retired

items.question:
What is a key condition for a person aged 50 or above to be considered for contract 
employment in the civil service?
options: Being related to a senior official
Possession of special skills and being at least 50 years old

items.question: Which of the following must be observed before engaging the applicant on contract?
options: All procedures governing contract of appointment must be followed

items.question:
What should the schedule officer recommend to the director regarding the 51-year-old 
applicant?
options: Inform the director that contract appointment is the only legal option

items.question: Which of the following best describes the age-based disqualification of the applicant?
options: He is above the mandatory age limit for permanent and pensionable employment.

items.question: What must be documented before a 51-year-old applicant can be engaged on contract?
options: Fulfillment of all procedures for contract appointment

items.question: When can a retired officer be re-engaged into the public service?
options: On a grade below their previous rank, and if all contract rules are followed

Only through union elections
Part 2: Understanding Recruitment

items.question: According to the Public Service Rules, which of the following best defines recruitment?
options: Transfer of officers from one ministry to another
Filling of vacancies by appointing persons not already in the civil service
Promotion from junior to senior cadre

items.question: Recruitment in the civil service excludes:
options: Transfer of officers already in the service

items.question: The rule that defines recruitment in the Public Service Rules is:

options: Persons not already in the civil service

items.question: Which of the following is not part of the recruitment process under PSR 020201?

options:
Civil service exit process
Part 3: What is Employment?

items.question: What does "employment" mean in the context of civil service?
options: Engagement into the service based on already acquired knowledge
Being part of a labor union

items.question: In public service, employment implies that the officer:
options: Has sufficient knowledge to begin work without formal training
Will be posted to rural areas only

items.question: Employment in the civil service is based on:
options: Possession of already acquired skills relevant to the job

Attending 3-month bootcamp
Part 4: What is an authorized establishment?
62

items.question: What does "authorized establishment" mean in the public service?
options: Number of staff an officer can supervise
Total budget allocated to an agency
Approved structure and manning level allowed for an organization

Number of training sessions per year
Part 5: Sources of Consequential Vacancies

items.question: Which of the following is a source of consequential vacancy in the public service?
When an officer is promoted, what happens to their previous position?
options: It is handed over to the subordinate.

items.question: Retirement of an officer creates:
Which of the following does not typically result in a consequential vacancy?
A transfer that leads to an empty position in a department is known as:
options:
Leave relief:
Part 6: Declaring Vacancy Positions in the Public Service

items.question: What is a primary method of identifying a vacancy in the public service?
options: Comparing approved establishment with number in post

items.question: One of the indicators for declaring a vacancy is:
options: Number of positions arising from retirement

items.question: A post becomes vacant due to promotion when:
options: The promoted officer leaves the service.
The officer moves up, leaving the previous position unoccupied.

items.question: Death of an officer may lead to:
options: The promotion of the subordinate officer

items.question: Which of the following may be used to declare a vacancy not previously budgeted?
When planning recruitment, which factor must be considered first?
Which tool helps in determining excess or shortage of staff?
options: Establishment versus number in post analysis

items.question: Promotion exercises may lead to:
options: Creation of vacancies at lower levels

items.question: A waiver is important when:
options: No one is willing to apply.

A position must be filled outside regular establishment control.
64

items.question: Who is responsible for tracking and declaring vacancies for APD action?
options: Officer in charge of Appointment, Promotion, and Discipline (APD)
Head of Service of the Federation

Permanent Secretary and heads of departments
Part 7: Classes of Direct Appointment into the Federal Civil Service

items.question: Which of the following is a class of direct appointment into the Federal Civil Service?
A person appointed on probation is placed in what type of post?
A non-pensionable contract appointment is:
options: A direct appointment to a non-pensionable post for a specific period

items.question: A special appointment in the civil service usually refers to:
options: Unique roles not part of the regular structure

items.question: A pupil officer is usually:
options: A new entrant employed for training purposes

items.question:
Which appointment class leads to eventual confirmation and full civil service benefits if 
successful?
options: On probation in a pensionable post

items.question: A person employed on a special appointment may not:
Non-pensionable contract appointments are valid for:
options: A specific period stated in the contract

items.question: One key feature of probationary appointment is:
options: Evaluation for confirmation into the permanent service

items.question: Direct appointment as a trainee means:
options: The officer is a political appointee.
The officer is undergoing learning or development on the job.

The officer is on a permanent post
Part 8: New Introductions in the Revised Public Service Rules (PSR)

items.question: What is the new tenure limit for directors in the revised PSR?
The tenure of a Permanent Secretary is now limited to:
What system is replacing APER in the revised PSR?
FCSSIP 25 introduced which of the following initiatives?

items.question: Which of the following types of leave was introduced in the new PSR?
Annual leave calculation has now been revised to:

options: E-governance using Webmail, WhatsApp, virtual meetings, etc.

items.question: Appointment criteria for Permanent Secretary is now:
options: Clearly prescribed in the new PSR

items.question: Which expense has been increased under the new PSR?
options: Burial expenses for officers who died in active service

items.question: Disciplinary procedures for offenses against state security are now:
options: Separated from general misconduct in the PSR

items.question: Which of the following is now given its own dedicated chapter in the revised PSR?

Which new type of employment has been introduced in the PSR?
Performance appraisals will now be guided by:
What is the purpose of FCSSIP 25?
The calculation of annual leave is now based on:
Increased burial expenses apply to:
options: Officers who die in active service

items.question: What replaces the old APER system?
How are serious misconduct and national security offenses now handled?
Who benefits from the new sabbatical and short-term appointment policy?
Recognition and reward systems in the PSR are based on:
Which of the following reforms promotes digital interaction in the workplace?
What is one major change to the management of training in the PSR?
options: It has been given a full chapter.

items.question: Who now has a formal disciplinary framework under the new PSR?
options:
N-Power staff
Part 9: Eligibility Criteria for Appointment into the Federal Civil Service

items.question:
What is the minimum age required to be eligible for permanent appointment in the Federal 
Civil Service?
What is the maximum age allowed for appointment into the Federal Civil Service?
To be eligible for appointment into the Federal Civil Service, an applicant must:
options: Have applied for the job as advertised
Be married to a civil servant

items.question:
For appointment into the Federal Civil Service, the valid certificate of state of origin must be 
signed by:

items.question: Which health-related document must an applicant for a Federal Civil Service job present?
options: Certificate of medical fitness from a certified healthcare provider

items.question: For appointment into the Federal Civil Service, good conduct must be attested by:
options: School or former employer through a testimonial

items.question: To be appointed into the Federal Civil Service, the applicant must possess:
options: A minimum educational qualification as required

items.question: Which of the following must be clearly stated by the applicant regarding criminal offenses?
options: Whether or not they have been convicted without prior FCSC approval
Whether their family has been arrested

items.question:
What employment details must be disclosed by the applicant for appointment into Federal 
Civil Service?
70
options: Record of previous employment and reason for leaving

items.question: Who verifies the certificate of origin during recruitment into the Federal Civil Service?
options: The recruiting office in line with public service rules

items.question:
The age and nationality requirement for appointment into the Federal Civil Service is backed 
by:
options: The Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria

items.question:
In accordance with FCSC standards, the eligibility criteria for appointment into the Federal 
Civil Service are aimed at:
options:
Limit access to youth
Performance Management System
Part 10: Progress Reports on Officers on Probation

items.question: What is the primary purpose of progress reports for officers on probation?
options: To assess suitability for confirmation or re-engagement

items.question:
How often are Permanent Secretaries/Heads of Extra-Ministerial Offices responsible for 
rendering Progress Reports?

What is the maximum period within which the Final Progress Report should be rendered 
after the first appointment?

If an officer's probationary period is due to expire when they are on leave, when should the 
Final Progress Report be rendered?

Who is responsible for addressing progress reports to the Permanent Secretary, Federal

The Officer on Probation
Part 11: Performance Management System for Serving Officers

items.question: What does PMS stand for in the context of the Public Service Rules?

What is the primary tool for individual performance tracking and assessment in the Federal 
Public Service?

Who is primarily responsible for detailing and candidly reporting on officers' capabilities and 
overall performance?
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation

items.question:
If a reporting officer is below one substantive grade above the officer being reported on, who 
should prepare the report?
options: The next senior officer directly above the Reporting Officer

The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation
72

items.question:
What action should be taken if there is any adverse comment on an officer's work or conduct 
included in a report?
options: The officer should be immediately dismissed.
The officer must be given a copy of the comment and acknowledge receipt.

The officer should be transferred to another department.
Part 12: Annual Performance Management Timeline

items.question: When does the Annual Performance Management Cycle typically run?
options: January to December of every year
April to March of the following year
July to June of the following year
October to September of the following year.

items.question: Who is responsible for finalizing the annual institutional and departmental goals and targets?
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation
The Department of Human Resource Management (DHRM)

items.question:
What are Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) used for in the Performance Management

options: To measure how a staff has performed within a calendar year

items.question: When should the final appraisal be done?
options: End of January of the following year
End of February of the following year
End of March of the following year
End of December of the current year

items.question:
What is the primary purpose of the Employee Performance Results in the Performance 
Management Cycle?
options:
To determine employee development options, rewards, sanctions, and other associated 
actions
To provide a basis for promotion

To assess the overall performance of the Ministry
Part 13: Performance Management Cycle

items.question:
Who is primarily accountable for ensuring the successful implementation of the PMS across 
all levels of officers?
73
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation
The Accounting Officer of every Federal Public Service Institution

items.question:
What is the first step in the Individual Performance Planning phase of the Performance 
Management Cycle?
options: Job Descriptions (JDs) should be available for each job.

items.question:
What is the purpose of challenging but achievable tasks and performance targets derived 
from the MDA's strategic objectives?

To provide a basis for salary increments.

options: Both the Officer and the Supervisor

Instruction: This examination consists of 100 multiple-choice questions designed to assess

Performance Management System

items.question: What is the aim of performance monitoring?
options: To provide continuous, interactive, and transparent feedback

items.question: Who is responsible for the performance review with the employee?

What should a Performance Review and Appraiser discuss regarding the Appraisee's 
performance?

What is the purpose of identifying further support needed by the Appraisee during the 
performance review?
options: To ensure continuous improvement and development

Performance Appraisals should be done to evaluate progress with achievement of targets on 
a periodic basis, leading to what?

Self-appraisal and further appraisal by a Supervisor or such individual saddled with this 
responsibility

The Accounting Officers of respective MDAs and the Head of the Civil Service of the 
Federation

An appraisal outcome which is below average and/or not considered to be in line with

An Officer who consistently exceeds expectations

An Officer who refuses to take on new duties

Promptly gain an understanding of root causes via periodic performance monitoring

To provide a basis for promotion

items.question:
For employees who have been away on approved absence for a part of the performance

At the end of the year

items.question:
How are the remaining performance management process steps applied for employees on

COMPUTER BASED TEST (CBT) PROMOTION EXAMINATION FOR OFFICERS IN 
GRADE 15 TO 17 IN THE FEDERAL CIVIL SERVICE OF THE FEDERATION ON 
PUBLIC SERVICE RULE (PSR).
Instruction: This examination consists of 100 multiple-choice questions designed to assess your 
readiness for promotion to the next level in the Federal Civil Service. You have exactly one 
hour (60 minutes) to complete all questions; manage your time wisely to ensure you attempt 
every question. Read each question carefully before selecting the best answer from the options.

Part 1: Definitions and General

To provide guidelines for virtual meetings and engagements.

To detail training and capacity development programs.

items.question: What is the recommended break duration for meetings expected to last beyond one hour?

What is the policy on virtual engagements by the Ministry of Communications and Digital 
Economy?
options: They should be limited to essential meetings.
They should handle all activities during virtual meetings.
They should be avoided if possible.
They should be used only for emergencies.

items.question:

protocol?

official engagement issued from time to time by the Federal Ministry of Communications and 
Digital Economy.
Personal devices are prohibited for official use.

They must be provided by the Government.

organization.

items.question: What is the rule regarding paid appointments during leave?

items.question: What is the rule regarding private agency?
options: Officers can undertake any private agency.

COMPUTER BASED TEST (CBT) PROMOTION EXAMINATION FOR OFFICERS IN 
GRADE 15 TO 17 IN THE FEDERAL CIVIL SERVICE OF THE FEDERATION ON THE 
PUBLIC SERVICE RULE (PSR).
Instruction: This examination consists of 100 multiple-choice questions designed to assess your 
readiness for promotion to the next level in the Federal Civil Service. You have exactly one 
hour (60 minutes) to complete all questions; manage your time wisely to ensure you attempt 
every question. Read each question carefully before selecting the best answer from the options.

options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
The Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office.

items.question:
What is the nature of the Government's decision regarding compensation for loss of private 
property?
options: It is at the discretion of the Government.
It is determined by the Officer's grade level.

The case shall be reported to the appropriate authorities for investigation, and the Officer's

Who is responsible for ensuring that Officers on official assignments outside Nigeria are

options: The Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office.

options: Provisions for officers who are transferred to a new station.

Provisions for officers who are required to reside in a temporary location due to official duties,

items.question: What is the aim of providing "Step Over Facilities"?

GRADE 15 TO 17 IN THE FEDERAL CIVIL SERVICE OF THE FEDERATION.
Instruction: This examination consists of 100 multiple-choice questions designed to assess your 
readiness for promotion to the next level in the Federal Civil Service. You have exactly one 
hour (60 minutes) to complete all questions; manage your time wisely to ensure you attempt 
every question. Read each question carefully before selecting the best answer from the options.

items.question:
According to PSR 170202, what is the primary function of Boards/Councils in Federal 
Government Parastatals?

options: They are entitled to full civil service benefits.
Their privileges are determined by the enabling Act or instrument establishing the Parastatal.
They receive the same allowances as Permanent Secretaries.
They are exempt from all Public Service Rules.

items.question:
Who has the authority for appointments, promotions, and discipline within Federal 
Government Parastatals, as per PSR 170301?
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
The Permanent Secretary of the supervising Ministry.
The Board/Council of the respective Parastatal.

items.question: According to PSR 170302, what is the process for making appointments in Parastatals?
options: Appointments are made solely by the Chief Executive Officer.

Appointments are made by the Board/Council based on recommendations from the Chief

Appointments are subject to approval by the Federal Civil Service Commission.

Training and Development Committee.

Part 3: Nigerian Foreign Service Regulations

items.question: What is the primary purpose of the Nigerian Foreign Service Regulations (NFSR)?
options: To govern the conduct of all Public Servants.
To provide specific regulations for Officers serving in Nigeria's Diplomatic Missions abroad.

items.question: To whom do the Nigerian Foreign Service Regulations apply?
options:
Officers of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs and other Ministries/Extra-Ministerial Offices serving 
in Nigeria's Diplomatic Missions abroad.

items.question:
If there is a conflict between the general Public Service Rules and the Nigerian Foreign 
Service Regulations, which takes precedence for Officers serving abroad?
options: The decision of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
The local laws of the host country.

items.question: Who is the Head of Mission in a Diplomatic Mission?
options: The most senior Officer from any Ministry.
The Ambassador, High Commissioner, or other principal officer in charge of a Mission.

items.question:
What is the role of the Head of Mission regarding the welfare and discipline of all Nigerian 
staff in the Mission?
options: They have no responsibility for staff not from the Ministry of Foreign Affairs.

They are responsible for the welfare and discipline of all Nigerian staff, irrespective of their 
parent Ministry/Extra-Ministerial Office.
They delegate all disciplinary matters to Abuja.

items.question: What is the "Home Service" in the context of the NFSR?
options: Service in Nigeria's Diplomatic Missions abroad.

items.question: What does "Tour of Duty" refer to?
options: A short visit to a foreign country.
The period an Officer is officially assigned to serve in a Diplomatic Mission abroad.

items.question: What is the "Foreign Service Allowance (FSA)" designed to cover?
options: The additional cost of living incurred by an Officer serving in a foreign mission.
Travel expenses to and from Nigeria.

items.question: How often are Foreign Service Allowances typically reviewed?
options: From time to time, based on economic conditions and cost of living indices.

items.question: What is the purpose of "Hardship Allowance" in certain Missions?
options: To compensate for difficult living or working conditions.
To encourage officers to stay longer in a Mission.
To provide a bonus for good performance.

items.question: What is the "Education Allowance" for?
options:
To assist in covering the cost of education for the Officer's eligible dependent children 
abroad.
To fund training courses for staff.
To support local schools in the host country.

items.question: What is the "Medical Expense Reimbursement" for Officers serving abroad?
options: All medical expenses are covered automatically.
Only emergency medical treatment is covered.

Reimbursement for approved medical expenses incurred by the Officer and eligible 
dependents, subject to specific guidelines.
Officers must use their personal insurance.

items.question: What is the "Rent Allowance" for Officers serving in Diplomatic Missions?
options: To cover the cost of housing in Nigeria.
To provide for the cost of suitable accommodation in the host country.

items.question: What is the "Furniture Allowance" for?
options: To purchase personal furniture for the Officer's home in Nigeria.
To cover maintenance costs of furniture.
To assist in furnishing the official residence or rented accommodation in the Mission.

items.question: What is the purpose of the "Representational Allowance"?
options:
To enable the Head of Mission and other designated Officers to meet official entertainment 
and hospitality expenses.

To pay for public relations campaigns.

items.question: What is the "Outfit Allowance" for?
options:
To assist Officers in acquiring suitable attire for official functions and representation in the

items.question: What is the "Child Welfare Allowance" intended for?
options:
To assist with expenses related to the general welfare of an Officer's eligible dependent 
children.
To provide a bonus for having children.

items.question: What is the "Home Leave" provision for Officers serving abroad?
options: Annual leave taken in the host country.

Periodic leave granted to Officers and their eligible families to travel to Nigeria at Government

items.question: What is the typical duration of a "Tour of Duty" for Officers in the Foreign Service?
options: Two to three years, subject to extension.

items.question: What is the "Transfer Grant" for?
options:
A payment to assist Officers with expenses incurred during official transfer from one Mission 
to another or to/from Home Service.

items.question:
When an Officer is transferred from one Mission to another, who is responsible for arranging

options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.

items.question: What is the "Baggage Allowance" for Officers on transfer?
options:
A specified weight or volume of personal effects allowed to be transported at Government 
expense.
132

items.question: What is the purpose of "Installation Allowance"?
options: To cover the cost of installing new office equipment.
To assist Officers with initial expenses related to settling into a new Mission and residence.

options: They continue to receive full foreign service allowances.

options:

To cover travel expenses for a vacation.
To fund international travel for training.
To cover expenses for a new posting abroad.

options: They are solely responsible for all aspects.

They are the primary Ministry responsible for the interpretation and implementation of these

They are responsible for financial disbursements only.

Differences in the cost of living between the host country and Nigeria.

items.question: What is the "Security Allowance" for?

To fund security systems for official buildings.

items.question: What is the "Language Allowance" for?
options: For Officers who speak multiple Nigerian languages.

For Officers who acquire proficiency in the official language of the host country, where

items.question: What is the "Domestic Staff Allowance" for?
options: To pay for personal household help in Nigeria.

To assist Officers in employing domestic staff in the host country, where permissible and

To cover expenses for official drivers.

items.question: What is the "Medical Evacuation" provision for Officers serving abroad?
options:
Provision for emergency medical evacuation to Nigeria or another country if adequate

It is not provided by the Government.
Only for the Head of Mission.

options: The body is repatriated at the family's expense.

Provisions for the repatriation of the deceased Officer's remains and payment of death

The Officer's contract is immediately terminated.

It is determined by the Pension Reform Act and any specific foreign service provisions.

An Officer may be appointed to act in a higher position in the Mission, subject to approval and

It is at the sole discretion of the Head of Mission.
It is less strict than in Home Service.

items.question: What is the "Leave" entitlement for Officers serving in Diplomatic Missions?
options: They are not entitled to annual leave.

They are entitled to annual leave, home leave, and other special leaves as per the PSR, with

They are only allowed to leave in the host country.

The Government provides an official residence for the Head of Mission.

Officers can use personal devices for classified information.

items.question: What is the "Code of Conduct" for Officers in the Foreign Service?
options: It is less strict than for Home Service Officers.

Officers are expected to uphold the highest standards of conduct, integrity, and

It is determined by the host country's cultural norms.

items.question:
What is the "Role of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs" in managing the personnel in Diplomatic

It is the primary Ministry responsible for the administration and management of all Nigerian

135
It delegates all responsibilities to other Ministries.

items.question: What is the "Recall to Home Service" provision for Officers serving abroad?

It is the primary Ministry responsible for the administration and management of all Nigerian

items.question: What is the "Post-Service Benefits" for Officers of the Foreign Service?
options:
They are entitled to pension, gratuity, and other benefits as per the Pension Reform Act and

They receive a lump sum payment.

items.question: What is the "Reporting System" for Officers in Diplomatic Missions?
options: They only report to the Head of Mission.

They are subject to a reporting system that ensures accountability to both the Head of

They report directly to the President.
They report only to the host country's authorities.

items.question:
What is the "Training and Capacity Development" provision for Officers in the Foreign

options:
They are provided with specialized training and capacity development programs relevant to

They are only trained in Nigeria.

items.question: What is the "Succession Planning" for key positions in Diplomatic Missions?
options: It involves identifying and developing suitable Officers to fill critical roles in the Missions.

It is based solely on seniority.

items.question: What is the "Welfare of Families" of Officers serving abroad?
options:
Provisions are made for the welfare of eligible family members, including education, medical

What is the "Role of the Federal Civil Service Commission" concerning Officers in the

options:
They retain oversight functions for appointments, promotions, and disciplinary matters, in

Part 4: Procedures for Amending the Public Service Rules.

items.question: What is the primary purpose of Chapter 19 of the Public Service Rules?
options: To detail the process for reviewing and amending the Public Service Rules.
To establish guidelines for foreign service.

items.question: Who is ultimately responsible for approving amendments to the Public Service Rules?
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
The President of the Federal Republic of Nigeria, through the Federal Executive Council.

items.question: What is the initial step for proposing an amendment to the Public Service Rules?
options: Any Officer can directly submit a proposal to the President.

Proposals for amendments are typically initiated by Ministries, Extra-Ministerial Offices, or 
relevant stakeholders.
The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation unilaterally decides on amendments.
Amendments are proposed by external consultants.

items.question:
After a proposal for amendment is initiated, to whom is it typically forwarded for 
consideration and review?
options: The Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF).

items.question:
What role does the Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF) play in 
the amendment process?
137
options: They coordinate the review, collate inputs, and prepare the draft amendments.
They implement the amendments without further approval.
They are responsible for publishing the final rules.

items.question:
Before final approval, what kind of consultation process is usually undertaken for proposed 
amendments?
options: Consultation with relevant Ministries, Extra-Ministerial Offices, and other stakeholders.

items.question: What is the significance of the Federal Executive Council (FEC) in the amendment process?
options:
They are the body that gives final approval to the proposed amendments before presidential 
assent.
They are responsible for drafting the amendments.

items.question:
Once approved by the Federal Executive Council, who gives the final assent to the 
amended Public Service Rules?
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
The President of the Federal Republic of Nigeria.
The Attorney General of the Federation.

items.question:
After presidential assent, how are the amended Public Service Rules officially 
disseminated?
options: Through publication in the Federal Republic of Nigeria Official Gazette.
Via email to all public servants.

items.question:
What is the overarching goal of having a structured procedure for amending the Public 
Service Rules?
options: To make the rules difficult to change.

To ensure that the rules remain dynamic, relevant, and responsive to evolving governance 
needs.
To limit the scope of the Public Service.

To centralize all decision-making power.
Part 5: The National Council on Establishment (NCE)

items.question: What is the National Council on Establishment (NCE)?
options: A body responsible for judicial appointments.

The highest decision-making body on establishment matters in the Public Service of the
138
Federation.
A committee that oversees disciplinary actions.
A council that manages international relations.

items.question: Which level of government is represented in the National Council on Establishment (NCE)?
What is one of the primary functions of the NCE?
options: To approve annual budgets for all Ministries.

To consider and approve policies relating to the structure, organization, and grading of posts 
in the Public Service.
To manage the recruitment process for all civil servants.
To conduct performance appraisals for senior officers.

items.question: Who typically chairs the meetings of the National Council on Establishment?
options: The President of the Federal Republic of Nigeria.
The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
The Attorney General of the Federation.

items.question: Decisions made by the NCE are binding on:
options: All Public Service institutions at Federal and State levels.

items.question: What is the role of the NCE in standardizing conditions of service across the Public Service?
options: It ensures uniformity in conditions of service to promote equity and fairness.
It allows each Ministry to set its own conditions.
It only advises on conditions of service.

items.question: How frequently does the National Council on Establishment usually meet?
options: As deemed necessary by the Chairperson.

items.question: What type of recommendations does the NCE consider regarding the Public Service?
options: Only those related to staff welfare.

Recommendations on new schemes of service, review of existing ones, and other 
establishment matters.

items.question: Who provides the secretariat services for the National Council on Establishment?
options: The Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF).

items.question: What is the significance of the NCE's decisions for the Public Service Rules?
options: They form the basis for amendments and updates to the Public Service Rules.
They are independent of the Public Service Rules.
They only apply to new policies.

items.question: What is the NCE's role concerning the creation of new departments or agencies?
options: It considers and approves the establishment structure and grading of posts for such entities.

items.question:
What mechanism does the NCE use to ensure that its decisions are implemented across all 
levels of government?
options:
Through circulars and guidelines issued by the Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the 
Federation.

items.question: What is the NCE's role in addressing disparities in the Public Service?
options: It works to harmonize and standardize conditions of service to minimize disparities.
It only addresses disparities in salary.
It focuses on increasing disparities for efficiency.

items.question: What kind of expertise is typically represented among the members of the NCE?
options:
Expertise in public administration, human resources, and finance from both federal and state 
levels.

items.question: What is the primary benefit of having a body like the NCE for the Nigerian Public Service?
options: To centralize all power in one office.

To ensure consistency, equity, and effective management of human resources across the
140
federal and state public services.
To reduce the overall size of the civil service.

items.question: How does the NCE contribute to good governance in Nigeria?
options: By promoting standardized and fair practices in public service employment and management.

items.question:
What is the relationship between the NCE and the Federal Civil Service Commission 
(FCSC)?
options: The NCE is superior to the FCSC.

The FCSC implements policies and decisions approved by the NCE regarding establishment

They are independent bodies with no interaction.
The FCSC advises the NCE on all matters.

items.question: What is the NCE's stance on the continuous professional development of public servants?
options: It is not a concern of the NCE.

It promotes policies that encourage training and capacity development to enhance service 
delivery.
It leaves training decisions to individual Ministries.
It only supports training for senior management.

items.question: What is the NCE's role in ensuring the sustainability of the Public Service?
options: It focuses on short-term solutions only.

It makes decisions that contribute to the long-term viability and effectiveness of the Public 
Service.
It is not concerned with sustainability.
It focuses on reducing the public service.

items.question: What is the importance of the NCE's decisions for the career progression of public servants?
options: They establish the framework for promotions, transfers, and other career-related matters.
They are only for disciplinary purposes.

items.question: What kind of issues might be presented to the NCE for deliberation?
options: Proposals for new allowances or review of existing ones.
Day-to-day administrative challenges within a Ministry.

items.question:
What is the NCE's role in ensuring that the Public Service remains competitive and attractive 
to talent?
options: It has no role in talent attraction.

It reviews and approves conditions of service that aim to attract and retain qualified 
personnel.

items.question: What is the primary document that guides the NCE's operations and decisions?
options: The Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria.
The Public Service Rules and relevant government circulars and policies.

items.question: What is the NCE's role in promoting accountability in the Public Service?
options: It has no direct role in accountability.

By establishing clear rules and guidelines for personnel management, it indirectly promotes 
accountability.

items.question: What is the ultimate aim of the NCE's efforts in the Public Service?
options:
To foster a professional, efficient, and effective Public Service capable of delivering on national 
development goals.

To centralize all decision-making.

Chapter 3: Financial Regulations (FR)
As a director, you are a key custodian of public funds. This chapter focuses on the Financial 
Regulations (FR), which are the bedrock of financial management in the public service. It covers 
the rules on revenue collection, expenditure, accounting procedures, and auditing. The 
questions here will assess your ability to ensure accountability, transparency, and prudence in 
managing the financial resources of your department. The chapter consists of Sections A, B, C, 
D, and F. Section A is a 100 questions, answers and
items.explanation:
s taken from chapter one and 
two of the book, Section B consists of 100 questions, answers, and explanation taken from 
chapter 3 and 4 of the book, Section C consists of 100 questions, answers, and explanations 
taken from chapter 5,6,7, and 8 of the book, Section D consists of questions, answers, and 
explanations from chapter 9,10,11, and 12 of the book, Section E consists of 100 questions, 
answers, and explanations from chapter 13, 14, 15, and 16 of the book. Section F consists of 
100 questions, answers, and explanations from chapter 17, 18, 19, 20, and 21 of the book. 
Finally sections G and H are 100 questions each from all chapters of the book for student 
practice.
Section A
Part 1: Financial Regulations—Financial Authorities and Responsibilities of Public 
Officers

items.question: What are financial authorities, as defined in the Financial Regulations?
options: Guidelines for personal investments of public officers.

Legal instruments empowering and guiding public officers in government financial 
transactions.
Documents outlining the budget allocation for each ministry.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 101 states that "Financial authorities are the legal 
instruments that empower and guide all public officers in carrying out government financial 
transactions."

items.question:
Which of the following is NOT explicitly mentioned as a type of government financial 
transaction covered by financial authorities?
options: Custody of government stores and assets.
Disbursement of funds from major government funds.
Personal loan applications for public officers.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 101 lists receipt, custody, accounting for revenue, 
procurement, custody, utilization of stores and assets, and disbursement of funds as 
government financial transactions. Personal loan applications are not included.
144

items.question: The principal financial authorities are primarily dealt with in which part of Chapter 1?
options: Part II - Financial Responsibilities of Public Officers.

items.question: What is the primary source of provisions for government financial business in Nigeria?
options: The 1999 Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria. 3
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 102 states, "The 1999 Constitution of the Federal Republic 
of Nigeria makes ample provisions for government financial business."

items.question: Which of these financial matters are regulated by the relevant sections of the Constitution?
options: Only the payment of revenue into the Federation Account.
Authorization of revenue allocation and disbursement from major funds.
Audit of public accounts and other financial matters.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 102 specifies that the Constitution regulates "The payment 
of revenue into the Federation Account and the Consolidated Revenue Fund, the authorization 
of allocation of revenue from the federation account, the disbursement of funds from the 
Consolidated Revenue Fund, the Development Fund, and the Contingencies Fund, the audit of 
public accounts, and other financial matters."

items.question:
Who has been assigned the responsibility for all financial business of the government of the 
federation by the President?
options: The Head of the Civil Service.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 103 states, "The President of the Federal Republic of Nigeria 
has assigned to the Minister of Finance the responsibility for all financial business of the 
government of the federation."
145

items.question:
Warrants issued by the Minister of Finance authorize the Accountant-General of the 
Federation to disburse funds from which accounts?
options: Consolidated Revenue Fund and Development Fund.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 103 mentions "Warrants issued by the Minister from time to 
time authorizing the Accountant-General of the Federation to disburse funds from the 
Consolidated Revenue Fund and Development Fund."

items.question:
According to Financial Regulation 103, no payments shall be made except on the authority of 
a warrant duly issued and signed by whom?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 103 explicitly states, "no payments shall be made except on 
the authority of a warrant duly issued and signed by the Minister."

items.question:
What is the scope of application for the financial regulations issued by the Minister of 
Finance?
options:
The Federal Public Service, including ministries, extra-ministerial offices, and other arms of 
government.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 105 states, "The financial regulations so issued shall 
generally apply to the Federal Public Service, which means 'ministries, extra-ministerial offices, 
and other arms of government."

items.question: Who is the Head of the Federal Government Accounting Services and the Treasury?
options: The Head of Finance and Accounts of a ministry.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 106 states, "The Accountant-General of the Federation is the 
Head of the Federal Government Accounting Services and the Treasury."

items.question:
What is a key responsibility of the Accountant-General of the Federation regarding 
accounting systems and controls?
options: To personally manage all financial transactions.
To provide adequate accounting systems and controls in all government arms.
To audit all public accounts annually.

To formulate national economic policy.
Q
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 106 states the Accountant-General "has the responsibility for 
providing adequate accounting systems and controls in the ministries, extra-ministerial offices, 
and other arms of Government."

items.question: Which of the following is a duty of the Accountant-General of the Federation?
options: To serve as the Chief Accounting Officer for receipts and payments of the government.
To supervise accounts of federal ministries.
To collate, prepare, and publish statutory financial statements.
items.explanation: Financial Regulation 107 lists all these as duties of the Accountant-General.

items.question:
The Accountant-General is responsible for maintaining and operating the accounts of which 
major public funds?
options: Consolidated Revenue Fund, Development Fund, and Contingencies Fund.
Private bank accounts of public officers.a 147
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 107(e) states the Accountant-General maintains and 
operates accounts of the "Consolidated Revenue Fund, Development Fund, Contingencies 
Fund and Other Public Funds."

items.question:
Who is responsible for establishing and supervising Federal Pay Offices in each state capital 
of the federation?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 107(g) states the Accountant-General shall "establish and 
supervise Federal Pay Offices in each state capital of the federation."

items.question:
What is one of the Accountant-General's duties regarding compliance with rules and 
regulations?
options: To issue warnings to defaulting officers.
To conduct routine and in-depth inspection of books of accounts to ensure compliance.
To manage all federal government investments.3
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 107(h) states the Accountant-General shall "conduct routine 
and in-depth inspection of the books of accounts of federal ministries... to ensure compliance 
with rules, regulations, and policy decisions."

items.question:
Who is responsible for approving and ensuring compliance with accounting codes, internal 
audit guides, and stock verification manuals?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 107(i) states the Accountant-General shall "approve and 
ensure compliance with accounting codes, internal audit guides and stock verification manuals."

items.question: The Accountant-General is tasked with investigating which of the following?
options: Loss of funds, assets, and store items.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 107(j) states the Accountant-General shall "investigate 
cases of fraud, loss of funds, assets, and store items and other financial malpractices."

items.question:
How does the Accountant-General provide financial guidelines to federal ministries and 
other arms of government?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 107(k) states the Accountant-General shall "provide financial 
guidelines through the issuance of treasury circulars."

items.question:
Who is responsible for supervising and controlling the computerization of the accounting 
system in federal ministries?
options:
The Head of Finance and Accounts.
Q
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 107(1) states the Accountant-General shall "supervise and 
control the computerization of the accounting system."

items.question: Question: What is one of the Accountant-General's duties related to revenue?
options: To collect all federal government revenue.
To carry out revenue monitoring and accounting.
To set revenue targets for ministries.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 107(m) states the Accountant-General shall "carry out 
revenue monitoring and accounting."

items.question:
Who issues officially approved forms bearing Treasury numbers for use in all federal 
ministries to ensure uniformity?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 107(n) states the Accountant-General shall "issue officially 
approved forms bearing Treasury numbers for use in all federal ministries... to ensure 
uniformity."

items.question:
What is the Accountant-General's role concerning the accounting policy of the federal 
government?
options: To implement policies set by the Minister of Finance.
To advise on the accounting policy.Q
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 107(o) states the Accountant-General shall "formulate the 
accounting policy of the federal government."

items.question:
Who is the officer responsible under the Constitution for the audit and report on the public 
accounts of the federation?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 108 identifies "the Auditor-General for the Federation" as this 
officer.

items.question: The Auditor-General's examination of accounts aims to ascertain whether:
options: Public monies have been fully accounted for.
Monies have been expended for appropriated purposes.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 108 lists all these as aspects the Auditor-General shall 
ascertain.

items.question: Which of the following is a statutory function of the Auditor-General?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 109(i) lists all these as statutory functions of the 
Auditor-General.
150

items.question:
What is the purpose of a Value-for-Money (Performance) Audit carried out by the 
Auditor-General?
options: To determine if government accounts are faithfully kept.
To ensure funds are expended as appropriated.
To ascertain the level of economy, efficiency, and effectiveness from government projects.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 109(i)(d) defines Value-for-Money (Performance) Audit as 
being "to ascertain the level of economy, efficiency, and effectiveness derived from government 
projects and programs."

items.question: The scope of work of the Auditor-General includes auditing the accounts of which entities?
options: Federal government establishments in states and FCT Area Councils.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 109(ii) lists all these as within the scope of the 
Auditor-General's work.

items.question:
Who has free access to books of accounts, files, safes, security documents, and other 
records relating to government accounts?
options: The Accountant-General and the Auditor-General or their representatives.
Accounting Officers and Heads of Finance.3
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 110 states that "the Accountant General and the 
Auditor-General or their representatives shall, at all reasonable times, have free access to 
books of accounts, files, safes, security documents, and other records and information relating 
to the accounts."

items.question: What does the term "Accounting Officer" mean?
options: Any officer involved in financial transactions.

The Permanent Secretary of a ministry or head of an extra-ministerial office is responsible for 
human, material, and financial resources.
The Chief Executive Officer of a parastatal.
An officer who keeps a cash book.3
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 111 (i) defines "Accounting Officer" as "the Permanent 
Secretary of a ministry or the head of extra-ministerial office and other arms of government who 
is in full control of, and is responsible for, human, material, and financial resources."
151

items.question: Which of the following is a function of the Accounting Officer?
options: Ensuring proper budgetary and accounting systems are established.
Ensuring the safety and proper maintenance of all government assets.
Ensuring accurate collection and accounting for public moneys.
items.explanation: Financial Regulation 112(1) lists all these as functions of the Accounting Officer.

items.question: An Accounting Officer is responsible for ensuring that any revenue collected is:
options: Remitted to the appropriate authorities promptly and not spent.
Used to cover immediate operational expenses.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 112(l)(l) states the Accounting Officer is responsible for 
"ensuring that any revenue collected is not spent but remitted to the appropriate authorities 
promptly."

items.question:
In compliance with the Public Procurement Act, Accounting Officers are charged with the 
responsibility of:
options: Presiding over the activities of their Tenders Boards.
Ensuring adequate appropriation for procurements in their annual budget.
Integrating their entity's procurement expenditure into its yearly budget.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 113 lists all these as special responsibilities of Accounting 
Officers under the Public Procurement Act.

items.question:
If an Accounting Officer fails to comply with the provisions of the Public Procurement Act, 
who shall be held personally liable for any breach or contravention?
options: Only the subordinates who caused the breach.
The Accounting Officer, whether or not caused by them in person or their subordinates.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 113(i) states the Accounting Officer "shall be personally 
liable for any breach or contravention thereof, whether or not such breach or contravention was 
caused by them in person, their subordinates, or any person to whom they may have delegated 
their responsibilities."

items.question: What is one of the duties of the Head of Finance and Accounts of a ministry?
options: To ensure compliance with Financial Regulations and the Accounting Code by staff.
To directly disburse all public funds.
To conduct external audits of the ministry.Q
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 114(a) states the Head of Finance and Accounts is 
responsible for "ensuring compliance with Financial Regulations and the Accounting Code by all 
staff under his control and supervision."

items.question:
The Head of Finance and Accounts is responsible for ensuring prompt rendition of which of 
the following returns?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 114(e) lists all these as returns for which the Head of 
Finance and Accounts is responsible for prompt rendition.

items.question: Question: What does the term "Sub-Accounting Officer" mean?
options: An officer who prepares financial reports for the Accountant-General.

An officer entrusted with receipt, custody, and disbursement of public money, required to 
keep a cash book.
An officer who audits the accounts of a ministry.

The Head of Finance and Accounts.
□
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 115(1) defines "Sub-Accounting Officer" as "an officer who is 
entrusted with the receipt, custody, and disbursement of public money and who is required to 
keep one of the recognised Cash books."

items.question: Which of the following is NOT included in the definition of a "Sub-Accounting Officer"?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 115(ii) lists various officers included, but the "Minister" is the 
Political Head (FR 116), not a Sub-Accounting Officer.

items.question:
Any directive to the Accounting Officer by a Political Head having financial implications shall 
be:
options: Approved by the National Assembly first.

Only for minor financial matters.
Q
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 117(i) states, "Any directive to the Accounting Officer by a 
Political Head having financial implications on the ministry, agency or parastatal shall be in 
writing."

items.question:
If a directive from a Political Head results in unauthorized expenditure, who is responsible 
unless a report was made?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 117(ii) states, "the Accounting Officer shall be responsible for 
such an unauthorized expenditure unless a report had been made by the Accounting Officer to 
the Head of Service... or to the Minister."

items.question:
What is the responsibility of the Political Head regarding his ministry, agency, and/or 
parastatal?
options: To supervise and control its activities.
To manage its day-to-day financial transactions.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 118(i) states, "It shall be the responsibility of the Political 
Head to supervise and control the activities of his ministry, agency, and/or parastatal."

items.question: "Revenue Collector" is defined as an officer who:
options:
Is entrusted with an official receipt, licence, or ticket booklet for regular revenue collection 
and keeps a cash book.
Only deals with large sums of money.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 119 defines a "Revenue Collector" as "an officer, other than 
a Sub- Accounting officer, who is entrusted with an official receipt, license, or ticket booklet for 
the regular collection of some particular forms of revenue and who is required to keep a cash 
book."

items.question: What is the definition of an "Imprest-holder"?
options:
An officer who manages the ministry's main bank account.
154

An officer entrusted with the disbursement of public money for which vouchers cannot be 
immediately presented to a Sub-Accounting Officer, and who keeps a cash book.
An officer who approves all capital expenditure.

An officer responsible for auditing revenue collections.
□
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 120 defines "Imprest-holder" as "an officer other than a 
Sub-Accounting officer who is entrusted with the disbursement of public money, for which 
vouchers cannot be presented immediately to a Sub-Accounting Officer and who is required to 
keep a cash book."

items.question: Who is an "Officer Controlling Expenditure"?
options:
The officer to whom the control of a subhead or specified amount has been delegated by the 
appropriate Accounting Officer.

Any officer who signs payment vouchers.
□
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 121 defines an "Officer Controlling Expenditure" as "the 
officer to whom the control of a sub-head, or specified amount of a sub-head, has been 
delegated by the appropriate Accounting Officer."

items.question:
When a Ministry or Department is constituted as a Self Accounting Unit, who is charged with 
the installation and maintenance of a proper system of accounts for that unit?
options:
The Head of Finance and Accounts.
Q
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 122 states that "the Accounting Officer as defined in 
Financial Regulation 111 is charged, in relation to that unit, with the installation and maintenance 
of a proper system of accounts."

items.question:
Which of the following is a general duty of public officers with monetary or financial 
responsibilities?
options: To ensure prompt collection of public revenue.
To promptly bring to account all moneys under proper Heads and Sub-heads.
To ensure proper provision for safekeeping of public moneys and documents.
items.explanation: Financial Regulation 123(i) lists all these as general duties.

items.question:
How many days does a public officer have to reply to formal queries or other enquiries 
addressed to him by the Accountant-General and Auditor-General?
155
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 123(i)(p) states "within twenty-one days reply to formal 
queries or any other inquiries addressed to him by the Accountant-General and 
Auditor-General."

items.question:
Who is personally and peculiarly responsible for the due performance of the financial duties 
of their ministries and for any inaccuracies in accounts rendered under their authority?
options:
Only the Head of Finance and Accounts.
E3
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 124 states, "All officers are personally and peculiarly 
responsible for the due performance of the financial duties of their ministries/extra-ministerial 
offices and other arms of government."

items.question:
If an officer delegates or entrusts the performance of duties to a subordinate officer, is the 
delegating officer relieved of responsibility?
options:
Only if the delegation is in writing.
□
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 125 states, "No officer shall be relieved of his responsibility 
should he delegate or entrust the performance of duties or any part thereof to his subordinate 
officer."

items.question:
Who is responsible for causing surprise inspections to be carried out on the accounts of 
officers with financial responsibilities?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 126 states, "The Accountant-General shall from time to time 
cause surprise inspections to be carried out on the accounts of any of the officers referred to in 
Regulation 123."
Part 2: Financial Regulations: "Revenue Collection and Accounting,"

items.question:
Who is responsible for ensuring the full collection of all government revenues and for 
promptly bringing them to account?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 201 explicitly states that Accounting Officers are responsible 
for the full collection of all revenues due to the government and for their prompt lodgment and 
accounting.

items.question:
According to Financial Regulation 202, what must an officer receiving public money issue for 
every sum of money paid to the government?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 202 mandates that an official receipt shall be issued by the 
officer receiving public money for every sum paid to the government.

items.question: What information should an official receipt contain, as per Financial Regulation 204?
options: Only the amount of money received
The date, amount, and the name of the officer receiving it
The date, the name of the person paying, and a brief description of the transaction
The date, amount, and a brief description of the transactiona
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 204 requires that official receipts must show the date, the 
amount received, and a brief description of the transaction for which payment is being made.

items.question:
Where should notices informing the public that a numbered official receipt must be obtained 
for money paid to the government be displayed?
options: In every office where public money is received

Only at the main treasury office
157
Q
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 205 stipulates that notices to this effect must be exhibited 
prominently in every office where public money is received.

items.question:
What type of receipt book is used by all Sub-Accounting Officers, unless a specially printed 
one or a receipt machine is used?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 206(i) specifies that General Receipts (Treasury Book 6) 
shall be used by all Sub-Accounting Officers for this purpose.

items.question:
What is the process for making duplicate and triplicate copies of a receipt when using 
General Receipts (Treasury Book 6)?
options:
By using a special duplicating machine
□
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 206(ii) states that double-sided carbon paper shall be used 
to make the duplicate and triplicate copies of the receipt.

items.question: Who is responsible for giving the original receipt to the payer?
options: The Head of Finance and Accounts
The officer who issued the receipt
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 206(ii) states that the original receipt shall be handed or 
delivered to the payer by the officer who issued the receipt.

items.question: What must a Revenue Collector do immediately upon receiving money?
options: Hand it over to the Sub-Accounting Officer
Enter the receipt in their cash book without delay
Issue a verbal receipt and collect a written one later

Keep the money in a safe until the end of the week
□ 158
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 209(i) requires every Revenue Collector to keep a cash book 
and enter all receipts without delay on the day of receipt.

items.question: How often must a Revenue Collector make payments to a Sub-Accounting Officer?
options: At least once a week, unless otherwise approved

Only when the amount is substantial
□
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 209(i) specifies that such payments shall be made at least 
once a week unless an alternative specific period is approved by the Accountant-General.

items.question:
What document must support the payments made by a Revenue Collector to a 
Sub-Accounting Officer?
options: A Revenue Collector's paying-in Form (Treasury Form 15A)
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 209(i) states that the payments must be supported by a 
Revenue Collector's paying-in Form (Treasury Form 15A).

items.question:
What must a Revenue Collector do with the official receipt they receive from the 
Sub-Accounting Officer?
options: Paste it in their cash book
Give it to the Accounting Officer
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 210 requires the Revenue Collector to paste this official 
receipt in their cash book on the payment side.

items.question:
Who is responsible for ensuring that Revenue Collectors pay in their collections and 
produce their cash books for examination?
options: The Head of Finance and Accounts
Sub-Accounting Officers and federal pay officers
items.explanation:
According to Financial Regulation 212, Sub-Accounting officers and federal pay 
officers are responsible for ensuring compliance with these regulations.

items.question:
What is the maximum interval for a Revenue Collector's cash book to be produced for 
examination under normal circumstances?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 211 states that unless there are exceptional circumstances, 
the interval for examination shall not be greater than one month.

items.question:
What kind of checks should the Accountant-General or Accounting Officer use to 
supplement routine checks on Revenue Collectors' cash books?
options: Surprise inspections at the offices concerned
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 211 states that the Accountant-General or Accounting Officer 
shall supplement routine checks with surprise inspections at the offices.

items.question:
Where must each ministry, extra-ministerial office, and other arms of government maintain a 
separate bank account for revenue?
options: In a commercial bank of their choice
At the Central Bank of Nigeria or any other designated bank
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 213(i) mandates a separate bank account for Revenue at 
the Central Bank of Nigeria or any other bank designated by the Accountant-General.

items.question:
What is the only purpose for which a withdrawal can be made from a ministry's revenue 
account?
options: To make transfers to the Consolidated Revenue Fund (CRF) account
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 213(ii) strictly states that on no account shall any withdrawal 
be made from the revenue account other than for the purpose of transfer to the consolidated 
account.

items.question:
Where must ministries/extra-ministerial offices that earn revenue in foreign currency pay this 
revenue?
options: To the Central Bank of Nigeria
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 213(iii) requires that such revenue must be paid to the 
Central Bank of Nigeria without exemption.

items.question: What is the purpose of the Revenue Collector's Chart?
options: To track the daily activities of all staff
To record the number of receipts and licences in use
To give a continuous account of the value of receipts and licences
To serve as a monthly report of all expendituresa
items.explanation:
According to Financial Regulation 214(i), the chart is to give a continuous account 
of the value of receipts, licenses, and other revenue books issued for collections.

items.question:
What should an officer who is neither a Revenue Collector nor a Sub-Accounting Officer do 
if they come into possession of public money?
options: Keep it safe until the end of the month
Lodge it without delay with a Sub-Accounting Officer
Hand it over to their Head of Department
Deposit it directly into the ministry's bank accounta
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 218 states that such an officer will lodge the money without 
delay with a Sub-Accounting Officer who will issue an official receipt.

items.question:
What is the required procedure for a lodgement made by an officer who is neither a 
Revenue Collector nor a Sub-Accounting Officer?
options: It must be accompanied by a hand-written note
It must be supported by a paying-in form (Treasury Form 15)
It must be entered in a private cash booka 161
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 218 specifies that the lodgement will be supported by a 
paying-in form (Treasury Form 15).

items.question:
What happens if a Sub-Accounting Officer determines that an officer who has come into 
possession of public money is likely to do so again?
options: The officer is given a new job
The officer is given a promotion
The officer is supplied with an official receipt booklet
The officer is advised to open a private account for such fundsa
items.explanation:
According to Financial Regulation 218, the Sub-Accounting Officer will arrange for 
the officer to be supplied with an official receipt booklet.

items.question:
Who is responsible for checking Revenue Collectors' cash books and cash balances at 
irregular intervals?
options: The Head of Finance and Accounts
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 217 states that the Accounting Officer may consider it 
necessary for a revenue collecting office to have its cash books and cash balances checked at 
irregular intervals.

items.question:
What must Accounting Officers do upon the establishment of any new revenue collecting 
office?
options: Notify the Head of Internal Audit
Notify the appropriate Sub Accounting Officer and the Auditor-General
Notify the Central Bank of Nigeriaa
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 217 specifies that Accounting Officers will notify the 
appropriate Sub Accounting Officer and the Auditor-General of the establishment of any new 
revenue-collecting office.

items.question: What should be done with interest earned on bank accounts?
options: It can be used for administrative expenses.
It must be properly classified and paid to the Consolidated Revenue Fund.
It can be left in the bank account to accrue more interest.

It should be used to make up for any shortfalls in revenue.
□ 162
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 222 states that interest earned on bank accounts must be 
properly classified and paid to the Consolidated Revenue Fund.

items.question:
What is the rule regarding deductions from revenue collections to adjust a previous 
over-credit?
options: It is allowed with the Accounting Officer's approval.
It can be done if the amount is small.
It is allowed as long as the Head of Accounts is notified.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 223 states that no deductions shall be made from any 
revenue collections to adjust a previous over-credit. The gross amount must always be 
accounted for in full.

items.question: What is the rule for Accounting Officers regarding the return of arrears of revenue?
options: They must include only the arrears in respect of the headquarters.
They must include all arrears for which they are responsible to collect.
They should only return arrears that are substantial.

They are not responsible for returning arrears.
□
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 230(i) states that arrears of revenue returns must include all 
arrears for which the Accounting Officer is responsible.

items.question:
What is required for the abandonment of claims in the case of irrecoverable arrears of 
revenue?
options: The approval of the Accounting Officer.
The approval of the Head of Finance and Accounts.
The authority of the Minister of Finance.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 231 (i) states that the authority of the Minister of Finance is 
required for any abandonment of claims, except where other authority is established by law.

items.question:
How is a cheque or bank draft returned to a sender, redirected, or remitted to another station 
recorded?
options: By a verbal notification to the sender.
An appropriate entry is made in the Paper Money Register.
It is recorded in the General Ledger.
The cheque is simply returned without any record.a 163
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 233(iii) specifies that an appropriate entry will be made in the 
Paper Money Register, giving a reference to the covering memorandum or remittance voucher.

items.question: What is the purpose of the Paper Money Register?
options: To record all incoming and outgoing letters.
To record the receipt of cheques and other negotiable instruments received through the post.
To record the daily transactions of the Sub-Accounting Officer.

To track the movement of staff.
□
items.explanation:
The context of Financial Regulations 232 and 233 indicates that the Paper Money 
Register is for recording cheques, bank drafts, and other negotiable instruments received 
through the post.

items.question:
Who is responsible for ensuring that provisions for Value Added Tax (VAT) and Withholding 
Tax (WHT) are made and remitted?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 234(i) makes it mandatory for Accounting Officers to ensure 
full compliance with the dual roles of making provision for and remitting VAT and WHT

items.question: What is the rule concerning the collection of revenue by a Sub-Accounting Officer?
options: They can delegate this responsibility to a junior staff member.
They are responsible for departmental control and collection.
They are only responsible for revenue received in cash.

They are not involved in revenue collection.
□
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 217 implies a departmental control over revenue collection, 
and the Sub-Accounting Officer plays a key role in this.

items.question: What is the process for correcting an error that has caused an apparent surplus in revenue?
options: The surplus must be immediately transferred to the Consolidated Revenue Fund.

The surplus can be withdrawn from the deposit to make good a subsequent deficiency, with 
specific approval.

The error cannot be corrected once the money is in the account.
D) The surplus can be used for departmental expenses.3 164
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 221 allows for the withdrawal of a surplus from deposit to 
make good a subsequent deficiency, provided it is directly attributable to the error and has 
specific approval from the Accountant-General or the Accounting Officer.

items.question: Who must be notified of any amounts collectable by a department's officers?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 230(i) states that Heads of Department/Divisions should be 
notified of any amounts collectable by their department officers.

items.question:
What is the role of the cashier in the process of handling cheques and other negotiable 
instruments received by post?
options: The cashier maintains the Paper Money Register.

The cashier enters the details in the Paper Money Register after it is opened by the Head of 
Accounts.
The cashier is not involved in this process.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 233(ii) mentions the cashier making entries in the Paper 
Money Register, and Financial Regulation 233(iv) states that the Paper Money Register shall 
not be maintained, or incoming mail opened, by the cashier.

items.question:
Who is responsible for the overall supervision of the disbursement of funds and the proper 
monitoring and accounting for revenue?
options: The Head of Finance and Accounts of a ministry
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 114(b) states that one of the duties of the Head of Finance 
and Accounts is to ensure "adequate supervision of the disbursement of funds and proper 
monitoring and accounting for revenue."

items.question:
What is the name of the form used by a Revenue Collector to pay in collections to a 
Sub-Accounting Officer?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 209(i) mentions the Revenue Collector's paying-in Form 
(Treasury Form 15A).

items.question:
What is the function of the duplicate receipt in the monthly accounts of a Sub-Accounting 
Officer?
options: It is discarded after the transaction.
It is used as a cash book voucher.
It is sent to the Accountant-General.
It is given to the payer.3
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 206(ii) states that the duplicate receipt shall be used as a 
cash book voucher.

items.question:
When a Revenue Collector pays in his collections, what must he insert in the paying-in form 
(Treasury Form 15A)?
options: The Head and Sub-Head to which the collections are to be credited

The name of the receiving cashier
^
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 209(i) states that the Revenue Collector will insert the Head 
and subhead to which the collections are to be credited.

items.question:
Who is responsible for verifying the Head and subhead inserted by the Revenue Collector 
on the paying-in form?
options:
The Head of Internal Audit
166
Q
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 209(i) states that the receiving cashier will, in each case, 
verify the Head and subhead.

items.question: What is the purpose of the Revenue Collector's paying-in Form (Treasury Form 15)?
options: To track the movements of the Revenue Collector.
To serve as an internal audit document.
To support the lodgement of moneys with a Sub-Accounting Officer.

To report a loss of funds.
Q
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 218 mentions that a lodgement with a Sub-Accounting 
Officer will be supported by a paying-in form (Treasury Form 15).

items.question:
What is the role of the Head of Accounts in the process of handling cheques and bank drafts 
received through the post?
options: Opening the mail and maintaining the Paper Money Register.

Cross-checking the particulars of the original receipts with the details in the Paper Money 
Register.

Depositing the funds in the bank.
Q
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 233(ii) states that the Register shall be returned to the Head 
of Accounts, who will cross-check the particulars of the original receipts or licenses with the 
details entered in the Paper Money Register.

items.question:
