What is the term for a specific, objectively assessable, non-personality based, academic or 
factual concept?
items.explanation:
This is a meta-question about the nature of the quiz material. A "concept" is the 
term for an educational topic, which is what is being quizzed on.

items.question: What happens to arrears of revenue still outstanding from previous years?
options: They must be included in the current year's arrears of revenue returns.
They are reported to the Minister of Finance.

They are the responsibility of the previous Accounting Officer.
Q 167
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 230(iii) states that arrears of revenue returns must include all 
arrears still outstanding from previous years if not written off.

items.question:
What is the consequence of a loss of government revenue through direct payment of VAT 
and WHT to a contractor?
options: The Minister of Finance is notified.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 234(ii) states that any loss of government revenue through 
direct payment of VAT and WHT to a contractor shall result in the Accounting Officer being 
surcharged.

items.question: What must the Revenue Collector's cash book entries include?
options: Only the amount of money received.
The dates and numbers of all receipts issued.
The name of the person who paid the money.

The signature of the receiving cashier.
^
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 210 states that all receipt entries in the Revenue Collectors' 
cash book shall include the dates and numbers of all receipts issued.

items.question: When is a Revenue Collector not relieved of their responsibility by delegation?
options: Only if the delegation is in writing.

According to Financial Regulation 125, the delegation of responsibility does not relieve the 
officer of their pecuniary responsibilities.
When the person delegated to is also a Revenue Collector.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 125 states that a delegation of responsibility to a subordinate 
officer does not relieve the delegating officer of their pecuniary responsibility, which applies to 
Revenue Collectors.

items.question: How are the official receipts from a Revenue Collector's cash book to be numbered?
options: In any order that is convenient.
In a random order to prevent fraud.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 209(i) specifies that all receipts will be entered without delay 
in strict serial number order.

items.question:
What is the name of the register used for incoming cheques and other negotiable 
instruments received by post?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 232(i) requires the use of a "Paper Money Register" for this 
purpose.

items.question:
What is the purpose of the audit stamps that a Revenue Collector may be required to 
collect at the end of each duty?
options: To track the number of receipts issued.
To confirm the collection of revenue.

To verify the identity of the payer.
D To serve as proof of payment.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1712, found in the index, describes the collection of audit 
stamps at the end of each duty, which are used to confirm the collection of revenue.
169
Section B
Part 1: Financial Regulations: Expenditure Authorities for Recurrent and Capital 
Expenditure

items.question:
What are the primary instruments used to limit and arrange the disbursement of Federal 
Government funds?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 301 states: "The Annual Estimates and Appropriation Act are 
instruments used to limit and arrange the disbursement of the funds of the Federal 
Government."

items.question:
Who is responsible for any expenditure incurred without proper authority, as stated in 
Financial Regulation 301?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 301 clearly states that "Any officer controls a vote, or part 
thereof, who incurs expenditure without such authority does so on his sole responsibility and will 
consequently be held peculiarly responsible for his actions."

items.question: From which fund is Recurrent Expenditure primarily paid?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 302(i) states: "Recurrent expenditure is paid from the 
Consolidated Revenue Fund..."

items.question:
Which of the following is NOT a form of warrant by which the Minister of Finance conveys 
authority for recurrent expenditure?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 302(ii) lists the forms of warrants for recurrent expenditure. 
The Development Fund (Special) Warrant is for Capital Expenditure (FR 318).

items.question: When is a Provisional General Warrant issued?
options: At the end of the financial year.

When the Appropriation Act has not come into operation at the commencement of the 
financial year.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 303 states: "A provisional General Warrant shall be issued 
where the Appropriation Act has not come into operation at the commencement of the 'financial 
year."

items.question:
For how long can a Provisional General Warrant authorize the withdrawal of money from the 
fund?
items.explanation: Financial Regulation 303 specifies "a period not exceeding six months."

items.question: To whom is the original copy of a Provisional General Warrant addressed?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 303 states: "The original copy of a Provisional General 
Warrant is addressed to the Accountant-General with a signed copy forwarded to the 
Auditor-General."

items.question: What does the Annual General Warrant authorize the Accountant-General to do?
options: Only to pay for personal emoluments.

To issue funds to pay for personal emoluments and other services provided in the Annual 
Estimates.
To transfer funds from the Contingencies Fund.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 304 states: "The General Warrant authorises the 
Accountant-General to issue funds to pay for the personal emoluments and other services 
provided in the Annual Estimates..."

items.question:
Who may exclude an item of expenditure from the Annual General Warrant if special control 
is desired?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 304 states: "The Minister of Finance may exclude from the 
Annual General Warrant any item of expenditure over which it is desired to exercise special 
control."

items.question:
A Supplementary General Warrant authorizes funds for additional services approved in 
which document?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 305 states: "A Supplementary General Warrant authorises 
the Accountant-General to issue funds to pay for the additional personal emoluments and other 
services which must have been approved in Supplementary Estimates..."

items.question: Under what circumstances may a Supplementary (Contingencies) Warrant be issued?
options: In very exceptional cases where virement is not possible and urgency is high.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 306 specifies "very exceptional cases, where virement is not 
possible and where an application for additional provision reveals such a degree of urgency..."

items.question:
Funds transferred from the Contingencies Fund by a Supplementary (Contingencies) 
Warrant are transferred to which fund?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 306 states it authorizes the Accountant-General "to transfer 
the necessary funds from the Contingencies Fund to the Consolidated Revenue Fund."

items.question:
Can expenditure incurred under the Contingency Fund procedure be charged directly to the 
Contingency Fund?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 307 explicitly states: "In no circumstances will expenditure 
incurred under this Contingency Fund procedure be charged, or the relevant vouchers classified 
directly, to' the Contingency Fund."

items.question:
What is the authority for the Accountant-General to transfer sums appropriated from the 
Consolidated Revenue Fund to the Contingency Fund for replenishment?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 308 states: "A Supplementary General Warrant shall be the 
authority for the Accountant-General to transfer any sum appropriated from the Consolidated 
Revenue Fund to the Contingency Fund by way of replenishment."

items.question: When is a Virement Warrant issued?
options: When funds are required for a new project.

When additional provision is required under a sub-head and equivalent savings can be made 
under another sub-head of the same Head.
To authorize expenditure from the Contingencies Fund.

At the beginning of each financial year.
□
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 309 defines a Virement Warrant as being issued "when... 
additional provision is required under a particular sub-head (or a new sub-head is required) 
while, at the same time, equivalent savings can be made under another sub- head of the same 
Head."

items.question: Which of the following conditions applies to virement of funds?
options: It can be used to create a new sub-head.

It can re-introduce items disallowed by the Estimates Committee.
173

The amount to be vired under over-head costs should not exceed limits prescribed by the 
National Assembly.
It applies to queries raised by the Auditor-General for improper expenditure.Q
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 310(a) states: "the amount to be vired from any sub-head 
within a financial year under over-head costs should not exceed any limit prescribed by the 
National Assembly in the approved estimates for each sub head under the over-head costs."

items.question:
Virement from a Recurrent Expenditure Head of Account to a Capital Expenditure Head of 
Account is:
options: Allowed with Minister of Finance approval.
Allowed only in the last quarter of the financial year.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 316(iii) states: "Virement from one Head of Account in the 
Recurrent Expenditure estimates to another Head of account in Capital Expenditure estimates 
shall not be allowed and vice versa."

items.question:
Who must approve any variation in the Establishment or rates of allowances authorized for 
the Public Service?
options: Only the Office of the Head of Civil Service.
The Minister of Finance and the Office of the Head of Civil Service.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 311 states: "Any variation in the Establishment or rates of 
allowances... must receive the approval of the Minister of Finance and the Office of the Head of 
Civil Service..."

items.question: What is the purpose of a Supplementary (Statutory) Expenditure Warrant?
options: To provide funds for new capital projects.

To authorize additional expenditure for votes chargeable against the Consolidated Revenue 
Fund by virtue of the Constitution.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 312 states it is for "additional expenditure over and above 
that included in the General Warrant or in a Supplementary General Warrant, in respect of votes 
which are chargeable against the Consolidated Revenue Fund by virtue of the Constitution..."
174

items.question:
What is the limit of authority conveyed by Recurrent Expenditure Warrants to officers 
controlling votes?
options: Unlimited, as long as funds are available.

Limited to the amounts provided under each sub-head in the Approved Estimates and 
Supplementary Estimates.
Subject to the discretion of the Accounting Officer.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 313 states: "The authority conveyed... is limited to the 
amounts provided under each sub-head in the Approved Estimates and Supplementary 
Estimates."

items.question:
Can an unspent balance on a recurrent expenditure vote be revoted for the next financial 
year?
options: Yes, if approved by the Minister of Finance.
Yes, if approved by the National Assembly.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 314 states: "An unspent balance on a recurrent, expenditure 
vote cannot be revoted."

items.question: To whom should applications for supplementary provision be submitted?
options: To the Head of Civil Service.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 315 states: "applications for supplementary provision should 
be submitted to the Minister of Finance..."

items.question:
When must applications for virement relating to grading and establishment be submitted and 
approved?
options: Not later than the end of the financial year.
Not later than the first quarter of the Financial Year.
Not later than the tenth month of the budget year.

At any time during the financial year.
□
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 316(i) states: "Such application shall be made and approval 
obtained not later than first quarter of the Financial Year."
175

items.question:
Who is personally responsible for ensuring that expenditure against a sub-head from which 
provision was transferred by a virement warrant does not exceed the reduced balance?
options: Officers authorised to incur expenditure (officers controlling votes).
The Head of Finance and Accounts.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 317 states: "Officers controlling votes are personally 
responsible for ensuring compliance with this regulation."

items.question: From which fund is Capital Expenditure paid?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 318 states: "Capital Expenditure is paid from the 
Development Fund..."

items.question:
What is the maximum period a Provisional Development Fund General Warrant can 
authorize withdrawal of money?
items.explanation: Financial Regulation 319 states: "for a period not exceeding six months."

items.question:
Who authorizes the withdrawal of money from the Development Fund through a Provisional 
Development Fund General Warrant?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 319 states: "the President shall authorise the withdrawal of 
money from the Development fund as may be necessary through the issuance of a Provisional 
Development Fund General Warrant..."

items.question:
When does the Development Fund General Warrant authorize the Accountant-General to 
issue funds for capital projects?
176
options: At the beginning of the financial year.

After the Capital Expenditure Estimates have received approval by resolution of the National 
Assembly.
Upon the Minister of Finance's discretion.
Only for projects exceeding a certain threshold.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 320 states: "Such authority will be conveyed after the Capital 
Expenditure Estimates have received approval by resolution of the National Assembly."

items.question: What is the purpose of a Development Fund Supplementary Warrant?
options: To fund new projects not in the estimates.
To accelerate the provision of funds formally allocated but not yet voted for a project.
To transfer funds between different economic program sections.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 322(i) states its purpose is "to accelerate the provision of 
funds formally allocated but not yet voted for a project, by bringing forward an amount from the 
'Balance to Complete' column of the Estimates."

items.question:
What is the limitation on the amount of funds brought forward by a Development Fund 
Supplementary Warrant?
options: It can exceed the estimated total cost of the project.

It must not exceed the estimated total cost of the project as shown in the Annual or 
Supplementary Estimates.
It must be less than 50% of the project cost.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 322(i) states: "the amount of the provision brought forward, 
when added to the total expenditure authorised for the current year, must not exceed the 
estimated total cost of the project as shown in the Annual or Supplementary Estimates."

items.question: Who can authorize a Development Fund (Special) Warrant?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 323 states: "A Development Fund (Special) Warrant may be 
authorised by the Federal Executive Council (FEC)..."

items.question:
When is a Development Fund (Special) Warrant typically authorized?
177
options: When an application for Supplementary provision reveals a degree of urgency.
To reallocate funds between different Heads of Expenditure.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 323 states it is authorized "when an application for 
Supplementary provision reveals a degree of urgency which makes it essential that funds be 
immediately made available..."

items.question: What is the primary purpose of a Development Fund Virement Warrant?
options:
To permit the issue of additional funds for the completion of a capital project where earlier 
funds were insufficient.
To transfer funds from the Consolidated Revenue Fund.
To authorize expenditure from the Contingencies Fund.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 324 states: "The purpose of a Development Fund Virement 
Warrant is to permit the issue of additional funds required for the completion of a capital project, 
for which the funds earlier provided... was insufficient or inadequate..."

items.question: Which of the following is a limitation for a Development Fund Virement Warrant?
options: Re-allocation can be made between any Heads of Expenditure.
Funds can be provided for a new project.

Re-allocation may be made only within the same Head of Expenditure in the Capital 
Estimates.

It can increase provision to raise doubts about National Assembly intentions.
□
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 324(a) states: "re-allocation may be made only within the 
same Head of Expenditure in the Capital Estimates."

items.question: Who issues a Development Fund Virement Warrant?
options: The Minister of Finance after approval by the National Assembly.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 324 states: "A Development Fund Virement Warrant shall be 
issued by the Minister of Finance after approval by the National Assembly."

items.question: To whom should applications for additional capital provision be made?
options: To the Head of Civil Service.Q
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 325 states: "applications for additional capital provision shall 
be made by a letter addressed to the Ministry of Finance, with a copy to the National Planning 
Commission."

items.question:
The authority to incur expenditure from Public Funds other than the Consolidated Revenue 
Fund and Development Fund is what?
options: A general directive from the President.

The appropriate warrant signed by the Minister of Finance, addressed to the 
Accountant-General.
A resolution from the Federal Executive Council.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 326 states: "The authority to incur expenditure from any of 
the other Public Funds of the Government is the appropriate warrant signed by the Minister of 
Finance, addressed to the Accountant-General..."

items.question:
What happens to an unspent balance on a recurrent expenditure vote at the end of the 
financial year?
options: It is carried forward to the next year.
It is transferred to the Development Fund.
It is returned to the Federation Account.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 314 states, "An unspent balance on a recurrent, expenditure 
vote cannot be revoted." and FR 413(i) states "The authority for recurrent expenditure... lapses 
at the end of the financial year..."

items.question:
If a Capital Expenditure amount conveyed by a warrant has not been cash-backed from the 
Consolidated Revenue Fund Account to the Central Capital Account (CCA) by year-end, what 
happens?
options: It is transferred to the Contingencies Fund.
It requires re-appropriation by the National Assembly.a 179
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 413(ii)(a) states: "any amount conveyed by any of the 
warrants in chapter 3 which has not been cash-backed from the Consolidated Revenue Fund 
Account to the Central Capital Account (CCA) automatically lapses at the end of the Financial 
year."

items.question:
What happens to any balance in the Central Capital Account (CCA) at the end of the 
financial year that has not been cash-backed into the Capital Account of the spending 
Ministry/Agency?
options: It lapses by March 31st of the following year.
It is transferred back to the Consolidated Revenue Fund.

It is subject to a new appropriation.
□
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 413(ii)(b) states: "any balance in the CCA at the end of the 
Financial year which has not been cash-backed into the Capital Account of the spending 
Ministry/Agency shall lapse by 31st March of the following year."

items.question:
If an amount is cash-backed into the account of a spending Ministry/Agency for capital 
expenditure, does it lapse?
options: Yes, at the end of the financial year.
Yes, by March 31st of the following year.
No, it shall be utilized for the purpose stated in the warrant.
Only if the project is completed.3
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 413(ii)(c) states: "Any amount cash-backed into the Account 
of the spending Ministry/ Agency shall not lapse but shall be utilised for the purpose- stated in 
the warrant."

items.question: Who is responsible for ensuring that expenditure is spread evenly over the year?
options: Officers controlling votes and officers acting under their instructions.
The Head of Finance and Accounts.Q
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 419 states: "Officers controlling votes and officers acting 
under their instructions shall arrange, r “ far as it is practicable, to spread expenditure evenly 
over the year."

items.question:
If an Accounting Officer's directive results in unauthorized expenditure and contravenes 
extant rules, who is responsible unless a report was made to the Head of Service or Minister?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 117(ii) states: "the Accounting Officer shall be responsible for 
such an unauthorised expenditure unless a report had been made by the Accounting Officer to 
the Head of Service... or to the Minister."

items.question: What is the primary responsibility of the Political Head of a Ministry regarding its activities?
options: To supervise and control its activities.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 118(i) states: "It shall be the responsibility of the Political 
Head to supervise and control the activities of his ministry, agency and/or parastatal."

items.question: What is the definition of a "Revenue Collector"?
options: An officer entrusted with receipt, custody, and disbursement of public money.

An officer entrusted with an official receipt, licence, or ticket booklet for regular revenue 
collection and keeps a cash book.
An officer who approves revenue expenditure.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 119 defines a "Revenue Collector" as "an officer, other than 
a Sub- Accounting officer, who is entrusted with an official receipt, licence or ticket booklet for 
the regular collection of some particular forms of revenue and who is required to keep a cash 
book."

items.question: What is the definition of an "Imprest-holder"?
options: An officer who manages the ministry's main bank account.

An officer entrusted with the disbursement of public money for which vouchers cannot be 
immediately presented to a Sub-Accounting Officer, and who keeps a cash book.
An officer who approves all capital expenditure.

An officer responsible for auditing revenue collections.
Q
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 120 defines "Imprest-holder" as "an officer other than a 
Sub-Accounting officer who is entrusted with the disbursement of public money, for which 
vouchers cannot be presented immediately to a Sub-Accounting Officer and who is required to 
keep a cash book."

items.question:
Who is an "Officer Controlling Expenditure"?
181
options:
The officer to whom the control of a sub-head or specified amount has been delegated by the 
appropriate Accounting Officer.
Any officer who signs payment vouchers.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 121 defines an "Officer Controlling Expenditure" as "the 
officer to whom the control of a sub-head, or specified amount of a sub head, has been 
delegated by the appropriate Accounting Officer."

items.question:
In a Self Accounting Unit, who is charged with the installation and maintenance of a proper 
system of accounts?
options:
The Head of Finance and Accounts.
□
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 122 states that "the Accounting Officer as defined in 
Financial Regulation 111 is charged, in relation to that unit, with the installation and maintenance 
of a proper system of accounts."

items.question:
What is the maximum number of days a public officer has to reply to formal queries or other 
enquiries from the Accountant-General and Auditor-General?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 123(i)(p) states "within twenty-one days reply to formal 
queries or any other enquiries addressed to him by the Accountant-General and 
Auditor-General..."

items.question:
Who is personally and peculiarly responsible for the due performance of the financial duties 
of their ministries and for any inaccuracies in accounts rendered under their authority?
options: Only the Head of Finance and Accounts.a 182
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 124 states, "All officers are personally and peculiarly 
responsible for the due performance of the financial duties of their ministries/extra-ministerial 
offices and other arms of government..."
Part 2: Financial Regulations: Expenditure Classification and Control

items.question: Who is primarily responsible for making disbursements of public funds?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 401 (i) states: "All disbursements of public funds shall be 
made by the Accountant General on the authority of the appropriate Warrant issued to him and 
also by Accounting Officers entrusted with the expenditure of public fund falling within the votes 
of charge of their ministries or extra ministerial offices and other arms, of government."

items.question:
Who retains the ultimate responsibility for the control of votes to the Federal Executive 
Council?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 401 (ii) states: "the ultimate responsibility to the Federal 
Executive Council for the control of votes rests with the Account log Officers at all times."

items.question:
What is the personal consequence for any officer making, allowing, or directing any 
disbursement without proper authority?
options: They will be personally responsible for the amount involved.
Their subordinates will be held responsible.

The expenditure will be absorbed by the Contingencies Fund
□ .
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 401 (ii) states: "any officer making, allowing or directing any 
disbursement without proper authority shall be personally responsible for the amount involved."

items.question: What is the primary duty of every officer controlling a vote?
options:
To monitor the expenditure pattern of his ministry with reference to the amounts provided in 
the Estimates.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 402(i) states: "It is the duty of every officer controlling a vote 
to monitor the expenditure pattern of his ministry, extra ministerial office and other arms of 
government with reference to the amounts provided in the Estimates."

items.question: What must a departmental vote book clearly show in respect of each sub-head?
options: The amount sanctioned in the Estimates.
Any additional provision by supplementary warrants.
Any reduction of the provision by virement.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 402(i)(a) lists all these as details to be shown in the vote 
book.

items.question: How should any reduction of provision in a vote book by virement be indicated?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 402(i)(a)(iii) specifies: "Any such reduction should be shown 
in red ink."

items.question: What should be entered in the vote book in addition to expenditure incurred?
options: Both liabilities and commitments as soon as they are known or incurred.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 403 states: "Similarly all liabilities and commitments will be 
entered as-soon as they are known or incurred."

items.question: Who may delegate the day-to-day control of expenditure to a subordinate officer?
options: The Head of Finance and Accounts.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 404 states: "An Accounting Officer, at his discretion, may 
delegate the day-to-day control of expenditure to a subordinate officer..."
184

items.question:
Can an officer to whom expenditure control has been delegated sub-delegate such 
responsibility?
options: Yes, with the approval of the Accounting Officer.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 404 states: "He shall not sub-delegate such responsibility 
wholly or partly, without the knowledge and approval of the officer controlling the vote."

items.question:
What document is issued by an Accounting Officer to authorize other officers to incur 
expenditure and sign vouchers on his behalf?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 405 states: "...he shall do so by issuing Authority to Incur 
Expenditure (A.I.E)..."

items.question:
What must accompany an Authority to Incur Expenditure (A.I.E) issued by an Accounting 
Officer?
options: A copy of the Annual Estimates.
A list of all previous expenditures.
A letter of approval from the Minister of Finance.a
items.explanation: Financial Regulation 405 states: "...supported by appropriate cash backing..."

items.question:
When an A.I.E. is issued to another ministry, what is the duty of the responsible officer in the 
receiving ministry?
options: To immediately spend all the funds.
To maintain a Vote Book and forward monthly returns of expenditure to the issuing Ministry.
To return the A.I.E. to the issuing ministry.
To report directly to the Accountant-General.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 406 states: "it will be the duty of the responsible officer in the 
receiving Ministry or Department to maintain a Vote Book and forward a monthly returns of 
expenditure to the issuing Ministry/ Department..."
185

items.question:
What must accompany the monthly returns of expenditure when an A.I.E. is issued between 
ministries?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 406 states: "The monthly returns must be supported with 
copies of paid vouchers."

items.question:
What stamp must be affixed to all vouchers, indents, local purchase orders, job orders, and 
agreements when entered in the vote book?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 407 states: "...all vouchers, indents, local purchase orders, 
job orders, agreements shall be stamped 'ENTERED IN VOTE BOOK' and initialed by the 
officer responsible for maintaining the vote book."

items.question:
Who is responsible for ensuring that the requisite vote book entries are made before 
payments are authorized?
options: The Head of Finance and Accounts.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 407 states: "Officers empowered to incur expenditure must 
ensure by adding their initials against the. relevant entry that the requisite vote book entries are 
made before the payments are authorised by their signatures to the payment vouchers."

items.question:
How often does the Accountant-General furnish Accounting Officers with schedules of 
payments and adjustments?
options: As soon as possible after the end of each month.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 408 states: "As soon as possible after the end of each 
month, the Accountant General will' furnish Accounting Officers with schedules of all payments 
and adjustments..."

items.question:
If a serious irregularity in a voucher is established or suspected, what is the first action the 
officer controlling the vote should take?
options: Report to the Minister of Finance.
Immediately investigate and report the circumstances.
Transfer the funds to a suspense account.Q
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 409 states: "Where a serious irregularity in a voucher is 
established or suspected the officer controlling the vote shall immediately investigate and report 
the circumstances..."

items.question:
In the event of an unauthorized payment due to an incorrect certificate or entry on a 
voucher, who shall be held responsible and surcharged?
options: The certifying officer or the Sub-Accounting Officer.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 409 states: "the certifying officer or the sub-accounting 
officer as provided under Regulation 420 shall be held responsible and surcharged for the 
amount involved."

items.question: The signature of the officer controlling a vote on a voucher certifies the accuracy of what?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 410 states: "The signature of the officer controlling a vote, or 
an officer signing for him, certifies to the accuracy of every detail on the voucher."

items.question: What is the minimum rank for officers authorized to sign payment vouchers?
options: Senior Executive Officer (Accounts) or Accountant I.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 411 states: "Officers authorised to sign payment vouchers 
shall not be below the rank of Accountant I or a Senior Executive Officer- (Accounts)."

items.question:
What must be attached to a voucher where a contract involves supplies of goods or work 
done?
options: A certificate that payments are in accordance with contract terms.
A copy of the budget allocation.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 412 states: "there will be attached to the voucher a certificate 
that the payments are in accordance with terms of the contract agreement..."

items.question:
If a deduction is made from a contract payment for a penalty or fine, what happens to the 
deduction?
options: It is kept by the ministry.
It is credited to the appropriate revenue head.
It is transferred to a suspense account.

It is returned to the contractor.
□
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 412 states: "while the deduction shall be credited to the 
appropriate revenue head."

items.question: When does the authority for recurrent expenditure conveyed by warrants lapse?
options: At the end of the calendar year.
At the end of the financial year to which it relates.
When the funds are fully expended.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 413(i) states: "The authority for recurrent expenditure 
conveyed by any of the warrants contained in Chapter 3 lapses at the end of the financial year 
to which it relates."

items.question:
For capital expenditure, what happens to any amount conveyed by warrants that has not 
been cash-backed from the Consolidated Revenue Fund Account to the Central Capital Account 
(CCA) by year-end?
options: It is carried forward to the next year.
It is transferred to the Development Fund.

It requires re-appropriation by the National Assembly.
Q 188
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 413(ii)(a) states: "any amount conveyed by any of the 
warrants in chapter 3 which has not been cash-backed from the Consolidated Revenue Fund 
Account to the Central Capital Account (CCA) automatically lapses at the end of the Financial 
year."

items.question:
What happens to any balance in the Central Capital Account (CCA) at the end of the 
financial year that has not been cash-backed into the Capital Account of the spending 
Ministry/Agency?
options: It lapses by March 31st of the following year.
It is transferred back to the Consolidated Revenue Fund.

It is subject to a new appropriation.
□
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 413(ii)(b) states: "any balance in the CCA at the end of the 
Financial year which has not been cash-backed into the Capital Account of the spending 
Ministry/Agency shall lapse by 31st March of the following year."

items.question:
If an amount is cash-backed into the account of a spending Ministry/Agency for capital 
expenditure, does it lapse?
options: Yes, at the end of the financial year.
Yes, by March 31st of the following year.
No, it shall be utilized for the purpose stated in the warrant.

Only if the project is completed.
□
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 413(ii)(c) states: "Any amount cash-backed into the Account 
of the spending Ministry/ Agency shall not lapse but shall be utilised for the purpose- stated in 
the warrant."

items.question: What is the rule regarding anticipated savings for payments?
options: They can be utilized at any time.
They shall not be utilized for payments before they are due.
They can be transferred to a deposit account.

They are automatically carried forward.
Q
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 414 states: "Under no circumstances shall anticipated 
savings be utilised for' payments before they are due."

items.question: What is the federal government's requirement for all officers responsible for expenditure?
options:
To maintain large cash reserves
189
Q .
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 415 states: "The federal government requires all officers 
responsible for expenditure to exercise due economy. Money must not be spent merely because 
it has been voted on."

items.question: How should expenditure be classified?
options: According to the discretion of the Accounting Officer.
Strictly in accordance with the Estimates.
Based on the amount of the expenditure.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 417 states: "Expenditure shall strictly be classified in 
accordance with the Estimates..."

items.question: What happens if expenditure is incorrectly charged to a vote?
options: It will be adjusted in the next financial year.
It will be transferred to a general fund.

It will be reported to the Minister of Finance for approval.
□
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 417 states: "Expenditure incorrectly charged to a vote shall 
be disallowed."

items.question:
How should payments made on behalf of self-accounting units by Accounting Officers of 
other units be classified?
options: Directly to the expenditure sub-head of the Estimates.
To the appropriate Cash Transfer Account.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 418 states: "shall be classified to the appropriate' Cash 
Transfer Account and not-direct to the expenditure sub-head of the Estimates."

items.question: Who is solely liable for unauthorized expenditure in excess of the sum allocated?
options: The Head of Finance and Accounts.a 190
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 419 states: "Officers controlling votes are solely liable for 
unauthorised expenditure in excess of the sum allocated."

items.question:
Before making any payment, what must a Sub-Accounting Officer satisfy himself about the 
voucher?
options: That it is authorized by Warrant and correctly classified.
That the information furnished is correct and certificates completed.
That all deductions have been duly made.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 420 lists all these as conditions a Sub-Accounting Officer 
must satisfy himself about.

items.question: Who can effect an alteration to the classification of a voucher?
options:
The Head of Finance and Accounts.
□
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 421 states: "An alteration to the classification of a voucher 
shall only be effected by the officer controlling expenditure who will amend his Vote Book 
accordingly."

items.question:
What is the responsibility of officers controlling votes regarding payment for services 
rendered?
options: To defer payments to the next financial year for budgetary reasons.
To ensure payments are settled within the financial year in which they were rendered.
To only pay for services after all claims have been perfectly validated.
To transfer all outstanding claims to a suspense account.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 412(i) states: "Officers controlling votes are responsible for' 
ensuring that, as far as possible, payment for services rendered are settled within the financial 
year in which they were rendered."

items.question: What must be entered into the Vote Book as liabilities?
options: All instruments of commitments, indents, LPOs, job Orders, and signed contracts.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 412(ii) states: "All instruments of commitments, indents, 
LPOs, job Orders, and signed contracts must be entered into the Vote Book as liabilities."

items.question:
What happens if an officer neglects to press for claims from private parties, causing 
prejudice to the private party?
options: The private party is solely responsible.
The officer who neglected to press for claims shall be held responsible.
The claim will be written off.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 412(i) states: "Neglect to do this will not prejudice the private 
party but the Officer who has neglected to press for claims shall be held responsible."

items.question: What is the purpose of stamping vouchers "ENTERED IN VOTE BOOK"?
options:
To confirm that the expenditure has been recorded as a commitment or payment in the vote 
book.
To show that the voucher has been audited.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 407 implies this purpose, ensuring that financial 
commitments and payments are properly tracked against the allocated budget.

items.question: If a payment is made on account for a contract, what is the money claimed limited to?
options: The cost of the work certified to have been performed.
An arbitrary percentage of the contract.
The amount available in the vote.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 412 states: "In the case o. payment on account, no money 
will be claimed other than the cost of the work certified to have been performed."

items.question:
What is the implication of an unexpended portion of any sub-head not being drawn for the 
purpose of setting it in reserve?
options: It can be carried to a deposit account.
It can be placed in a suspense account.
It should not be drawn for setting in reserve or carried to a deposit/suspense account.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 414 states: "Likewise the 'unexpended portion of any 
subhead shall not be drawn for the purpose of setting it in reserve to meet impending payments, 
or be carried to a deposit or a suspense account."

items.question: What is the rule regarding drawing unallocated stores for use?
options: They can be drawn at any time.

They should not be drawn before they are required to utilize provision in the Estimates which 
would otherwise lapse.
They must be drawn by year-end.
They can be drawn to meet any unforeseen need.Q
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 414 states: "Un allocated stores may not be drawn for use 
before they are required in order to utilise provision in the Estimates which would otherwise 
lapse at the end of the financial year."

items.question:
Who is responsible for providing the Internal checking section, internal audit unit, and 
Paying Officer with specimen signatures of officers authorized to sign payment vouchers?
options: The Head of Finance and Accounts.3
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 411 states: "The Accounting Officer shall furnish the Internal 
checking section, internal audit unit and the Paying Officer with the specimen signatures along 
with the list of relevant votes chargeable."

items.question:
If a discrepancy is found when examining schedules of payments and adjustments from the 
Accountant-General, what should be done?
options: It should be ignored if minor.
It should be immediately brought to the notice of the Accountant-General.
It should be corrected by the Accounting Officer.
It should be reported to the Auditor-General only.Q
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 408 states: "Any discrepancy which such examinations may 
disclose should immediately be brought to the notice of the Accountant-General."

items.question:
What is the consequence if a loss of public fund is revealed following an investigation into a 
suspected irregularity?
options: The officer will be given a warning.
Action shall be taken in accordance with Chapter 25 of these Regulations.
The loss will be covered by the Contingencies Fund.

The matter will be referred to the National Assembly.
193
Q
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 409 states: "Where a loss of 'public fund is revealed, action 
shall be taken in accordance with Chapter 25 of these Regulations."

items.question: What is the definition of "Expenditure Classification"?
options: Categorizing expenses according to the Estimates.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 417 implies that expenditure classification is the act of 
categorizing expenses according to the approved Estimates.

items.question: What is the primary purpose of the Departmental Vote Book?
options: To monitor and control expenditure against budget allocations.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 402(i) clearly outlines the purpose of the vote book as 
monitoring expenditure against the Estimates.

items.question:
What is the consequence if an officer controlling a vote incurs expenditure without proper 
authority?
options: The expenditure will be absorbed by the Ministry.
The officer will be held pecuniarily responsible for his actions.
The Accountant-General will rectify the error.

The Minister of Finance will issue a new warrant.
□
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 301 (which is referenced in 401) states: "Any officer controls 
a vote, or part thereof, who incurs expenditure without such authority does so on his sole 
responsibility and will consequently be held peculiarly responsible for his actions."

items.question: When should liabilities and commitments be entered into the vote book?
options: At the end of the financial year.
As soon as they are known or incurred.
Only when actual payment is made.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 403 states: "Similarly all liabilities and commitments will be 
entered as-soon as they are known or incurred."

items.question: What is the role of the Sub-Accounting Officer regarding the classification of a voucher?
options: They cannot change it arbitrarily; only the officer controlling expenditure can.

They are not involved in classification.
□
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 421 states: "The classification of a voucher shall not be 
changed arbitrarily by a checking officer or Sub-Accounting Officer."

items.question:
Question: What is the primary objective of "Expenditure Control" as discussed in Chapter 
4?
options: To ensure all allocated funds are spent.
To prevent fraud and waste in public spending.
items.explanation:
The entire chapter emphasizes mechanisms (vote books, warrants, 
responsibilities) to ensure proper and authorized use of funds, directly aiming to prevent fraud, 
waste, and unauthorized expenditure.
195
Section C
Part 1: Financial Regulations: Standardization and Accounting Procedures

items.question:
Who has full authority to direct on all matters relating to the finance and accounting affairs of 
the federation, not assigned by law to any other Minister?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 501 states: "By the provisions of Sections 3 and 4 of the 
Finance (Control and Management) Act Cap 144 Laws of the Federation, 1990, the Minister of 
Finance shall have full authority to direct on all matters relating to the Finance and accounting 
affairs of the federation which are not by law assigned to any: other Minister."

items.question: What is the primary purpose of the Financial Regulations issued by the Minister of Finance?
options: To guide public officers on procurement only.

To guide public officers on the receipts and disbursements of Public Funds, and the 
management of public assets.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 501 states: "Specifically, the Minister of Finance shall from 
time to time direct the issuance of the Financial Regulations to guide all public officers on the 
receipts and disbursements of Public Funds, as well as the management of public assets."

items.question:
Who is responsible for issuing Treasury Circulars and Accounting Manuals to guide 
Accounting Officers and other employees?
options: The Head of Finance and Accounts.3
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 502 states: "Pursuant to FR 501, the Accountant-General 
shall issue from time to time Treasury Circulars and Accounting Manual to guide Accounting 
Officers and other employees of each ministries/extra- ministerial offices and other arms of 
government 01) all matters relating to the provisions of the Financial Regulations."

items.question:
What is the reason for standardizing accounting procedures across federal ministries and 
self-accounting units?
options:
To reduce the need for audits.
196
Due to the frequent movement of accounting staff.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 503 states: "The frequent movement of accounting staff 
between ministries, extra/ministerial offices and other arms of Government calls for a degree of 
standardisation in the accounting procedures in use in all federal ministries and self accounting 
units."

items.question:
What documents are federal ministries and other arms of government required to use for their 
daily financial transactions?
options:
The Financial Regulations, Treasury Accounting Manual, and Treasury Circulars issued by 
the Office of the Accountant General.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 503 states: "For this purpose, federal ministries/extra 
ministerial offices and other arms of government shall use the Financial Regulations, Treasury 
Accounting Manual and Treasury Circulars issued by the Office of the Accountant General in 
accounting for their daily financial transactions."

items.question:
Who must approve any material variation of an existing accounting procedure or a proposed 
new accounting procedure?
options: The Accountant-General after consultation with the Auditor-General.

The Head of Finance and Accounts.
□
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 504 states: "Any material variation of an existing accounting 
procedure, or a proposed accounting procedure to be introduced into a new office, must be 
approved by the Accountant-General after consultation with the Auditor-General."

items.question:
Who must approve the purchase of accounting equipment or machines for a variation of an 
existing procedure, a new installation, or a major replacement?
options: The Head of Finance and Accounts.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 505 states: "The purchase of accounting equipment or 
machines, in connection with a variation of an existing procedure, or an installation in a new 
office, or in replacement of a major item of equipment must be approved by the 
Accountant-General."

items.question:
What document must be submitted to the Accountant-General when applying for a variation 
of an existing accounting procedure or introducing a new one?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 506 states: "A detailed flow chart shall be submitted to the 
Accountant General when application is being made for a variation of an existing procedure or 
introduction of a procedure to a new office."

items.question:
To whom should the approved Accounting Code, Internal Audit Guide, and Treasury Circulars 
be circulated within a ministry or self-accounting unit?
options: Only to the Head of Finance and Accounts.

To all officers within the ministry, extra-ministerial office and other arms of government for 
their guidance and compliance.

Only to the internal audit unit.
□
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 507 states: "The approved Accounting Code and Internal 
Audit Guide of ministries/extra-ministerial offices and other arms of government or self 
accounting unit as well as Treasury circulars issued by the Accountant-General shall be 
circulated to all officers within the ministry, extra-ministerial office and other arms of government 
for their guidance and compliance."

items.question:
What is the relationship between an approved accounting code and the Financial 
Regulations if there is a conflict of detail?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 201 (which sets the precedent for accounting codes) states: 
"Where the approved code is at variance with these regulations, the code will prevail." This 
principle is implied for all accounting codes.

items.question:
What is the primary purpose of the Financial Regulations, as stated in the preface?
198
options:
To guarantee probity and transparency in the control and management of public funds and 
resources.

To reduce the number of public servants.
□
items.explanation:
The Preface states: "The issue of this edition is borne out of the concern of the 
government to ensure that requisite rules and regulations that would guarantee probity and 
transparency in the control and management of public funds and resources of government are 
put in place."

items.question:
Who is enjoined to acquaint themselves with these Regulations, the Finance (Control and 
Management) Act, and relevant Sections of the Constitution dealing with public finance?
items.explanation:
The Preface states: "Public Officers, are enjoined to acquaint themselves with 
these Regulations, the Finance (Control and Management) Act, Cap. 144 Laws of the federation 
of Nigeria 1990, as well as the relevant Sections of the Constitution dealing with public finance, 
for proper guidance."

items.question:
What does the Finance (Control and Management) Act Cap 144 Laws of the Federation, 
1990, regulate?
options: Certain financial matters of the government.
Only the salaries of public officers.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 104 states: "Certain financial matters of government are 
regulated by the laws of the Federation enacted by the National Assembly. These include the 
Finance (Control and Management) Act CAP 144 Laws of the Federation of Nigeria, 1990..."

items.question:
Who is responsible for providing adequate accounting systems and controls in ministries 
and extra-ministerial offices?
options:
The Head of Finance and Accounts.
□ 199
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 106 states: "The Accountant-General of the Federation is the 
Head of the Federal Government Accounting Services and the Treasury. He has the 
responsibility for providing adequate accounting systems and controls in the ministries, 
extra-ministerial offices and other arms of Government."

items.question:
What is one of the duties of the Accountant-General regarding accounting codes and 
manuals?
options:
To approve and ensure compliance with accounting codes, internal audit guides and stock 
verification manuals.
To formulate them without external input.

To distribute them to the public.
□
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 107(i) states: "approve and ensure compliance with 
accounting codes, internal audit guides and stock verification manuals of federal ministries, 
extra-ministerial offices and other arms of government."

items.question:
What type of audit ensures that laid down procedures are being observed in tendering, 
contracts, and store-keeping to prevent waste, pilferage, and extravagance?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 109(l)(c) states: "Financial Control Audit-to ensure that laid 
down procedures are being observed in tendering, contracts and store-keeping with a view to 
preventing waste, pilferage and Extravagance."

items.question:
Who has free access to books of accounts, files, safes, security documents, and other 
records relating to the accounts of all federal ministries?
options: The Accountant-General and the Auditor-General or their representatives.

The Head of Finance and Accounts.
Q
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 110 states: "By virtue of the responsibilities and functions of 
the Accountant General and the Auditor-General or their representatives shall, at all reasonable 
times, have free access to books of accounts, files, safes, security documents and other records 
and information relating to the accounts, of all federal ministries/extra ministerial offices and 
other arms of government, or units."

items.question:
What is the definition of an "Accounting Officer" in the context of these regulations?
200
options: Any officer who handles public money.

The Permanent Secretary of a ministry or the head of an extra-ministerial office who is in full 
control of and responsible for human, material, and financial resources.
The officer responsible for internal audit.
The officer who signs payment vouchers.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 111 (i) states: "The Term 'Accounting Officer' means the 
Permanent Secretary of a ministry or the head of extra-ministerial office and other arms of 
government who is in full control of, and is responsible for human, material and financial 
resources which are critical inputs' in the management of an organization."

items.question:
What is one of the key responsibilities of Accounting Officers under the Public Procurement 
Act?
options: To ensure adequate appropriation is available for Procurements in their annual budget.
To approve all payments without review.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 113(b) states: "ensure that adequate appropriation is 
available for Procurements in their annual budget."

items.question:
What is the role of the Head of Finance and Accounts regarding compliance with Financial 
Regulations?
options: To ensure compliance by all staff under his control and supervision.

To approve all variations in accounting procedures.
□
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 114(a) states: "ensuring compliance with Financial 
Regulations and the Accounting Code by all staff under his control and supervision."
Financial Regulations: Payments Procedure

items.question:
All payment entries in the cash book/accounts must be vouched for on one of the prescribed 
treasury forms. In whose favor should vouchers be made out?
options: The Head of Finance and Accounts.
The person or persons to whom the money is actually due.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 601 states: "All payment entries in the cash book/accounts 
shall be vouched for on one of the prescribed treasury forms. Vouchers are made out in favour 
of the person or persons to whom the money is actually due."

items.question: Can a cheque be raised or cash paid for services for which a voucher has not been raised?
options: Only if the amount is small.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 601 states: "Under no circumstances shall a cheque be 
raised or cash paid for services for which a voucher has not been raised."

items.question: How should vouchers be raised for different sub-heads or different services?
options: A single voucher can cover multiple sub-heads and services.
Separate vouchers shall be raised for separate sub-heads and different services.
Vouchers can be combined if the total amount is small.
It is at the discretion of the Accounting Officer.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 602 states: "Separate vouchers shall be raised for separate 
sub-heads. Separate vouchers shall also be raised for the payment of different services."

items.question: What kind of particulars should all vouchers contain?
options: Only the amount and payee name.
Full particulars such as dates, numbers, quantities, distances, and rates.
A brief description of the service.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 603(i) states: "All vouchers shall contain full particulars of 
each service, such as dates, numbers, quantities, distances and rates, so as to enable them to 
be checked without reference to any other documents and will invariably be supported by 
relevant documents such as local purchase orders, invoices, special letters of authority, time 
sheets, etc."

items.question:
Which of the following is NOT an acceptable form of warrant authority to be quoted on 
vouchers for payments?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 603(ii)(d) lists the acceptable forms of warrant authorities. 
"Emergency Fund Release (E.F.R.)" is not listed.

items.question: What is strictly prohibited when preparing payment vouchers?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 604(a) states: "On no account shall vouchers be written in 
pencil."

items.question:
What should be drawn immediately before and after the Naira (N) figure when it appears in 
words on a voucher?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 604(c) states: "A single thick horizontal line shall be drawn 
immediately before, and immediately after the Naira (N) figure where it appears in word."

items.question:
Who is responsible for maintaining a payment voucher register and confirming the accuracy 
of entries before signing a voucher?
options: The Head of Finance and Accounts.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 605 states: "Officer controlling vote shall maintain a payment 
voucher register... Under no circumstances shall he sign a voucher until it has been entered in 
the register and has accordingly confirmed the accuracy of the entries therein."

items.question:
Which copies of payment vouchers should be signed in full in ink or ball pens by the 
certifying officer and the payee?
options: Only the duplicate and triplicate copies.
Only the copy was sent to the Auditor-General.a 203
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 606 states: "Only the originals of payment vouchers shall be 
signed in full in ink or ball pens by the certifying officer and the payee."

items.question: Question: What is the rule regarding signing multiple certificates on a payment voucher?
options: Signatures can be written across multiple certificates.
Each certificate on a payment voucher shall be signed separately.
Only the first certificate needs a full signature.

A single signature at the bottom of the voucher is sufficient.
□
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 607 states: "Each certificate on a payment voucher shall be 
signed separately. Signatures shall not be written across one or more certificates."

items.question: How should duplicate and triplicate copies of vouchers be marked?
options: They do not need any special marking.
They should be clearly marked "Duplicate" and "Triplicate".

They should be marked "For Information Only".
Q
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 608 states: "Duplicate and triplicate copies shall be clearly 
marked "Duplicate", "Triplicate" and copies shall not be accepted as payment vouchers."

items.question: What is the maximum time a payment voucher should be held in the checking section?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 609(iii) states: "Payment vouchers received in the checking 
section must be promptly dealt with and under no circumstances shall a voucher be held in that 
unit for more than forty-eight (48) hours."

items.question: What is the procedure if an alteration to the amount of a voucher is necessary?
options: The alteration can be initiated by the certifying officer.
A new voucher must be prepared.
The alteration can be made and stamped "Corrected".
The Head of Finance and Accounts can approve the alteration.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 610 states: "Alterations to the amount of a voucher whether 
in words or figure are not allowed. A new voucher must be prepared when necessary."
204

items.question:
A Sub-Accounting Officer may not make payment against a voucher unless it is not more 
than how many months since it was signed by the officer controlling expenditure?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 611(c) states: "It is not more than three months since the 
voucher was signed by the officer controlling expenditure."

items.question:
When a voucher is submitted for payment at another station, how should it be sent after 
being checked and stamped?
options: Under the security schedule of Transfer of Payment Vouchers through the Sub-Treasurer.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 612 states: "When a voucher is submitted to a Sub-Treasury 
or Cash Office for payment at another station, it will, after being checked and stamped in 
accordance with Regulation 609, be sent under security schedule of Transfer of Payment 
Vouchers through the Sub- Treasurer (See TF 193)."

items.question:
Who is responsible for furnishing proof of identity if required when a payment is being 
claimed?
options: The ministry, extra-ministerial office, or other arms of Government authorising payment.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 613 states: "...it is the duty of the ministry, extra-ministerial 
office and other arms of Government authorising payment to furnish proof of identity if required."

items.question:
When a payee is illiterate, whose mark must be witnessed by a literate official other than the 
paying officer?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 614 states: "Where a payee is an illiterate, his or her mark 
must be witnessed by a literate official other than the paying officer."
205

items.question:
What should be presented to the Sub-Accounting Officer for inspection when payments are 
to be made to legal representatives?
options: Authorities such as Powers of Attorney, Letters of Administration, etc.
A copy of the deceased's will only.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 615 states: "When payments are to be made to legal 
representatives, authorities such as Powers of Attorney, Letters of Administration, etc. shall be 
presented to the Sub Accounting Officer for inspection..."

items.question: How should payments to firms be made?
options: By cheque crossed "Not Negotiable", "Account Payee only", in favour of the firm.
By bank transfer directly to the firm's account.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 616 states: "Payments to firms, shall be made only by 
cheques crossed "Not Negotiable", "Account Payee only", in favour of the firm."

items.question:
If a payment to a firm is in doubt due to loss of relevant documents and an initial 
investigation reveals no prior payment, what must the payee issue to indemnify the federal 
government against double payment?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 618 states: "...the payee shall be made to issue a certificate 
of indemnity to indemnify the federal government against double payment should an earlier 
payment be established at a later date."

items.question:
Any payment due to or receipted by any government officer in his official capacity shall be 
paid to whom?
options: The Head of Finance and Accounts.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 619 states: "Any payment due to or receipted by any 
government officer in his official capacity shall be paid to the Treasury for which official receipt 
shall be obtained."

items.question:
What is the minimum amount for a receipt given for payment for goods supplied or services 
rendered to be liable to N50.00 Stamp Duty?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 620(a)(i) states: "Receipts given for payment for goods 
supplied or services rendered. If the amount is N1,000 or over a N50.00 stamp is required in 
respect of each signature acknowledging an amount."

items.question: Which of the following payments is NOT exempt from Stamp Duty under all conditions?
options: Payments for goods supplied or services rendered if the amount is N1,500.00.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 620(b)(i) states that payments for goods supplied or services 
rendered are exempt if the amount is under N 1,000.00. Therefore, N 1,500.00 would be liable.

items.question:
Where banking facilities are available, all payments to the public other than firms covered 
under FR 514 must be made by cheque crossed in what manner?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 621 states: "Where banking facilities are available, all 
payments to the public other than firms covered under FR 514 must be made by cheque 
crossed "Account Payee only"."

items.question:
Immediately after payment is made, what should the paying officer stamp the original 
voucher and all copies, invoices, warrants, or other supporting documents with?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 622 states: "Immediately after payment is made, the paying 
officer shall stamp the original voucher and all copies, all invoices warrant or other supporting 
documents "PAID"."

items.question:
If a payment voucher is presented to obtain cash for payment elsewhere (e.g., salaries), 
what form must accompany it?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 623 states: "Where a payment voucher is presented at a 
Sub-Treasury or Cash Officer for the purpose of obtaining cash for payment to be made 
elsewhere, as in the case of drawing, cash for the payment of salaries, wages, etc. It shall be 
accompanied by a cheque/cash order form signed by the officer authorising the voucher, and 
bearing the signature of the officer authorised to receive the cheque/cash."

items.question:
Vouchers withdrawn against cheque/cash order forms must be returned to the Sub-Treasury 
or Cash Officer within how many days?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 624 states: "Vouchers withdrawn against cheque/cash order 
forms must be returned to the Sub-Treasury or Cash Officer within seven days."

items.question:
Question: What rank should officers authorized to draw cash/cheques on the strength of a 
Cheque Order Form NOT be below?
options:
Accountant II or Higher Executive Officer (Accounts).
208
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 628 states: "Officers authorised to draw cash/cheques on a 
strength of Cheque Order From should not be below the rank of Accountant II or Higher 
Executive Officer (Accounts)."

items.question:
If a payment voucher is reported lost, and it is established that no loss or fraud has 
occurred, to whom should the Accounting Officer submit a report on the circumstances of the 
loss?
options:
The Head of Civil Service.
E3
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 630 states: "Where the Accounting Officer is satisfied that no 
loss or fraud has taken place he will submit a report On the circumstances of the loss of the 
voucher to the Accountant -General..."
Financial Regulations: Bank Accounts and Cheques

items.question: Who is authorized to open an official bank account?
options:
The Director of Finance and Accounts.
□
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 701 (i) states: "No official bank account shall be opened 
unless authorised by the Accountant-General."

items.question: How many bank accounts is each establishment generally allowed to operate?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 701 (ii) states: "Each establishment shall be allowed to 
operate only three (3) bank accounts as follows: (a) Salary Account; (b) Overhead Cost 
Account; and (c) Revenue Account."

items.question:
Which of the following is NOT one of the three generally allowed bank accounts for an 
establishment?
209
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 701 (ii) lists Salary Account, Overhead Cost Account, and 
Revenue Account as the three allowed accounts.

items.question: What is required for any bank account beyond the three specified types?
options: Approval from the Minister of Finance.
A resolution from the National Assembly.Q
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 701 (ii) states: "No other bank account shall be allowed 
without the express approval of the Accountant-General."

items.question:
How often should every organization submit a return of their bank accounts to the 
Accou nta nt-G e n e ra I ?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 701 (v) states: "Every organisation shall submit a return of 
their bank accounts half yearly to the Accountant-General..."

items.question: Where should all Capital Accounts be maintained?
options: The Central Bank of Nigeria or any other designated banks by the government.
A foreign bank for international transactions.

At the discretion of the Accounting Officer.
□
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 702 states: "All Capital Accounts shall be maintained at the 
Central Bank of Nigeria or any other Banks as may be designated by the government of the 
federation and authorised by the Accountant-General."

items.question: What must be stamped on every cheque leaf?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 703(i) states: "Every cheque leaf shall be crossed and 
stamped with the correct account number."

items.question: Is the use of abbreviations allowed when writing the payee's name on a cheque?
options: No, the name must be written in full.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 703(iii) states: "The name of the payee shall be written in full 
and the use of abbreviation shall not be allowed."

items.question:
Who is responsible for furnishing certified specimen signatures of officers authorized to sign 
cheques drawn on official accounts?
options: The Head of Finance and Accounts.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 705(i) states: "All Accounting Officers shall furnish the details 
of commercial banks being used with certified specimen signatures of the officers authorised to 
sign cheques drawn on official accounts..."

items.question:
What is the rule regarding delegation of authority to notify the bank of changes in authorized 
signatories?
options: It can be delegated to a senior officer.
Delegation of this authority shall not be allowed.
It can be delegated with the Minister's approval.
It is at the discretion of the Accounting Officer.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 705(i) states: "Delegation of this authority shall not be 
allowed."

items.question: What is the minimum rank for signatories to an official bank account?
options: Accountant I or designated officer of equivalent rank.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 707 states: "No signatory shall be below the Rank of 
Accountant I or designated officer of equivalent rank."

items.question:
Under what circumstances should payment be made for services not yet performed or 
goods not yet supplied?
options: In urgent cases with special approval.
If a mobilization fee is required.
Only if a contract is in place.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 708 states: "On no account should payment be made for 
services not yet performed or for goods not yet supplied."

items.question:
Who has the right of access to records of all bank accounts of all ministries, extra-ministerial 
offices, and other arms of government?
options: The Accountant-General and the Auditor-General or their representatives.

The Head of Finance and Accounts.
□
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 709 states: "The Accountant-General and the 
Auditor-General or their representatives shall have the right of access to records of all bank 
accounts of all ministries, extra ministerial offices and other arms of government..."

items.question: What happens if a government bank account is overdrawn?
options: The bank will automatically cover the overdraft.
The officer responsible shall be made to refund any bank charges incurred thereon.
A report is sent to the Minister of Finance.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 710 states: "In the event of an account being overdrawn, the 
officer responsible shall be made to refund any bank charges incurred thereon."

items.question: Can idle funds in the accounts of missions abroad be invested in short-term deposits?
options: Yes, but only with the prior approval of the Accountant-General.
Yes, at the discretion of the Head of Mission.

Only if the amount is substantial.
□
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 711(i) states: "Idle funds in the accounts of missions abroad 
may be invested in short term deposits but with the prior approval of the Accountant- General."
212

items.question:
How should accrued interests from invested idle funds in missions abroad be classified and 
remitted?
options: To the personal account of the Head of Mission.
To the appropriate revenue head and sub-head and remitted to the Treasury.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 711(ii) states: "Where such idle funds are invested, the 
accrued interests received 'thereof, shall be classified to the appropriate revenue head and sub 
head and remitted to the Treasury."

items.question: What is the implication if an officer pays public money into a private bank account?
options: It is considered a minor irregularity.
The officer is deemed to have done so with fraudulent intention.

The money will be transferred back to the government account.
□
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 713 states: "An officer who pays public money into a private 
account is deemed to have done so with fraudulent intention."

items.question:
Where a bank account is kept, what type of payments should generally be made by 
cheques?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 714 states: "Where a Bank account is kept, only sufficient 
cash to meet small payments shall be allowed while all payments should be made by cheques."

items.question:
How often must officers authorized to keep bank accounts compare entries in bank 
statements with their cash books?
options: Weekly and at the close of each month.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 716 states: "All officers authorised to keep bank accounts 
must compare on- weekly basis, and at the close of each month, the entries appearing in the 
bank statements with those in their cash books..."
213

items.question: What additional register must Sub-Accounting Officers maintain besides a cash book?
options:
A register of bank statements.
a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 717 states: "Sub-Accounting Officers shall maintain a 
cheque summary register in addition to a cash book."

items.question: Who should sign every request for official cheque books?
options: One of the authorized signatories to the account and countersigned by the Head of Accounts.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 718 states: "Every request for official cheques books shall 
be signed by one of the authorised signatories to the account and countersigned by the Head of 
Accounts."

items.question: When a cheque is received, how should it be made payable?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 719(i) states: "A cheque received should be made payable 
to "Federal Government of Nigeria."

items.question:
What is the maximum amount for a cheque that must be properly confirmed with designated 
banks before acceptance?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 719(i)(h) states: "All cheques above N20, 000 must be 
properly confirmed with the designated banks before acceptance."

items.question: How soon after receipt should cheques received be paid to the bank account?
options:
Not later than the next business day.
214
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 720(i) states: "Cheques received shall be paid to the bank 
account not later than the next business day after receipt."

items.question:
Can cheques received by a Sub-Accounting Officer be endorsed or assigned to a third 
party?
options: Yes, if the third party is another government entity.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 720(ii) states: "On no account may cheques received by a 
Sub-Accounting Officer be endorsed or assigned to a third party."

items.question:
What is the processing period for cash transfers in respect of AIEs issued by 
ministries/extra-ministerial offices in favor of state offices, in view of the electronic banking 
system?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 721(i)(c) states: "the processing period ofcash transfers in 
respect of AIE's issued by ministries/extra ministerial offices and other arms of government in 
favour of state offices shall not exceed Seven (7) working days."

items.question: Who is ultimately responsible for ensuring the recovery of dishonored cheques?
options: The officer who accepted the cheque.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 730(i) states: "It shall be the ultimate responsibility of the 
Accounting Officer concerned to ensure that these amounts are recovered, instituting legal 
action if necessary."

items.question:
What is the consequence if an officer negligently disregards the provisions of Regulation 
719 when accepting a cheque that is subsequently dishonored?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 731 states: "An officer accepting a cheque which is 
subsequently dishonoured shall be surcharged for the full amount, if the provisions Of 
Regulation 719 were negligently disregarded."

items.question: Can a dishonored cheque be held as cash?
options: Yes, with the Accounting Officer's approval.
Only if the amount is small.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 732(ii) states: "Under no circumstances may a dishonoured 
cheque be held as cash."

items.question: What is the primary purpose of maintaining a departmental dishonored cheques register?
