options: To ensure clearances are pursued and recoveries affected.
To record all incoming and outgoing cheques.
To provide a summary of daily bank transactions a .
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 733(ii) states: "The register shall be checked monthly by the 
Head of accounts to ensure that clearances are pursued and recoveries effected."
Part 2: Financial Regulations: Cash Book and Monthly Accounts

items.question: Who is required to keep a Treasury Cash Book No. 153A or an approved cash book?
options: The Head of Finance and Accounts.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 801 states: "A Sub-Accounting Officer as defined by FR 115 
is required to keep a Treasury Cash Book No.153A (see Appendix 9) or a cash book in such 
form as may be approved by the Accountant-General, in consultation with the Auditor-General."

items.question: How should a Sub-Accounting Officer enter sums of money into the cash book?
options:
Promptly, in strict serial number order.
216

Monthly, by type of transaction.
Q .
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 802 states: "A Sub-Accounting Officer shall enter promptly 
into the cash book all sums of money received by him or paid by him as a public officer, 
supporting such entries by a receipt or payment voucher on the prescribed form. Receipt and 
payment vouchers shall be numbered consecutively, from NO.1 each month in the order in 
which the amounts are received or paid, and will be entered into the cash book accordingly."

items.question: How are receipt and payment vouchers numbered for entry into the cash book?
options: According to the type of service.a .
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 802 states: "Receipt and payment vouchers shall be 
numbered consecutively, from NO.1 each month in the order in which the amounts are received 
or paid, and will be entered into the cash book accordingly."

items.question: What do the terms "Revenue" and "Expenditure" refer to in the context of Chapter 8?
options: Receipts and payments under Heads in the recurrent and capital Estimates.
All financial transactions of the government.
Only income and outgoings from special funds.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 803 states: "The terms "Revenue" and "Expenditure" refer to 
receipts and payments, respectively under Heads appearing in the recurrent and capital 
Estimates..."

items.question: What does "Below-the-line" refer to in Chapter 8?
options: Receipts and payments for services specified in the approved Annual Budget estimates.
Receipts and payments for services not specified in the approved Annual Budget estimates.
Funds that are not subject to audit.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 803 states: "...while Below-the-line refers to receipts and 
payments for services that are not specified in the approved Annual Budget estimates."

items.question: How often should the cash book be ruled off and balanced?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 804 states: "The cash book shall be ruled off and balanced 
daily by the cashier who will enter the cash specifications of the balance in hand in the cash 
book."

items.question: What should be done with cheques held as cash at the close of business?
options: Paid to the bank or cleared on the next business day.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 804 states: "Cheques held as cash at the close of business 
will be paid to the bank or otherwise cleared on the next business day."

items.question: Can a cheque that has been presented to the bank and returned unpaid be held as cash?
options: Yes, with the Accounting Officer's approval.
Only if the amount is small.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 804 states: "In no circumstances may a cheque which has 
been presented to the bank and returned unpaid be held as cash."

items.question:
Who signs the cash book at the end of each month to certify the correctness of entries and 
cash balance?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 805 states: "The cash book shall be signed at the end of 
each month by the Sub Accounting Officer whose signature will be taken as certifying the 
correctness of the entries and cash balance."

items.question: What documents must be attached to the cash book where a bank account is maintained?
options:
A bank reconciliation statement and certified bank statement.
218

A copy of all cheques issued.
Q .
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 806 states: "Where a bank account is maintained, a bank 
reconciliation statement and certified bank statement as at the last business day of the month 
shall be attached to the cash book."

items.question: How should unpresented cheques that become stale after 6 months be handled?
options: Written back in the Cash Book after written confirmation of non-payment from the bank.
Reported as a loss of funds.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 807(a) states: "Unpresented cheques which become stale 
after 6 months should be written back in the Cash Book after written confirmation of 
non-payment from the bank."

items.question: How often should Sub-Accounting Officers submit their accounts?
options: By the seventh day of the following month.
By the fourteenth day of the following month.
By the end of the following month.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 808(i) states: "Sub-Accounting Officers shall submit their 
accounts by the fourteenth day of the following month or within such other period as may be 
prescribed by the Accountant-General."

items.question: What is the Monthly Consolidated Account also known as?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 809 states: "The Monthly Consolidated Account otherwise 
known as "Transcripts" will consist of..."

items.question:
What should be forwarded if all vouchers are not available to accompany the monthly 
account?
options:
A list of outstanding vouchers (Treasury Form 34) and voucher substitute (Treasury Form 
72).
A summary of the missing vouchers.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 810 states: "In the event of all vouchers not being available 
to accompany the account, a list of outstanding vouchers (Treasury Form 34) shall be forwarded 
and voucher substitute (Treasury Form 72) in (duplicate) inserted."

items.question: What information should a voucher substitute contain?
options:
Month and year of account, payment voucher number, classification, amount paid, and 
sufficient details to permit identification of the transaction.

The reason for the missing voucher.
□ .
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 810 states: "Voucher substitutes will show month and year of 
account and will also contain payment voucher number, classification, amount paid and 
sufficient details to permit identification of the transaction."

items.question: What register must a Sub-Accounting Officer maintain for outstanding vouchers?
options: A register of outstanding vouchers (Treasury Form 73).
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 811(i) states: "Sub-Accounting Officer shall maintain a 
register of outstanding vouchers (Treasury Form 73) which shall be marked off 11S they are 
received."

items.question:
Who is personally responsible for obtaining acquittal for payments out of public funds until 
the relevant payment vouchers are obtained?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 811(ii) states: "It is the personal responsibility of the 
Sub-Accounting Officer to obtain acquittal for the payments out of public funds and this 
responsibility exists until he has obtained the relevant payment vouchers (See Regulation 621)."
220

items.question:
Who brings to account all transactions conducted by Sub-Accounting Officers in his main 
and subsidiary ledgers for a self-accounting unit?
options: The Director of Finance and Accounts.
The Head of Accounts of a self-accounting unit.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 812 states: "The Head of Accounts of a self accounting unit 
will bring to account in his main and subsidiary ledgers all transactions conducted by his 
Sub-Accounting Officers and shall submit the consolidated account of the unit to the Accountant 
General as in Financial Regulation 808."

items.question:
What is the purpose of reconciling outstanding items in the Bank Reconciliation Statement 
expeditiously?
options: To ensure the effectiveness of the statement.

To identify new revenue sources.
Q .
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 807(c) states: "Prompt action should always be taken to 
obtain bank advice to confirm the various entries in the bank statement as the effectiveness of 
the Bank Reconciliation Statement lies in the prompt clearance of outstanding items."

items.question:
What kind of payments are Revenue and Expenditure vouchers submitted in duplicate to 
Sub-Accounting Officers?
options: All types of payments unless otherwise directed by the Accountant-General.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 803 states: "Revenue Expenditure, and Below-the-line 
vouchers will be submitted to Sub-Accounting Officers in duplicate, except as May be otherwise 
directed from time to time by the Accountant- General."
221
Section D
Part 1: Financial Regulations: Adjustments

items.question:
Question: What is the primary purpose of adjustments in financial accounting, as described in 
Chapter 9?
options: To increase or decrease cash on hand.
To make transfers between accounts without actual cash receipt or payment.
To record daily petty cash transactions.
To prepare annual financial statements for audit.3 .
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 901 states: "Adjustments are effected by means of Journal 
entries which enable transfers to be made from one account to another without actual receipt or 
payment of cash."

items.question:
Which of the following is an example of a transaction that would typically be handled through 
an adjustment?
options: Payment for a new office supply order.
Correction of accounting errors arising from misclassifications.
Receipt of monthly revenue from a government agency.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 901 lists "correction of accounting errors arising from 
misclassifications" as an example of an adjustment.

items.question: Which Treasury Form (TF) is primarily used for inter-ministerial transactions?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 903(i) states: "The Adjustment Voucher (TF 23) which is 
designed to adjust inter-ministerial transactions is mostly used in - ministries, extra-ministerial 
offices and other arms of government."

items.question: How many copies of the Adjustment Voucher (TF 23) are typically submitted?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 903(ii) states: "Adjustments shall be submitted on Treasury 
Form 23 in quadruplicate, or as otherwise directed."

items.question:
In the Office of the Accountant-General of the Federation (OAGF), what type of journal 
voucher is used for adjustments and transfers made before the "Below-the-line Statement" is 
extracted?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 904(i) states: "...the SN and PJV are used in the Office of the 
Accountant General of the Federation (OAGF) for the Preparation of Consolidated accounts, 
While SNs are used for all adjustments and transfers that are made before the "Below the-line 
Statement" is extracted..."

items.question: For what primary reason are Principal Journal Vouchers (PJVs) prepared in the OAGF?
options: To adjust differences between ministry transcripts and bank statements.
To correct misclassification of accounts detected by the main accounts.
To re-classify accounts before the Trial Balance is prepared.
To record daily cash transactions, a .
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 904(ii)(a) states that PJVs are prepared "to correct 
misclassification of accounts detected by the main accounts."

items.question: What information must an adjustment voucher clearly indicate?
options: Only the amount of the adjustment.
The reason for the adjustment and full reference to the original debit or credit.
The name of the officer who prepared the voucher.
The date of the next audit.a .
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 905 states: "Adjustment vouchers must clearly indicate the 
reason for the adjustment, and must make full reference to the original debit or credit being 
adjusted..."

items.question:
When adjustments affect expenditure sub-heads in a current financial year, what must be 
done with the amounts (debit or credit)?
options: They are ignored until the next financial year.
They must be entered in the vote book and the relevant vouchers stamped.
They are only recorded in the main ledger.

They are sent directly to the Auditor-General.
223
Q .
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 906 states: "Where adjustments affect expenditure 
sub-heads in a current financial year, the amounts whether debit or credit must be entered ill the 
vote book and tie relevant vouchers stamped accordingly before the officer controlling the vote 
accepts."

items.question:
Adjustments in which Unallocated Stores and Workshops Accounts are credited must be 
embodied in which document?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 907 states: "Adjustments in which Unallocated Stores and 
Workshops Accounts are credited must be embodied in the relevant Material Expense 
Statement (M.E~S.)."

items.question:
If an adjustment voucher supports an adjustment between votes that is not to be included in 
the Material Expense Statement (M.E.S.), whose signature must it bear?
options: The engineer or officer actually in charge of the vote.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 907 states: "Vouchers supporting adjustments between 
votes which are not to be included in the M.E.S. must bear the signature of the engineer or 
officer actually in charge of the vote."

items.question: Where should vouchers in respect of adjustments arising from reconciliations be submitted?
options: Wherever possible, they should accompany the Reconciliation Statements.

They should be filed separately and not submitted.
□
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 908 states: "Vouchers in respect of adjustments arising from 
reconciliations should, wherever possible, accompany the Reconciliation Statements."

items.question: What is the primary characteristic that distinguishes an adjustment from a cash transaction?
options: The use of a specific Treasury Form.

The absence of actual cash receipt or payment.
224
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 901 clearly defines adjustments as transfers "without actual 
receipt or payment of cash."

items.question:
What types of transfers are effected by Principal Journal Vouchers (PJVs) at the end of the 
month?
options: Transfers related to revenue collection only.a .
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 904(ii)(c) states that PJVs are prepared to "effect month-end 
transfer of accounts from Above-the-line Accounts to Below-the-line Accounts."

items.question: What is the purpose of Supplementary Journal Vouchers (SJVs) in the OAGF?
options:
To adjust differences between ministry transcripts and bank transfer statements, and to 
re-classify accounts before the Trial Balance.
To correct misclassifications and effect annual transfers.
To record all incoming and outgoing cash.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 904(iii) states SJVs are prepared "to adjust differences 
between ministry transcript's figures and those contained in the Bank Transfer Statements 
prepared by the Sub- Treasurer of the Federation; and to re-classify accounts before the Trial 
Balance is prepared."

items.question:
When an adjustment voucher is accepted by a ministry, extra-ministerial office, or other arm 
of government, what is returned as evidence of acceptance or rejection?
options: A copy of the original memo.
One of the fully signed adjustment vouchers.

items.question:
What is the definition of "Imprest" according to Financial Regulation 1001?
225
options: Any sum of money received by a public officer for revenue collection.

Sums advanced to a public officer to meet expenditure for which vouchers cannot 
immediately be presented for payment.

Money held in a reserve cash safe.
3
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1001 defines Imprest as "all sums advanced to a public 
officer to meet expenditure under current estimates, for which vouchers cannot immediately be 
presented to a Sub-Accounting Officer for payment."

items.question: Who is authorized to issue Imprests?
options:
The Accountant-General of the Federation and Accounting Officers of Self-Accounting 
ministries/extra-ministerialoffices.
The Head of Civil Service of the Federation.3 .
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1002 states that "Imprests are issued by the 
Accountant-General of the Federation and the Accounting Officers of Self Accounting 
ministries/extra-ministerial offices and other arms of government."

items.question: What document conveys the authority for issuing Imprests?
options: The General Imprest Warrant issued by the Minister of Finance to the Accountant-General.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1002 specifies that "The authority for issuing Imprests is 
conveyed in the Annual General Imprest Warrant issued by the Minister of Finance to the 
Accountant-General (see Appendix 5)."

items.question: When is the General Imprest Warrant issued by the Minister of Finance?
options: At the beginning of each calendar year.
Immediately after the enactment of the Annual Appropriation Act.
Only when a special imprest is required.3 .
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1003(i) states: "The General Imprest Warrant shall be issued 
by the Minister of Finance immediately after the enactment of the Annual Appropriation Act."
226

items.question: What are the two main types of Imprests?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1004(i) identifies the two types as "(a) Standing Imprests" 
and "(b) Special imprests."

items.question: Which type of imprest can be replenished from time to time during a financial year?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1004(i)(a) describes Standing Imprests as those "which may 
be replenished from time to time during a financial year by the submission of paid vouchers to 
Sub-Accounting Officers for reimbursement."

items.question: When must a Special Imprest be retired in full?
options: At the end of the financial year.
Immediately the purpose for which it was granted has been achieved.

When the imprest holder is transferred.
□ .
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1004(i)(b) states that Special Imprests "must be retired in full 
when the purpose has been achieved."

items.question:
What is explicitly prohibited regarding the use of personal advances in disguise of Special 
Imprest?
options: Using them in the procurement of stores.
Using them for minor office repairs.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1004(ii) explicitly states: "On no account shall personal 
advance be used in disguise of Special Imprest, in the procurement of stores which is governed 
by the provision of chapter 23 of these Regulations."

items.question:
To whom should applications for imprests be made?
227
options:
The Accountant-General or, in the case of a self-accounting agency, to the Accounting 
Officer.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1005 directs that "Applications for imprests should be made 
to the Accountant-General or in the case of a self-accounting agency, to the Accounting 
Officer..."

items.question: Where should all imprests issued and their retirement be recorded?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1006 specifies: "All imprests issued and their retirement shall 
be recorded in a special imprest ledger."

items.question: How should issues of imprest be classified in the General Ledger Account?
options: To "Expenditure" in the name of the Imprest Holder.
To "Revenue" in the name of the Imprest Holder.
To "Imprests" in the name and designation of the Imprest Holder.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1006 states: "Issues shall be classified to "Imprests" General 
Ledger Account in the name and designation of the Imprest Holder."

items.question: To obtain reimbursement for amounts paid from an imprest, what must the holder submit?
options:
Properly completed and receipted payment vouchers for the amount expended.
228

A memo to the Accountant-General.
Q .
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1010(i) states: "To obtain reimbursement of amounts paid 
from an imprest, the holder shall submit the properly completed and receipted payment 
vouchers for the amount expended to the Sub-Accounting Officer who issued the imprest."

items.question: How often should replenishment of amounts paid from an imprest be effected?
options: Only at the end of the financial year.

As needed, without a fixed schedule.
□ .
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1010(i) specifies: "Replenishment of amount paid from 
imprest will be effected at least once a month."

items.question: When must all standing imprests be retired?
options: On or before 31st December of the financial year in which they are issued.
Within one month of the issue date.
When the imprest holder goes on leave.

Only upon the explicit instruction of the Accountant-General.
□ .
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1011(i) states: "All standing imprests must be retired on or 
before the 31st December of the financial year in which they are issued..."

items.question:
If an imprest is retired at a station other than where the warrant was drawn, who must the 
imprest holder immediately notify?
options: His ministry or department and the Sub-Accounting Officer who issued the imprest.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1011(ii) states that the officer "must immediately notify his 
ministry or department and the Sub-Accounting Officer who issued the imprest..."

items.question:
What is the responsibility of the Sub-Accounting Officer who issued the imprest when it is 
retired at another station?
options: To inform the Minister of Finance.
To verify the receipt voucher particulars.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1011(ii) clarifies: "It is the responsibility of the 
Sub-Accounting Officer who issued the imprest to verify the receipt voucher particulars."

items.question: What is required to support all imprest payments?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1012(b) states: "All imprest payments shall be supported by 
Sub Receipts (Treasury Form 10)."

items.question: How often should the cash in the imprest holder's cash book be checked by a senior officer?
options: Regularly, with frequency determined by the Accounting Officer.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1012(e) states: "The cash book shall be balanced regularly, 
ruled off, and the cash on hand regularly checked by a senior officer, who will certify the cash 
book accordingly. Accounting Officers shall issue instructions concerning the frequency of the 
check..."

items.question: What is a key responsibility of imprest holders regarding the use of funds?
options: They can use other public funds for imprest purposes if needed.
They are responsible for the correctness of vouchers signed by them.
They can delegate their responsibilities without supervision.
They are only responsible for cash, not vouchers.a .
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1008 states: "Imprest holders must duly observe all 
regulations regarding the control of expenditure and the disbursement of public money and are 
responsible for the correctness of vouchers signed by them (see Regulation 410). They are also 
responsible for ensuring the early submission of paid vouchers for reimbursement. The imprest 
may be used only for the purpose for which it is issued and Imprest holders shall not use other 
public funds for imprest purposes."

items.question:
When an imprest is granted, what must the imprest holder do regarding a bank account, 
unless otherwise authorized?
options: Open a bank account in his official designation.

Use the ministry's main bank account.
Q
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1009 states: "Where an imprest is granted, the imprest 
holder must open a bank account In his official designation (see Chapter 7), unless authority is 
given to the contrary by the Accountant-General."

items.question: Who has the authority to inspect the accounts of Imprest Holders?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1013 states: "The accounts of Imprest Holders are subject to 
inspection by the Accountant-General and the Auditor-General."

items.question:
What is the maximum reimbursable limit for a Standing Imprest held by a Permanent 
Secretary or Director-General?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1014(i) specifies: "Permanent Secretary and 
Director-General.. N 200,00."

items.question:
What is the maximum reimbursable limit for a Standing Imprest held by a Head of Formation 
in each state and any other Imprest Holder?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1014(i) specifies: "Head of Formation in each state and any 
Other Imprest Holder.. N 60,000."

items.question: What is the maximum frequency for obtaining reimbursement of any standing imprest?
options: Not more than twice in the quarter, where the need arises.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1014(ii) states: "The frequency of obtaining reimbursement 
of any standing imprest shall be limited to once in a quarter, but where the need arises, should 
not be more than twice in the quarter."
Part 2: Financial Regulations: Custody of Public Moneys, Stamps, Security Books, and 
Documents

items.question: What is the primary instruction regarding the use of banking facilities for public funds?
options: All cash must be kept in the office safe.

The fullest possible use shall be made of banking facilities, and all cash in excess of daily 
requirements shall be paid into the bank account immediately.
Banking facilities are only for large transactions.

Cash should be held for at least a week before banking.
Hl .
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1101 states, "The fullest possible use shall be made of 
banking facilities and all cash in excess of daily requirements shall be paid into the bank 
account immediately."

items.question:
For the custody of cash, stamps, or receipt and licence books beyond immediate 
requirements, what security measure is required for strong-rooms or reserve cash safes?
options: Not less than two locks, with keys held by different officers.

No locks are necessary if the room is secure.
□ .
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1102 mandates that strong-rooms or reserve cash safes 
should be "secured if possible by not less than two locks, the key to one being held by the 
cashier and the key to the other by a more senior official than the cashier."

items.question:
What type of safe is required for the safe custody of the daily working balance of cash, 
stamps, and receipt/licence books during office hours?
options: A safe that is built into the wall of the building or securely attached to its structure.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1103 specifies that such safes "should be built in to the wall 
of the building or otherwise securely attached to the structure of the building."
232

items.question:
When cash has to be transported from one place to another (e.g., for salary payments), 
what must the responsible officer be provided with?
options: An approved cash tank or box, government vehicle, and police escort.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1104 states that the officer "must be provided with an 
approved cash tank or box, government vehicle and police escort."

items.question: What periodic examination is required for strong-rooms?
options: To ensure no vents or openings exist through which contents could be extracted.
To count the number of items stored.a .
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1105 requires strong-rooms to be "examined periodically to 
ensure that no vents or openings exist through which any of the contents could be extracted 
either by hand or by other means."

items.question:
Who is responsible for ensuring that departmental officers who hold public money, stamps, 
etc., are provided with proper safe custody facilities?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1106(i) states, "It is the duty of Accounting Officers to ensure 
that their departmental officers who are required to hold public money, stamps, etc., are 
provided with proper safe custody facilities."

items.question:
Before procuring safes, strong-rooms, cash tanks, and boxes, whose specification must first 
be obtained?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1106(ii) requires that "their specification must first be 
obtained from the Accountant-General before procurement."
233

items.question:
Who is responsible for keeping all duplicate keys to strong-rooms, safes, or departmental 
cash tanks and boxes?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1107(i) states, "All duplicate keys shall be kept by the 
Accounting Officer."

items.question: If a safe is contained within a strong-room, what is the rule regarding key-holding?
options: One officer can hold all keys.
One officer should not hold the keys of both strong-room and safe.
The cashier must hold both keys.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1108 stipulates, "Where a safe is contained in a 
strong-room, one officer should not hold the keys of both strong-room and safe."

items.question: What action must be taken if duplicate keys of safes are not available or untraceable?
options:
Report to the Accounting Officer and request that the locks be altered and new keys 
provided.

Replace the safe immediately without reporting.
□ .
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1109 requires reporting to the Accounting Officer and 
requesting "that the locks be altered and new keys provided."

items.question:
Who is personally responsible for the safe custody of original keys of strong-rooms, safes, 
cash tanks, or cash boxes, and collectively responsible for their contents?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1110 states, "Officers holding the keys of strong-rooms, 
safes, cash tanks or cash boxes are personally responsible for their safe custody and 
collectively responsible for the contents."
234

items.question:
What immediate action is required if keys to any strong-room, safe, cash tank, or box are 
lost?
options:
Report immediately to the Accounting Officer, inform other key holders, seal the safe, and 
place a police guard if cash is inside.
Only report if a large sum of money is involved.s .
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1111 outlines a detailed procedure including immediate 
reporting, informing other key holders, sealing the safe, and placing a police guard if cash is 
present.

items.question:
Who is liable to meet the cost of altering the lock and providing new keys if a key to a 
Government safe is lost due to negligence?
options: The officer responsible for the loss.

The supplier of the safe.
Q .
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1112 states, "The officer responsible for the loss of a key to a 
Government safe, is liable to be called upon to meet the cost of altering the lock and the 
provision of new keys, if found negligent."

items.question:
If a key holder suspects interference with the keys or locks of a strong-room or safe, what 
must they do?
options: Ignore it unless a loss occurs.

Remove the contents to another safe, report the circumstances, and request immediate steps 
to alter the locks and provide new keys.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1113 directs the key holder to "remove the contents to 
another safe, report the circumstances as directed in Regulation 1111, and ask that immediate 
steps be taken to have the locks altered and new keys provided."

items.question:
Question: Who must approve alterations to locks of safes and the provision of new keys, 
and under what conditions?
options: The Head of Department, under normal conditions.
The Accounting Officer, under security conditions.

The Minister of Finance, without any specific conditions.
235
Q .
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1114 states, "Alterations to locks of safes and the provision 
of new keys must be made by the Accounting Officer under security conditions."

items.question:
How often should annual returns of all Government strong-rooms and safes be rendered, 
and to whom?
options: Quarterly, to the Minister of Finance.
Annually on March 31st, to the Accountant-General.
Bi-annually, to the Head of Civil Service.a .
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1115 requires "Annual returns in duplicate, of all Government 
strong-rooms and safes will be rendered on the 31st March by all ministries and extra-ministerial 
offices and other arms of government to the Accountant-General."

items.question: What action is required when safes are transferred between stations or departments?
options:
Notification must be sent at once to the Accountant-General, and duplicate key holders must 
be informed.
Only the receiving department needs to be informed.
Only the Accountant-General needs to be informed, not key holders.a .
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1116 states, "Any transfers of safes between stations, or 
from one department to another, must be notified at once to the Accountant-General. The 
duplicate, key holders must likewise be informed..."

items.question:
When locks to a strong-room or safe are changed, or a safe is condemned and destroyed, 
what must duplicate key holders be informed to do?
options: Keep the old keys as souvenirs.
Destroy the old keys and write them off their record.
Return the old keys to the Accountant-General.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1117 requires duplicate key holders to be "informed 
accordingly so that the old keys may be destroyed and written off his record."

items.question: Who is permitted to open a strong-room or safe?
options: Only the authorised key holders, who must remain present while it is open.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1118 states, "No strong-room or safe may be opened by a 
person other than the authorised key holders, who must remain present while the strong-room 
or safe is open."

items.question: What is prohibited regarding private money or personal effects in an official safe?
options: They can be kept if space allows.

Officers are prohibited from keeping private money or personal effects in an official safe, and 
any found will be credited to revenue.
They can be kept with permission.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1119 explicitly states, "Officers are prohibited front keeping 
private money or personal effects in an official safe, etc. Any private money found in an official 
safe shall be credited to revenue."

items.question:
Who is personally responsible for ensuring that the balance of cash with the cashier is 
locked in a strong-room or safe at the close of business daily?
items.explanation: Financial Regulation 1120 assigns this responsibility to "Sub-Accounting Officers."

items.question:
What must be kept in each strong-room or reserve cash safe for all cash, stamps, 
receipt/licence books, and other articles deposited?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1121 requires that "Registers shall be kept in each 
strong-room or reserve cash safe of all cash, stamps, receipt or licence books and other articles 
deposited."

items.question: What is the rule regarding the monthly check of contents in strong-rooms or safes?
options:
It should be done monthly by the officer in charge of the keys, who must initial and date the 
register.
It is only required if a discrepancy is suspected.3 .
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1122 states, "The contents of strong-rooms or safes shall be 
checked monthly by the officer in charge of the keys, who will initial and date the register."

items.question:
What is required for the safe custody of all bonds, agreements, and other valuable 
documents?
options: They can be kept in a regular office drawer.
They must be kept in a strong-room or safe, with a register maintained.
They only need to be scanned and stored digitally.3 .
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1123 mandates that "All bonds, agreements and other 
valuable documents shall be kept in a strong-room or safe, and a register shall be maintained 
for all such documents deposited."

items.question:
When an officer is transferred, what must happen with the keys and contents of safes or 
strong-rooms under their charge?
options: The keys can be left with a junior officer.

The keys and contents must be formally handed over to the relieving officer, and a certificate 
of handing-over obtained.
The keys can be mailed to the new officer.
The contents can be disposed of.3 .
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1124 requires that "When an officer is transferred, the keys 
of any safe or strong-room under his charge, and the contents thereof, must be formally handed 
over to the relieving officer, and a certificate of handing-over obtained."

items.question: What is the responsibility of the relieving officer during a handover of keys and contents?
options: To verify the contents and sign the certificate of handing-over.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1125 states, "The relieving officer must satisfy himself that 
the contents are correct, and will sign the certificate of handing-over."
238

items.question: What is required for the destruction of condemned safes?
options: Destruction in the presence of a Board of Survey, with a certificate issued.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1126 states, "Condemned safes shall be destroyed in the 
presence of a Board of Survey, and a certificate of destruction shall be issued."

items.question: What is the rule regarding the custody of main stocks of postage stamps?
options: They must be kept in the strong-room or reserve cash safe, and a register maintained.
They are not considered security items.a .
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1127 states, "Main stocks of postage stamps shall be kept in 
the strong-room or reserve cash safe, and a register shall be maintained for all such stamps 
deposited."

items.question:
How should registered and unregistered mail containing public funds or security documents 
be delivered?
options: By locked bag, with a register maintained.
By hand, without any special precautions.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1128 states, "Registered and unregistered mail containing 
public funds or security documents shall be delivered by locked bag, and a register shall be 
maintained for all such mail."

items.question: What is the responsibility of Sub-Accounting Officers regarding receipt and licence books?
options: To ensure their safe custody and proper use.
To dispose of them after use.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1130 states, "Sub-Accounting Officers shall be responsible 
for the safe custody and proper use of all receipt and licence books issued to them."
239
Financial Regulations: Receipt and Licence Books

items.question:
Who is responsible for the general control of all Treasury and other receipt and licence 
books?
options:
The Head of Civil Service.
Q .
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1201 states that the Accountant-General is responsible for 
the general control of all Treasury and other receipt and licence books.

items.question: Who is responsible for the printing of all Treasury and other receipt and licence books?
options:
The Central Bank of Nigeria.
q .
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1202 specifies that the Federal Government Printer is 
responsible for printing these books.

items.question: What information must be printed on all receipt and licence books?
options: Only the title of the book.
The title of the book, the serial number of each receipt/licence, and the number of the book.

The name of the issuing officer.
□ .
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1203 requires that the title of the book, the serial number of 
each receipt/licence, and the number of the book must be printed on all receipt and licence 
books.

items.question:
What is the procedure for dealing with any defects found in receipt or licence books upon 
receipt from the Federal Government Printer?
options: The books should be used as is.
The books should be immediately returned to the printer.

The defects must be reported immediately to the Accountant-General and the 
Auditor-General.

The defects should be noted for future reference.
□ .
240
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1204 mandates immediate reporting of defects to the 
Accountant-General and Auditor-General.

items.question: To whom should requests for Treasury Receipts Books be submitted?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1205 states that requests for Treasury Receipts Books 
should be submitted to the Accountant-General.

items.question: What must accompany every issue of a receipt book or books?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1206(i) specifies that every issue must be accompanied by a 
Receipt Book Issue Note.

items.question: How many copies of the Receipt Book Issue Note are printed, and what is their distribution?
options: Two copies; original to officer, duplicate retained.
Three copies; original to officer, duplicate to Auditor-General, triplicate retained.

Four copies; original and duplicate to officer (duplicate receipted and returned), triplicate to 
Auditor-General, quadruplicate retained.

Five copies; all retained by the issuing officer.
Q .
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1206(i) details the four-copy distribution: original and 
duplicate to the officer (duplicate returned receipt), triplicate to the Auditor-General, and 
quadruplicate retained.

items.question:
Who is responsible for issuing Treasury Receipt Book 6A to all Revenue Collectors in their 
respective states?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1207 assigns this responsibility to Federal Pay Officers.
241

items.question: In what order shall receipt and licence books be issued for use?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1208 clearly states that they "shall be issued for use in strict 
serial order."

items.question:
What is prohibited for Federal Revenue Collectors stationed in a State regarding state 
receipts?
options: They can use them with permission.

They shall not under any circumstances request, stock, or issue state receipts for Federal 
Government earnings.
They can issue them for small amounts.

They can use them if federal receipts are unavailable.
Q .
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1209 explicitly prohibits Federal Revenue Collectors from 
using state receipts for federal earnings.

items.question:
What is the maximum number of receipt and licence forms that should be issued to 
Revenue Collectors?
options: The number required for their daily use.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1210 states that issues "shall not exceed the number 
required for their daily use."

items.question:
Who is responsible for checking every issue of receipt and licence books with the supporting 
Receipt Books Issue Note upon receipt?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1211 places this responsibility on the receiving officer.
242

items.question:
If defects or discrepancies are found in receipt or licence books upon receipt, to whom must 
they be reported immediately?
options: The supplying officer, with a copy to the Accountant-General and the Auditor-General.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1211 requires reporting to the supplying officer, with copies 
to the Accountant-General and Auditor-General.

items.question:
Who is held responsible for any errors discovered in receipt/licence books after the duplicate 
copy of the Receipt Book Issue Note has been receipted and returned?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1211 states that the "receiving officer shall be held 
responsible for any errors discovered after the duplicate copy of the Receipt Book Issue Note 
has been receipted and returned to the supplying officer."

items.question:
Where must all Treasury receipt books issued from main stocks for use be recorded by the 
receiving officer?
items.explanation: Financial Regulation 1212(i) mandates recording in the Receipt Book Register.

items.question:
Question: What security measures are required for receipt books at the close of business 
daily?
options: They can be left on the desk.
They must be locked in a safe or strong-room.
They should be taken home by the officer.
They can be left with a colleague.3 .
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1212(i) states that the receiving officer "will be responsible 
for ensuring that the books are locked in a safe or strong-room at the close of business."
243

items.question:
Question: If a cashier or officer temporarily leaves receipt books with another officer, who is 
held responsible for any misuse?
options: The cashier or officer who left the receipts.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1212(iii) clearly states, "Such cashiers or officers who leave 
the receipts so issued to them with another officer on a temporary basis shall be held 
responsible for any misuse."

items.question: Can receipt and licence books be transferred between Sub-Accounting Officers?
options: Yes, with the consent of the Accounting Officer, or in special emergency cases with reporting.
Only if the books are unused.3 .
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1213 allows transfers "with the consent of the Accounting 
Officer, except in special cases of emergency when the circumstances will be reported to the 
issuing officer."

items.question:
Can used or unused receipt or licence books be transferred between one Revenue Collector 
and another?
options:
Only if one Revenue Collector is officially relieved by another, or with the approval of the 
Acco u nta nt-G e n e ra I.
Only if the books are unused.a .
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1214 permits this "Except when one Revenue collector is 
officially relieved by another, no used or unused receipt or licence books shall be transferred 
between one Revenue Collector and another except with the approval of the Accountant 
General."

items.question: What action must be taken when an incorrect entry is made on a receipt or licence?
options: The incorrect entry should be crossed out and corrected.
The receipt or licence shall be cancelled and a new one completed.
The receipt or licence should be filed as is.

The issuing officer should be surcharged.
□ .
244
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1216 states, "Whenever an incorrect entry is made on a 
receipt or licence, the receipt or licence shall be cancelled and a new one completed."

items.question: What must be done with the cancelled receipt or licence, including the original?
options: It will be retained in the receipt or licence book.
It should be sent to the Accountant-General.

It should be given to the payer.
□ .
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1216 specifies that the "cancelled receipts or licence, 
including the original, will be retained in the receipt or licence book."

items.question: Who is responsible for delivering the original receipt or licence to the payer?
options: The officer who issued the receipt.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1217 states that the "original receipt or licence shall be 
delivered to the payer by the officer who issued the receipt."

items.question: What is the disposition of duplicate copies of receipts?
options: They are used as cash book vouchers and forwarded to the Accountant-General.
They are retained by the issuing officer.

They are given to the payer.
Q .
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1218 states that "duplicate copies of receipts shall be used 
as cash book vouchers and shall be forwarded to the Accountant-General with the monthly 
accounts."

items.question: What is the disposition of triplicate copies of receipts?
options: They are forwarded to the Auditor-General.
They are retained in the receipt book.

They are returned to the Federal Government Printer.
□ .
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1219 states that "triplicate copies of receipts shall be 
retained in the receipt book."
245

items.question:
For receipts and licences with fixed fees, what additional information should be printed on 
them?
options: The amount of the fixed fee.

The signature of the issuing officer.
□ .
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1220 requires that "the amount of the fixed fee should be 
printed on the receipt or licence."

items.question:
How often must monthly returns of all receipt and licence books issued, used, and unused 
be rendered, and to whom?
options: Annually, to the Minister of Finance.
Bi-annually, to the Head of Department.Q .
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1221 mandates "monthly returns of all receipt and licence 
books issued, used and unused... to the Accountant-General."

items.question: What is the procedure for the disposal of unused stock of receipt and licence books?
options: They must be returned to the Accountant-General for destruction by a Board of Survey.
They can be destroyed by the issuing officer.
They can be given to other departments.a .
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1223 requires returning them to the Accountant-General for 
destruction by a Board of Survey.

items.question: What action is required if a receipt or licence book is discovered to be missing?
options: No action is needed if it's an old book.

The circumstances must be reported immediately to the Accountant-General and the 
Auditor-General.
A new book should be ordered without reporting.

Only the Head of Department needs to be informed.
□ .
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1225 states, "The circumstances must be reported 
immediately to the Accountant-General and the Auditor-General."
246

items.question:
Question: What must be obtained when receipt and licence books are handed over from one 
officer to another?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1226 states, "When receipt and licence books are handed 
over from one officer to another, a handing-over certificate must be obtained."

items.question: What is the procedure for the destruction of used receipt and licence books?
options: They can be burned without supervision.

They must be destroyed by burning in the presence of a Board of Survey, and a certificate of 
destruction issued.
They can be shredded and recycled.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1227 and 1228 specify destruction by burning in the 
presence of a Board of Survey, with a certificate issued.
247
Section E
Part 1: Financial Regulations: Deposits

items.question:
According to Financial Regulation 1301, under what classification shall deposits be 
accounted for?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1301 specifies that deposits shall be accounted for under the 
"Below-the-line classification" list issued by the Accountant-General.

items.question:
Which of the following may NOT be placed on deposit, according to Financial Regulation 
1302?
options:
A refundable payment for a service
Q
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1302 explicitly states that "unclaimed salaries wages, 
unspent balances of allocation, sum due to Merchants or Contractors or any form of revenue 
shall not be placed on deposit."

items.question: Who is responsible for maintaining a Deposit register?
options: The Sub-Accountant authorized to record deposit transactions

The Head of Finance and Accounts
□
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1304 mandates that a Deposit register shall be maintained 
by the Sub-Accountant authorized to record all transactions in respect of the deposits.

items.question:
What information is required on a receipt voucher relating to a deposit, as per Financial 
Regulation 1305?
options: The name of the person on whose behalf the deposit is made and the date
The name of the depositor, the nature of the transaction, and the necessity for the deposit
The amount of the deposit and the signature of the cashier

The account number of the depositor and the Head of Accounts' approval
Q 248
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1305 requires that receipt vouchers for deposits must clearly 
indicate the person on whose behalf the deposit is made, the nature of the transaction, and the 
necessity for placing the sum on deposit.

items.question: What is required to support a withdrawal from a deposit?
options: A photocopy of the original receipt
A handwritten note from the depositor
The original receipt issued when the money was placed on deposit
A verbal confirmation from the Accounting Officera
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1306 states that withdrawals from deposit must be 
supported by the original receipt issued when the money was placed on deposit.

items.question: What is the procedure for an original receipt after a withdrawal is made from a deposit?
options: It is returned to the depositor
It is filed for seven years
It is marked "repaid" and attached to the withdrawal voucher
It is sent to the Accountant-Generala
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1306 specifies that when a withdrawal is made, the original 
receipt will be marked "repaid" and attached to the withdrawal voucher.

items.question: When should a refund of a deposit be made?
options: At the end of the financial year
When there is sufficient balance in the deposit account
As soon as it is due, provided that all necessary checks have been made

Only with the explicit approval of the Minister of Finance
□
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1307 states that refunds of deposits will be made as soon as 
they are due, provided that all necessary checks and reconciliations have been completed.

items.question: What must a Sub-Accountant do with unclaimed deposits at the end of each financial year?
options: Transfer them to a suspense account
Send a list to the Accounting Officer
items.explanation:
According to Financial Regulation 1310, a list of all unclaimed deposits shall be 
forwarded to the Accounting Officer at the end of each financial year.
249

items.question:
What is the minimum period of time a deposit must remain unclaimed before it is transferred 
to revenue?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1311 states that if the money has remained on deposit for 
more than three years, it will be transferred to revenue.

items.question:
What must be done with bank guarantees received to cover possible default or negligence 
of a contractor or public officer?
options: They can be kept in a private office safe.
They must be lodged with the Accountant-General.
They should be submitted to the Auditor-General for safekeeping.
They must be retained in the custody of the Accounting Officer.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1314 stipulates that bank guarantees must be retained in the 
custody of the Accounting Officer to be produced on request by the Accountant-General or the 
Auditor-General.

items.question:
Who has the authority to approve special instructions for the operation of deposit accounts 
peculiar to a ministry?
A The Minister of Finance
options: The Accountant-General in consultation with the Auditor-General
The Head of Finance and Accounts
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1303 requires that special instructions for the operation of 
deposit accounts shall be subject to the approval of the Accountant-General in consultation with 
the Auditor-General.

items.question:
What document is used to transfer a deposit to revenue after it has remained unclaimed for 
the prescribed period?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1311 specifies that the transfer will be effected by means of 
a journal voucher.

items.question: What is the process for deposits held in foreign currency?
options: They must be converted to local currency immediately.
They should be treated in the same manner as deposits in local currency.
They are not subject to these regulations.

They are the responsibility of the Central Bank of Nigeria.
□
items.explanation:
While not explicitly stated, the general principle of the financial regulations is that 
all government funds are subject to proper accounting procedures, regardless of the currency. 
The regulations for deposits apply universally.

items.question: What must be included in the Deposit Register for each transaction?
options: Only the amount of the deposit.
The name of the depositor, particulars of the deposit, and withdrawal.
The date of the transaction and the signature of the officer.
The reason for the deposit and the expected withdrawal date.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1304 requires the register to contain the name of the 
depositor and particulars of all deposits and withdrawals made.

items.question: If a deposit is transferred to revenue, can the original depositor claim the money later?
options: No, the claim is permanently extinguished.
Yes, but the claim must be made within one year.
The claim will be treated as a claim against public funds.
The money can be re-transferred from revenue back to the deposit account.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1311 states that if the deposit is transferred to revenue and a 
valid claim is later made, it will be treated as a claim against public funds.
Part 2: Financial Regulations: Advances.

items.question: What does Financial Regulation 1401 primarily address?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1401 explicitly deals with "Personal advances"
251

items.question:
Which of the following is considered a "Non-personal advance" according to Financial 
Regulation 1402?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1402 addresses "Non-personal advances" by specifically 
mentioning "Non-personal Account, Opening of

items.question: What details are required in advances payment vouchers as per Financial Regulation 1403?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1403 states that "Details in advances payment vouchers" are 
required, indicating specific particulars

items.question:
What does Financial Regulation 1404 cover regarding advances?
A The approval process
options:
The interest rates on advances
Q
items.explanation: Financial Regulation 1404 deals with "Advances records"

items.question: What type of advance is mentioned in Financial Regulation 1405?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1405 explicitly refers to "Salary advance on first 
appointment".

items.question:
What happens if an officer fails to ensure the "full repayment" of an advance, as per 
Financial Regulation 1419?
options: The advance is automatically converted to a grant
The outstanding balance is transferred to another department
The officer is granted an extensiona
items.explanation:
While not explicitly stated in the provided snippet for FR 1419, failure to comply 
with financial regulations generally leads to disciplinary action.

items.question:
Which of these is NOT directly addressed under "Advances" in Chapter 14?
A Correspondence advance
items.explanation:
While the document mentions "Boards of Survey" in other chapters (e.g., Chapter 
18 for Cash and Stamps), it is not listed as a sub-topic under "Advances" in Chapter 14
Part 3: Financial Regulation: Salaries

items.question: According to Financial Regulation 1501, what is the definition of "Salary"?
options: A bonus paid for good performance.

The personal emolument paid to an employee, usually monthly, for services rendered at a 
predetermined rate.
A one-time payment for a special project.
An allowance for housing and transportation.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1501 defines salary as the personal emolument paid to an 
employee, usually monthly, for services rendered at a predetermined rate of pay

items.question: How are all officers in the public service eligible for payment of salaries?
options: They must be in the employment of the public service.
They must have worked for at least one year.
They must be on a management level.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1502 states that all officers in the employment of the public 
service are eligible for the payment of salaries
257

items.question: What is forbidden regarding salaries, allowances, and pensions?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1503(iii) explicitly states that salaries, allowances, and 
pensions must not be paid in advance.

items.question: How are salaries and allowances based on an annual rate paid?
options: Monthly installments of one twelfth of the annual rate.

As a lump sum at the end of the year.
Q
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1504 specifies that salaries and allowances based on an 
annual rate shall be paid by monthly installments of one twelfth of the annual rate.

items.question:
Who is responsible for prescribing the dates in the month on which salaries and allowances 
shall be paid?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1505 states that the Accountant-General shall prescribe the 
dates for salary and allowance payments each month.
258

items.question:
What is the consequence for an officer who receives information about a colleague's 
resignation but fails to promptly inform the Accounts Department?
options: They will be transferred to another department.
They will be liable to surcharge and discipline for negligence.
They will receive a formal warning.
They will have their own salary delayed.a
items.explanation:
According to Financial Regulation 1508, an officer who fails to promptly pass on 
information to the Director, Finance and Accounts Department is liable to surcharge and 
discipline for negligence and dereliction of duty.

items.question:
What should be done with any balance of salary or other moneys due to an officer who has 
been dismissed for misappropriation of government funds?
options: It should be paid immediately to the officer's family.
It should be held indefinitely by the government.
It shall not be paid without the authority of the Accounting Officer.

It must be used to pay off their debts.
¡3
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1511 states that any balance of salary or other money due to 
an officer who has been convicted or dismissed for misappropriation or theft shall not be paid 
without the Accounting Officer's authority.

items.question:
How long does the Sub-Accounting Officer have to pay unclaimed salaries, allowances, and 
pensions to the Treasury?
options: At the end of the financial year.
As soon as the cash is withdrawn.a 259
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1513(i) specifies that unclaimed salaries, allowances, and 
pensions must be paid to the Sub-Accounting Officer within seven days from the date of 
withdrawal

items.question:
What two documents are required to be issued when an officer is transferred from one 
ministry to another?
options: A Last Pay Certificate and a certified true copy of the Personal Emolument Records.
A letter of recommendation and a transfer letter.
An updated curriculum vitae and a new contract.
A bank statement and a new Treasury Form 14.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1514(i) requires that a Last Pay Certificate along with a 
certified true copy of the Personal Emolument Records shall be issued when an officer is 
transferred.

items.question: What information must the Last Pay Certificate and Personal Emolument Record show?
options: The last pay date, details of deductions, and the balance recoverable.
items.explanation:
According to Financial Regulation 1514(ii), the last pay certificate and personal 
emolument record must show the last pay date, full details of deductions, and the balance 
recoverable.

items.question: What must a paying officer do with the total of each type of deduction from emoluments?
options: Transfer it to a suspense account.
Enter it as a receipt in his cash book.
Ignore it for the final payment calculation.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1515 states that the paying officer shall enter the total of 
each type of deduction from emoluments as a receipt in his cash book.

items.question: What must the gross amount of emoluments be charged against?
options: The relative personal emoluments sub-head of the current year's estimates.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1515 specifies that the gross amount of the emoluments 
must be charged against the relative personal emoluments sub-head of the current year's 
estimates.

items.question:
Who must approve the procedure and method for the recording and calculation of personal 
emoluments?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1517 mandates that the procedure and method for recording 
and calculating personal emoluments must be approved by the Accountant-General

items.question:
Which of the following is not a record required for the standard payroll system under FR 
1518?
options:
Pay Statement to be issued to the individual officer.
□
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1518(i) lists the Personal Emoluments Record, payroll, and 
Pay Statement as part of the standard payroll system, but makes no mention of a birth 
certificate.

items.question: What is a key duty of the officer controlling expenditure as per FR 1519?
options: To personally approve every single salary payment.
To ensure the standard personal emoluments records are kept securely.

To ensure the records show the officer's standard rate of pay, incremental date, allowances, 
and deductions.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1519 details the information that the officer controlling 
expenditure must ensure is shown in the standard personal emoluments records, including rate 
of pay, incremental date, and deductions

items.question:
Who is responsible for ensuring that personal emolument record cards are kept under strict 
security arrangements in a manual accounting system?
options: The Head, Finance and Accounts Department.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1520 states that in a manual system, the Head, Finance and 
Accounts Department shall ensure that the personal emolument record cards are kept under 
strict security arrangements.

items.question: What is the purpose of the completed personal emolument form according to FR 1521?
options:
To serve as the basis for opening the group registers, which must be audited before an officer 
is included in the payroll.
To justify the officer's annual leave.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1521(ii) states that the completed personal emolument form 
shall be the basis for opening the group registers, which must be audited before any officer is 
included in the payroll.

items.question: What must the salary section be informed of in writing at the earliest possible time?
options: Any variation that may affect the salary of any officer.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1522(i) requires that the salary section must be informed in 
writing of any variation that may affect an officer's salary at the earliest possible time.

items.question:
Who should not be the officer directly in charge of preparation of the payroll, according to FR 
1523(ii)?
options: An officer not below the rank of Senior Accountant.
The officer directly in charge of variation control.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1523(ii) specifies that the officer directly in charge of 
variation control must not be the officer directly in charge of preparation of the payroll and must 
work independently.

items.question: Which of the following is an action required of the payroll section, as per FR 1524?
options: To personally deliver cash to each employee.

To effect necessary entries in the Personnel Emolument records of each employee.
262
To write the officer's performance review.

To conduct a surprise inspection.
Answer: B
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1524(a) lists effecting necessary entries in the Personnel 
Emolument records of each employee as an action the payroll section must take

items.question: What should the audited summary vouchers be scheduled to, as per FR 1525?
options:
The Central Pay Office (CPO) or Federal Pay Office (FPO).
C.The officer's personal bank.
D.The Head of Department.
Q Correct: B
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1525(e) states that the audited vouchers should be 
scheduled to the Central Pay Office (CPO) or Federal Pay Office (FPO).

items.question: What should cheques for cash payments be drawn in the name of, according to FR 1526?
options:
The bank manager.
B.The Accounting Officer or head of the field headquarters office in the state.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1526(d) states that for cash payments, cheques should be 
drawn in the name of the Accounting Officer or head of the field headquarters office in the state

items.question: What must be sought and secured for cash in-transit?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1527(i) states that the assistance of police escort must be 
sought and secured for cash in-transit

items.question:
How are salary payments for part of a month calculated on retirement or termination of 
appointment?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1504 specifies that on retirement or termination of 
appointment, payment for part of a month shall be calculated on a pro-rata basis.

items.question: What is the role of the Accounting Officer concerning personal emolument records?
options:
To ignore them.
B.To ensure they are maintained to prevent over-payments and ensure correct deductions are 
made.

To only maintain them for senior staff.
Q
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1506(ii) states that the Accounting Officer shall ensure 
personal emolument records are maintained to ensure payments are made when due, that 
over-payments are not made, and that all required deductions are made

items.question:
What is a key duty of the Central Pay Office/Federal Pay Office regarding payroll 
documents?
options: To check the documents against forwarding schedules to ensure no documents are missing.
To personally hand out cash to each officer.

To write the officer's performance review.
264
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1526(a) states that the Central Pay Office (CPO) or Federal 
Pay Office (FPO) must check payroll documents against entries in the forwarding schedules to 
ensure that no documents are missing
Part 4: Financial Regulation: Self Accounting Status

items.question:
What does Financial Regulation 1601 primarily define?
A, The responsibilities of an Auditor-General.
options: The role of an Accounting Officer in a self-accounting unit.

Procedures for revenue collection.
D Guidelines for capital expenditure.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1601 explicitly defines the "Role in a self-accounting unit" for 
an Accounting Officer

items.question:
Who approves the grant of self-accounting status to a Ministry, Extra-Ministerial Office, or 
other arms of government?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1602 states that the Accountant-General's approval is 
required for self-accounting status

items.question:
What is one of the key responsibilities of the Accounting Officer in a self-accounting unit, as 
per Financial Regulation 1603(a)?
options: To personally conduct all internal audits.
To ensure strict observance of existing financial rules and regulations.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1603(a) mandates that the Accounting Officer shall ensure 
strict observance of existing financial rules and regulations governing the receipt and 
disbursement of public funds.

items.question:
According to Financial Regulation 1603(b), who must the Accounting Officer issue 
accounting instructions to?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1603(b) states that Accounting Officers shall issue detailed 
accounting instructions to all officers in the Ministry, Extra-Ministerial Office, or other arms of 
government.

items.question:
What is the Accounting Officer responsible for regarding the preparation and defense of 
annual budget proposals?
options: Personal compilation and defense under their supervision.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1603(i) states that the Accounting Officer is responsible for 
compiling and defending the annual budget proposals under his supervision.

items.question:
What kind of statements must the Accounting Officer ensure are produced monthly and 
quarterly, according to FR 1603(j)?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1603(j) requires monthly and quarterly statements of budget 
performance to be produced by the Accounting Officer.

items.question:
Who is responsible for ensuring the adequacy of internal control over revenue collection, as 
per FR 1603(g)?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1603(g) assigns the responsibility for adequate internal 
control over revenue collection to the Accounting Officer.

items.question:
What must the Accounting Officer ensure regarding the total of receipts and payments for 
the month, as per FR 1603(vi)?
options: They are included in the account totals.
They are forwarded directly to the Minister of Finance.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1603(vi) states that the account totals of the receipts and 
payments for the month must be ensured by the Accounting Officer.

items.question:
What type of balance must the Accounting Officer ensure is included in the monthly 
accounts (FR 1603(vii))?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1603(vii) requires the Accounting Officer to ensure the 
closing cash/bank balance is included.

items.question:
What is the role of the Accountant-General in relation to the accounting instructions issued 
by the Accounting Officer, according to FR 1603(h)?
options: To provide specific directives to embrace the general provisions of Financial Regulations.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1603(h) mentions that specific directives from the Minister of 
Finance or the Accountant-General should be embraced in the detailed accounting instructions.

items.question:
What is the fundamental principle regarding public funds in a self-accounting unit, as implied 
by Chapter 16?
options: They can be used for personal expenses.
They must be managed with strict adherence to financial regulations.

They are to be invested without oversight.
□
items.explanation:
The entire chapter emphasizes the Accounting Officer's responsibility to ensure 
strict observance of financial rules and regulations governing public funds.

items.question: What does "Self Accounting Status" imply for a Ministry or Extra-Ministerial Office?
options:
They are independent of all financial regulations.
267

They have the authority to manage their own financial affairs within the framework of 
approved regulations.
They do not need to report to the Accountant-General.

They can create their own financial rules.
Q
items.explanation:
The term "Self Accounting Status" and the responsibilities outlined in Chapter 16 
indicate that these units manage their finances but are still bound by the Financial Regulations.

items.question:
What is a core element of proper financial management in a self-accounting unit that the 
Accounting Officer must ensure?
options: Transparency and accountability in financial operations.
items.explanation:
The regulations emphasize strict observance of rules, proper accounting, and 
budget performance reporting, all pointing to transparency and accountability.

items.question:
What happens if an Accounting Officer in a self-accounting unit fails to comply with the 
Financial Regulations?
options: They may face disciplinary action and surcharges.
Their unit loses self-accounting status automatically.
items.explanation:
While not explicitly detailed in the provided Chapter 16 snippet, financial 
regulations generally impose disciplinary actions and surcharges for non-compliance. The 
emphasis on "strict observance" 26 implies consequences for failure.

items.question: What is the primary objective of monthly and quarterly statements of budget performance?
options: To monitor financial progress and ensure adherence to approved budgets.
To prepare for external audits only.
items.explanation:
These statements are crucial for monitoring financial performance against the 
budget.

items.question:
What kind of records must the Accounting Officer ensure are properly maintained within the 
self-accounting unit?
268
options: All financial and accounting records as required by regulations.
items.explanation:
The responsibilities outlined in FR 1603 imply the maintenance of all necessary 
financial and accounting records.

items.question:
What type of control is emphasized for the collection of all revenues under the responsibility 
of the Accounting Officer?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1603(g) specifically highlights an "adequate system of 
Internal control".

items.question:
What is the general rule regarding the disbursement of public funds in a self-accounting 
unit?
options: Funds can be disbursed at the discretion of the Head of Department.
Disbursements must strictly follow existing financial rules and regulations.
Funds can be transferred to private accounts.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1603(a) states the need for strict observance of existing 
financial rules and regulations governing the disbursement of public funds.

items.question: What should detailed accounting instructions issued by the Accounting Officer embrace?
options: Only the Accounting Officer's personal preferences.

The general provisions of Financial Regulations and specific directives from higher 
authorities.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1603(h) specifies that detailed accounting instructions 
should embrace general provisions of Financial Regulations and directives from the Minister of 
Finance or the Accountant-General.
269

items.question:
What document outlines the responsibilities of an Accounting Officer in a self-accounting 
unit?
items.explanation:
Chapter 16 of the Financial Regulations book is dedicated to "Self Accounting 
Status" and outlines these responsibilities..

items.question: What action is crucial for newly established revenue collecting offices regarding notification?
options: They must notify the local police.
They must notify the appropriate Sub Accounting Officer and the Auditor-General.
They must notify the general public.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 217 (from a related section but relevant to accounting officer 
duties) states Accounting Officers will notify the appropriate Sub Accounting Officer and the 
Auditor-General of the establishment of any new revenue collecting office

items.question:
Who primarily bears the responsibility for any discrepancies in financial records within a 
self-accounting unit?
items.explanation:
The Accounting Officer is charged with ensuring strict observance of regulations 
and proper accounting, implying ultimate responsibility for discrepancies.

items.question:
What is the frequency for producing accounts to the Accounting Officer to show cumulative 
yearly totals of expenditure and revenue?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1603(f) states that monthly accounts are produced to the 
Accounting Officer to show cumulative yearly totals.
270

items.question: What must the Accounting Officer ensure regarding the reconciliation of accounts?
options: It is done by external parties only.
It is carried out promptly and accurately.
items.explanation:
The overall tenor of FR 1603 emphasizes proper and timely financial 
management, which includes accurate reconciliation.

items.question:
What is specifically forbidden in handling money received in the course of official duties by 
an officer who is not a Revenue Collector or Sub-Accounting Officer?
options: Lodging them with a Sub-Accounting Officer.

Supporting lodgement with a paying-in form.
□
items.explanation: Financial Regulation 218 states that such monies must be lodged "without delay".

items.question: What happens to interest earned on bank accounts, according to Financial Regulations?
options: It can be retained by the unit.

It must be properly classified to the appropriate revenue head and paid to the Consolidated 
Revenue Fund.
It can be used for departmental welfare.
