It is transferred to a suspense account.
□
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 222 (from a related chapter but a general principle) states 
that interest earned on bank accounts must be properly classified and paid to the Consolidated 
Revenue Fund.

items.question:
What is the rule about making deductions from revenue collections to adjust a previous 
over-credit?
options: It is allowed for small amounts.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 223 explicitly states, "No deductions shall be made from any 
revenue collections or other receipts to adjust a previous over-credit".

items.question:
What general principle applies to all monies due to the federal government?
271
options: The gross amount received must, on all occasions, be accounted for in full.
They are subject to annual write-offs.3
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 223 states that the gross amount received must always be 
accounted for in full.

items.question:
What is the implication of the Accounting Officer ensuring payments are made when due 
and that over-payments are not made?
options: They are only concerned with timely payments.
They are responsible for the accuracy and timeliness of payments.
They only focus on preventing fraud.3
items.explanation:
This responsibility encompasses both the punctuality and correctness of 
payments, indicating a dual focus on timeliness and accuracy.

items.question:
What type of reporting must the Accounting Officer ensure is accurate for management and 
control purposes?
options: Comprehensive financial reports including budget performance.
items.explanation:
The Accounting Officer is responsible for budget proposals, defense, and 
monthly/quarterly budget performance statements, all indicating comprehensive financial 
reporting.
272
Section F
Part 1: Financial Regulation: Internal Audit

items.question: What is the overall policy of internal audit according to Financial Regulation 1701?
options:
To replace external auditors.
B To provide a complete and continuous audit of accounts and records of revenue, expenditure, 
plant, and stores.
To focus only on revenue generation.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1701 states the policy is to provide a complete and 
continuous audit of the accounts and records of revenue, expenditure, plant, and stores.

items.question: Who is responsible for the appointment of the Internal Auditor?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1702 specifies that the Accountant-General shall be 
responsible for the appointment and deployment of Internal Auditors.

items.question: What is a key duty of the Internal Auditor as per Financial Regulation 1703?
options: To conduct an annual audit of the entire Ministry.
To audit and certify all payment vouchers.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1703(d) lists one of the duties as auditing and certifying all 
payment vouchers.

items.question:
What type of reports must be submitted to the Accounting Officer, according to Financial 
Regulation 1706?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1706 requires the Internal Auditor to submit periodic audit 
reports to the Accounting Officer.

items.question: What is the Internal Auditor's guide for auditing procedures known as, according to FR 1708?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1708 states that the Head of Internal Audit Unit shall prepare 
and issue an "Audit Guide" for the use of audit staff.

items.question: Which of the following is a restriction on the duties of audit staff, as per FR 1709?
options: They cannot make suggestions for improvements.
They are not allowed to handle cash or stores.
They must not report their findings.

They are not allowed to communicate with other staff.
□
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1709(a) prohibits audit staff from handling cash, receipts, 
stamps, or stores.

items.question:
What must an Internal Auditor do when he discovers an apparent abuse of Financial 
Regulations?
options: Report it immediately to the Accountant-General.
Call the attention of the appropriate officer in writing.

Take disciplinary action against the officer.
□
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1703(a) states the Internal Auditor shall call the attention of 
the appropriate officer in writing where there is any apparent abuse of Financial Regulations.

items.question: What is the role of the Head of the Internal Audit Unit regarding the annual audit program?
options: To prepare it for the approval of the Accounting Officer.
To prepare it for the approval of the Auditor-General.

To ignore it if he is too busy.
Q
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1704 states that the annual audit program must be prepared 
for the approval of the Accounting Officer.

items.question: What is required for the issuance of audit stamps to Internal Audit staff?
options: Verbal permission from the Head of Department.
A form signed by the Accountant-General.

A requisition form (Treasury Form 65A) in triplicate.
a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1711 details the procedure for issuing audit stamps, 
requiring a requisition form (Treasury Form 65A) in triplicate.

items.question: What must an Internal Auditor do with audit stamps at the end of each duty?
options: Keep them for the next day's work.
Account for and return them to the Head of the Internal Audit Unit.

Distribute them to other staff.
a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1712 states that Internal Auditors must account for and 
return their audit stamps to the Head of the Internal Audit Unit at the close of each day's duty.

items.question: What is strictly prohibited regarding audit stamps?
options: Transferring them to another staff member.
Collecting them from the Head of the Internal Audit Unit.

Keeping them in a safe.
a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1713 states that "Audit stamps are not transferable" and 
must not be used by anyone other than the officer to whom they were issued.

items.question:
What happens if an Internal Auditor discovers that an officer is negligently disregarding 
Financial Regulations?
options: The Internal Auditor should write a note to the Auditor-General.
The Internal Auditor should inform the officer's supervisor.

The Internal Auditor should communicate his discovery to the Accounting Officer in a 
"management letter."
275
The Internal Auditor should ignore it if it is a small error.Q
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1703(e) requires the Internal Auditor to communicate such 
discoveries to the Accounting Officer in a "management letter."

items.question: What is the primary purpose of the Internal Audit Unit?
options: To perform administrative tasks for the department.
To provide an independent appraisal of accounting, financial, and other controls.
To sign off on all procurement contracts.
To manage the department's human resources.Q
items.explanation:
The policy of internal audit and its duties, as described in Chapter 17, are 
centered on providing an independent appraisal of financial and other controls to the Accounting 
Officer.

items.question: To whom does the Internal Auditor submit periodic audit reports?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1706 explicitly states that periodic audit reports are 
submitted to the Accounting Officer.

items.question:
What is the relationship between the Internal Auditor and the staff whose accounts they are 
auditing?
options: The Internal Auditor is superior to the staff.
The Internal Auditor is subordinate to the staff.
The Internal Auditor should be independent of the staff.

The Internal Auditor works under the supervision of the staff.
□
items.explanation:
The entire purpose of internal audit is to provide an independent appraisal, which 
implies independence from the staff whose work is being audited.

items.question: What type of register must be maintained by the Head of the Internal Audit Unit?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1705 requires the Head of the Internal Audit Unit to maintain 
a "progress register" to record the status of work done by the audit staff.

items.question: What is a key function of the Head of the Internal Audit Unit concerning the audit program?
options: To delegate all tasks to junior staff.
To amend the program as he deems fit, without approval.
To ensure the audit program is effectively carried out.
To present the program to the general public.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1704 mandates that the Head of the Internal Audit Unit shall 
"ensure that the programme is effectively carried out."

items.question: What does the "management letter" from the Internal Auditor primarily address?
options: Irregularities and failures of internal control.
items.explanation:
The management letter is used to communicate discoveries of negligent disregard 
of financial regulations or abuse of internal controls.

items.question:
In addition to auditing payment vouchers, what other type of vouchers does the Internal 
Auditor audit and certify?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1703(d) states that the Internal Auditor's duties include 
auditing and certifying "all payment and revenue vouchers."
Part 2: Financial Regulation: Board of Survey: Cash and Stamps

items.question:
According to Financial Regulation 1801, what is the primary purpose of an Annual Board of 
Survey?
options:
To inspect the condition of government vehicles.
C.To physically inspect, count, and verify the cash, stamps, and other security documents held 
by Accounting Officers.
To review the annual budget proposal.3 277
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1801 states that an Annual Board of Survey shall be held to 
physically inspect, count, and verify all cash, stamps, and other security documents held by 
Accounting Officers.

items.question:
Who is responsible for convening the Annual Board of Survey for a Ministry, Extra-Ministerial 
Office, or other arm of government?
options: The Head of the Internal Audit Unit.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1802 specifies that the Accounting Officer is responsible for 
convening the Annual Board of Survey.

items.question: What is the minimum number of members on a Board of Survey, according to FR 1802?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1802 states that the Board shall consist of at least three 
members.

items.question: What must the members of the Board of Survey be, as a requirement for their appointment?
options: They must be from the same department as the officer being surveyed.
They must be from different departments or units.
They must be senior accounting officers.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1802 requires that the members shall be from different 
departments or units other than the one whose accounts are being surveyed.

items.question:
What is the duty of the Board of Survey concerning the cash, stamps, and other security 
documents?
options: To sell them at a public auction.
To physically count and verify them against the relevant accounting records and registers.
To destroy them if they are found to be old.a 278
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1803 outlines the duties of the Board, which include 
physically counting and verifying the items against the relevant accounting records.

items.question:
Who must be present during the physical count and verification of cash, stamps, and other 
security documents by the Board of Survey?
options: The officer-in-charge of the items being surveyed.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1803 requires that the officer-in-charge of the cash, stamps, 
or other security documents must be present throughout the physical count and verification.

items.question: What must the Board do with any surplus or shortage of cash discovered during the survey?
options: Keep the surplus and ignore the shortage.
Report the matter immediately to the Accounting Officer for prompt investigation.
Adjust the books to reflect the new balance.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1807 mandates that any surplus or shortage must be 
reported immediately to the Accounting Officer for prompt investigation.

items.question: When should the business of the office being surveyed be suspended?
options: Throughout the period of the physical inspection.
At the beginning and end of the financial year.
Only if a shortage is discovered.a CorrectAnswer: B
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1806 states that the business of the office or unit shall be 
suspended for the duration of the physical inspection.

items.question: What type of report must the Board of Survey prepare and submit?
options: A verbal report to the Accounting Officer.
A report in triplicate, which must be signed by all members.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1807 states that the Board of Survey shall prepare a report 
in triplicate, signed by all members.
279

items.question: Who receives the three copies of the Board of Survey report?
options: The Accounting Officer, the Accountant-General, and the Auditor-General.
The Accounting Officer, the Head of the Internal Audit Unit, and the Minister of Finance.
The Accounting Officer, the Head of Department, and the officer-in-charge.
The Auditor-General, the Head of Internal Audit, and the Head of Department.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1807 directs that one copy of the report goes to the 
Accounting Officer, one to the Accountant-General, and one to the Auditor-General.

items.question: What is the purpose of a "surprise" Board of Survey?
options:
To give notice to the officer-in-charge.
B To check for discrepancies without prior warning.
To only be conducted when there is a known shortage.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1804 allows for the convening of "surprise" Boards of Survey 
to check cash, stamps, and other security documents without prior warning.

items.question:
What action should be taken if an Accounting Officer in a field headquarters office is absent 
when a surprise Board of Survey is convened?
options: The survey must be postponed until the officer returns.
The survey can proceed without them.
The next most senior officer shall act in his place.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1804 states that if the Accounting Officer is absent, the next 
most senior officer shall be required to act in his place for the purposes of the survey.

items.question: What is the responsibility of the members of the Board of Survey regarding the report?
options: They are not responsible for the contents of the report.
They are jointly responsible for the accuracy of the report.
Only the Chairman is responsible for the report's accuracy.
They are only responsible for the physical counting.a
items.explanation:
The fact that the report must be signed by all members implies that they are jointly 
responsible for its accuracy.

items.question: What is the key function of the Board of Survey?
options:
To act as a control mechanism to ensure that public funds and assets are properly 
safeguarded and accounted for.
items.explanation:
The duties of the Board, such as physical inspection, counting, and verification of 
cash and stamps, all point to its function as a control mechanism for safeguarding public assets.

items.question: What is the implication of the Board of Survey's findings for an officer-in-charge?
options: The findings have no consequences for the officer.
The officer is solely responsible for any discrepancies.
The officer is immediately dismissed if a shortage is found.
The officer's responsibility is discharged only after a satisfactory report is submitted.3
items.explanation:
The officer-in-charge must be present during the count and the Board's report is 
submitted for investigation of discrepancies, which implies the officer is responsible for the items 
under their charge.
Part 3: Financial Regulations book: Pension Scheme in the Federal Public Service

items.question: What is the primary purpose of Chapter 19 of the Financial Regulations book?
options: To define the roles of Accounting Officers.
To outline the pension scheme in the Federal Public Service.
To detail procedures for internal audit.
items.explanation: Chapter 19 is titled "Pension Scheme in the Federal Public Service."

items.question:
According to Financial Regulation 1904(a), who is responsible for opening Retirement 
Savings Accounts (RSA) for each employee?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1904(a) states that the National Pension Commission (NPC) 
will open Retirement Savings Accounts (RSA) for each employee.

items.question:
What action is the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) required to take upon receipt of payments 
and advice from the National Pension Commission?
options:
To disburse funds directly to employees.
281
To invest the funds as provided in the Pension Reform Act.
To transfer funds to the Consolidated Revenue Fund.

To hold the funds in a suspense account.
Q
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1904(b) requires the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) to invest 
the funds as provided in the Pension Reform Act.

items.question:
What is one of the responsibilities of the National Pension Commission regarding 
accounting records?
options: To destroy accounting records after five years.
To maintain accounting records for contributions and ensure proper periodic reconciliation.
To delegate all accounting responsibilities to ministries.

To only report to the Auditor-General.
□
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1904(c) states that the Commission shall maintain 
accounting records for the contributions and ensure proper periodic reconciliation of the 
accounts.

items.question:
How often should the National Pension Commission generate and distribute Retirement 
Savings Account (RSA) statements to contributing employees?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1904(d) mandates that on a quarterly basis, the commission 
shall generate and distribute Retirement Savings Account (RSA) statements to each 
contributing employee.

items.question:
What rank must a liaison officer designated by each ministry or agency for pension matters 
not be below?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1904(e) specifies that each ministry or agency shall 
designate a liaison officer not below the rank of a Director.
282

items.question:
What must all Accounting Officers ensure is created in their ministries/extra-ministerial 
offices regarding pensions?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1905 states that all Accounting Officers shall ensure that a 
Subsidiary Account Pension Unit is created.

items.question:
How should the Subsidiary Account Pension Unit function in relation to the main subsidiary 
accounts section of ministries?
options: It should be integrated with the main section.
It should report directly to the Accountant-General.
It should be supervised by the main accounts section.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1905 states that the section shall function independently of 
the main subsidiary accounts section.

items.question:
Who allocates a Below-the-Line Classification code for the recording of monthly pension 
contributions?
options: The Director of Finance and Accounts.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1906 states that the Accountant-General shall allocate a 
Below-the-Line Classification code.

items.question:
On receipt of monthly personnel cost warrants, what should the Director of Finance and 
Accounts ensure is issued?
options: A payment advice to the Central Bank of Nigeria.
A Treasury Receipt Book 6 in respect of the gross amount on the warrant. 19
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1907 states that on receipt of monthly personnel cost 
warrants, the Director of Finance and Accounts shall ensure the issuance of Treasury Receipt 
Book

items.question:
To what classification code should both the Treasury Receipt Voucher and Payment Voucher 
for pension deductions be classified?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1907 specifies that both the Treasury Receipt Voucher and 
Payment Voucher should be classified to cash transfer code 2000 series.

items.question: What is the overall goal of the pension scheme outlined in Chapter 19?
options: To ensure proper administration and accounting of pension contributions.

To provide short-term loans to employees.
□
items.explanation:
The chapter details the administration, responsibilities of various entities, and 
accounting procedures for the pension scheme, indicating its goal is proper management of 
contributions.

items.question:
What document provides the legal framework for the investment of pension funds by the 
Central Bank of Nigeria?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1904(b) states that investments are made "as provided in 
the Pension Reform Act."

items.question:
What is a "Below-the-Line Classification code" used for in the context of pension 
contributions?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1906 states that this code is used "for the recording of 
monthly pension contributions."
284

items.question: What is the significance of the Subsidiary Account Pension Unit functioning independently?
options: It ensures specialization and clear accountability for pension-related financial procedures.
It simplifies the overall accounting process.a
items.explanation:
The independent functioning, as per FR 1905, suggests a focus on specialized 
and accountable management of pension funds.

items.question:
Who issues the monthly personnel cost warrants that trigger the pension accounting 
procedures?
options: The Director of Finance and Accounts.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1907 indicates that the Director of Finance and Accounts 
receives "monthly personnel cost warrants from the Budget Office."

items.question: What type of account is the Retirement Savings Account (RSA)?
options: An account specific to each employee for pension contributions.
A suspense account for unclaimed funds.

An investment account managed by ministries.
□
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1904(a) states the NPC will open "Retirement Savings 
Accounts (RSA) for each employee."

items.question:
What aspect of contributions does the National Pension Commission credit to the 
Retirement Savings Accounts (RSA)?
options: Both employer and employee contributions, and interest earned.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1904(a) specifies that the NPC will credit "both the 
collections and interest earned to the account."

items.question:
What is the significance of a "nominal payment voucher" in the pension accounting 
procedure?
285
options: It is used for direct cash payments to pensioners.
It accounts for deductions at source from the gross amount on the warrant.
It Is a placeholder for future payments.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 1907 states that a "nominal payment voucher is raised to 
account for the deduction at source."

items.question:
What fundamental principle of financial management is supported by the detailed 
accounting procedures for pensions in Chapter 19?
options: Transparency and accountability in public finance.
items.explanation:
The detailed regulations, reconciliation requirements, and independent unit 
creation all support principles of transparency and accountability in managing pension funds.
Part 4: Financial Regulation: Government Vehicles

items.question:
What is the primary purpose of government vehicles, as stated in Financial Regulation 
2001?
options: For personal use by government officials.
For official duties of the government.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 2001 states that government vehicles are provided for official 
duties of the government.

items.question:
Who is held personally responsible for the efficient and economical use of government 
vehicles within their Ministry, Extra-Ministerial Office, or other arm of government?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 2002 states that the Accounting Officer is personally 
responsible for the efficient and economical use of government vehicles.
286

items.question:
What document must every government vehicle have attached to its logbook, as per FR 
2003?
options: A copy of the driver's license.
A copy of the vehicle's insurance policy.
A copy of the "Authority to Use Government Vehicle" (Treasury Form 140).

A copy of the vehicle purchase invoice.
□
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 2003 states that a copy of the "Authority to Use Government 
Vehicle" (Treasury Form 140) must be attached to the logbook.

items.question:
What information must be clearly displayed on the front and rear number plates of all 
government vehicles?
options:
The official registration number and the inscription "Federal Government" or "State 
Government."
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 2004 requires that all government vehicles must display the 
official registration number and the inscription "Federal Government" or "State Government" on 
both front and rear number plates.

items.question: Which document must be carried in every government vehicle, and why?
options: Vehicle purchase invoice for proof of ownership.
The logbook (Treasury Form 141 ) for recording journey details.
The vehicle service history for maintenance records.

The driver's medical certificate for emergencies.
□
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 2005 mandates that a logbook (Treasury Form 141) must be 
carried in every vehicle for recording details of journeys.

items.question: Who is responsible for ensuring the proper and up-to-date maintenance of the logbook?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 2005 states that the driver is responsible for the proper and 
up-to-date maintenance of the logbook.
287

items.question: What specific action must a driver take at the end of each journey, according to FR 2006?
options: Park the vehicle in the garage.
Record details in the logbook and sign against the entries.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 2006 requires the driver to record details of the journey in 
the logbook and sign against the entries.

items.question: Who is responsible for examining the logbook regularly and signing against the entries?
options: The Permanent Secretary or Head of Extra-Ministerial Office.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 2007 states that the Permanent Secretary or Head of 
Extra-Ministerial Office shall examine the logbook regularly and sign against the entries.

items.question:
What is the frequency for the Mechanical Engineer to verify and certify the fuel and 
lubricants consumption?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 2008 requires the Mechanical Engineer to verify and certify 
fuel and lubricants consumption monthly.

items.question:
What action must be taken for all journeys outside office hours or during weekends and 
public holidays?
options: They require the written permission of the Accounting Officer or his delegated representative.
They are permitted for personal use.
They must be reported to the police.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 2009 states that journeys outside office hours, weekends, 
and public holidays require the written permission of the Accounting Officer or his delegated 
representative.

items.question:
What is the consequence if a driver fails to keep the logbook up to date?
288
options: Forfeiture of the relevant vehicle allowance for the period.
No consequence, as long as the journey was official.3
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 2010 states that failure to keep the logbook up-to-date will 
result in the forfeiture of the relevant vehicle allowance for the period.

items.question:
What action should be taken by the Head of Division or Unit when a government vehicle is 
involved in an accident?
options: Ignore it if there is no major damage.
Report it immediately to the Accounting Officer.
Take the vehicle for repairs without reporting.
Attempt to resolve the matter privately.3
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 2011 requires the Head of Division or Unit to report vehicle 
accidents immediately to the Accounting Officer.

items.question:
What document must the Accounting Officer ensure is filled out immediately after a 
government vehicle accident?
options: An accident claim form from the insurance company.

A Report of Loss of Government Funds or Stores (Treasury Form 146).
C/ A police incident report.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 2012 requires the Accounting Officer to ensure that a Report 
of Loss of Government Funds or Stores (Treasury Form 146) is filled out immediately.

items.question: What is the role of the Mechanical Engineer after a government vehicle accident?
options: To inspect the vehicle and prepare an estimate of the cost of repairs.
To dispose of the damaged vehicle.3
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 2013 states that the Mechanical Engineer shall inspect the 
vehicle and prepare an estimate of the cost of repairs.

items.question:
Whose reports must be obtained for all accidents involving government vehicles, as per FR 
2014?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 2014 requires that police reports must be obtained for all 
accidents involving government vehicles.

items.question:
What must the officer-in-charge of the vehicle do upon receiving a police report on an 
accident?
options: Forward it immediately to the Accounting Officer.
Discard it if it is unfavorable.
Send it directly to the Auditor-General.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 2015 requires the officer-in-charge to forward the police 
report immediately to the Accounting Officer.
72 . What type of Board is to be convened if a driver is responsible for an accident involving a 
government vehicle?
A. A Disciplinary Board.
B. A Board of Survey.
C. A Board of Enquiry.
D. A Promotion Board.
□ Correct Answer: C
Explanation: Financial Regulation 2016 mandates that a Board of Enquiry shall be convened if 
a driver is found responsible for an accident

items.question:
What action should be taken if a driver is found negligent by the Board of Enquiry in a 
vehicle accident case?
A, A verbal warning.
options: Surcharge for the cost of repairs or disciplinary action.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 2017 states that if a driver is found negligent, they shall be 
surcharged for the cost of repairs or face appropriate disciplinary action.

items.question:
What quarterly returns must be submitted by each Ministry, Extra-Ministerial Office, or other 
arm of government regarding government vehicles?
options:
Returns of all vehicles, showing mileage covered, quantity of fuel consumed, and cost of 
repairs.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 2018 requires quarterly returns of all vehicles, showing 
mileage covered, quantity of fuel consumed, and cost of repairs.

items.question: To whom are the quarterly returns on government vehicles submitted?
options: Both the Accountant-General and the Auditor-General.3
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 2018 specifies that the quarterly returns are submitted to the 
Accountant-General and the Auditor-General.

items.question:
What happens if a government vehicle becomes unserviceable as a result of an accident 
caused by the negligence of a driver?
options: The driver is required to pay for a new vehicle.
The driver may be surcharged the book value of the vehicle.

The vehicle is sold for scrap.
□
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 2020 states that the driver may be surcharged the book 
value of the vehicle if it becomes unserviceable due to their negligence.

items.question:
Who is responsible for taking disciplinary action against a driver who causes an accident 
through negligence?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 2022 states that the Accounting Officer is responsible for 
taking disciplinary action.

items.question:
What should the Accounting Officer do if a civil or criminal action is contemplated regarding 
a government vehicle accident?
options: Refer the matter to the Legal Department for necessary advice and action.
Ignore it if the damage is minor.
Rely on the insurance company to handle all legal matters.Q
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 2023 requires the Accounting Officer to refer the matter to 
the Legal Department for advice and action.

items.question: What happens to a driver's pay if disciplinary action is pending due to an accident?
options: It is increased as a form of compensation.
It may be suspended until the matter is resolved.
It is paid to the victim of the accident.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 2024 states that the Accounting Officer may suspend the 
driver's pay until the disciplinary action is finalized. This is implied by the need for prompt action 
and resolution of such cases24.

items.question:
What is the process for disposing of a government vehicle that has been declared 
unserviceable?
options:
It must be disposed of in accordance with relevant financial regulations governing the 
disposal of government assets.
It can be kept by the driver.

It must be hidden from public view.
□
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 2026, while brief, implies adherence to the general 
regulations for the disposal of government assets.

items.question:
What information should be included in the quarterly returns on government vehicles, in 
addition to mileage and fuel consumption?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 2018 explicitly lists "cost of repairs" as part of the quarterly 
returns

items.question: What is the minimum standard for recording vehicle usage in the logbook?
options: Departure/arrival times, mileage, and purpose of journey.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 2005 details the requirement for recording "full details of 
every journey performed including mileage, departure and arrival times and destination" in the 
logbook.

items.question: What should be done with government vehicles when not in use?
options: Parked in designated government garages or secure premises.
items.explanation:
While not explicitly stated in a single regulation in this chapter, the emphasis on 
proper control and accountability (FR 2002) and security of government assets (related general 
regulations) implies secure parking.

items.question: What is the main objective of strict control over government vehicles?
options: To prevent abuse, ensure efficiency, and safeguard public resources.

To make them available for private hire.
□
items.explanation:
The regulations consistently emphasize efficient and economical use, prevention 
of abuse, and proper accounting, all aimed at safeguarding public resources.

items.question:
What is the immediate step a driver must take if involved in an accident causing injury or 
death?
options: Report it to the nearest police station immediately.

Attempt to settle with the injured party.
□
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 2014 mentions obtaining police reports for all accidents, 
implying immediate reporting, especially in severe cases.

items.question: What is the role of the Accounting Officer in the context of vehicle accident investigations?
options:
To ensure that all necessary reports are obtained, investigations conducted, and disciplinary 
actions taken.

To pay for all damages.
Q
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 2012, 2016, 2022, and 2023 all assign oversight and 
responsibility to the Accounting Officer for accident procedures.

items.question: What type of expenses related to government vehicles must be accurately accounted for?
options: All expenses, including fuel, maintenance, repairs, and insurance.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 2018 requires accounting for mileage, fuel, and cost of 
repairs in quarterly returns, implying comprehensive accounting for all related expenses.

items.question:
What must the driver produce if they are found to have damaged a government vehicle due 
to recklessness?
options: The full cost of the repairs.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 2017 specifies that a driver found negligent shall be 
surcharged for the cost of repairs

items.question: What is the primary method of tracking the usage of government vehicles?
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 2005-2007 consistently emphasizes the use and 
maintenance of the logbook for tracking vehicle usage.

items.question: What defines "official duties" in relation to government vehicle use?
options: Any activity requested by a government official.

Activities directly related to the statutory functions and responsibilities of the government 
agency.
294
Personal errands if the driver is on duty.

Transportation for family members of government officials.
Q
items.explanation:
While not explicitly defined in a single regulation, the overall context of 
government operations and financial regulations implies that "official duties" are those strictly 
aligned with the agency's legitimate functions.
Part 5: Financial Regulations: Stores Accounting and Custody

items.question: What is the primary purpose of Chapter 21 of the Financial Regulations book?
options: To outline procedures for bank reconciliation.
To provide guidelines for stores accounting and custody.
To define the roles of internal auditors.
items.explanation:
Chapter 21 is titled "Stores Accounting and Custody," indicating its primary 
purpose is to provide guidelines on this subject.

items.question:
According to Financial Regulation 2101, what must Accounting Officers do to ensure proper 
control over government stores?
options: Keep all stores in their personal custody.

Employ a system of stores accounting and control that provides an efficient check on all 
receipts, issues, and balances.
C/ Delegate all store management to a single junior officer.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 2101 mandates that Accounting Officers employ a system of 
stores accounting and control to provide an efficient check on all transactions and balances.

items.question: What is the key document used to record the receipt of stores?
items.explanation:
The Store Receipt Voucher (SRV) (Treasury Form 14) is the key document used 
to record the receipt of stores.

items.question: What must be done with all entries in the store ledger?
options: They must be made in pencil for easy correction.

They must be made in red ink.
295
They must be made in permanent ink.

They must be made electronically only.
Q
items.explanation:
All entries in the store ledger are to be made in permanent ink to prevent 
unauthorized alterations.

items.question: What is the role of the Storekeeper regarding the Store Ledger?
options: The Storekeeper is the only person allowed to make entries.

The Storekeeper is responsible for the proper keeping of the Store Ledger and other stores 
records.
The Storekeeper is not allowed to touch the Store Ledger.
The Storekeeper only approves the entries made by others.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 2107(a) assigns the responsibility for the proper keeping of 
the Store Ledger and other stores records to the Storekeeper.

items.question: What is the rule regarding the use of store forms, as per FR 2108?
options: They can be adapted to suit personal preferences.
The official store forms issued by the Accountant-General must be used without modification.
Any form can be used as long as it has a signature.
Electronic forms are preferred, and paper forms are obsolete.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 2108 states that the official store forms issued by the 
Accountant-General must be used without modification.

items.question: Who is responsible for ensuring that all stores are physically secure?
options: The Permanent Secretary or Head of Extra-Ministerial Office.
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 2128(b) places the responsibility for the physical security of 
all government stores on the Permanent Secretary or Head of Extra-Ministerial Office.

items.question: What must be provided for all unallocated stores?
options: A dedicated staff member to manage them.
Secure and properly maintained storage facilities.

A detailed list of all items.
Q 296
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 2128(a) mandates that secure and properly maintained 
storage facilities must be provided for all unallocated stores.

items.question: What is the process for issuing stores?
options: Verbal requests are sufficient for issuing stores.

Stores should only be issued against a properly completed and authorized Store Issue 
Voucher (SIV) or other authorized stores requisition forms.
The Storekeeper can issue stores at their discretion.
Stores are issued only on an annual basis.a
items.explanation:
Financial Regulation 2132 states that no stores shall be issued except on the 
authority of a properly completed and authorized Store Issue Voucher (SIV) or other authorized 
stores requisition forms.

items.question: What is a "Store Receipt Voucher" used for?
options: To authorize payment for a purchase.
To acknowledge the receipt of goods into a store.
To request a new item from a supplier.

To record the transfer of a store to another department.
□
items.explanation:
A Store Receipt Voucher (SRV) is used to acknowledge the receipt of goods into 
a store.
297
Section G
COMPUTER-BASED TEST (CBT) PROMOTION EXAMINATION FOR OFFICERS IN 
GRADES 15 TO 17 IN THE FEDERAL CIVIL SERVICE OF THE FEDERATION ON 
FINANCIAL REGULATION
Instruction: This examination consists of 100 multiple-choice questions designed to assess your 
readiness for promotion to the next level in the Federal Civil Service. You have exactly one 
hour (60 minutes) to complete all questions; manage your time wisely to ensure you attempt 
every question. Read each question carefully before selecting the best answer from the options.

items.question:

within a Ministry, Extra-Ministerial Office, or other arm of government?
What is the primary duty of an Internal Auditor, as per Financial Regulation 1703(d)?
options: To audit and certify all payment vouchers.

items.question:
What is the minimum number of members on a Board of Survey for Cash and Stamps, 
according to FR 1802?

What is the purpose of a "Subsidiary Account Pension Unit" as stated in Financial Regulation 
1905?
options: To function independently of the main accounts section to manage pension contributions.

items.question: What is the main principle regarding the use of government vehicles as per FR 2001?

Section G
items.explanation:
Explanation 1 Financial Regulation 2002 states that the Accounting Officer is personally 
responsible for the efficient and economical use of government vehicles.
Explanation 2: Financial Regulation 1703(d) explicitly lists auditing and certifying all payment 
and revenue vouchers as a duty of the Internal Auditor.
Explanation 3: Financial Regulation 1802 states that the Board shall consist of at least three 
members.
Explanation 4: Financial Regulation 1905 requires the creation of a Subsidiary Account Pension 
Unit that functions independently of the main subsidiary accounts section.
Explanation 5: Financial Regulation 2001 states that government vehicles are provided for the 
official duties of the government.
Explanation 6: Financial Regulation 1702 specifies that the Accountant-General is responsible 
for the appointment and deployment of Internal Auditors.
Explanation 7: Financial Regulation 2008 requires the Mechanical Engineer to verify and certify 
the consumption of fuel and lubricants on a monthly basis.
Explanation 8: Financial Regulation 2003 states that a copy of the Authority to Use Government 
Vehicle (Treasury Form 140) must be attached to the logbook
Explanation 9: Financial Regulation 2006 requires the driver to record details of the journey in 
the logbook and sign against the entries.
Explanation 10: Financial Regulation 1703(a) states that the Internal Auditor must call the 
attention of the appropriate officer in writing when they discover an apparent abuse of Financial 
Regulations.
Explanation 11: These statements are crucial for monitoring financial performance against the 
budget.
Explanation 12: Financial Regulation 1602 states that the Accountant-General's approval is 
required for self-accounting status.
Explanation 13: Financial Regulation 1603(a) mandates that the Accounting Officer shall ensure 
strict observance of existing financial rules and regulations governing the receipt and 
disbursement of public funds.
Explanation 14: While not explicitly detailed in the provided Chapter 16 snippet, financial 
regulations generally impose disciplinary actions and surcharges for non-compliance. The 
emphasis on "strict observance" implies consequences for failure.
314
Explanation 15: Financial Regulation 1801 states that an Annual Board of Survey shall be held 
to physically inspect, count, and verify all cash, stamps, and other security documents.
Explanation 16: Financial Regulation 1807 mandates that any surplus or shortage must be 
reported immediately to the Accounting Officer for prompt investigation.
Explanation 17: Financial Regulation 1904(d) mandates that the commission shall generate and 
distribute Retirement Savings Account (RSA) statements on a quarterly basis.
Explanation 18: Financial Regulation 1904(a) states that the National Pension Commission 
(NPC) will open Retirement Savings Accounts (RSA) for each employee.
Explanation 19: A Store Receipt Voucher (SRV) is the key document used to acknowledge the 
receipt of goods into a store.
Explanation 20: Financial Regulation 2101 mandates that Accounting Officers employ a system 
of stores accounting and control that provides an efficient check on all receipts, issues, and 
balances
Explanation 21: The Minister of Finance has the full authority to direct on all matters relating to 
the finance and accounting affairs of the federation.
Explanation 22: The Accountant-General of the Federation is the Chief Accounting Officer of the 
Federation.
Explanation 23: The policy is to provide a complete and continuous audit of the accounts and 
records of revenue, expenditure, plant, and stores.
Explanation 24: Financial Regulation 114(a) states that the Head of Finance and Accounts is 
responsible for ensuring compliance with Financial Regulations and the Accounting Code by all 
staff under their control and supervision.
Explanation 25: Financial Regulation 205 requires that a notice be prominently exhibited in 
every office where public money is received, stating that a numbered official receipt shall be 
obtained for every sum of money paid.
Explanation 26: A Revenue Collector's cash book is a record of all cash received.
Explanation 27: Unless there are exceptional circumstances, the intervals at which revenue 
collectors' cash books are produced for examination shall not be greater than one month.
Explanation 28: Financial Regulation 230(ii) states that officers should not overlook the 
possibility of fraud and should take steps to test this possibility by issuing bills through other 
means.
315
Explanation 29: The Local Purchase Order (LPO) is used for local purchases of stores and 
supplies.
Explanation 30: Stores should only be issued against a properly completed and authorized 
Store Issue Voucher (SIV).
Explanation 31: Treasury Form 146 is the standard form for reporting the loss of government 
funds or stores.
Explanation 32: Both the Accountant-General and the Auditor-General have the authority to 
convene a Board of Enquiry to investigate a loss or shortage of government funds.
Explanation 33: Financial Regulation 2128(a) mandates that secure and properly maintained 
storage facilities must be provided for all unallocated stores.
Explanation 34: Financial Regulation 2128(b) places the responsibility for the physical security 
of all government stores on the Permanent Secretary or Head of Extra-Ministerial Office.
Explanation 35: The Public Procurement Act is designed to promote honesty and transparency 
in the management of government business.
Explanation 36: Financial Regulation 217 requires Accounting Officers to notify the appropriate 
Sub-Accounting Officer and the Auditor-General of the establishment of any new revenue 
collecting office.
Explanation 37: The Stock Verification Unit is established to ensure the accuracy of stock 
records through physical verification.
Explanation 38: The disposal of unserviceable stores must follow the relevant financial 
regulations.
Explanation 39: The Accountant-General issues Treasury Circulars and Accounting Manuals to 
guide Accounting Officers and other employees.
Explanation 40: A General Warrant is an authority for incurring expenditure.
Explanation 41: The index mentions "Virement control on savings" under expenditure, indicating 
its role in expenditure control.
Explanation 42: The Accountant-General is responsible for the arrangement for opening of bank 
accounts for missions overseas.
Explanation 43: Financial Regulation 713 states that private money is to be kept separate.
Explanation 44: A strong room or safe is the best protection facility for these documents.
316
Explanation 45: Local Purchase Order Books and Receipt Booklet are to be kept for two years 
after the audit by the Auditor-General.
Explanation 46: Vouchers must be free from erasures or alterations.
Explanation 47: An Imprest is an advance for regularly recurrent services for which vouchers 
cannot be presented directly.
Explanation 48: The Imprest Cash Book is used to record imprest transactions.
Explanation 49: A Correspondence Advance is an advance for expenses related to 
correspondence courses.
Explanation 50: "Per diem" is a form of Touring Allowance.
Explanation 51: Financial Regulation 726 states that post-dated cheques are not to be 
accepted.
Explanation 52: Financial Regulation 730 outlines the specific treatment of dishonored cheques.
Explanation 53: The Vote Book is used to record vouchers and track expenditure against the 
budget.
Explanation 54: A Departmental Warrant is an authority for expenditure.
Explanation 55: Financial Regulation 97 requires all monies to be brought to account under the 
proper Heads and Sub-heads of the Estimates.
Explanation 56: Appendix 1, "Public Funds of the Federation (Disbursement) Rules," provides 
the rules for the disbursement of these funds.
Explanation 57: A General Imprest Warrant authorizes the issuance of imprest for "regularly 
recurrent service for which vouchers cannot be presented directly" to the Accountant-General.
Explanation 58: Chapter 31, Part V, is titled "Sanctions for making payments by using cheques 
and cash".
Explanation 59: A Board of Survey is convened to inspect unserviceable stores and make 
recommendations.
Explanation 60: Financial Regulation 1310 mentions the treatment of unclaimed deposits.
Explanation 61: A Station Diary is used to record daily activities and events at a government 
station.
Explanation 62: Financial Regulation 2108 states that the official store forms issued by the 
Accountant-General must be used without modification.
317
Explanation 63: Financial Regulation 2010 states that failure to keep the logbook up-to-date will 
result in the forfeiture of the relevant vehicle allowance for the period.
Explanation 64: Financial Regulation 502 states that the Accountant-General shall issue 
Treasury Circulars and Accounting Manuals from time to time.
Explanation 65: A Treasury Cash Book (Try 153A) is a key document for cash accounting.
Explanation 66: Imprest must be retired at the end of each financial year.
Explanation 67: The chapter is titled "Codification of offences and sanctions" and details 
irregularities and the penalties for them.
Explanation 68: A Board of Enquiry is established to determine responsibility for a loss of funds.
Explanation 69: Financial Regulation 1704 states that the annual audit program must be 
prepared for the approval of the Accounting Officer.
Explanation 70: Financial Regulation 1801 states that an Annual Board of Survey shall be held 
to physically inspect, count, and verify all cash, stamps, and other security documents held by 
Accounting Officers.
Explanation 71: Financial Regulation 2020 states that the driver may be surcharged the book 
value of the vehicle if it becomes unserviceable due to their negligence.
Explanation 72: The Paper Money Register is used to record the dates and numbers of receipts 
or licences issued.
Explanation 73: Appendix 11, "Treasury Form 50A," is an agreement form.
Explanation 74: Treasury Book 6A, the "Revenue Collectors' Receipt Book," is for use by 
Revenue Collectors.
Explanation 75: Advances, such as a Correspondence Advance, are provided for specific official 
purposes that will be accounted for.
Explanation 76: Financial Regulation 1312 mandates the monthly reconciliation of deposit 
accounts.
Explanation 77: Financial Regulation 1709(a) prohibits audit staff from handling cash, receipts, 
stamps, or stores.
Explanation 78: The management letter is used to communicate discoveries of negligent 
disregard of financial regulations or abuse of internal controls.
318
Explanation 79: The Accounting Officer is responsible for ensuring that all financial records are 
satisfactorily and faithfully kept.
Explanation 80: Financial Control Audit ensures that laid-down procedures are being observed 
in tendering, contracts, and store-keeping with a view to preventing waste, pilferage, and 
extravagance.
Explanation 81: A Value-for-Money (Performance) Audit is to ascertain the level of economy, 
efficiency, and effectiveness derived from government projects and programmes.
Explanation 82: Security documents and books shall be retained for specific periods after use, 
such as permanently, ten years, seven years, or two years, depending on the document.
Explanation 83: Financial Regulation 710 states that the overdrawing of bank accounts is not 
permitted.
Explanation 84: Bills of Exchange and Promissory Notes require the authorization of the 
Accou nta nt-G e n e ra I.
Explanation 85: Financial Regulation 2107(a) assigns the responsibility for the proper keeping of 
the Store Ledger and other stores records to the Storekeeper.
Explanation 86: The Revenue Collector's Chart enables the officer-in-charge to realize at once 
the situation regarding Revenue Collectors who account to him.
Explanation 87: Financial Regulation 724 mentions the collection of cheques drawn on banks 
outside Nigeria, implying there are specific procedures for them.
Explanation 88: Financial Regulation 1904(c) states that the commission shall maintain 
accounting records for the contributions and ensure proper periodic reconciliation of the 
accounts.
Explanation 89: Financial Regulation 1906 states that this code is used "for the recording of 
monthly pension contributions".
Explanation 90: The Accounting Officer has the responsibility to keep proper records of salaries 
and emoluments.
Explanation 91: The regulations on the custody of stamps imply they are for official use only.
Explanation 92: The Accountant-General may conduct surprise inspections of the accounts of 
an Accounting Officer.
Explanation 93: The General Imprest Warrant is an authorization to the Accountant-General to 
issue imprest to Permanent Secretaries and other senior officers.
319
Explanation 94: Financial Regulation 2011 requires the Head of Division or Unit to report vehicle 
accidents immediately to the Accounting Officer.
Explanation 95: Financial Regulation 1713 states that "Audit stamps are not transferable" and 
must not be used by anyone other than the officer to whom they were issued.
Explanation 96: Stores should only be issued against a properly completed and authorized 
Store Issue Voucher (SIV).
Explanation 97 : Financial Regulation 734 mentions that unauthorized commission charges are 
liable to surcharge.
Explanation 98: General Receipts (Treasury Book 6) shall be used by all Sub-Accounting 
Officers.
Explanation 99: The purpose of a stock verification unit is to provide an independent check on 
stores.
Explanation 100 : Financial Regulation 114(c) states that one of the duties of the Head of 
Finance and Accounts is to advise the Accounting Officer on financial regulations.
320
Section H
COMPUTER-BASED TEST (CBT) PROMOTION EXAMINATION FOR OFFICERS IN 
GRADES 15 TO 17 IN THE FEDERAL CIVIL SERVICE OF THE FEDERATION ON 
FINANCIAL REGULATION.
Instruction: This examination consists of 100 multiple-choice questions designed to assess your 
readiness for promotion to the next level in the Federal Civil Service. You have exactly one 
hour (60 minutes) to complete all questions; manage your time wisely to ensure you attempt 
every question. Read each question carefully before selecting the best answer from the options.

items.question: Who is the "Chief Accounting Officer of the Federation"?
options: The Permanent Secretary, Federal Ministry of Finance

items.question:

accounts of the Federal Government?

Office, as an Accounting Officer?
options: To ensure the regularity and propriety in the expenditure of public funds.

items.question: What must be provided for every sum of money paid to the government?

321

items.question:
Who is responsible for the overall financial control and management of public funds and 
assets of the Federation?

options: By handing cash to the bank teller.

options: To record all payments made by cheque.

Who is responsible for ensuring that all financial records of a Ministry are "satisfactorily and

options: They can be used for personal expenses.

Section H
items.explanation:
s:
Explanation 1 : The Accountant-General of the Federation is the Chief Accounting Officer of the 
Federation.
Explanation 2: The Accountant-General of the Federation has the responsibility for the overall 
supervision of the accounts of the Federal Government.
Explanation 3: As an Accounting Officer, their primary responsibility is to ensure the regularity 
and propriety in the expenditure of public funds.
Explanation 4: A notice must be prominently exhibited in every office where public money is 
received, stating that a numbered official receipt shall be obtained for every sum of money paid.
Explanation 5: A Revenue Collector's cash book is a record of all cash received by a Revenue 
Collector.
Explanation 6: The Minister of Finance has the full authority to direct on all matters relating to 
the finance and accounting affairs of the federation.
Explanation 7: All lodgements to bank accounts must be supported by a paying-in-form, which is 
a record of the transaction.
Explanation 8: Financial Regulation 210 states that government funds must not, under any 
circumstances, be kept at the private residence of an officer.
Explanation 9: A Treasury Cash Book is a key document for cash accounting and serves as the 
primary record of cash receipts and payments.
Explanation 10: A General Warrant is an authority for incurring expenditure issued by the 
Minister of Finance.
Explanation 11: The index mentions "Virement control on savings" under expenditure, indicating 
its role in expenditure control.
Explanation 12: The Accountant-General is responsible for the arrangement for opening of bank 
accounts for missions overseas, and by extension, for all government accounts.
Explanation 13: Financial Regulation 713 states that private money is to be kept separate from 
public funds.
Explanation 14: A strong room or safe is the best protection facility for these documents.
Explanation 15: Local Purchase Order Books and Receipt Booklet are to be kept for two years 
after the audit by the Auditor-General.
Explanation 16: Vouchers must be free from erasures or alterations.
337
Explanation 17: An Imprest is an advance for regularly recurrent services for which vouchers 
cannot be presented directly..
Explanation 18: The Imprest Cash Book is used to record imprest transactions.
Explanation 19: Financial Regulation 726 states that post-dated cheques are not to be 
accepted.
Explanation 20: Financial Regulation 730 outlines the specific treatment of dishonored cheques.
Explanation 21: The Vote Book is used to record vouchers and track expenditure against the 
budget.
Explanation 22: A Departmental Warrant is an authority for expenditure.
Explanation 23: Financial Regulation 97 requires all moneys to be brought to account under the 
proper Heads and Sub-heads of the Estimates.
Explanation 24: Appendix 1, "Public Funds of the Federation (Disbursement) Rules," provides 
the rules for the disbursement of these funds.
Explanation 25: A General Imprest Warrant authorizes the issuance of imprest for "regularly 
recurrent service for which vouchers cannot be presented direct" to the Accountant-General.
Explanation 26: Chapter 31, Part V, is titled "Sanctions for making payments by using cheques 
and cash".
Explanation 27: A Board of Survey is convened to inspect unserviceable stores and make 
recommendations.
Explanation 28: Financial Regulation 1310 mentions the treatment of unclaimed deposits.
Explanation 29: A Station Diary is used to record daily activities and events at a government 
station.
Explanation 30: Financial Regulation 2108 states that the official stores forms issued by the 
Accountant-General must be used without modification.
Explanation 31: Financial Regulation 2010 states that failure to keep the logbook up-to-date will 
result in the forfeiture of the relevant vehicle allowance for the period.
Explanation 32: Financial Regulation 502 states that the Accountant-General shall issue 
Treasury Circulars and Accounting Manuals from time to time.
Explanation 33: A Treasury Cash Book (Try 153A) is a key document for cash accounting.
Explanation 34: Imprest must be retired at the end of each financial year.
338
Explanation 35: The chapter is titled "Codification of offences and sanctions" and details 
irregularities and the penalties for them.
Explanation 36: A Board of Enquiry is established to determine responsibility for a loss of funds.
Explanation 37: Financial Regulation 1704 states that the annual audit program must be 
prepared for the approval of the Accounting Officer.
Explanation 38: Financial Regulation 1801 states that an Annual Board of Survey shall be held 
to physically inspect, count, and verify all cash, stamps, and other security documents held by 
Accounting Officers.
Explanation 39: Financial Regulation 2020 states that the driver may be surcharged the book 
value of the vehicle if it becomes unserviceable due to their negligence.
Explanation 40: The Paper Money Register is used to record the dates and numbers of receipts 
or licences issued.
Explanation 41: Appendix 11, "Treasury Form 50A," is an agreement form.
Explanation 42: Treasury Book 6A, the "Revenue Collectors' Receipt Book," is for use by 
Revenue Collectors.
Explanation 43: Advances, such as a Correspondence Advance, are provided for specific official 
purposes that will be accounted for.
Explanation 44: Financial Regulation 1312 mandates the monthly reconciliation of deposit 
accounts.
Explanation 45: Financial Regulation 1709(a) prohibits audit staff from handling cash, receipts, 
stamps, or stores.
Explanation 46: The management letter is used to communicate discoveries of negligent 
disregard of financial regulations or abuse of internal controls
Explanation 47: The Accounting Officer is responsible for ensuring that all financial records are 
satisfactorily and faithfully kept.
Explanation 48: Financial Control Audit ensures that laid-down procedures are being observed 
in tendering, contracts, and store-keeping with a view to preventing waste, pilferage, and 
extravagance.
Explanation 49: A Value-for-Money (Performance) Audit is to ascertain the level of economy, 
efficiency, and effectiveness derived from government projects and programmes.
339
Explanation 50: Security documents and books shall be retained for specific periods after use, 
such as permanently, ten years, seven years, or two years, depending on the document.
Explanation 51: Financial Regulation 710 states that the overdrawing of bank accounts is not 
permitted.
Explanation 52: Bills of Exchange and Promissory Notes require the authorization of the 
Accou nta nt-G e n e ra I.
Explanation 53: Financial Regulation 2107(a) assigns the responsibility for the proper keeping of 
the Store Ledger and other stores records to the Storekeeper.
Explanation 54: The Revenue Collector's Chart enables the officer-in-charge to realize at once 
the situation regarding Revenue Collectors who account to him.
Explanation 55: Financial Regulation 724 mentions the collection of cheques drawn on banks 
outside Nigeria, implying there are specific procedures for them.
Explanation 56: Financial Regulation 1904(c) states that the commission shall maintain 
accounting records for the contributions and ensure proper periodic reconciliation of the

Explanation 57: Financial Regulation 1906 states that this code is used "for the recording of 
monthly pension contributions".
Explanation 58: The Accounting Officer has the responsibility to keep proper records of salaries 
and emoluments.
Explanation 59: The regulations on the custody of stamps imply they are for official use only.
Explanation 60: The Accountant-General may conduct surprise inspections of the accounts of 
an Accounting Officer.
Explanation 61: The General Imprest Warrant is an authorization to the Accountant-General to 
issue imprest to Permanent Secretaries and other senior officers
Explanation 62: Financial Regulation 2011 requires the Head of Division or Unit to report vehicle 
accidents immediately to the Accounting Officer.
Explanation 63: Financial Regulation 1713 states that "Audit stamps are not transferable" and 
must not be used by anyone other than the officer to whom they were issued.
Explanation 64: Stores should only be issued against a properly completed and authorized 
Store Issue Voucher (SIV).
340
Explanation 65: Financial Regulation 734 mentions that unauthorized commission charges are 
liable to surcharge.
Explanation 66: General Receipts (Treasury Book 6) shall be used by all Sub-Accounting 
Officers.
Explanation 67: The purpose of a stock verification unit is to provide an independent check on 
stores.
Explanation 68: Financial Regulation 114(c) states that one of the duties of the Head of Finance 
and Accounts is to advise the Accounting Officer on financial regulations.
Explanation 69: Financial Regulation 1603(i) states that the Accounting Officer is responsible for 
compiling and defending the annual budget proposals under his supervision.
Explanation 70: Financial Regulation 1603(j) requires monthly and quarterly statements of 
budget performance to be produced by the Accounting Officer.
Explanation 71: Financial Regulation 1603(g) assigns the responsibility for adequate internal 
control over revenue collection to the Accounting Officer.
Explanation 72: Financial Regulation 1603(vi) states that the account totals of the receipts and 
payments for the month must be ensured by the Accounting Officer.
Explanation 73: Financial Regulation 1603(vii) requires the Accounting Officer to ensure the 
closing cash/bank balance is included.
Explanation 74: Financial Regulation 1603(h) mentions that specific directives from the Minister 
of Finance or the Accountant-General should be embraced in the detailed accounting 
instructions.
Explanation 75: The entire chapter emphasizes the Accounting Officer's responsibility to ensure 
strict observance of financial rules and regulations governing public funds.
Explanation 76: The term "Self Accounting Status" and the responsibilities outlined in Chapter 
16 indicate that these units manage their finances but are still bound by the Financial 
Regulations.
Explanation 77: The regulations emphasize strict observance of rules, proper accounting, and 
budget performance reporting, all pointing to transparency and accountability.
Explanation 78: The policy of internal audit and its duties, as described in Chapter 17, are 
centered on providing an independent appraisal of financial and other controls to the Accounting 
Officer.
341
Explanation 79: Financial Regulation 1603(g) specifically highlights an "adequate system of 
Internal control".
Explanation 80: Financial Regulation 1603(a) states the need for strict observance of existing 
financial rules and regulations governing the disbursement of public funds.
Explanation 81: Financial Regulation 1807 states that the Board of Survey shall prepare a report 
in triplicate, signed by all members.
Explanation 82: Financial Regulation 1712 states that Internal Auditors must account for and 
return their audit stamps to the Head of the Internal Audit Unit at the close of each day's duty.
Explanation 83: Financial Regulation 1703(e) requires the Internal Auditor to communicate such 
discoveries to the Accounting Officer in a "management letter."
Explanation 84: Financial Regulation 1907 indicates that the Director of Finance and Accounts 
receives "monthly personnel cost warrants from the Budget Office."
Explanation 85: The independent functioning, as per FR 1905, suggests a focus on specialized 
and accountable management of pension funds.
Explanation 86: Financial Regulation 1905 states that all Accounting Officers shall ensure that a 
Subsidiary Account Pension Unit is created.
Explanation 87: Financial Regulation 2026, while brief, implies adherence to the general 
regulations for the disposal of government assets.
Explanation 88: Financial Regulation 2018 explicitly lists "cost of repairs" as part of the quarterly 
returns.
Explanation 89: Financial Regulation 2017 specifies that a driver found negligent shall be 
surcharged for the cost of repairs.
Explanation 90: Financial Regulation 2005-2007 consistently emphasizes the use and 
maintenance of the logbook for tracking vehicle usage.
Explanation 91: Financial Regulation 1806 states that the business of the office or unit shall be 
suspended for the duration of the physical inspection.
Explanation 92: Financial Regulation 2023 requires the Accounting Officer to refer the matter to 
the Legal Department for advice and action.
Explanation 93: Financial Regulation 2132 states that no stores shall be issued except on the 
authority of a properly completed and authorized Store Issue Voucher (SIV) or other authorized 
stores requisition forms.
342
Explanation 94: A Store Receipt Voucher (SRV) is used to acknowledge the receipt of goods 
into a store.
Explanation 95: All entries in the store ledger are to be made in permanent ink to prevent 
unauthorized alterations.
Explanation 96: Financial Regulation 2107(a) assigns the responsibility for the proper keeping of 
the Store Ledger and other stores records to the Storekeeper.
Explanation 97: Financial Regulation 2108 states that the official stores forms issued by the 
Accountant-General must be used without modification.
Explanation 89: Financial Regulation 2014 mentions obtaining police reports for all accidents, 
implying immediate reporting to the police, especially in severe cases.
Explanation 99: Financial Regulation 1803 requires that the officer-in-charge of the cash, 
stamps, or other security documents must be present throughout the physical count and 
verification.
Explanation 100: Financial Regulation 2024 states that the Accounting Officer may suspend the 
driver's pay until the disciplinary action is finalized.
343
Chapter 4: Guide to Administrative Procedures in the Federal Public 
Service
This chapter delves into the practical day-to-day administrative procedures that keep the civil 
service running smoothly. It examines the official methods of communication, such as memos 
and official letters, and the procedures for handling personnel records and reports. Mastering 
this guide ensures that you can effectively manage administrative processes and maintain the 
high standards of bureaucratic efficiency expected of a director. The first edition of this book 
contains questions extracted from a paper on guide to administrative procedures in the federal 
public service presented by Dr. Goke Adegoroye, PhD, OON, and presented to the Bureau of 
Public Service Reform. Subsequent editions will contain direct questions from the Guide to 
administrative procedures in the federal public service written by the office of the Head of Civil 
service of the Federation. The questions in this section will test your knowledge of these 
essential administrative protocols.
Section A
COMPUTER-BASED TEST (CBT) PROMOTION EXAMINATION FOR OFFICERS IN 
GRADES 15 TO 17 IN THE FEDERAL CIVIL SERVICE OF THE FEDERATION ON 
GUIDE TO ADMINISTRATIVE PROCEDURES IN THE FEDERAL PUBLIC SERVICE
Instruction: This examination consists of 100 multiple-choice questions designed to assess your 
readiness for promotion to the next level in the Federal Civil Service. You have exactly one 
hour (60 minutes) to complete all questions; manage your time wisely to ensure you attempt 
every question. Read each question carefully before selecting the best answer from the options.

items.explanation:
Explanation 1: The paper states that friends and family of a newly appointed board member "are 
gleefully looking forward to favours of contracts, disbursements and/or public service 
appointments for their yet-to-be-employed graduate wards".
Explanation 2: The document specifies that for board members who are politicians, there is "the 
additional expectation that their appointment is a leverage in terms of where to raise funds to 
meet the demands of the party for re-election activities".
Explanation 3: The paper lists the four values as Democratic, Professional, Ethical, and People 
Values, which are the "basis of the expectations of Government and the public, on one hand, for 
public servants".
Explanation 4: The paper outlines the "Reciprocal Expectations of Public Servants" which 
include a good working environment, job deployment, career development/progression, and 
adequate remuneration.
Explanation: The document states that it is "inappropriate for Board members to see themselves 
in the mold of their colleagues occupying ministerial appointments and enjoying the privileges 
attached thereto".
Explanation 6: The paper, referencing a circular from the NSIWC, states that "the maximum 
number of sittings per year is pegged at four (4)".
Explanation 7: The paper mentions that remuneration is "anchored on a fixed sitting allowance 
depending on the category of the parastatal and agency".
Explanation 8: The paper concludes that their privilege "is in the intangible, being the honour 
that society would accord them in recognition of their respective personal integrity and the 
acknowledgement of their sacrifice".
Explanation 9: The document clarifies that in the Nigerian setting, "MDAs is not intended to 
mean Ministries, Departments and Agencies, BUT Ministries, extra-Ministerial Departments and 
Agencies.
Explanation 10: The paper defines the PSR as "the compendium of administrative rules guiding 
the entire public service of the federation".
Explanation 11: The Financial Regulations are described as "the second of twin documents 
(PSR & FR) that jointly serve as the principal instrument of administrative and financial controls 
in the public service".
Explanation 12: The document states that the "Public Service Rules (PSR) [are] issued by the 
Head of the Civil Service of the Federation".
Explanation 13: The paper states that the Bureau of Public Service Reforms (BPSR) "has 
already produced a revised and updated version" under this title.
Explanation 14: The document lists these four modes as the ways a meeting can be conducted.
Explanation 15: The document states that "the traditional mode entailing physical presence of
363
members remains the preferred standard for statutory purposes".
Explanation 16: Requisitioning a meeting "shall be the responsibility of the Board/Council 
chairman in consultation with the CEO".
Explanation 17: The document states that "the rule of the thumb especially where there are no 
such provisions is quarterly meetings as provided in the extant circular of the National Salaries, 
Incomes and Wages Commission (NSIWC)".
Explanation 18: The document identifies "the setting up of Standing Committees" as the 
standard arrangement for discharging responsibilities.
Explanation 19: One of the benefits listed is that committees "are smaller in size and are able to 
devote more time to the assignments on hand".
Explanation 20: The document lists the three typical committees as Human Resource, 
Administration and Legal; Audit, Risk and Internal Control; and Finance Management & 
Investment.
Explanation 21: The document lists several factors for an effective meeting, but the CEO's 
personality is not one of them.
Explanation 22: The paper states that "The Chairperson and the Secretary are to draw up the 
agenda of the meeting in consultation with the Chief Executive Officer and other Board/Council 
members".

Chapter 5: The Civil Service Handbook
The Civil Service Handbook is more than just a rulebook; it is a document that encapsulates the 
history, evolution, and core values of the Nigerian Civil Service. This chapter provides a 
historical context to the institution you serve and outlines the roles and responsibilities of the 
directorate cadre. It helps you understand your place within the broader civil service structure 
and the ethical standards you are expected to uphold. The questions are designed to test your 
knowledge of the service's history, values, and structure. This chapter consists of section A, B, 
C, D, E and F. Section A consists of 100 questions from chapter 1, 2, 3, and 4 of the book, 
Section B consists of 100 questions from chapter 4, 5, 6, and 7 of the book, Section C consists 
of 100 questions from chapter 7, 8, and 9, Section D consists of 100 questions from Chapters 9 
and 10, while Section E consists of 100 questions from Chapters 11, 12, Section F consists of 
100 questions from Chapters 13, 14, and 15 of the book, and Section G and H consists of 
Multiple Choice CBT objectives questions taken from Chapters 16, 17 and 18 of the book and 
contains from all the chapters of the book to assist student practice CBT exams questions.
Section A
Part 1: Civil Service Handbook: Government Business

items.question:
According to the 1999 Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria, what is the primary 
purpose of Government?
options: To maximize wealth for a few citizens.
To provide security and welfare to the people.
items.explanation:
The 1999 Constitution provides that "the security and welfare of the people shall 
be the primary purpose of Government".

items.question: How many arms of Government are there in Nigeria, according to the handbook?
items.explanation:
Government is made up of three arms: the Legislature, the Executive, and the 
Judiciary

items.question: Which arm of Government is responsible for law making, representation, and oversight?
items.explanation:
The Legislature is saddled with the responsibilities of law making, representation, 
and oversight.

items.question: What is the primary responsibility of the Executive arm of Government?
options: Law making and confirmation of appointments.
Day-to-day management of Government, including policy formulation and execution.
items.explanation:
The Executive is responsible for the day-to-day management of Government, and 
conceives, formulates, executes and monitors policies, programmes and projects.

items.question: Who heads the Federal Government in Nigeria's Presidential system?
options: The President, Commander-in-Chief of the Armed Forces

The Speaker of the House of Representatives
□
items.explanation:
Nigeria practices the Presidential system of Government under which the Federal 
Government is headed by the President, Commander-in-Chief of the Armed Forces

items.question: At the Federal level, who heads the Legislature?
options: The Speaker of the House of Representatives

The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation
□
items.explanation:
At the Federal level, the President of the Senate heads the Legislature,
