To provide a comprehensive resource of information to answer any questions from the 
National Assembly.
To be a list of all staff in the MDA.

To track the progress of ongoing projects.
□ .
items.explanation:
The "Fact Book" is a key document that contains all relevant information to 
address any queries from the National Assembly during the budget defence.
15 . Who is responsible for the career development of professional and technical staff below the 
senior management level?
A. The Federal Civil Service Commission.
B. The Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
C. The professional and line Ministries.
D. The National Council on Establishments.
□ Correct Answer:: C.
Explanation: The professional and line Ministries are responsible for the career development of 
their professional and technical staff below the senior management level.
Part 2:Code of Ethics and Conduct in the Civil Service

items.question: Traditionally, what was the general perception of the Civil Service in Nigeria?
options: It was seen as an inefficient and corrupt body.
It attracted the very best of talents and enjoyed high recognition and respect.
It was considered a less desirable career path compared to the private sector.

It was a place for political patronage and incompetence.
424
Q .
items.explanation:
Traditionally, the Civil Service used to attract the very best of talents and enjoy 
high recognition and respect from other groups in society.

items.question:
The decline in the image of the Civil Service started a couple of decades ago. What has the 
leadership of the Service done in response to this decline?
options: Nothing, the decline has been accepted as an inevitable trend.
Taken steps to reverse the trend and make the service more relevant to national aspirations.
Reduced the number of civil servants to save costs.

Outsourced all public services to the private sector.
q .
items.explanation:
The leadership of the Service has taken steps to reverse the trend, burnish its 
image, and make it more relevant to national aspirations.

items.question: What does the Code of Ethics aim to achieve in the Civil Service?
options: To provide guidelines for political appointments.
To continue to move the Civil Service in the right direction.

To reduce the number of government ministries.
Q .
items.explanation:
The handbook states that in order to continue to move the Civil Service in the 
right direction, the requisite Code of Ethics should be embraced by its members

items.question:
According to the handbook, what is the best way to treat a new Civil Servant joining a 
Ministry?
options: Ignore them and let them learn on their own.
Subject them to a probationary period without any support.
Receive them with kindness and courtesy, and assist them to settle down.

Give them the most difficult tasks to test their resolve.
□ .
items.explanation:
New Officers joining a Ministry should be received with kindness and courtesy 
and should be assisted in every way to settle down.

items.question: A Civil Servant is advised to show loyalty to whom?
options: The Head of the Civil Service.
items.explanation: The Civil Servant owes his loyalty to the government of the day.

items.question: What is considered an act of disloyalty for a Civil Servant?
options: Giving constructive criticism to a superior in private.
Refusing to carry out an unlawful order.
items.explanation:
Public criticism of government policies and acts is an act of disloyalty on the part 
of a Civil Servant.

items.question: While Civil Servants are free to vote in elections, what are they forbidden from doing?
items.explanation:
A Civil Servant is expected to be neutral and is forbidden from engaging in 
partisan political activities.

items.question:
What should a Civil Servant who has served in a particular Ministry for a very long time 
avoid doing?
options: Assuming that their experience is the only thing that matters.
items.explanation:
Long-serving Officers in a particular Ministry should not become so set in their 
own ways as to assume that their experience is the only thing that matters.

items.question: The principle of "impartiality" in the Civil Service means that a Civil Servant should do what?
options: Serve only the ruling political party.
Show favoritism to people from their ethnic group.
Serve the public interest without fear or favour.
items.explanation:
Impartiality means that a Civil Servant must, in the discharge of their duties, serve 
the public interest without fear or favor.

items.question:
What is the purpose of the "Principle of Impartiality" as it relates to members of the public?
426
options: To allow civil servants to treat citizens differently based on their political views.
To ensure that everyone is treated equally and fairly, regardless of their background.
To prioritize government officials over ordinary citizens.

To allow civil servants to use their discretion to make exceptions.
□ .
items.explanation:
The principle of impartiality ensures that a Civil Servant treats everyone who 
comes to them in the course of their duty equally and fairly, irrespective of who they are.

items.question: When a Civil Servant receives an unlawful order, what should they do?
options: Report the order to the press.
Refuse to carry out the order, and if necessary, inform the appropriate authority.
Carry out the order but secretly document it for future reference.a .
items.explanation:
A Civil Servant should refuse to carry out an unlawful order, and if necessary, 
should inform the appropriate authority.

items.question: What is the danger of a Civil Servant accepting gifts from contractors and business people?
options: It will create a good relationship.
It could compromise the integrity of the Civil Servant.
It is an act of kindness.

It is a normal part of business.
□ .
items.explanation:
The Civil Servant should remember that the acceptance of gifts from contractors 
and business people could compromise his position and his integrity

items.question:
The handbook advises against using official working time for which of the following 
activities?
items.explanation:
The Civil Servant is expected to give his best in the execution of his official duties, 
and should not use official working time for personal errands or private business

items.question: What does the principle of "diligence" require from a Civil Servant?
options: To only work during official hours.

To be a hardworking and conscientious officer who gives his best to the job.
427
To delegate all tasks to subordinates.Q .
items.explanation:
Every Civil Servant is expected to be a hardworking and conscientious officer who 
gives his best to the job

items.question: What is the consequence of lack of diligence in the Civil Service?
options: It may lead to a promotion.
It will lead to government achieving its objectives faster.
It will lead to inefficiency, loss of productivity and waste.
It has no effect on the government or the service.a .
items.explanation:
A lack of diligence will lead to inefficiency, loss of productivity and waste of public 
funds.

items.question: What is a key purpose of the "Official Secret Act"?
options: To encourage civil servants to share all government information.
To prevent the leaking of government secrets.
To protect civil servants from prosecution.
To allow for the free flow of information to the public.a .
items.explanation:
The Official Secret Act is to prevent the leaking of government secrets to 
unauthorized persons.

items.question:
The handbook states that every Civil Servant should avoid making a living from "touting". 
What does "touting" refer to in this context?
options: Giving out official information to the public.
Earning a living from official matters, like processing files for people.

Working a second job outside of the Civil Service.
E3 .
items.explanation:
The practice of making a living from "touting" for official matters, by charging 
people to process their files, is a disgraceful and corrupt act.

items.question: What should be the primary consideration for a Civil Servant when spending public funds?
options: To spend as much as possible.
To be cost-conscious and avoid extravagance.
To prioritize personal needs over official ones.

To spend the money on things that are not relevant to the job.
□ .
428
items.explanation:
All Civil Servants should be cost-conscious in spending public funds on behalf of 
the Government, and extravagance can be checked in many ways

items.question: How should an officer justify their employment to the government?
options: By getting paid a high salary.
By giving efficient service in return fortheir earning.

By being on time to work every day.
□ .
items.explanation:
Every Officer or employee should justify his employment by giving efficient service 
in return for his earnings.

items.question: How is an officer's expenditure considered "official"?
options: If it is done at the end of the day.
If it is incurred with the authority of the Head of the Department or the Accounting Officer.
If it is less than a certain amount.

If it is done without supervision.
□ .
items.explanation:
An expenditure can be regarded as official only if it is incurred with the authority of 
the Head of the Department or the Accounting Officer

items.question:
The handbook advises against wasting public funds. Which of the following is a way to 
ensure this?
options: Making ill-advised purchases near the end of the financial year.
Justifying every expenditure and ensuring the economy is always maintained.
items.explanation:
Civil Servants should justify every expenditure and ensure that economy is always 
maintained.

items.question: What should a Civil Servant do if they detect financial abuses?
options: Ignore them, as it is not their responsibility.
Report them through the correct channel.
Report them to a friend or colleague.

Wait for a superior officer to detect it.
Q .
items.explanation:
As a good citizen and a good civil servant, one should discourage financial 
abuses by making suitable reports through the correct channel.

items.question:
What is the rationale for preferring Made-in-Nigeria goods over imported ones?
429
options: It is a requirement for all government purchases.
It encourages the growth of local industries and employment.
items.explanation:
Patronage of local goods encourages the growth of local industries and 
employment

items.question:
The principle of "National Consciousness" means that Civil Servants should prioritize what 
interest?
options: The interest of their own ethnic group.
The interest of their town or clan.
The interest of their political party.
items.explanation:
The interest of any group should not be pursued at the expense of what is best for 
Nigeria

items.question:
What can result from indifference to or disguised encouragement of nepotism and 
favouritism?
items.explanation:
Sometimes, civil unrest may arise from indifference to or disguised 
encouragement of nepotism, favouritism and their associated vices.

items.question:
The handbook emphasizes the importance of a good image for the Civil Service. What is the 
role of top-level civil servants in achieving this?
options: To hide any mistakes made by the government.
To provide honest and courageous leadership.
To avoid interacting with the public.

To blame subordinates for failures.
Q .
items.explanation:
In achieving a good image of the Civil Service, Nigerians, particularly those at the 
top, should continue to provide honest and courageous leadership.

items.question: What is the purpose of the Oath of Secrecy that some Civil Servants are required to swear?
options: To hide personal assets from the public.

To protect government secrets and classified information.
430
To prevent them from talking to the media.
To ensure they are loyal to a political party.Q
items.explanation:
Officers who have access to classified information should, as soon as possible, 
swear to the Oath of Secrecy

items.question: What is the primary role of the Civil Service in a democracy?
options: To provide continuity through reference to the decisions of past governments.
To act as an opposition party.

To manage the country's economy.
Q .
items.explanation:
The duty of the Civil Service to provide continuity, mainly through reference to the 
decisions of past governments, can as such be better appreciated when we visualize what 
would have been the case were we not to have the Service to perform this most critical role of 
preservation of official records.

items.question:
The continued relevance of the Civil Service is very much a function of how well it manages 
what?
options: Its relationship with the political leadership.
items.explanation:
It can be safely asserted that the continued relevance of the Civil Service in the 
scheme of things, is very much a function of how well it manages these records and materials.

items.question:
What is the implication of failing to take reasonable care of documents entrusted to a Civil 
Servant?
options: It is a normal part of the job.Q .
items.explanation:
It is an offence for a Civil Servant to fail to take reasonable care of any documents 
entrusted to his care.

items.question: What is the name of the registry where non-classified documents are kept?
items.explanation:
There are two types of Registry in the Civil Service: the Open Registry where 
non-classified documents and correspondences are kept and processed and the 
Secret/Confidential Registry where classified documents are handled.

items.question: Who is required to swear to the Oath of Secrecy?
options: Those who have access to classified information.
items.explanation:
Officers who have access to classified information should, as soon as possible, 
swear to the Oath of Secrecy.

items.question: What is the importance of having "Absent File cards" and "card slips"?
options: To track the movement of files.
To keep a record of all staff.
items.explanation:
The correct use of Absent File cards and card slips is essential for tracking file 
movements

items.question: What is a key role of the supervisor in a registry?
options: To do all the work themselves.
To enforce discipline with justice and fairness at all times.
To allow staff to do as they please.

To prevent any staff from leaving the office.
□ .
items.explanation: The supervisor should enforce discipline with justice and fairness at all times.

items.question: The handbook advises Civil Servants to avoid what when dealing with the public?
options: Being helpful, but still being impartial.

Asking for gifts or favors.
Q .
432
items.explanation:
It is a disgrace for a Civil Servant to tout for official matters and demand money or 
gifts from members of the public.

items.question: What is the purpose of classifying documents in the Civil Service?
options: To make them difficult to access.
To prevent new staff from seeing them.
To sensitize officers about the degree of care required for each document.

To hide their contents from other Ministries.
□ .
items.explanation:
Classified documents are duly classified so as to sensitize officers about the 
degree of care required for each.

items.question: In a Secret Registry, what is typically kept?
items.explanation:
Where the registry is split into two, only highly classified subject files are kept in 
the Secret Registry whilst the Confidential Registry keeps the confidential personal files of the 
staff.

items.question:
What is the potential risk of a document being destroyed, especially with older methods of 
record management?
options: It can never be recalled and has to be recreated.
It will automatically reappear in a "Recycle Bin."

It can be recalled with a simple command.
Q .
items.explanation:
Pre-computer, unwanted records must be physically destroyed either in a paper 
shredding machine or burnt, and once a document is destroyed, it can never be recalled. It can 
only be recreated again.

items.question: According to the handbook, what is a key asset for a schedule officer?
options: Accurate information based on facts and figures.
items.explanation:
An important asset to most scheduled officers is accurate information based on
433
facts and figures.

items.question: What is the danger of using obsolete and incorrect information?
options: It can lead to a promotion.
It can help an officer make sound decisions.
It can undermine an officer's maturity and sound judgment.
It has no effect on the decision-making process.a
items.explanation:
Obsolete and incorrect information do not help officers to make sound decisions 
and can easily undermine factors such as maturity and sound judgment.

items.question: When a driver detects a minor defect in a government vehicle, what should they do?
options: Wait until it becomes a major problem.
Report it immediately to the officer-in-charge of transport.

Ignore it, as it is a minor defect.
□ .
items.explanation:
Drivers should report immediately to the officer-in-charge of transport whenever 
defects (even of a minor nature) are detected.

items.question: The hours of work in Federal offices are from what time to what time?
items.explanation:
The hours of work in Federal offices are from 8.00 a.m. to 4.00 p.m. on Monday to 
Friday, except as may be officially varied

items.question: Who is required to sign the Attendance Register?
options: All staff, regardless of grade level.

Only senior management.
□ Correct Answer:: A.
items.explanation:
All staff on GL. 03-13 are required to sign the Attendance Register on arrival at 
the Office in the morning as well as at the close of work at the end of the day.

items.question:
How does the Attendance Register distinguish between staff who arrive on time and those 
who are late?
options:
Latecomers have to sign on a different sheet.
434
A red line is drawn across the register, and latecomers sign below it.
There is no distinction; everyone signs in the same place.

A supervisor makes a note next to the latecomer's name.
Q .
items.explanation:
Not later than 8.00 a.m., a red line should be drawn across the register each day 
so that staff who come late have to sign below the red line

items.question: Who should be required to explain why they are late to work?
options: All staff who signed below the red line.
items.explanation:
Late-comers should be required to explain why they are late. This applies to all 
staff who signed below the red line.

items.question: Why is it important for a Civil Servant to be helpful to others?
options:
To discourage the unfortunate tendency of people seeking help only through personal 
connections.

To create a good personal image.
□ .
items.explanation:
The unfortunate tendency of people only getting help from those they know 
should be discouraged by all Civil Servants.

items.question: What is a key component of being a "public servant" in terms of kindness?
options: To give people what they are not entitled to.
To respect the privileges and rights of officers, employees, and members of the public.
To be strict with everyone to show authority.

To only be kind to those you know personally.
□ Correct Answer:: B.
items.explanation:
Kindness involves respecting the privileges and rights of officers, employees and 
members of the public, irrespective of the fact that those concerned are unknown to you. A Civil 
Servant is a public servant, and should therefore serve impartially.

items.question:
According to the handbook, what is the proper action if papers or documents come to you by 
mistake from members of staff or the general public?
options:
Find out the correct destination and take necessary directive as to re-routing it from your 
superior officers.
Take them to the wrong office.Q .
items.explanation:
Should papers or documents come to you by mistake from members of staff or 
the general public, you should find out the correct destination and take necessary directive as to 
re-routing it from your superior officers.

items.question: What is the purpose of the Code of Conduct Bureau?
options: To manage the finances of the government.
To receive a written declaration of assets and liabilities from public officers.
To set the salary structure for the Civil Service.a .
items.explanation:
Every public officer shall submit to the Code of Conduct Bureau a written 
declaration of all his properties, assets and liabilities and those of his spouse, or unmarried 
children under the age of 18 years.

items.question: A public officer must submit a declaration of assets and liabilities at what frequency?
options: At the beginning and end of their term of office and at the end of every four years.
Only at the beginning of their term.
items.explanation:
Every public officer shall, within 3 months after the coming into force of this Code 
of Conduct or immediately after taking office and thereafter at the end of every four years and at 
the end of his term of office, submit a written declaration of all his properties, assets, and 
liabilities to the Code of Conduct Bureau.

items.question: What is the consequence of a false statement in a public officer's declaration of assets?
options: Nothing, as it is a minor error.
It shall be deemed to be a breach of the Code of Conduct.
They will be given a warning.
They will be fined a small amount.a .
items.explanation:
Any statement in such declaration that is found to be false... shall be deemed to 
be a breach of this Code.

items.question:
What is the implication if a public officer acquires property or assets that were not declared 
in their initial declaration?
436
options: It is considered a normal acquisition.

It shall be deemed to have been acquired in breach of this Code unless the contrary is 
proved.
It must be declared in a supplementary declaration.
It is only a problem if the value is very high.a .
items.explanation:
Any property or assets acquired by a public officer, any declaration required by 
sub-paragraph (1) (a) of this gift, or loan approved by the Code shall be deemed to have been 
acquired in breach of this Code unless the contrary is proved.

items.question:
Who is responsible for receiving allegations that a public officer has committed a breach of 
the Code of Conduct?
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
items.explanation:
Any allegation that a public officer has committed a breach of or has not complied 
with the provisions of this Code shall be made to the Code of Conduct Bureau.

items.question:
A public officer who does an act prohibited by the Code through a nominee, trustee, or other 
agent is deemed to have done what?
options: Nothing, as they did not do it personally.
To have committed a breach of this Code.
items.explanation:
A public officer who does any act prohibited by this Code through a nominee, 
trustee, or other agent shall be deemed ipso facto to have committed a breach of this Code

items.question: What is the correct way to address a minute or a file submission through official channels?
options:
The most senior officer's title first, followed by other officers in descending order of status 
prefixed by the word "through."

The most junior officer's title first, followed by other officers in descending order of status 
prefixed by the word "through."
The Permanent Secretary's title first, then everyone else.
There is no specific format; any order is acceptable.a .
items.explanation:
The style of address in general use for the submission of minutes through official 
channels is by placing the most senior officer's title first followed by other officers in descending
437
order of status prefixed by the word "through".

items.question: When papers are routed back through official channels, what is the purpose?
options: To get a second opinion from junior staff.

To allow all intermediate officers to be acquainted with the decisions reached by the higher 
authority.
To give more time for junior officers to review the documents.

To give an opportunity for the papers to be delayed.
□ .
items.explanation:
Papers routed upwards through official channels are routed back in the same 
way, and all intermediate officers are, therefore, acquainted with decisions reached by the 
higher authority.

items.question: According to the handbook, what does "unprofessional" behavior imply in a Civil Servant?
options: Treating members of the public with respect.
Exhibiting rudeness, discourtesy, insensitivity, and inefficiency.
items.explanation:
The handbook aims to improve the standards of performance of Civil Servants 
and remove the ugly image of unprofessionalism which manifests itself in rudeness, discourtesy, 
insensitivity, and inefficiency.

items.question:
The handbook lists a number of attributes that Civil Servants should possess to ensure 
quality service delivery. Which of the following is NOT one of them?
items.explanation:
The handbook lists a number of attributes for Civil Servants to ensure quality 
service delivery, including being punctual, polite, cheerful, efficient, and loyal. Accepting bribes 
is a corrupt act that is discouraged..

items.question: Who, in the end, pays the salaries of Civil Servants?
items.explanation:
Civil Servants must strive to offer quality service without let or hindrance at all 
times to the Nigerian tax payers who are, afterall, the ones who pay their salaries.

items.question: What is the proper attitude for a Civil Servant towards the Nigerian tax payers?
options: To be indifferent to their needs.
To offer quality service without let or hindrance at all times.

To charge them for every service provided.
□ .
items.explanation:
Any other attitude apart from offering quality service without let or hindrance at all 
times is unacceptable, unpatriotic and should not be tolerated in the reforming Civil Service.
Service Delivery

items.question: According to the Civil Service Handbook, what is a primary element in good governance?
items.explanation:
The handbook states that service delivery is a primary element in good 
governance, and efficient and effective delivery of good service is the raison d'etre of 
government.

items.question: The Charter for the Public Service in Africa was adopted at a conference in which city?
items.explanation:
The Charter for the Public Service in Africa was adopted by the Third Biennial 
Pan-African Conference of Ministers of Civil Service in Windhoek, Namibia.

items.question: What is the purpose of the Charter for the Public Service in Africa?
options: To create a new political structure for African nations.
To standardize salaries for civil servants across Africa.
To streamline services provided to citizens and ensure the efficient use of resources.
To promote a single currency for all African countries.3 .
items.explanation:
The Charter expresses the will and commitment of African States to streamline
439
the services provided to citizens and the desire to ensure the efficient use of the resources 
allocated for such services.

items.question:
The Principle of Equality of Treatment states that persons in a comparable situation vis-à-vis 
the administration shall be treated:
items.explanation:
The Principle of equality of treatment states that persons in a comparable 
situation vis-à-vis the administration shall be treated equally without any distinctions 
whatsoever.

items.question:
Which of the following is NOT a prohibited distinction under the Principle of Equality of 
Treatment?
items.explanation:
The principle prohibits any discrimination based on the place of origin, race, 
gender, religion, ethnic group, philosophical or political convictions or other personal 
considerations.

items.question:
The Principle of Neutrality means the public service as a whole remains neutral with respect 
to whom?
items.explanation:
The public service as a whole remains neutral in respect to the government of the 
day, and this fundamental principle will be respected by all administrations.

items.question: What does the Principle of Legality require?
options: Public service shall be provided in strict compliance with the law.
Public service shall be provided continuously.
Public service shall be accessible to all.
items.explanation:
The Principle of legality states that public service shall be provided in strict 
compliance with the law, and administrative decisions shall be taken in conformity with existing 
regulations.

items.question:
The Principle of Continuity states that public service shall be provided in accordance with 
what?
options: The demands of the public.a Correct Answer:: B.
items.explanation:
Public service shall be provided on an ongoing basis and in all its component 
parts, in accordance with the rules governing its operation.

items.question: How can physical proximity and accessibility of services be achieved?
options:
By the application of appropriate information and communication technologies 
(E-Governance)IO.

By reducing the number of services offered.
□ .
items.explanation:
The handbook states that physical proximity and accessibility can be achieved by 
the application of appropriate information and communication technologies (E-Governance).

items.question:
What is the responsibility of the administration regarding participation, consultation, and 
mediation?
options: To ensure that mechanisms of participation and consultation are effectively put in place.
To prevent civil society from participating.
To only consult with government officials.a .
items.explanation:
It is the responsibility of the administration to ensure that the mechanisms of 
participation and consultation involving civil society and other stakeholders are effectively put in 
place through consultative forums or advisory bodies.

items.question:
The public service is responsible for putting in place a system of mediation through an 
institution that has sufficient moral and social authority to allow for what?
options:
Means of redress other than administrative and/or legal remedies14.
441
items.explanation:
The public service is responsible for putting in place a system of mediation 
through an institution that has sufficient moral and social authority that enables users as well as 
public service employees themselves to have means of redress other than administrative and/or 
legal remedies.

items.question:
The Principle of Quality, Effectiveness, and Efficiency requires the public service to make 
optimal use of what?
items.explanation:
The public service shall ensure that the highest quality and the most effective and 
efficient services are provided by making optimal use of the resources at its disposal.

items.question: What should adjustments to the way services are organized and operated lead to?
items.explanation:
Adjustments to the way in which services are organized and operated must lead 
to better service delivery and must be informed by best practices in the application of the 
information and communication technologies.

items.question: How should the evaluation of services be based?
options:
On objectives and programmes of activities defined beforehand, accompanied by 
performance indicators and criteria18.
On the number of complaints received.
On the amount of money spent.a .
items.explanation:
The evaluation of the effectiveness and productivity of services shall be based on 
objectives and programmes of activities defined beforehand, accompanied by performance 
indicators and criteria.

items.question: What should administrative units make available to the public?
options:
Only information that is requested in person.
442
All necessary information on acts and procedures in their respective domains20.
Only the information that has already been published in a newspaper.

No information, as it is all classified.
Q .
items.explanation:
All administrative units shall make available all the necessary information on acts 
and procedures in their respective domains, as well as the information required to assess their 
management, with a view to enabling those interested to have full access.

items.question: What must the administration do when it takes a decision concerning a person?
options:
Inform the person concerned, indicating the reasons for such decisions and stating legal 
remedies where necessary.
Announce the decision to the public at large.a .
items.explanation:
The administration shall inform the person concerned of any decision taken 
concerning him/her, indicating the reasons for such decisions and stating, where necessary, the 
legal remedies open to him/her, should he/she decide to challenge the decision.

items.question:
The public service must establish or strengthen reception and information units for users in 
order to assist them in what?
options: Gaining access to services and in recording their views, suggestions or complaints24.

items.question:
The Principle of Speed and Responsiveness states that the public service shall determine 
and respect what?
options: The number of hours in a workday.
Deadlines in the delivery of its services.
items.explanation:
The public service shall determine and respect deadlines in the delivery of its 
services.
443

items.question: How should the deadlines for service delivery be determined?
options: By the whims of the head of department.
In a realistic manner and must be communicated to users27.
By the political party in power.a .
items.explanation:
Such deadlines shall be determined in a realistic manner, and must be 
communicated to users of public services.

items.question:
The Principle of Accountability states that public officers shall be personally accountable for 
what?
options: Their individual actions or omissions in the performance of their duties.

The actions of the government as a whole.
□ .
items.explanation:
The Principle of Accountability states that public officers shall be personally 
accountable for their individual actions or omissions in the performance of their duties, and shall 
respond, where necessary, to their superiors.

items.question: What is the purpose of setting performance targets for individual public officers?
options: To make the work more difficult.

To facilitate the objective and fair evaluation of the quality and quantity of the service 
rendered.
To increase the workload of the officer.a Correct Answer:: B.
items.explanation:
Clear and verifiable performance targets must be set for individual public officers 
so as to facilitate the objective and fair evaluation of the quality and quantity of the service 
rendered.

items.question:
The Principle of Motivation states that public service staff must be motivated to achieve 
what?
options: The political goals of the government.a .
items.explanation:
The principle of Motivation states that public service staff must be motivated and 
encouraged to achieve the objectives of the organization.
444

items.question: A key part of the Principle of Motivation is that employees must be provided with what?
options: A pleasant and favorable working environment35.
An opportunity to make their own rules.
A chance to work from home exclusively.a .
items.explanation:
The administration shall ensure that employees are provided with a pleasant and 
favorable working environment so that they can realize their full potential.

items.question: The Principle of Integrity states that public officers must be known for what?
options: Their ability to make a lot of money.
Their high standards of morality and integrity37.
items.explanation:
The Principle of Integrity states that public officers must be known for their high 
standards of morality and integrity in the conduct of official business.

items.question: What is prohibited under the Principle of Integrity?
options: Nepotism, corruption, influence peddling, and illicit enrichment39.
items.explanation:
It prohibits the use of public office for personal gain, nepotism, corruption, 
influence peddling and illicit enrichment.
Section D
Part 1: Service Delivery

items.question:
The Principles in the Civil Service for discharging its functions are accountability, 
responsiveness, courtesy, objectivity, impartiality and upholding what?
items.explanation:
The principles in the discharge of these laudable functions are accountability, 
responsiveness, courtesy, objectivity, impartiality and upholding the public trust at all levels.

items.question:
According to the handbook, the Civil Service should discharge its catalytic role of facilitating 
what?
options:
Successful conception, planning, execution and monitoring of the policies, projects and 
programmes of Government.

The affairs of private citizens.
a .
items.explanation:
The Civil Service should discharge its catalytic role of facilitating successful 
conception, planning, execution and monitoring of the policies, projects and programmes of 
Government.

items.question: What is the purpose of preparing a "Fact Book" for the budget defence?
options: To serve as a personal diary for the Minister.

To provide a comprehensive resource of information to answer any questions from the 
National Assembly.
To be a list of all staff in the MDA.

To track the progress of ongoing projects.
□ .
items.explanation:
The "Fact Book" is a key document that contains all relevant information to 
address any queries from the National Assembly during the budget defence. This question is 
from a previous chapter but is included as it is a common question within the quiz context.

items.question: What is the role of the Permanent Secretary in a Ministry?
options: The Chief Advisor to the Minister and the Ministry's Accounting Officer.

The Head of the Civil Service.
□ . .
items.explanation:
The Permanent Secretary is the Minister's Chief Adviser and is also the Ministry's 
Accounting Office

items.question: What does "E-Governance" refer to in the context of service delivery?
options: The application of appropriate information and communication technologies.

A method for organizing government meetings.
Q .
items.explanation:
The handbook mentions that physical proximity and accessibility of services can 
be achieved by the application of appropriate information and communication technologies 
(E-Governance.

items.question:
The continued relevance of the Civil Service is very much a function of how well it manages 
what?
options: Its relationship with the political leadership.
items.explanation:
The continued relevance of the Civil Service in the scheme of things is very much 
a function of how well it manages these records and materials.

items.question: A key principle of service delivery is to be responsive. What does this mean?
options: Only serving those who are powerful.
Serving all stakeholders within a reasonable timeframe.
Only serving those who ask for help.

Delaying services as much as possible.
□ .
items.explanation:
The principle of Responsiveness states that institutions and processes try to 
serve all stakeholders within a reasonable timeframe.

items.question:
Which of the following is NOT one of the fundamental and universal principles for the 
provision of public services listed in the chapter?
items.explanation:
The principles listed include equality of treatment, neutrality, legality, continuity, 
proximity and accessibility of services, participation, quality, evaluation, transparency, speed and 
responsiveness, accountability, motivation, and integrity. Nepotism is a prohibited act under the 
Principle of Integrity.

items.question:
What is the purpose of performance indicators and criteria in the evaluation of services?
447
options: To make the evaluation more complex.
To provide a basis for an objective and fair evaluation50.
items.explanation:
The evaluation of the effectiveness and productivity of services shall be based on 
objectives and programmes of activities defined beforehand, accompanied by performance 
indicators and criteria.

items.question: What is the role of a "Fact Book" during the budget defense process?
options: To list all of the staff in a department.
To provide comprehensive information to address queries from the National Assembly.
To document all a Ministry's expenses for the year.
To serve as a personal diary for the Minister.3 .
items.explanation:
The Fact Book is a key document that contains all relevant information to address 
any queries from the National Assembly during the budget defence. This question is from a 
previous chapter but is included as it is a common question within the quiz context.
Pat 2: Records Management in the Civil Service

items.question: What is the primary purpose of records in the Civil Service?
options: To create a historical archive of government actions.
To provide a basis for continuity and reference to the decisions of past governments.
To document the personal lives of civil servants.
To generate a paper trail for all financial transactions.3 .
items.explanation:
Records are kept to provide continuity through reference to the decisions of past 
governments. The Civil Service's continued relevance is a function of how well it manages these 
records.

items.question: What is the main danger of an organization's records not being kept properly?
options: Civil servants will not be paid on time.
It could lead to the loss of government revenue.
It could lead to policy reversals and conflicts.

The public will lose trust in the government.
Q .
items.explanation:
If records are not kept properly, it could lead to policy reversals, conflicts within 
and between agencies, and chaos in the public sector.
448

items.question:
Who, according to the document, is responsible for the proper keeping of records in the Civil 
Service?
options: Only the staff of the Records/Registry Unit.
All Civil Servants, regardless of their position.

Only the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
□ .
items.explanation:
The proper keeping of records is the responsibility of every Civil Servant, 
irrespective of their position.

items.question:
What is the term for a document created in response to an instruction or a matter referred to 
an officer?
items.explanation:
A minute is a note or document that is created in response to an instruction or a 
matter referred to an officer for his attention, advice, or action.

items.question: A minute should contain a summary of the facts of the case, as well as what?
options: A list of all staff involved.
items.explanation:
A minute should contain a summary of the facts of the case, the officer's 
recommendations, and his conclusions.

items.question: Which of the following is a key characteristic of a good minute?
options: It should be verbose and include all personal thoughts.
It should be written in a conversational tone.
It should be brief, factual, and free from grammatical errors.
It should only be written by senior officers.a .
items.explanation:
A good minute should be brief, factual, well-reasoned, and free from grammatical 
errors, spelling mistakes, and ambiguity.

items.question: When writing a minute, what should an officer reference to support their position?
items.explanation:
When writing a minute, an officer should support their position by referring to 
previous government decisions, rules, or regulations on the matter.

items.question: What is the correct way to address a minute or a file submission through official channels?
options: The most junior officer's title first, followed by other officers in descending order of status.

The most senior officer's title first, followed by other officers in descending order of status 
prefixed by the word "through."
The Permanent Secretary's title first, then everyone else.
There is no specific format; any order is acceptable.a .
items.explanation:
The style of address in general use for the submission of minutes through official 
channels is by placing the most senior officer's title first followed by other officers in descending 
order of status prefixed by the word "through".

items.question:
What is the purpose of routing papers back through official channels after a decision has 
been reached by a higher authority?
options: To delay the implementation of the decision.
To allow all intermediate officers to be acquainted with the decisions reached.
To give junior officers more work to do.

To provide an opportunity for the decision to be reversed.
□ .
items.explanation:
Papers routed upwards through official channels are routed back in the same 
way, and all intermediate officers are, therefore, acquainted with decisions reached by the 
higher authority.

items.question: What is the proper procedure for a Civil Servant who has been transferred to a new office?
options: They should take all the files with them to their new office.
They should submit a formal handing-over note.
They should leave immediately without any handover.

They should ask their new supervisor to get the files.
□ .
items.explanation:
An officer being transferred to a new office must prepare a formal handing-over 
note to their successor.

items.question: Who should receive a copy of a handing-over note?
options: The Head of Department, the Personnel Officer, and the officer's successor.
items.explanation:
A copy of the handing-over note is to be sent to the Head of Department, the 
Personnel Officer, and the officer's successor.

items.question: What is the purpose of an "Absent File card" or a "card slip"?
options: To track the movement of a file.
items.explanation:
The correct use of Absent File cards and card slips is essential for tracking file 
movements, and ensuring that files are not misplaced.

items.question: What should a Civil Servant do with a file when they are finished with it?
options: Keep it in their desk for future reference.
Put it on a "bring-up" pad if action is required at a later date.
Throw it away if it is not needed anymore.
Pass it to a colleague without informing them.a .
items.explanation:
When an officer has finished with a file, he should ensure that it is returned to the 
registry. If a file is required at a later date, it should be put on a "bring-up" pad.

items.question:
What is the term for a set of official correspondence and documents on a particular subject 
matter?
items.explanation:
A file is a collection of official correspondence and other documents on a 
particular subject matter.

items.question: What is the purpose of the "File Title"?
options: To make the file look nice.
To accurately reflect the subject matter contained in the file.
To indicate who created the file.

To indicate the date the file was created.
451
Q .
items.explanation:
Every file must have a title that accurately reflects the subject matter contained in 
the file.

items.question: The handbook mentions that all files should be classified into what?
items.explanation: All files should be classified into subject files and personal files.

items.question: What is typically contained in a "Subject File"?
options: Documents relating to a staff member's personal life.
Correspondence and documents relating to a particular subject matter.
The personal assets of a public officer.a .
items.explanation:
A subject file is a collection of official correspondence and other documents on a 
particular subject matter.

items.question: What is typically contained in a "Personal File"?
options: Documents on a particular subject matter.
Correspondence and documents relating to a particular staff member.
The official history of a Ministry.a .
items.explanation:
Personal files are files that contain all correspondence and other documents 
relating to a particular staff member.

items.question: How should all documents and correspondence in a file be arranged?
options: In reverse chronological order, with the most recent at the front.
In chronological order, with the most recent at the back.
items.explanation:
All documents and correspondence in a file must be arranged in reverse 
chronological order, with the most recent on top.
452

items.question: What is the purpose of having separate "Minutes" and "Correspondence" sides in a file?
options:
To ensure a clear distinction between the official notes of officers and the documents 
received or sent out.
To hide the minutes from the public.
To make the file easier to read.a
items.explanation:
The minutes are separated from the correspondence to make a clear distinction 
between the official notes of officers and the actual documents.

items.question:
The handbook advises against removing documents from a file. What is the exception to this 
rule?
options: When an officer is in a hurry.
When the document is no longer needed.
When it is done to correct a grave error and is approved by a senior officer.
items.explanation:
No document shall be removed from a file unless it is to correct a grave error and 
is approved by the Head of the Department or the Accounting Officer.

items.question: Why is the "Secret/Confidential Registry" maintained separately from the "Open Registry"?
options: To keep all personal files secret.
To protect classified documents and correspondences from unauthorized access.
To store documents that are not yet ready for public view.

To keep a record of all secret meetings.
Q Correct Answer:: B.
items.explanation:
The Secret/Confidential Registry is maintained separately to protect classified 
documents and correspondences from unauthorized access.

items.question: What is the danger of a file being left on a desk unattended?
options: It could get lost or misplaced.

It could be read by unauthorized persons.
453
items.explanation:
Documents and files must not be left lying on desks where they could get lost or 
misplaced, or read by unauthorized persons.

items.question: What is the purpose of classifying documents?
options: To make them difficult to access.
To sensitize officers about the degree of care required for each document.
To hide their contents from other Ministries.

To prevent the public from ever seeing them.
□ .
items.explanation:
Classified documents are duly classified so as to sensitize officers about the 
degree of care required for each.

items.question: What is the lowest level of classification for an official document?
items.explanation:
The various classification ratings in descending order of security are TOP 
SECRET, SECRET, CONFIDENTIAL, and RESTRICTED.

items.question: What is required for an officer to handle "TOP SECRET" documents?
options: They must be a senior officer.
They must have a special security clearance.
They must be a member of the armed forces.

They must be a new hire.
a .
items.explanation:
Only officers with the requisite security clearance, who have been instructed to 
handle Top Secret documents, may do so.

items.question: What is the best way to destroy classified documents that are no longer needed?
options: Tear them up and throw them in the waste bin.
Leave them on a desk for others to see.

Put them in the Open Registry.
□ .
454
items.explanation:
Unwanted records must be physically destroyed either in a paper shredding 
machine or burnt.

items.question: What is the purpose of a "handing-over note"?
options: To record the personal assets of an officer.
To detail the projects, files, and responsibilities of a position for a successor.
To document all the money spent by an officer.
To explain why an officer is leaving the Service.3 .
items.explanation:
The handing-over note is a detailed document that summarizes all the projects, 
files, and responsibilities that an officer is handing over to their successor.

items.question: What should be done with all files in an office at the close of work each day?
options: They should be left on the desk for the next day.
They should be locked up in a cabinet.
They should be taken home by the officer.
They should be given to a junior officer to keep.3 .
items.explanation:
All files and documents must be locked up in a cabinet at the close of work each 
day.

items.question: The process of creating a new file should be guided by what?
options:
The opinion of the Head of Department.
Q .
items.explanation:
New files should be created based on the existing File Index to ensure the 
consistency and logic of the file numbering system.

items.question: What is the purpose of the "File Index"?
options: To list all the staff in a Ministry.
To provide a list of all the files in a Ministry and their unique reference numbers.
To track the salary of each officer.
To record all the meetings held in a Ministry.3 .
items.explanation:
The File Index is a list of all the files in a Ministry, with their corresponding unique 
reference numbers.

items.question:
What is the danger of creating a new file without referencing the File Index?
455
options: It can lead to a promotion.
It can lead to the creation of duplicate files and disorganization.
It can save a lot of time.
items.explanation:
Creating new files without referencing the File Index can lead to the creation of 
duplicate files, which causes disorganization and confusion.

items.question: What is the purpose of a "Memorandum" in the Civil Service?
options: To communicate internally within the same department or Ministry.
items.explanation:
A memorandum is a type of correspondence used to communicate internally 
between officers within the same department or Ministry.

items.question:
The handbook advises against using obsolete and incorrect information because it can 
undermine what?
options: An officer's maturity and sound judgment.

An officer's ability to attend meetings.
□ .
items.explanation:
Obsolete and incorrect information do not help officers to make sound decisions 
and can easily undermine factors such as maturity and sound judgment.

items.question: What is the purpose of an "official letter" in the Civil Service?
options: To communicate with other Ministries and the public.
items.explanation:
An official letter is used to communicate with other Ministries, Parastatals, and the 
general public.

items.question: What is the purpose of an "official gazette"?
options: To publish all official government notices and announcements.
To publish the personal assets of public officers.

To publish the minutes of official meetings.
456

To publish a list of all civil servants.
Q .
items.explanation:
An official gazette is a publication of the government that contains all official 
notices and announcements.

items.question: What is the purpose of a "circular"?
options:
To communicate an official announcement, directive, or information to all staff in a Ministry or 
the Service at large.
items.explanation:
A circular is used to communicate an official announcement, directive, or 
information to all staff in a Ministry or the Service at large.

items.question:
Who is responsible for ensuring that all official correspondences are properly filed and 
stored?
options: Only the Head of the Civil Service.

Only the staff of the Records/Registry Unit.
a .
items.explanation:
The responsibility for proper filing and storage of all official correspondences rests 
with every Civil Servant.

items.question: What is the purpose of a "bring-up" pad?
options: To store files that are not needed.
To keep a list of all meetings.
To store a file that requires action at a future date.

To keep a list of all staff.
□ .
items.explanation:
When an officer has finished with a file, he should ensure that it is returned to the 
registry. If a file is required at a later date, it should be put on a "bring-up" pad.

items.question:
What is the consequence of failing to take reasonable care of documents entrusted to a Civil 
Servant?
options:
It is a normal part of the job.
457
Q .
items.explanation:
It is an offense for a Civil Servant to fail to take reasonable care of any documents 
entrusted to his care.

items.question:
How should a Civil Servant handle a file that has been left on their desk and has been 
forgotten?
options: Report it to their superior officer and return it to the registry.
Read the contents to see if it is important.a .
items.explanation:
Should a file be left on a desk and has been forgotten, it should be reported to the 
superior officer and returned to the registry.

items.question:
The handbook advises Civil Servants to avoid leaving what on their desks at the end of the 
day?
items.explanation:
Documents and files must not be left lying on desks where they could get lost or 
misplaced, or read by unauthorized persons.

items.question: What is the purpose of having a "confidential" classification for a document?
options: To make it difficult to access.
To protect information that could be harmful if disclosed.
To hide its contents from other Ministries.

To make it a permanent record.a .
The "confidential" classification is used to protect information that could be harmful if disclosed.

items.question: What is the purpose of the "TOP SECRET" classification?
options: To protect information that could cause grave damage to the government if disclosed.
To protect information that is not important.
To make it difficult to access.

To hide its contents from other Ministries.
□ .
458
items.explanation:
The "TOP SECRET" classification is used for documents whose unauthorized 
disclosure could cause grave damage to the security of the government.

items.question: What is a key purpose of a "handing-over note"?
options: To detail the personal assets of the officer.
To ensure a smooth transition of responsibilities from one officer to another.
To provide a list of all official meetings.

To document all the money spent by an officer.
□ .
items.explanation:
The handing-over note is a detailed document that ensures a smooth transition of 
responsibilities from one officer to another.

items.question: What is the purpose of a "personal file"?
options: To document the personal lives of civil servants.
To contain all correspondence and documents relating to a particular staff member.

To store all official government notices.
□ .
items.explanation:
A personal file is a file that contains all correspondence and other documents 
relating to a particular staff member.

items.question: What is the purpose of an "official stamp"?
options: To indicate who created the document.
To indicate that a document is authentic and has been received by an office.
To make a document look nice.

To indicate the date the document was created.
Q .
items.explanation:
An official stamp is used to indicate that a document is authentic and has been 
received by an office.

items.question: What is the purpose of the "Official Secret Act"?
options: To encourage civil servants to share all government information.
To prevent the leaking of government secrets to unauthorized persons.
To protect civil servants from prosecution.

To allow for the free flow of information to the public.
□
items.explanation:
The Official Secret Act is to prevent the leaking of government secrets to 
unauthorized persons.

items.question:
What is the purpose of the "Oath of Secrecy"?
459
options: To hide personal assets from the public.
To protect government secrets and classified information.
To prevent civil servants from talking to the media.
To ensure they are loyal to a political party.3 .
items.explanation:
Officers who have access to classified information should, as soon as possible, 
swear to the Oath of Secrecy.

items.question:
What should a Civil Servant do if they come across a classified document that they are not 
authorized to view?
options: Read it anyway to be informed.
Report it to their superior officer.
items.explanation:
An officer who comes across a classified document that they are not authorized to 
view should report it to their superior officer.

items.question: What is the purpose of a "despatch book"?
items.explanation: A despatch book is used to record all outgoing mail from a department or Ministry.

items.question: What is the purpose of a "receipt book"?
options:
To acknowledge the receipt of all official mail.
□ .
items.explanation: A receipt book is used to acknowledge the receipt of all official mail.

items.question: What is the purpose of a "register of files"?
options: To list all the files in a Ministry and their unique reference numbers.
To list all the staff in a Ministry.
items.explanation:
The register of files is a list of all the files in a Ministry, with their corresponding 
unique reference numbers.

items.question: What is the purpose of a "register of minutes"?
options: To list all the minutes written in a Ministry.
To list all the staff in a Ministry.
items.explanation: The register of minutes is a list of all the minutes written in a Ministry.

items.question: What is the purpose of a "register of correspondences"?
options: To list all the correspondences received and sent out by a Ministry.
To list all the staff in a Ministry.
items.explanation:
The register of correspondences is a list of all the correspondences received and 
sent out by a Ministry.

items.question:
The handbook advises Civil Servants to avoid leaving their work to their subordinates. What 
is the reason for this?
options: It is a sign of laziness.
It is a sign of incompetence.
It can lead to errors and delays.
items.explanation:
Leaving one's work to subordinates can lead to laziness, incompetence, and 
errors and delays.

items.question: What is a key role of a supervisor in a registry?
options: To do all the work themselves.
To enforce discipline with justice and fairness at all times.
To allow staff to do as they please.

To prevent any staff from leaving the office.
Q .
items.explanation:
The supervisor should enforce discipline with justice and fairness at all times.
461

items.question:
What is the danger of a document being destroyed, especially with older methods of record 
management?
options: It can never be recalled and has to be recreated.
It will automatically reappear in a "Recycle Bin."

It can be recalled with a simple command.
□ .
items.explanation:
Pre-computer, unwanted records must be physically destroyed either in a paper 
shredding machine or burnt, and once a document is destroyed, it can never be recalled. It can 
only be recreated again.

items.question: According to the handbook, what is a key asset for a schedule officer?
options: Accurate information based on facts and figures.
items.explanation:
An important asset to most scheduled officers is accurate information based on 
facts and figures.

items.question: What is the danger of using obsolete and incorrect information?
options: It can lead to a promotion.
It can help an officer make sound decisions.
It can undermine an officer's maturity and sound judgment.
It has no effect on the decision-making process.a .
items.explanation:
Obsolete and incorrect information do not help officers to make sound decisions 
and can easily undermine factors such as maturity and sound judgment.

items.question:
The handbook advises against leaving files unattended on a desk. What is the reason for 
this?
options: It is a sign of laziness.
It can get lost or misplaced.
It can be read by unauthorized persons.
items.explanation:
Documents and files must not be left lying on desks where they could get lost or 
misplaced, or read by unauthorized persons.
462

items.question:
What should a Civil Servant do with a file that has been on their desk for a long time without 
any action?
options: Report it to their superior officer and return it to the registry.
items.explanation:
Should a file be left on a desk for a long time without any action, it should be 
reported to the superior officer and returned to the registry.

items.question: What is the purpose of an "open registry"?
options: To keep all non-classified documents and correspondences.

To keep a record of all secret meetings.
□ .
items.explanation:
The open registry is where non-classified documents and correspondences are 
kept and processed.

items.question: What is the purpose of a "secret registry"?
options: To keep all classified documents and correspondences.
To keep a record of all open meetings.a .
items.explanation: The secret registry is where classified documents are handled.

items.question: What is the purpose of an "official stamp"?
options: To indicate who created the document.
To indicate that a document is authentic and has been received by an office.
To make a document look nice.
To indicate the date the document was created.3 .
items.explanation:
An official stamp is used to indicate that a document is authentic and has been 
received by an office.

items.question: What is the purpose of a "circular"?
options:
To communicate an official announcement, directive, or information to all staff in a Ministry or 
the Service at large.
463

To provide a confidential report.
Q .
items.explanation:
A circular is used to communicate an official announcement, directive, or 
information to all staff in a Ministry or the Service at large.

items.question: What is the purpose of an "official gazette"?
options: To publish all official government notices and announcements.
To publish the personal assets of public officers.
To publish the minutes of official meetings.

To publish a list of all civil servants.
□ .
items.explanation:
An official gazette is a publication of the government that contains all official 
notices and announcements.

items.question: What is the purpose of a "memorandum"?
options: To communicate with other Ministries and the public.
To communicate internally within the same department or Ministry.
items.explanation:
A memorandum is a type of correspondence used to communicate internally 
between officers within the same department or Ministry.

items.question: What is the purpose of an "official letter"?
options: To communicate with other Ministries and the public.
items.explanation:
An official letter is used to communicate with other Ministries, Parastatals, and the 
general public.

items.question: What is the purpose of a "file title"?
options: To make the file look nice.
To accurately reflect the subject matter contained in the file.
To indicate who created the file.

To indicate the date the file was created.
□ .
464
items.explanation:
Every file must have a title that accurately reflects the subject matter contained in 
the file.

items.question:
How should a Civil Servant handle a file that has been on their desk for a long time without 
any action?
options: Report it to their superior officer and return it to the registry.
items.explanation:
Should a file be left on a desk for a long time without any action, it should be 
reported to the superior officer and returned to the registry.

items.question:
The handbook advises against leaving files unattended on a desk. What is the reason for 
this?
options: It is a sign of laziness.
It can get lost or misplaced.
It can be read by unauthorized persons.
items.explanation:
Documents and files must not be left lying on desks where they could get lost or 
misplaced, or read by unauthorized persons.

To allow for the free flow of information to the public.
Q .

To document all the money spent by an officer.

To store a file that requires action at a future date.
To keep a list of all staff.a .
items.explanation:
When an officer has finished with a file, he should ensure that it is returned to the

To hide their contents from other Ministries.
To prevent the public from ever seeing them.a .
items.explanation:

items.explanation:

Put them in the Open Registry.a .
items.explanation:
Unwanted records must be physically destroyed either in a paper shredding

To make a document look nice.
466

To indicate the date the document was created.
Q .
items.explanation:
An official stamp is used to indicate that a document is authentic and has been

items.question: What is the purpose of an "official letter"?
options: To communicate with other Ministries and the public.
items.explanation:
An official letter is used to communicate with other Ministries, Parastatals, and the 
general public.

items.question: How should all documents and correspondence in a file be arranged?
options: In reverse chronological order, with the most recent at the front.
In chronological order, with the most recent at the back.
items.explanation:
All documents and correspondence in a file must be arranged in reverse 
chronological order, with the most recent on top.

The handbook advises against removing documents from a file. What is the exception to this 
rule?
options: When an officer is in a hurry.
When the document is no longer needed.
When it is done to correct a grave error and is approved by a senior officer.
items.explanation:
No document shall be removed from a file unless it is to correct a grave error and

To generate a paper trail for all financial transactions.
□ .
467

It could lead to the loss of government revenue.
It could lead to policy reversals and conflicts.

The public will lose trust in the government.
□ .
items.explanation:
If records are not kept properly, it could lead to policy reversals, conflicts within

Who, according to the document, is responsible for the proper keeping of records in the Civil

options: Only the staff of the Records/Registry Unit.
All Civil Servants, regardless of their position.
Only the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.a .
items.explanation:
The proper keeping of records is the responsibility of every Civil Servant, 
irrespective of their position.
