When writing a minute, an officer should support their position by referring to 
previous government decisions, rules, or regulations on the matter.
Section E
Part 1: Chairing and Servicing of Meetings and Forums
469

items.question: What is the most fundamental demand on an officer chairing a meeting?
options: To ensure everyone is paid for their attendance.
To understand the purpose of the gathering.
To be the most senior officer in the room.
To take notes and prepare the minutes.a .
items.explanation:
The most fundamental demand on the Chairman of a meeting is to understand 
the purpose of the gathering.

items.question:
According to the handbook, when the purpose of a meeting is complex, what should be 
done?
options: The chairman should make a unilateral decision.
The purpose should be ignored to save time.
The purpose must be spelled out in formal Terms of Reference.
The chairman should ask each member individually what they think the purpose is.a .
items.explanation:
The purpose of a forum may be so complex that it has to be spelled out in formal 
Terms of Reference.

items.question:
If the chairman and other members cannot agree on the purpose of a meeting, what is the 
chairman's responsibility?
options: To move on with the meeting anyway.
To revert to the convening authority to seek clarification.

To force his own interpretation of the purpose on the members.
Q .
items.explanation:
Where the chairman and the other members cannot achieve such an 
understanding or agreement on what the purpose is, it is the chairman's duty to revert to the 
convening authority to seek and secure clarification.

items.question: What is a key element for a meeting's efficiency and effectiveness, besides a good chairman?
items.explanation:
Just as every meeting needs a Chairman to achieve its purpose, it equally needs 
a good Secretariat for efficiency and effectiveness.

items.question:
What should a chairman do in relation to the Secretariat?
470
options: Ignore the Secretariat and handle all tasks personally.
Ensure a Secretariat exists, liaise with it, and brief its head on expectations^.
Tell the Secretariat what to do and then leave them alone.
Leave all decisions to the Secretariat.3 .
items.explanation:
It is in the interest of the Chairman to ensure a Secretariat exists, to liaise with it, 
and to brief its head fully on what he expects of it.

items.question: Why is it important for a chairman to prepare adequately for a meeting?
options: To show off his intelligence to the members.

To ensure a smooth and effective control of the meeting and earn the respect of the 
members..
To waste time before the meeting starts.
To give the Secretariat less work to do.3 .
items.explanation:
It is only by preparing adequately that the chairman can assure himself of an 
effective control of the meeting and earn the full respect of the members.

items.question: What is a crucial step a chairman should take to prepare for a meeting?
options:
Read the files and other relevant documents to become fully conversant with the 
subject-matter.
Arrive late to assert his authority.

Avoid discussing the agenda with the Secretariat.
□ .
items.explanation:
The chairman should read the files as well as other relevant documents and 
materials so as to become fully conversant with the subject-matter

items.question: What should the chairman do with the draft agenda prepared by the Secretariat?
options: Ignore it and create his own.
Vette the draft agenda and direct its circulation to all members in advance21.
Hand it out to members at the beginning of the meeting.
Keep it secret from the members until the meeting starts.3
items.explanation:
The chairman should get the Secretariat to prepare and submit for his vetting the 
Draft Agenda for the meeting. Once he clears this, he should direct its circulation to all members 
well in advance of the meeting.

items.question: If the chairman has follow-up actions to take before a meeting, what must he do?
options:
He can do them after the meeting.
471
He must address such issues promptly to avoid undermining the meeting's effectiveness25.
He can ask a junior officer to do them for him.
items.explanation:
If there are actions for him to take before the meeting, he must address such 
issues promptly, otherwise, the effectiveness of the meeting may be undermined.

items.question:
What is the purpose of the chairman arriving promptly at the venue and starting the meeting 
on time?
options: To finish the meeting as quickly as possible.
To show he is more important than the members.
To encourage members to take the forum and its objectives seriously28.
To rush the members into making decisions.a
items.explanation:
The chairman should make it a point of duty to arrive promptly at the venue, with 
the full intention of starting the meeting on time, as this will encourage the members to take the 
forum and its objectives seriously.

items.question: How should a chairman conduct a meeting?
options: In a dictatorial manner, enforcing his will on the members.
In a fair, orderly and effective manner, using the adopted Agenda31.
By allowing the most senior members to dominate the discussions.

By allowing the most talkative members to speak as much as they want.
Q .
items.explanation:
A chairman should conduct the meeting in a fair, orderly and effective manner, 
using as much as possible the adopted Agenda.

items.question:
A chairman should consciously give room for opposing views and protect which members 
from being intimidated?
items.explanation:
He should consciously give room for opposing views and protect the weak or 
junior members from being intimidated by their superiors.

items.question: What should a chairman prevent from dominating and monopolizing discussions?
options: A few over-active and loquacious members37.
items.explanation:
The chairman must prevent a few over-active and loquacious members from 
dominating and monopolizing discussions.

items.question: What is one of the key responsibilities of the chairman in closing a meeting?
options: To summarize the conclusions of the forum to the agreement of the other members40.
To tell the members to go home.
To take all the credit for the meeting's success.Q .
items.explanation:
In bringing the meeting to a close, the chairman should summarise the 
conclusions of the forum to the agreement of the other members

items.question: In closing a meeting, what should the chairman do regarding follow-up actions?
options:
He should highlight the follow-up actions required, the party responsible for each, and the 
time frame for action43.
He should assign all follow-up actions to the Secretariat.
He should assign all follow-up actions to the junior members.a .
items.explanation:
The chairman should highlight the follow-up actions required, the party 
responsible for each action, and the time frame for action.

items.question: What is the chairman's role regarding the minutes of the meeting?
options:
He should direct the Secretariat as to the time for the production and circulation of the 
minutes46.
He should tell the Secretariat to keep the minutes secret.

He should not be concerned with the minutes.
□
items.explanation:
He should direct the Secretariat as to the time for the production and circulation of 
the minutes of the meeting.

items.question: The servicing of a meeting incorporates all requisite activities from what point in time?
options: From the moment the chairman walks into the room.

From the time an officer is designated as the Secretary to the time the forum rises and 
essential follow-up actions are completed.

From the moment the meeting is announced.
473
From the moment the minutes are circulated.Q
items.explanation:
The servicing of a typical meeting will incorporate all requisite activities from the 
time an officer is designated as the Secretary to the time the forum rises and essential follow-up 
actions are completed.

items.question: What should a secretary do after receiving a notice to serve a meeting?
options: Do nothing until the chairman calls them.

Contact the convening authority to understand the purpose of the meeting and get the Terms 
of Reference.
Immediately prepare the agenda without any consultation.
items.explanation:
The secretary should contact the convening authority to understand the purpose 
of the meeting and get the Terms of Reference

items.question: What is the purpose of preparing an "Agenda"?
options: To make the meeting more complex.
To set out the various items that will be considered at the meeting.
To tell the members what to say.a Correct Answer:: C.
items.explanation:
The Agenda is a document that sets out the various items that will be considered 
at the meeting.

items.question: How should the agenda be structured?
options: By placing the most controversial items first.
Logically, starting with opening formalities and ending with closing formalities.

By placing the most junior members' items first.
□ .
items.explanation:
The Agenda should be structured logically, starting with opening formalities, 
moving to the main business, and ending with closing formalities.

items.question:
A secretary should send out the notice of meeting and agenda to all members at least how 
many days before the meeting?
items.explanation:
The notice of meeting and agenda should be sent out to all members at least five 
working days before the meeting.

items.question: What should be attached to the notice and agenda?
options: The minutes of the last meeting and any other relevant documents.
The personal files of all members.
The financial statements of the Ministry.
The private phone numbers of the members.a .
items.explanation:
The minutes of the last meeting and any other relevant documents should be 
attached to the notice and agenda.

items.question: Why is it important to circulate the agenda and other documents in advance?
options: To give members an opportunity to do their work.
To allow members to prepare themselves for the meeting.
items.explanation:
Circulating the agenda and other documents in advance allows members to 
prepare themselves for the meeting.

items.question: What is the role of the secretary during the meeting?
options: To act as the official recorder of all discussions, decisions, and action points.
To take charge of all financial matters.3 .
items.explanation:
The secretary is the official recorder of all discussions, decisions, and action 
points of the meeting.

items.question: What is the purpose of "Minutes" of a meeting?
options: To provide a brief summary of the chairman's opinions.
To serve as the official record of the proceedings and decisions of a meeting.
To provide a list of all members who attended.
To document the personal lives of the members.a .
items.explanation:
The Minutes are the official record of the proceedings and decisions of a meeting.
475

items.question: A good minute should be what?
options: A faithful and accurate summary of what transpired at the meeting.
A reflection of the secretary's personal opinions.
A list of all the jokes told at the meeting.a .
items.explanation:
A good minute should be a faithful and accurate summary of what transpired at 
the meeting.

items.question:
The minutes must be written and circulated to all members as soon as possible after the 
meeting. What is the reason for this?
options: To prevent the members from forgetting what was discussed.
To ensure that the decisions are fresh in the minds of the members.
To show that the secretary is a fast writer.
To make the meeting look more important.3 .
items.explanation:
The minutes should be circulated as soon as possible after the meeting to ensure 
that the decisions are fresh in the minds of the members.

items.question: What is the purpose of having a "Matter Arising" section in the minutes of a meeting?
options: To review the decisions and actions taken from the previous meeting82.
items.explanation:
The "Matters Arising" section is used to review the decisions and actions taken 
from the previous meeting.

items.question: What is the purpose of the "Action Points" section in the minutes of a meeting?
options: To list the names of the members who attended.

To list the people who are responsible for taking action, what the action is, and the timeframe 
for completion85.
To list the names of the people who spoke the most.

To list the topics that were not discussed.
□ .
items.explanation:
The "Action Points" section lists the people who are responsible for taking action, 
what the action is, and the timeframe for completion.

items.question: What is the secretary's responsibility after the meeting has closed?
options:
To forget about the meeting and move on.
476

To ensure that the minutes are written and circulated, and that all follow-up actions are 
completed88.
To blame others for the failure of the meeting.3 .
items.explanation:
It is the secretary's responsibility to ensure that the minutes are written and 
circulated, and that all follow-up actions are completed.

items.question: What is the purpose of a "handing-over note"?
options: To record the personal assets of an officer.
To detail the projects, files, and responsibilities of a position for a successor.
To provide a list of all official meetings.

To document all the money spent by an officer.
□ .
items.explanation:
A handing-over note is a detailed document that summarizes all the projects, files, 
and responsibilities that an officer is handing over to their successor.

items.question: Who should sign a handing-over note?
options: Both the officer handing over and the officer taking over.
items.explanation:
The original and the two copies of the notes should be signed by both the Officer 
Handing Over and the Officer Taking Over.

items.question: What is the purpose of an "official letter" in the Civil Service?
options: To communicate with other Ministries and the public97.

To provide a confidential report.
Q .
items.explanation:
Letters to other Ministries are always addressed to the Honourable Minister. An 
official letter is used to communicate with other Ministries, Parastatals, and the general public.

items.question: What is the purpose of an "official stamp"?
options: To indicate who created the document.
To indicate that a document is authentic and has been received by an officer..
To make a document look nice.

To indicate the date the document was created.
477
Q .
items.explanation:
An official stamp is used to indicate that a document is authentic and has been 
received by an officer

items.question: What is the purpose of a "circular"?
options:
To communicate an official announcement, directive, or information to all staff in a Ministry or 
the Service at large103.
items.explanation:
A circular is used to communicate an official announcement, directive, or 
information to all staff in a Ministry or the Service at large.

items.question: What is the purpose of a "memorandum"?
options: To communicate with other Ministries and the public.
To communicate internally within the same department or Ministry106.

To provide a confidential report.
Q .
items.explanation:
A memorandum is a type of correspondence used to communicate internally 
between officers within the same department or Ministry.

items.question:
The style of address for submitting minutes through official channels is to place the most 
senior officer's title first, followed by other officers in descending order of status prefixed by what 
word?
items.explanation:
The style of address in general use for the submission of minutes through official 
channels is by placing the most senior officer's title first followed by other officers in descending 
order of status prefixed by the word "through".

items.question:
What is the purpose of routing papers back through official channels after a decision has 
been reached by a higher authority?
options: To delay the implementation of the decision.
To allow all intermediate officers to be acquainted with the decisions reached.
To give junior officers more work to do.

To provide an opportunity for the decision to be reversed.
478
Q .
items.explanation:
Papers routed upwards through official channels are routed back in the same 
way, and all intermediate officers are, therefore, acquainted with decisions reached by the 
higher authority.

items.question:
What should intermediate officers do with papers that are routed through them for 
information only?
options: They should not delay them unnecessarily.
They should take a copy for themselves.
items.explanation:
Intermediate officers should not delay unnecessarily papers which are routed 
through them for information only.

items.question: All Civil Servants are required to take what?
items.explanation: All Civil Servants are required to take an Oath of Secrecy.

items.question: What is considered a very serious offense for a Civil Servant?
options: Unauthorized disclosure of any information acquired in the course of their duties121.
items.explanation:
Unauthorized disclosure of any information acquired in the course of their duties 
is a very serious offence.

items.question: What is the purpose of having a "confidential" classification for a document?
options: To make it difficult to access.
To protect information that could be harmful if disclosed.
To hide its contents from other Ministries.

To make it a permanent record.
□ .
479
items.explanation:
Although not directly stated in the provided text for this chapter, this is a standard 
concept of document classification, and the document itself mentions the Oath of Secrecy and 
the "unauthorized disclosure of any information acquired in the course of their duties" as a 
serious offense, suggesting the need for confidentiality

items.question: What is the purpose of the "TOP SECRET" classification?
options: To protect information that could cause grave damage to the government if disclosed.
To protect information that is not important.
To make it difficult to access.
To hide its contents from other Ministries.a .
items.explanation:
Although not directly stated in this chapter, this is a standard concept of document 
classification.

items.question:
What should a Civil Servant do if they come across a classified document that they are not 
authorized to view?
options: Read it anyway to be informed.
Report it to their superior officer.
items.explanation:
This is a logical action in line with the "Oath of Secrecy" and the seriousness of 
unauthorized disclosure.

items.question: What is the purpose of the National Council on Establishments?
options: To set the salary for all government workers.

To facilitate inter-governmental consultations on establishment matters and to avoid 
unnecessary disparities in general conditions of service in all the Civil Services of the 
Federation.
To manage the budget of the Federal Government.

To hire and fire civil servants.
□ .
items.explanation:
The National Council on Establishments was established to facilitate 
inter-governmental consultations on establishment matters and to avoid unnecessary disparities 
in general conditions of service in all the Civil Services of the Federation.

items.question: What are the hours of work in Federal offices, as mentioned in the handbook?
items.explanation:
The hours of work in Federal offices are from 8:00 am to 4:00 pm on Monday to 
Friday.

items.question: What is required for staff on GL. 03 -13 on arrival at the office and at the close of work?
options: They must sign the Attendance Register..
They must report to their superior officer.
They must send an email to their supervisor.

They must punch a time clock.
a .
items.explanation:
All staff on GL. 03-13 are required to sign the Attendance Register on arrival at 
the Office in the morning as well as at close of work at the end of the day..

items.question: What is the role of a chairman in summarising the conclusions of a meeting?
options: To do it quickly without consultation.
To summarize the conclusions to the agreement of the other members135.
To write the summary himself and not show it to anyone.

To only summarize the points he agrees with.
a .
items.explanation:
The chairman should summarise the conclusions of the forum to the agreement of 
the other members and for the benefit of the Secretariat which should reflect these in the 
minutes.

items.question: A good minute should be what?
options: A faithful and accurate summary of what transpired at the meetingl

items.question:
A secretary should send out the notice of meeting and agenda to all members at least how 
many days before the meeting?
items.explanation:
The notice of meeting and agenda should be sent out to all members at least five 
working days before the meeting.

items.question: What should be attached to the notice and agenda?
options: The minutes of the last meeting and any other relevant documents.
The personal files of all members.
The financial statements of the Ministry.

The private phone numbers of the members.
□ .
items.explanation:
The minutes of the last meeting and any other relevant documents should be 
attached to the notice and agenda.

items.question: What is the purpose of preparing an "Agenda"?
options: To make the meeting more complex.
To set out the various items that will be considered at the meeting.

To tell the members what to say.
□ Correct Answer:: C
items.explanation:
The Agenda is a document that sets out the various items that will be considered 
at the meeting.

items.question:
What is a key element for a meeting's efficiency and effectiveness, besides a good 
chairman?
items.explanation:
Just as every meeting needs a Chairman to achieve its purpose, it equally needs 
a good Secretariat for efficiency and effectiveness.

items.question: What is the most fundamental demand on an officer chairing a meeting?
options: To ensure everyone is paid for their attendance.
To understand the purpose of the gathering.
To be the most senior officer in the room.

To take notes and prepare the minutes.
Q .
items.explanation:
The most fundamental demand on the Chairman of a meeting is to understand 
the purpose of the gathering.

items.question:
What is the purpose of the "Action Points" section in the minutes of a meeting?
482
options: To list the names of the members who attended.

To list the people who are responsible for taking action, what the action is, and the timeframe 
for completion156.
To list the names of the people who spoke the most.

To list the topics that were not discussed.
□ .
items.explanation:
The "Action Points" section lists the people who are responsible for taking action, 
what the action is, and the timeframe for completion.

items.question: What is the purpose of an "official letter" in the Civil Service?
options: To communicate with other Ministries and the public.
items.explanation:
Letters to other Ministries are always addressed to the Honourable Minister. An 
official letter is used to communicate with other Ministries, Parastatals, and the general public

items.question: What is considered a very serious offense for a Civil Servant?
options: Unauthorized disclosure of any information acquired in the course of their duties162.
items.explanation:
Unauthorized disclosure of any information acquired in the course of their duties 
is a very serious offence.

items.question: What is the purpose of the "Official Secret Act"?
options: To encourage civil servants to share all government information.
To prevent the leaking of government secrets to unauthorized persons.
To protect civil servants from prosecution.
To allow for the free flow of information to the public.a .
items.explanation:
The Official Secret Act is to prevent the leaking of government secrets to 
unauthorized persons.

items.question:
Who, according to the document, is responsible for the proper keeping of records in the Civil 
Service?
options: Only the staff of the Records/Registry Unit.
All Civil Servants, regardless of their position.
Only the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.Q .
items.explanation:
The proper keeping of records is the responsibility of every Civil Servant, 
irrespective of their position. This question is from a previous chapter but is included as it is a 
common question within the quiz context.

items.question: What is the main danger of an organization's records not being kept properly?
options: Civil servants will not be paid on time.
It could lead to the loss of government revenue.
It could lead to policy reversals and conflicts.
The public will lose trust in the government.a .
items.explanation:
If records are not kept properly, it could lead to policy reversals, conflicts within 
and between agencies, and chaos in the public sector. This question is from a previous chapter 
but is included as it is a common question within the quiz context.
Part 2: Basic Techniques of Clear Writing in Government Business

items.question: What is considered a cardinal importance for Schedule Officers, according to the handbook?
options: Their ability to speak multiple languages.
Their familiarity with basic techniques for clear writing.
Their knowledge of all government policies.
items.explanation:
Since much of the time of most officers is devoted to drafting and minuting, it is of 
cardinal importance that they should be familiar with the basic techniques for clear writing.

items.question: How is "Clear writing" defined in the context of the handbook?
options: Both legibility and clarity of style.

Writing that is difficult to understand.
□ .
items.explanation: "Clear writing" in this context means not only legibility but also clarity of style.

items.question: What is "Style" defined as in the context of writing?
options: The speed at which one writes.

The type of pen used for writing.
Q ..
items.explanation: Style may be defined as the distinctive manner of writing.

items.question: What should a Schedule Officer have, regarding their writing?
items.explanation: A Schedule Officer should have a good style.

items.question: What is a "draft" in government business?
options: A proposed reply, circular, or any other document put up by an officer to a higher authority.

A confidential report that is not meant for circulation.
Q .
items.explanation:
A draft is a proposed reply, circular or any other document put up by an officer to 
a higher authority.

items.question: When is a draft considered good and approved?
options: If it serves the prescribed purpose.
items.explanation:
If the proposed reply or suggested circular serves the prescribed purpose, it is 
considered good and approved.

items.question: How might a draft be amended?
options: By filling in gaps or incorporating additional facts by a senior officer.

By discarding it and starting anew.
□ .
items.explanation:
A draft may be amended by filling in the gaps in the efforts of the officer or by the 
incorporation of additional facts by the senior officer.
485

items.question: When should an officer draft a reply to an item of correspondence?
options: Where an item of correspondence requires a reply.

Never, as it is the Secretariat's job.
□ .
items.explanation:
Where an item of correspondence requires a reply, it is the duty of the Schedule 
Officer to whom it has been allocated to prepare a draft reply.

items.question: What should typed drafts of minutes, briefs, and letters have?
options: Headings which give an idea of the matter in the drafts.
items.explanation:
Typed drafts of minutes, briefs, letters, etc. should have headings which give an 
idea of the matter in the drafts.

items.question: Why is reasonable spacing used in typed drafts?
options: So that there is ample space for amendments.

To make them look more professional.
□ .
items.explanation:
Reasonable spacing is used in typed drafts so that there is ample space for 
amendments.

items.question: What should never be used for drafting, especially if it contains classified information?
items.explanation:
Discarded papers containing classified information should never be used for 
drafting.

items.question:
When drafts are given to the Secretary for fairing, what should be clearly recorded at the top 
of page 1?
items.explanation:
When drafts of any documents are given to the Secretary for fairing, the number 
of copies required should always be clearly recorded at the top of page 1 of every draft.

items.question: Where does the top left-hand side of a minute refer to?
options: The person, by appointment, to whom it is written.
The date the minute was written.
items.explanation:
The top left-hand side of a minute refers to the person, by appointment, to whom 
it is written.

items.question: What follows the text of a minute, concluding it?
items.explanation:
The text of the minute follows. It is concluded by the signature or initials of the 
writer.

items.question: What should be shown immediately under the writer's signature or initials in a minute?
options: The typewritten or stamped name of the Officer writing it.
The names of all people involved.

A summary of the minute.
a .
items.explanation:
Immediately under his signature or initials is the typewritten or stamped name of 
the Officer writing it.

items.question: What is one of the things a minute may be built up by stating briefly?
items.explanation: A minute may be built up as follows: (i) state briefly the matter at issue.

items.question: When is it advisable to type short drafts or minutes if one's handwriting is not legible?
items.explanation: Unless you can write legibly, it is advisable to type short drafts or minutes.

items.question:
What should be done if one's handwriting is not easily readable for minutes or drafts 
covering more than half of an A4 page?
options: They should be sent back to the sender.a .
items.explanation:
Unless one’s hand writing is easily readable, all minutes or drafts covering more 
than half of an A4 page should be typewritten.

items.question: What should officers try to avoid, as mentioned in the "Irrelevance" section?
options: Having a grasp of the subject at issue.
Drifting away from the question being considered.
items.explanation:
Try to have a grasp of the subject at issue, and do not drift away from the 
question being considered.

items.question: What should letters always have?
options: A heading briefly descriptive of the subject matter, a reference number, and a date.
A list of all previous correspondence.Q .
items.explanation:
Letters should always have a heading briefly descriptive of the subject matter and 
must bear a reference number and date.

items.question: What should Appendices or Schedules attached to a letter always be marked with?
options: The reference of the letter to which they were attached.
items.explanation:
When Appendices or Schedules, etc. are attached, they should always be marked 
with the reference of the letter to which they were attached so that if they become detached at 
any time they can readily be connected to the relevant letter.

items.question: What abbreviations should be included at the foot of the letter if there are enclosures?
items.explanation:
A dash should be typed in the margin in line with references in the letter to any 
enclosures or, the abbreviations Enc. or Encs. should be included at the foot of the letter.

items.question: If a letter has more than one enclosure, what should be indicated?
items.explanation:
If the letter has more than one enclosure, the number should be shown in 
brackets, e.g. Enc. (2). 24

items.question: How should communication addressed to the Overseas Government never be made?
options: Through the Ministry of Foreign Affairs.
items.explanation:
Communication addressed to Overseas Government should never be made 
directly.

items.question:
How should information be conveyed to the Ministry of Foreign Affairs for overseas 
communication?
options: By quoting specific extracts verbatim or conveying the gist of the information.

By sending a blank document.
Q Correct Answer:: A.
items.explanation:
The required information should be conveyed to the Ministry of Foreign Affairs 
with a request that the appropriate message be dispatched either by quoting verbatim specific 
extracts from the letter, or by conveying the gist of the information to be passed on.

items.question: What is a "Brief in government business?
options: A summary of a subject prepared for a superior authority.
items.explanation: A Brief is a summary of a subject prepared for a superior authority.

items.question: What should a good Brief aim to do?
options: Be concise and constructive and recommend the solution to an outstanding problem.
items.explanation:
A good brief should be concise and constructive. It should try to recommend the 
solution to an outstanding problem.

items.question: What are "File Notes"?
options: A simple form of making a quick written record of something that has happened.
items.explanation:
File Notes are a simple form of making a quick written record of something that 
has happened.

items.question: When should File Notes be made frequently?
options: When verbal discussions are held by telephone or otherwise.
Only at the end of the day.

Never, as they are not formal.
□ .
490
items.explanation:
They should, therefore, be made as frequently as possible when verbal 
discussions are held by telephone or otherwise.

items.question: What should always be included with File Notes?
items.explanation: Such notes should always be initiated and dated.

items.question: What is one of the included items in Handing Over Notes?
options: Details regarding content of the schedule of duties of the post.
items.explanation:
Handing Over Notes may include the following: (i) Details regarding content of the 
schedule of duties of the post.

items.question: Who should sign the original and copies of the Handing Over Notes?
options: Both the Officer Handing Over and the Officer Taking Over.
items.explanation:
The original and the two copies of the Notes should be signed by both the Officer 
Handing Over and the Officer Taking Over.

items.question:
What is the purpose of passing the filed original of the Handing Over Notes to the 
Supervisor and/or a superior officer?
options: To ensure that the duties of that post have been properly taken over.
To allow them to rewrite it.
items.explanation:
The filed original should then be passed to the Supervisor and/or a superior 
officer who should know that the duties of that post have been properly taken over.
491

items.question:
What is a key ingredient of good governance, as mentioned in the introduction to Chapter 
13?
options: Communication to the various stakeholders as the occasion demands.
items.explanation:
A key ingredient of good governance is communication to the various 
stakeholders as the occasion demands.

items.question: What serves little purpose if used in writing, according to the handbook?
options: Words that are big but communicate little.
items.explanation: It serves little purpose if you use words that are big but communicate little.

items.question: What kind of style and choice of words should be used in government communication?
items.explanation: Your style and choice of words should be simple, smooth and free- flowing.

items.question: What is the main system of filing in Government offices?
items.explanation: The main system of filing in Government offices is the Book File system.

items.question: In the Book File system, how are letters and minutes enclosed and numbered?
options: On the left-hand side-face to cover in chronological order and numbered as a book.
items.explanation:
The Book File system is the method of filing whereby letters and minutes are all 
enclosed on the left-hand side-face to cover in chronological order and are numbered in the 
same fashion as a book.

items.question: What is the purpose of the Duplicate Note-Book System for recording file transmissions?
options: To keep track of personal notes.
To record the file number, date, and destination whenever an officer sends out a file.
items.explanation:
Some officers still use the duplicate note-book system for recording transmission 
of files whereby whenever an officer sends out a file, his Clerk records its number, date and 
destination in a duplicate note-book.

items.question:
What should a Clerk do with the top copy of their list from the duplicate note-book system 
at the end of each day?
options: Give it to the officer in charge of the most appropriate Registry.
items.explanation:
On completion of each page and always at the end of each day, the Clerk must 
give the top copy of his list to the officer in charge of the most appropriate Registry, which 
records the movements on the Absent File Cards.
Section F
Forms of Official Communication in Government Business
493

items.question: What is considered a key ingredient of good governance, according to Chapter 13?
options: Communication to the various stakeholders as the occasion demands.
items.explanation:
A key ingredient of good governance is communication to the various 
stakeholders as the occasion demands.

items.question: Government communication is described as having its own what?
items.explanation:
Government is a very large organization with its own culture, methods and 
practices.

items.question:
Who should an officer not communicate with on official business, outside of the proper 
channel?
items.explanation:
The golden rule is that an officer must not communicate on official business with a 
non-official or non-Government person unless the officer is specifically directed to do so by his 
superior officer.

items.question: What is the purpose of a "handing over note"?
options: To record the personal assets of an officer.

To provide a detailed and comprehensive document to a successor, outlining the duties and 
responsibilities of the post.
To provide a list of all official meetings.
To document all the money spent by an officer.Q .
items.explanation:
It is essential that an officer who is about to proceed on leave, transfer, or retire 
should prepare a detailed and comprehensive handing over note for the guidance of his 
successor.
494

items.question: When should the handing over note be written?
options: After the successor has taken over.
Before the officer proceeds on leave, transfer or retirement.
Only when requested by a superior officer.a
items.explanation:
It is essential that an officer who is about to proceed on leave, transfer or retire 
should prepare a detailed and comprehensive handing over note.

items.question: Who should sign the original and copies of the Handing Over Notes?
options: Both the Officer Handing Over and the Officer Taking Over.
items.explanation:
The original and the two copies of the Notes should be signed by both the Officer 
Handing Over and the Officer Taking Over.

items.question:
What is the purpose of passing the filed original of the Handing Over Notes to a superior 
officer?
options: To ensure that the duties of that post have been properly taken over.
To allow them to rewrite it.
items.explanation:
The filed original should then be passed to the Supervisor and/or a superior officer 
who should know that the duties of that post have been properly taken over.

items.question: What should be the first entry in a handing over note?
options: A summary of the officer's personal life.
The names of the most important people the officer knows.
Details regarding the contents of the schedule of duties of the post.
A list of all files in the office.a .
items.explanation:
Handing Over Notes may include the following: (i) Details regarding the contents 
of the schedule of duties of the post.

items.question: What is one of the things to be included in a handing over note regarding staff?
options:
Names of the staff supervised, their grades, and roles.
495
A list of all staff who have taken leave.

A list of all staff who have been promoted.
Q
items.explanation: (ii) Names of the staff supervised, their grades, and roles.

items.question: What should an officer do when leaving the office for an extended period?
options: Take all their files with them.
Make sure to prepare a comprehensive handing over note for their successor.

Do nothing, as a new officer will figure things out.
□ .
items.explanation:
It is essential that an officer who is about to proceed on leave, transfer, or retire 
should prepare a detailed and comprehensive handing over note for the guidance of his 
successor.

items.question: What is the purpose of a "Brief in government business?
options: A summary of a subject prepared for a superior authority.
items.explanation: A Brief is a summary of a subject prepared for a superior authority.

items.question: What is the main characteristic of a good brief?
options: It is full of personal opinions.

It is only factual without any recommendations.
Answer: B.
items.explanation:
A good brief should be concise and constructive. It should try to recommend the 
solution to an outstanding problem66.

items.question: When should a brief be prepared?
options: When a superior authority needs a summary of a subject.
Only when a junior officer requests it.

Never, as they are not used anymore.
□ .
496
items.explanation: A Brief is a summary of a subject prepared for a superior authority.

items.question: What should a brief try to recommend?
options: The solution to an outstanding problem.
items.explanation: A good brief... should try to recommend the solution to an outstanding problem

items.question: What is the purpose of a "Memorandum" (Memo)?
options: To communicate with other Ministries and the public.
To communicate internally within the same department or Ministry.
items.explanation:
A memorandum is a type of correspondence used to communicate internally 
between officers within the same department or Ministry.

items.question: What is the main system of filing in Government offices?
items.explanation: The main system of filing in Government offices is the Book File system.

items.question: In the Book File system, how are letters and minutes enclosed and numbered?
options: On the left-hand side-face to cover in chronological order and numbered as a book.
items.explanation:
The Book File system is the method of filing whereby letters and minutes are all 
enclosed on the left-hand side-face to cover in chronological order and are numbered in the 
same fashion as a book.

items.question: What is a key purpose of the Civil Service, as related to the Executive Council?
options:
To provide entertainment for the political leadership.
497

To provide the political leadership with the instruments with which to administer Government 
at the highest level.
To manage the finances of the country.
To create laws for the country.3 .
items.explanation:
A key purpose of the Civil Service is to provide the political leadership with the 
instruments with which to administer Government at the highest level, i.e. the Executive Council 
of the Federation.

items.question: What is the purpose of a "Minute"?
options: To serve as a personal reminder.
To provide a written submission to present a view or position on an issue.
To summarize a meeting for the public.3 .
items.explanation: Minute/Memo:- Written submission to present a view or position on an issue

items.question: What are "File Notes"?
options: A simple form of making a quick written record of something that has happened.
items.explanation:
File Notes are a simple form of making a quick written record of something that 
has happened.

items.question: What should always be included with File Notes?
items.explanation: Such notes should always be initiated and dated.

items.question: When should File Notes be made frequently?
options: When verbal discussions are held by telephone or otherwise.
Only at the end of the day.

Never, as they are not formal.3 .
498
items.explanation:
They should, therefore, be made as frequently as possible when verbal 
discussions are held by telephone or otherwise.

items.question: What is the purpose of an "official letter" in the Civil Service?
options: To communicate with other Ministries and the public.
items.explanation:
An official letter is used to communicate with other Ministries, Parastatals, and the 
general public.

items.question: What should letters always have?
options: A heading briefly descriptive of the subject matter, a reference number, and a date.

A list of all previous correspondence.
□ .
items.explanation:
Letters should always have a heading briefly descriptive of the subject matter and 
must bear a reference number and date.

items.question: What is the purpose of an "official stamp"?
options: To indicate who created the document.
To indicate that a document is authentic and has been received by an office.
To make a document look nice.

To indicate the date the document was created.
Q .
items.explanation:
An official stamp is used to indicate that a document is authentic and has been 
received by an office.

items.question: What is the purpose of a "circular"?
options:
To communicate an official announcement, directive, or information to all staff in a Ministry or 
the Service at large.
items.explanation:
A circular is used to communicate an official announcement, directive, or 
information to all staff in a Ministry or the Service at large.
499

items.question: What should a civil servant do with a circular that requires action to be taken?
options: Place a copy in the relevant file and pass it to the appropriate officer for action.
items.explanation:
The file copy of each such circular should be placed in the file relevant to the 
subject matter and should be passed to the appropriate officer for action to be taken.

items.question: What is the purpose of the "Official Secret Act"?
options: To encourage civil servants to share all government information.
To prevent the leaking of government secrets to unauthorized persons.
To protect civil servants from prosecution.

To allow for the free flow of information to the public.
□ .
items.explanation:
The Official Secret Act is to prevent the leaking of government secrets to 
unauthorized persons.

items.question: What is considered a very serious offense for a Civil Servant?
options: Unauthorized disclosure of any information acquired in the course of their duties.
items.explanation:
Unauthorized disclosure of any information acquired in the course of their duties 
is a very serious offence.

items.question: All Civil Servants are required to take what?
items.explanation: All Civil Servants are required to take an Oath of Secrecy.

items.question: What is the purpose of having a "confidential" classification for a document?
options: To make it difficult to access.
To protect information that could be harmful if disclosed.
To hide its contents from other Ministries.

To make it a permanent record.
500
Q .
items.explanation:
This is a standard concept of document classification. Although not explicitly 
stated in this chapter, the concepts of the Official Secret Act and the Oath of Secrecy imply the 
necessity of protecting confidential information.

items.question: What is the purpose of the "TOP SECRET" classification?
options: To protect information that could cause grave damage to the government if disclosed.
To protect information that is not important.
To make it difficult to access.
To hide its contents from other Ministries.a .
items.explanation:
This is a standard concept of document classification, implied by the seriousness 
of unauthorized disclosure.

items.question:
What should a Civil Servant do if they come across a classified document that they are not 
authorized to view?
options: Read it anyway to be informed.
Report it to their superior officer.
items.explanation:
This is a logical action in line with the "Oath of Secrecy" and the seriousness of 
unauthorized disclosure.

items.question: How should communication addressed to Overseas Government never be made?
options: Through the Ministry of Foreign Affairs.
items.explanation:
Communication addressed to Overseas Government should never be made 
directly.

items.question:
How should a schedule officer convey information to the Ministry of Foreign Affairs for 
overseas communication?
options: By quoting verbatim specific extracts or conveying the gist of the information.
items.explanation:
The Schedule Officer should write a letter conveying the gist of the information to 
be passed on or he may quote verbatim specific extracts from the letter.

items.question: What is the purpose of the Executive Council of the Federation?
options: To determine Government policies on various matters.
items.explanation:
The Executive Council of the Federation... has the duty and responsibility of 
determining Government policies on various matters.

items.question: What is the role of a Civil Servant regarding Executive Council Papers?
options: To make a policy pronouncement without approval.

To provide the political leadership with instruments to administer government at the highest 
level.
To keep them confidential from their superiors.
items.explanation:
A key purpose of the Civil Service is to provide the political leadership with the 
instruments with which to administer Government at the highest level, i.e. the Executive Council 
of the Federation.

items.question: What should officers do regarding Executive Council papers?
options: Be very careful with the facts, figures, and data provided.
Ignore them unless they are directly addressed.

Not be concerned with them at all.
□ .
items.explanation:
The officer must therefore be very careful with the facts, figures, and data 
provided in such papers because the political leadership would be using them to make policies.

items.question: What is the style of address for submitting minutes through official channels?
options: By placing the most junior officer's title first.

By placing the most senior officer's title first followed by others in descending order of status 
prefixed by "through".
By placing the most senior officer's title last.
items.explanation:
The style of address in general use for the submission of minutes through official 
channels is by placing the most senior officer’s title first followed by other officers in descending 
order of status prefixed by the word “through”.

items.question:
What happens to papers routed upwards through official channels after a decision has been 
reached?
options: They are routed back in the same way to all intermediate officers.
They are kept only by the higher authority.
They are sent to a different department.a .
items.explanation:
Papers routed upwards through official channels are routed back in the same 
way, and all intermediate officers are, therefore, acquainted with decisions reached by the 
higher authority.

items.question: What should intermediate officers do with papers routed through them for information only?
options: They should not delay them unnecessarily.
They should take a copy for themselves.
items.explanation:
Intermediate officers should not delay unnecessarily papers which are routed 
through them for information only.

items.question: What is the purpose of the "Duplicate Note-Book System"?
options: To keep track of personal notes.
To record the file number, date, and destination whenever an officer sends out a file.
items.explanation:
Some officers still use the duplicate note-book system for recording transmission 
of files whereby whenever an officer sends out a file, his Clerk records its number, date and 
destination in a duplicate note-book.

items.question:
What should a Clerk do with the top copy of their list from the duplicate note-book system at 
the end of each day?
options: Give it to the officer in charge of the most appropriate Registry.
items.explanation:
On completion of each page and always at the end of each day, the Clerk must 
give the top copy of his list to the officer in charge of the most appropriate Registry, which 
records the movements on the Absent File Cards.

items.question: What is the purpose of the Absent File Cards?
options: To track the location of files.
To list the names of all officers.
items.explanation:
The Absent File Cards record the movements of files, which helps to track their 
location.

items.question: What should a minute writer ensure when preparing a minute?
options: That it is free of all unnecessary details and information.
That it is full of personal opinions.
items.explanation: The minute must be free of all unnecessary details and information.

items.question: What should a schedule officer do when an enquiry is addressed to the wrong Ministry?
options: Reply to the letter and direct the sender to the appropriate Ministry.
Forward the letter to the appropriate Ministry and notify the sender.

Keep the letter in their file.
Answer: C.
items.explanation:
Where an enquiry from the public is addressed to the wrong Ministry or 
Department, the proper procedure is to forward the original letter to the appropriate Ministry or 
Department and to write a brief note to the sender informing him that the letter has been 
forwarded to the correct office.

items.question: What is the definition of "Flimsies"?
options: Copies of out-going letters and written submissions.
Copies of out-going letters and written submissions.
items.explanation: Flimsies:- Copies of out-going letters and written submissions.

items.question:
What is the purpose of including a "B.U." in a file note?
504
options: To indicate the file is to be destroyed.
To indicate the matter can be followed up on a specified date.
To indicate a final decision has been made.
To indicate the file is closed.3 .
items.explanation:
A note is made on the last page of the main file describing the action which has 
been taken and ending with a B.U. so that the matter can be followed up on a specified date.

items.question:
What is the consequence of a Minister making a policy pronouncement without Executive 
Council approval?
options: The Minister may cause massive discomfort for their principals or even lose their job.
items.explanation:
The chapter cautions an officer not to allow a Minister to make a policy 
pronouncement unless such a Council approval has been secured. Otherwise, you may cause 
your principals massive discomfort or even their jobs.

items.question: What kind of language should be used in government communication?
options: Colloquial and informal.Q Correct Answer: : B.
items.explanation: Your style and choice of words should be simple, smooth and free- flowing..

items.question: What is the purpose of including references to enclosures in the letter itself?
options: To make it easy to find them.
To ensure the enclosures do not become detached from the letter.3 .
items.explanation:
The abbreviations Enc. or Encs. should be included at the foot of the letter as a 
reminder that the enclosures are there, and that they are not detached from the letter.

items.question: What should be done with the second and subsequent pages of a letter with attachments?
options: They should be similarly marked with the reference of the letter.
They should be signed by the writer.Q .
items.explanation: The second and subsequent pages of the letter should be similarly marked.

items.question: What is a primary function of the Civil Service in relation to the Executive Council?
options: To keep the Council's decisions secret.
To prepare and present papers and memoranda on behalf of the Minister to the Council.
To write the laws for the Council.
items.explanation:
The Civil Service provides the instruments to administer government at the 
highest level, which includes preparing and presenting papers and memoranda on behalf of the 
Minister to the Council.

items.question: What should an officer ensure about papers presented to the Executive Council?
options: That they are based on personal opinion.
That they are based on verified facts, figures, and data.
That they are as short as possible.

That they are as long as possible.
□ ..
items.explanation:
The officer must therefore be very careful with the facts, figures, and data 
provided in such papers.

items.question: What is the purpose of keeping a "Reference" in the top right-hand corner of a letter?
options: To show the personal details of the sender.
To show the date of the letter.
To uniquely identify the letter for record-keeping.
items.explanation:
Every letter should bear a reference number, which serves as a unique identifier 
for record-keeping purposes.

items.question: What is the purpose of a "file copy" of a circular?
options: To be discarded after the circular is read.
To be placed in the file relevant to the subject matter.
To be sent to the public.

To be kept by the officer personally.
□ .
items.explanation:
The file copy of each such circular should be placed in the file relevant to the 
subject matter
506

items.question: When an officer receives a circular that does not require action, what should they do?
options: Minute the file to others for information and disposal.
items.explanation:
If he does not require actually to initiate any action he will either merely minute the 
file to others for information and disposal or he will file it.

items.question: What does the term "Minute/Memo" mean?
options: A written submission to present a view or position on an issue.
A short report for a supervisor.
items.explanation: Minute/Memo:- Written submission to present a view or position on an issue.

items.question: What does the term "Brief mean?
options: A written submission on an extant position on a subject matter.
A list of all government policies.a ..
items.explanation: Brief:- Written submission on an extant position on a subject matter.

items.question: What is the purpose of the phrase "For the attention of?
options: To address a specific person within a department.
items.explanation:
The phrase "For the attention of is used when a letter is addressed to a 
department or organization but is intended for a specific person within that organization.

items.question: Who is the "Officer Taking Over" in the context of a handing-over note?
options: The officer who is leaving the post.
The officer who is taking up the post.
items.explanation:
The Officer Taking Over is the person who is assuming the responsibilities of a 
post from a departing officer.

items.question: What is the "Head of Department's" role regarding copies of his own letters?
options: He circulates them to all his subordinates.
He does not circulate them to his subordinates.
He gives them to the most junior officers.

He files them in the confidential registry.
□ ..
items.explanation:
It is not customary for copies of the Head of Department’s own letters to be 
circulated to his subordinates.

items.question:
When a Head of Department wants a subordinate to see a copy of his letter, what does he 
do?
options: He minutes the relevant file to the person concerned.
He tells them about it verbally.
items.explanation:
When a Head of Department considers it necessary, he merely minutes the 
relevant file to the person concerned - who then sees the copy of the letter.

items.question: Why is it important to include file references on the file copy of a letter?
options: To avoid unnecessary research work later on.
To show that the letter is important.
items.explanation:
If this is done when the original letter is being written, unnecessary research work 
could be avoided later on.

items.question: What does a "Book File" contain?
options: A list of all government policies.
A collection of letters and minutes arranged chronologically.
A list of all government employees.

A personal diary of an officer.
Q .
items.explanation:
The Book File system is the method of filing whereby letters and minutes are all 
enclosed on the left-hand side-face to cover in chronological order and are numbered in the 
same fashion as a book.
508

items.question: How is a memo different from a letter?
options: A memo is used for external communication, while a letter is for internal communication.
A memo is for internal communication, while a letter is for external communication.
A memo is always longer than a letter.
A memo is always shorter than a letter.a .
items.explanation:
A memorandum is a type of correspondence used to communicate internally 
between officers within the same department or Ministry, while an official letter is used to 
communicate with other Ministries and the public.

items.question:
What is the first thing a schedule officer should do when preparing to write a 
communication?
options: Look up the facts, figures, and data.

Ask a junior officer to do it.
Q .
items.explanation:
The officer must be careful with the facts, figures, and data in his writing. This 
implies the need to verify them before drafting.

items.question: What is the purpose of "fairs" in the context of government business?
options: To correct errors in a draft.
To provide a clean, final version of a document.

To send a document to another department.
□ .
items.explanation:
Fairing refers to the process of creating a clean, final version of a document after 
a draft has been prepared and approved.

items.question: What does a "Minute" on a file often start with?
options: A brief statement of the matter at issue.
items.explanation: A minute may be built up as follows: (i) state briefly the matter at issue.

items.question: What is the final step in the communication process after a letter is drafted and approved?
options: Rerouting it to all intermediate officers.3 .
items.explanation:
The final step is to have the approved document signed and dispatched to its 
intended recipient.
Part 2: Notes on the Nigerian Foreign Service

items.question:
What happens to non-career Ambassadors when the President who appointed them ceases 
to hold office?
options:
They are automatically re-appointed by the new President.
510
They continue in their roles until their term expires.

They cease to function.
D They are transferred to other ministries.
□
items.explanation:
Non-career Ambassadors hold their appointments at the pleasure of the President 
and cease to function when the President ceases to hold office.
75: What training is required for newly appointed Foreign Service Officers?
A A six-month orientation course.
B A one-year training course in diplomatic practice at the Foreign Service Academy.
C An online certification in international relations.
D. On-the-job training at a foreign mission.3 Correct Answer: B
Explanation: All newly appointed Foreign Service Officers are expected to attend a one-year 
training course in diplomatic practice at the Foreign Service Academy.
76: What happens to officers who fail the Foreign Service Academy training course after two 
attempts?
A. They are given a third attempt.
B. They are redeployed to home ministries.
C They are relieved of their appointments.
D They are allowed to continue their service in a different capacity.3 Correct Answer: C.
Explanation: Officers who fail to pass the Foreign Service Academy training course after two 
attempts are relieved of their appointments.
77: What is the rule regarding transfer or lateral conversion into the Foreign Service above GL 
10?
A. It is allowed with special Presidential approval.
B. It is generally encouraged for experienced officers.
C. It is not allowed due to the uniqueness of the Foreign Service.
D. It is permissible only for officers with specialized skills3 Correct Answer: C
Explanation: Due to the uniqueness of the Foreign Service, no transfer or lateral conversion 
above GL 10 is allowed.

items.question:
How are officers from Home Ministries posted to Nigerian Diplomatic Missions abroad 
designated?
items.explanation:
Officers from Home Ministries posted to Nigerian Diplomatic Missions abroad are 
designated as Attaches in charge of the responsibilities of their Ministries.

items.question:
What is the consequence if a Foreign Service Officer's marriage to a foreigner is deemed 
not to be in the interest of the Service?
options: The officer will be required to leave the Foreign Service or return to the Home Service.
The officer's spouse will be denied entry to Nigeria.

The officer is placed on probation.
□
items.explanation:
If the marriage will not be in the interest of the Service, the officer will be required 
to leave the Foreign Service or return to the Home Service.

items.question: Are Foreign Service Officers allowed to accept gifts or presentations for services rendered?
options: Yes, if the value is minimal.
Yes, if declared to the Ministry.
No, they are not allowed to accept gifts or presentations from any person. 14
items.explanation:
No Foreign Service Officer is allowed to accept gifts or presentations, whether in 
form of money or otherwise, from any person for services rendered or to be rendered.

items.question: What is Nigeria's Foreign Policy primarily a reflection of?
options: Her political, economic, social, and cultural circumstances.
The foreign policies of allied nations.
items.explanation:
Nigeria's Foreign Policy is a reflection of her political, economic, social, and 
cultural circumstances and, to some extent, the immediate and remote domestic circumstances 
in Nigeria.

items.question: What is the major consideration in the pursuit of Nigeria's foreign policy choice of strategies?
options:
Global peace Initiatives.
C.Her national interest.
items.explanation:
The major consideration in the pursuit of Nigeria's foreign policy choice of 
strategies is her national interest.

items.question: What is the official protocol for MDAs and other bodies embarking on foreign trips?
options: They must obtain a travel visa from the Ministry of Foreign Affairs.

They are not expected to embark on foreign trips without informing the Ministry of Foreign 
Affairs.
C They only need to inform the Presidency.
D They can embark on trips independently if self-funded.
Q .
items.explanation:
MDAs and other bodies are not expected to embark on foreign trips without 
informing the Ministry of Foreign Affairs.
Part 3: Conditions of Service

items.question: What is the primary purpose of the National Council on Establishments?
options:
To manage the daily operations of the Civil Service.
B To negotiate salaries for all government employees.

To facilitate inter-governmental consultations on establishment matters and avoid 
unnecessary disparities in general conditions of servicel.

To approve all new appointments and promotions within the Civil Service.
Q 513
items.explanation:
The National Council on Establishments was created to facilitate 
inter-governmental consultations on establishment matters and to prevent unnecessary 
differences in the general conditions of service across all Federal and State Civil Services.

items.question: Who is the Chairman of the National Council on Establishments?
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
The Head of each state's Civil Service.Q .
items.explanation:
The council is composed of the Heads of Service of the States, and the Head of 
the Civil Service of the Federation serves as the Chairman.

items.question: How often does the National Council on Establishments meet?
options: As needed, when a new President takes office.
Only when there is a major policy change.3 .
items.explanation:
The National Council on Establishments meets at least once a year to deliberate 
on establishment matters.

items.question: What is one of the functions of the National Council on Establishments?
options:
To manage the budget for all government ministries.
B To serve as a consultative body on matters of salary structures, gradings, and conditions of 
service4.
To oversee the day-to-day work of civil servants.
To hire and fire civil servants.3 .
items.explanation:
The Council acts as a consultative body for the governments of the Federation on 
matters such as staff establishments, salary structures, gradings, and conditions of service to 
achieve uniformity in the Nigerian Civil Services.

items.question: What is the condition regarding transfers or lateral conversions into the Foreign Service?
options: It is encouraged for experienced staff from other ministries.
It is not allowed above GL 10 due to the uniqueness of the Foreign Service.
It is a common practice for promotions.
It is permissible with special approval from the President.3 .
items.explanation:
Due to the unique nature of the Foreign Service, lateral conversions or transfers 
into it are not permitted for officers above Grade Level

items.question:
What is the mandatory training period for newly appointed Foreign Service Officers at the 
Foreign Service Academy?
items.explanation:
All new Foreign Service Officers must attend a mandatory one-year training 
course in diplomatic practice at the Foreign Service Academy.

items.question:
What is the consequence for an officer who fails the Foreign Service Academy training 
course after two attempts?
options: They are redeployed to a home ministry.
They are given a final third attempt.
They are relieved of their appointment.

They are placed on a longer probation period.
□
items.explanation:
If an officer fails to pass the training course at the Foreign Service Academy after 
two attempts, they will be relieved of their appointment.

items.question: What is the rule regarding married Foreign Service Officers and their spouses?
options: Their spouses must also be employed in the Civil Service.
The officer must get a government-issued marriage certificate.
They are expected to provide full particulars of their spouses for registration.

Spouses are required to undergo a separate training program.
Q .
items.explanation:
All married officers in the Foreign Service are required to provide full details of 
their spouses for registration purposes.

items.question: What is required if a Foreign Service Officer intends to marry a foreigner?
options: They must get a promotion first.
They must obtain the prior permission of the GovernmentlO.
The officer must resign from the service.

The officer's spouse must become a Nigerian citizen.
Q
items.explanation:
An officer who wants to marry a foreigner must first seek the government's 
permission.
515

items.question:
What happens if a Foreign Service Officer’s marriage to a foreigner is deemed not to be in 
the interest of the Service?
options: The officer will be suspended without pay.
The officer will be demoted to a lower grade level.
The officer will be required to leave the Foreign Service or return to the Home Service11.
items.explanation:
If the marriage to a foreigner is not in the best interest of the Service, the officer 
will be required to either leave the Foreign Service or return to the Home Service.

items.question:
What is the official protocol for Ministerial Departments, Agencies, and other bodies 
embarking on foreign trips?
options: They must get permission from the Presidency.

They are not expected to embark on foreign trips without informing the Ministry of Foreign 
Affairs12.
They can go on trips independently as long as they are self-funded.

They must inform the Head of the Civil Service.
□ .
items.explanation:
MDAs and other government bodies are not supposed to travel abroad without 
first informing the Ministry of Foreign Affairs.
97.: Is a Foreign Service Officer allowed to accept gifts or presentations for services rendered? 
A. Yes, if they are of a small value.
B. Yes, as long as they declare them to the government.
C. No, they are not allowed to accept gifts or presentations from anyone for services rendered 
or to be rendered13.
D. Only from other diplomats as a sign of goodwill.
□ Correct Answer: C.
Explanation: Foreign Service Officers are forbidden from accepting any gifts or presentations, 
whether money or otherwise, for services they have rendered or will render.

items.question: What is the primary consideration in the pursuit of Nigeria's foreign policy?
options: The foreign policies of other African nations.
Economic benefits from a single trading partner.a .
items.explanation:
The main factor considered in Nigeria's foreign policy strategies is her national 
interest.
516

items.question: What is Nigeria's Foreign Policy a reflection of?
options: The policies of its colonial past.
Its political, economic, social, and cultural circumstances.

The prevailing global political ideologies.
a .
items.explanation:
Nigeria's foreign policy reflects its political, economic, social, and cultural 
circumstances.

items.question: What is the proper channel of communication for MDAs with foreign missions in Nigeria?
options:
Through the Ministry of Foreign Affairs.
D Through the National Assembly.
Q ..
items.explanation:
The document states that MDAs often violate the proper channel of 
communication, which is through the Ministry of Foreign Affairs[cite: 3288]. Proper and effective 
coordination is expected between the Ministry's Consular Section, Foreign Missions, and MDAs 
on matters like visas
517
Section G
COMPUTER-BASED TEST (CBT) PROMOTION EXAMINATION FOR OFFICERS IN 
GRADES 15 TO 17 IN THE FEDERAL CIVIL SERVICE OF THE FEDERATION ON 
CIVIL SERVICE HANDBOOK..
Instruction: This examination consists of 100 multiple-choice questions designed to assess your 
readiness for promotion to the next level in the Federal Civil Service. You have exactly one 
hour (60 minutes) to complete all questions; manage your time wisely to ensure you attempt 
every question. Read each question carefully before selecting the best answer from the options.
Pension Administration

items.question:
What is the purpose of the Contributory Pension Scheme established by the Pension Reform 
Act of June 25th, 2004?
options: To fund government projects and infrastructure.
To provide loans and financial assistance to civil servants.

To ensure that every person in the Public Service or Private Sector receives their retirement 
benefits when due, assist workers in saving for old age, and establish uniform rules for pension 
administration.
To manage the national economy and control inflation

items.question: Who is covered by the Contributory Pension Scheme in the Private Sector?
options: All employees, regardless of the number of workers in the organization.
Employees in organizations with a minimum of 10 workers.
Employees in organizations employing 5 workers or more.

items.question:
Which group of employees was exempted from the Contributory Pension Scheme at its 
inception?
options: All employees with more than 10 years of service.

Existing pensioners and employees who had 3 years or less to retire.
C.Employees earning above a certain salary threshold.
Only the military and police personnel.

items.question:
For most public service employees, what is the minimum contribution rate by the employee to 
the Contributory Pension Scheme?
What is the minimum contribution rate by the employer for most public service employees?
How do the contribution rates differ for the Military under the Contributory Pension Scheme?
options: The military does not contribute to the scheme.
The employee contributes 7.5% and the employer contributes 7.5%.

The employee contributes a minimum of 2.5% and the employer contributes a minimum of 
12.5%.
The military's contribution is a flat rate determined annually.

items.question:
What is the minimum total contribution for the Contributory Pension Scheme if an employer 
elects to bear the full burden?
A.Not less than 7.5% of the monthly emoluments.
B.Not less than 15% of the monthly emoluments.
options: Not less than 20% of the monthly emoluments.
The employer cannot bear the full burden.

items.question: Where are the total pension contributions paid?
options: Directly to the employee's bank account.
To the Central Bank of Nigeria.

items.question: Who is responsible for managing and investing the pension funds?
What is the role of the Pension Fund Administrator (PFA) in relation to the employee?
options: To issue a statement of account annually.
To open a Retirement Savings Account (RSA) and allocate a Personal Identity Number (PIN).
To deduct contributions from the employee's salary.
To provide loans to the employee.

items.question:
How often is the Pension Fund Administrator (PFA) required to issue a statement of 
account?
options:
Only upon request by the employee.
519

items.question:
How often does an employee have the freedom to move from one Pension Fund 
Administrator to another?
Are voluntary pension contributions taxable?
options: They are only taxable if they exceed a certain amount.
The handbook does not provide this information.

items.question:
When is a pensioner entitled to make withdrawals from his/her Retirement Savings 
Account?
options: At any time, provided they have a valid reason.
After attaining the age of 50 years.
After retirement or attaining the age of 50 years, whichever falls later.
The handbook does not specify the age or conditions.

items.question:
What are the three components of the monthly emolument used to calculate pension 
contributions?
options: Basic salary, hazard allowance, and medical allowance.
Basic salary, housing allowance, and transport allowance.
Gross income, tax, and social security.

Net salary, bonuses, and overtime pay.

According to the Civil Service Handbook, what is considered an important asset for most

Why is it crucial for schedule officers to be well-informed?

Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation, Office of the Secretary to the

Guidelines for Appointment, Promotion and Discipline issued by Federal Civil Service

The Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria.

items.question:
What is an example of a publication from a national or international body that is a source of 
information?
options: Publications of National and International Management Development Institutes.

items.question: What statistical data source is listed in the handbook?
options: Publications of the Federal Office of Statistics.

items.question: What are MDAs News Letters listed as a source of information?

options: Yes, officers must only use these sources.
No, officers are encouraged to explore and consult other sources.
Yes, but only for new employees.

Contact information for all government officials.

items.question:
Why should schedule officers familiarize themselves with sources of information early in

options: To be able to make sound decisions.
To learn the history of the civil service.

options: It should be from a senior official.
It should be accurate and based on facts and figures.

It should be easily accessible.
Miscellaneous Notes and General Information

items.question:
What is the purpose of typing the appropriate wording in block capitals and underlining it just 
above the inside address of a letter?
options: To make the letter look more official.
To ensure the letter is given special attention by the recipient.

To indicate that the letter is to be sent by a special means such as registered post, airmail, or 
under secret/confidential coverl.
To inform the recipient of the sender's identity.

items.question:
According to the handbook, what is a key responsibility of drivers regarding their vehicle's
