523
options:
They must customize the vehicle to their liking.
B They should ensure the vehicles are washed and polished at regular intervals and that the 
engines are also kept clean.

They are only responsible for the vehicle's internal components.
D They are responsible for paying for car washes.

items.question: What must drivers do immediately if they are involved in an accident?
options: Report at once to the Transport Officer.
Wait for the police to arrive before doing anything else.

items.question: Which document are drivers required to always hold when on duty?
options: A copy of the Civil Service Handbook.

items.question:
What is the standard time for the red line to be drawn across the Attendance Register each 
day?
options: At the end of the day.
It is left to the discretion of the supervisor.

items.question:
What action should be taken regarding latecomers who sign below the red line in the 
Attendance Register?
options: They should be given a warning.
They should be required to explain why they were Iate13.
Nothing, as long as they sign the register.

items.question:
What is a key responsibility of the officers responsible for staff regarding the Attendance 
Register?
options: They must sign the register for their staff.

They should keep a close watch on the register and take appropriate action against habitual
524
offenders15.
They must ensure the register is always clean and tidy.
They are responsible for drawing the red line.

items.question: What are the general office hours for Federal offices?
options: 8:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Monday to Friday17.
They vary depending on the Ministry.

items.question: When should drivers inform Transport Officers about vehicle servicing?
options: Only when a major defect is detected.
In good times, when vehicles are due for routine servicing19.
They should only inform them after the service is completed.
They are not responsible for informing anyone about servicing.

According to the handbook, what is one of the daily checks a driver must perform on their

options: Checking the air pressure in the tires.

Ensuring the radiators are full, the oil and battery levels are correct, and that there is 
sufficient petrol in the tanks21.
Taking the vehicle for a test drive.

items.question:
The notes for secretarial staff indicate that letters with attachments should include what?
A A separate table of contents.
options: A reference to the attachment in the body of the letter.
An indication that there are enclosures23.
A brief summary of the contents of the attachment.

items.question: What should be done with a letter that is to be sent under confidential cover?
options: It should be hand-delivered by a senior official.

The phrase "under confidential cover" should be typed in block capitals and underlined just

It should be marked with a special stamp.
The envelope should be sealed with wax.

items.question:
What are drivers responsible for in addition to the care and maintenance of government 
vehicles?
options:
Ensuring the vehicle has a full tank of petrol at all times.
525

Ensuring they wear their uniforms when on duty27.
D Driving at a specific speed limit.

items.question:
What are the two occasions that staff on GL. 03-13 are required to sign the Attendance 
Register?
options: At the end of the day only.
On arrival in the morning as well as at close of work at the end of the day29.
Whenever they leave the office for a short time.

items.question: What is the consequence for staff who habitually arrive late?
options: They are given extra work to compensate for lost time.
They are docked a day's pay.
The officers responsible for staff should take appropriate action against them31.
They are required to serve a period of detention.

items.question: What should a driver do if a vehicle defect is detected, even if it is minor?
options: Wait for the next routine service appointment.
Report immediately to the officer-in-charge of transport33.
Inform a colleague to report it for them.

items.question: What is the role of a red line in the attendance register?
options: To highlight the names of new employees.
To indicate which employees have been promoted.
To differentiate between staff who arrived on time and those who arrived Iate35.
To separate the names of men and women.

items.question: Which of the following is NOT one of the daily checks for a driver?
options: Checking that the radiators are full.

items.question:
When a letter has more than one attachment, what should be included at the foot of the 
letter?
options:
A brief summary of each attachment.
526

items.question: What is the main purpose of the Civil Service Handbook?
options: To serve as a historical document of the Nigerian Civil Service.
To provide guidance and information to newly appointed civil servants.
To act as a complete legal document for all civil service matters.
To serve as a reference guide for all civil servants, irrespective of their grade or position..

items.question: Who is the head of the Civil Service of the Federation?
options: The Secretary to the Government of the Federation.
The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
The Chairman of the Federal Civil Service Commission.

items.question: What is the primary role of the Federal Civil Service Commission (FCSC)?
options: To manage the day-to-day operations of all Ministries.
To serve as the employer of first resort for all civil servants.
To oversee appointments, promotions, and discipline within the Civil Service.
To provide welfare services for civil servants.

items.question: How many levels of government are recognized in Nigeria's Civil Service structure?
options: Four (Federal, State, Local Government, and Ministries).

Which of the following is NOT a fundamental principle of the Civil Service?

What is the official time for the red line to be drawn across the Attendance Register each

What is the minimum contribution rate by the employee to the Contributory Pension Scheme

Basic salary, housing allowance, and transport allowance.

options: To publish daily news about government activities.

To serve as a guide for civil service examinations.

items.question: Which of the following is considered a source of information for schedule officers?
According to the handbook, what is undermined by obsolete and incorrect information?

options:
It should have the appropriate wording typed in block capitals and underlined just above the

It should be marked with a special stamp.
The envelope should be sealed with wax.

items.question: What is a key responsibility of drivers regarding vehicle maintenance?
options: To ensure vehicles are washed and polished at regular intervals.

items.question: What is the primary purpose of writing a "Minute"?
options: To convey a short official message or opinion on a file.

items.question: What is the main difference between a "Letter" and a "Circular"?
options: A letter is a formal communication, while a circular is informal.
A letter is for a specific individual, while a circular is for a group of people or offices.
A letter is used for external communication, while a circular is for internal communication.
A letter is a short message, while a circular is a lengthy document.

options: The Director of Finance and Accounts.

options:
They must get permission from their immediate supervisor.
B They must apply to the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.

options: A formal request for information from a superior officer.
A document asking an officer to explain a specific act of misconduct.
A general question about a policy or procedure.

options: Writing a monthly report on all government activities.
Keeping accurate information based on facts and figures.

options: Based on an officer's seniority alone.
Based on an officer's educational qualifications.

items.question: What is a "Circular Letter" used for?
options: To send a personal message to an officer.

To formally introduce a new policy.

items.question:
What is the purpose of "Financial Regulations"?
A To set the salaries of civil servants.
options: To provide the rules and procedures for managing government finances.
To outline the budget for each Ministry.

To provide guidelines for international trade.
532

items.question: How are files numbered in the Civil Service?
options: Based on the date they were created.
Based on a unique numerical and alphabetical system.
Based on the officer in charge.

items.question:
What is the significance of a "Handing Over Note"?
A It is a personal note from a superior officer to a subordinate.
options: It is a confidential document detailing the officer's performance.

It is a document prepared by an officer leaving a post to brief their successor on the status of 
their responsibilities.
It is a receipt for government property.

items.question: What is the main difference between a "Civil Servant" and a "Public Servant"?
options: A civil servant works for the government, while a public servant works for the public.

A civil servant is part of the core civil service, while a public servant includes all government 
employees, including those in parastatals and the military.
A civil servant is a political appointee, while a public servant is a permanent employee.

items.question:
What should an officer do if they receive an official document that is not addressed to 
them?
options: Forward it to the correct recipient immediately.
Read the document to understand its contents.

Return it to the sender.
533

Section G
items.explanation:
Explanation 1: The objectives of the scheme are clearly stated in the handbook: to ensure that 
every person in either the Public Service or the Private Sector receives their retirement benefits 
as and when due, to assist careless workers to save for old age, and to establish a uniform set 
of rules, regulations, and standards for the administration and payment of retirement benefits.
Explanation 2.: The Pension Reform Act covers all employees in any organization in the Private 
Sector that employs 5 workers or more.
Explanation 3: The handbook specifies that "existing pensioners and employees who had 3 
years or less to retire were exempted from the scheme".
Explanation 4: Each employee is expected to contribute a minimum of 7.5% of his/her monthly 
emolument, which includes basic salary, housing, and transport allowances.
Explanation 5: The employer also contributes a matching 7.5% of the employee's monthly 
emoluments.
Explanation 6: For the military, the contribution rates are a minimum of 2.5% by the employee 
and 12.5% by the employer.
Explanation 7: An employer may agree to bear the full burden of the scheme, provided that the 
total contribution is not less than 15% of the monthly emoluments of the employee.
Explanation 8: The total contribution is paid out by the employer directly to a Pension Assets 
Custodian (PAC).
Explanation 9: The funds are "managed and invested by the Pension Fund Administrator (PFA) 
of the employee's choice".
Explanation 10: The PFA is expected to open a Retirement Savings Account (RSA) for all 
employees and allocate Personal Identity Numbers (PIN)..
Explanation 11: The PFA is required to issue a statement of account at least once every quarter.
Explanation 12: The employee has the freedom to move from one PFA to another once a year
Explanation 13: The handbook states, "the contributions are not taxable, but voluntary 
contributions are taxable".
535
Explanation 14: No pensioner is entitled to make any withdrawals from their Retirement Savings 
Account "unless he/she has retired or attained the age of 50 years, whichever falls later"
Explanation 15: The pension contribution is calculated based on "basic salary, housing and 
transport allowances".
Explanation 16: The handbook states that "An important asset to most scheduled officers is 
accurate information based on facts and figures".
Explanation 17: Officers should do all they can "to be well-informed so that their efficiency could 
be enhanced".
Explanation 18: The document lists "Public Service Rules" as one of the sources of information.
Explanation 19: "Financial Regulations" are listed as a source of information in the chapter.
Explanation 20: The document specifies that "Circulars and Circular Letters of Ministries and 
Departments, especially Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation, Office of the 
Secretary to the Government of the Federation and Federal Civil Service Commission" are 
sources of information.
Explanation 21: "Official Gazettes of the Federal and State Governments of Nigeria" are 
identified as a source.
Explanation 22: "The Revenue, Expenditure and Capital Estimates of the Federal and States 
Governments of Nigeria" are listed as a source.
Explanation 23: The document lists "Laws of the Federation of Nigeria" and "Laws of the various 
Nigerian States" as sources of information for schedule officers.
Explanation 24: The handbook mentions "Guidelines for Appointment, Promotion and Discipline 
issued by Federal Civil Service Commission" as a source.
Explanation 25: The document refers to the "Federal Civil Service Hand Book" as a source of 
information.
Explanation 26: "Reports of Commissions of Enquiries" are included in the list of sources.
Explanation 27: The document lists "Government White Papers" as a source of information.
536
Explanation 28: "Publications of National and International Management Development 
Institutes" are mentioned.
Explanation 29: The document lists "Publications of the Federal Office of Statistics".
Explanation 30: "News Letters of MDAs" are listed as a source.
Explanation 31: The chapter concludes with the statement, "As the above list is not exhaustive, 
Officers are encouraged to explore and consult other sources".
Explanation 32: The text notes that "such factors as maturity and sound judgment are easily 
undermined by misinformation".
Explanation 33: The Staff Lists of the Federal Government would contain information about the

and enhance their efficiency, which helps them in making sound decisions.

figures" is an important asset.
Explanation 36: The handbook specifies that letters to be sent by registered post, airmail, or 
under secret/confidential cover should have the appropriate wording typed in block capitals and

Explanation 37: Drivers are responsible for the care and maintenance of their assigned 
government vehicles, which includes ensuring they are washed, polished, and have clean

must report at once to the Transport Officer.
Explanation 39: The handbook states that drivers must ensure they always hold valid driving 
licenses.

than 8:00 a.m. so that latecomers are identified.
537

Attendance Register on arrival at the Office in the morning as well as at close of work at the end

Explanation 42: The document states that latecomers "should be required to explain why they

Explanation 44: The hours of work in Federal offices are from 8:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Monday

Explanation 45: Drivers "should inform Transport Officers in good time when vehicles are due

Section H
COMPUTER-BASED TEST (CBT) PROMOTION EXAMINATION FOR OFFICERS IN 
GRADES 15 TO 17 IN THE FEDERAL CIVIL SERVICE OF THE FEDERATION ON 
CIVIL SERVICE HANDBOOK..
Instruction: This examination consists of 100 multiple-choice questions designed to assess your 
readiness for promotion to the next level in the Federal Civil Service. You have exactly one 
hour (60 minutes) to complete all questions; manage your time wisely to ensure you attempt 
every question. Read each question carefully before selecting the best answer from the options.

Section H
items.explanation:
Explanation 1: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation is the highest-ranking career 
officer in the Civil Service and is appointed by the President.
Explanation 2: The Permanent Secretary is the administrative head and accounting officer of a 
Ministry, responsible for its general administration and finances.
Explanation 3: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation is responsible for the overall 
management of the Civil Service and advising the President on related matters.
Explanation 4: The Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation is responsible for 
ensuring that civil service rules and regulations are not contravened.
Explanation 5: The Personnel Division is the administrative division within a Ministry responsible 
for all matters related to staff.
Explanation 6: A typical Federal Ministry has three administrative departments: Personnel, 
Finance and Accounts, and Planning, Research and Statistics.
Explanation 7.: The Planning, Research and Statistics Department is responsible for research, 
data collection, and developing strategic plans for the Ministry.
Explanation 8: The General Administration Division handles a variety of day-to-day tasks, 
including security, transport, and general office maintenance.
Explanation 9: Extra-Ministerial Departments are government offices or agencies that are not 
attached to a particular Ministry but report directly to the President.
Explanation 10: The head of a parastatal or government agency is typically a Director General 
or Executive Secretary.
Explanation 11: Impartiality is the principle of performing one's duties without bias, favoritism, or 
prejudice.
Explanation 12: Anonymity means that officers should not publicly express their views on 
political issues or publicly criticize government policies.
Explanation 13: The principle of Political Neutrality ensures that the Civil Service remains 
committed to serving the government of the day, regardless of the political party in power.
560
Explanation 16: The General Order (now replaced by the Public Service Rules) was the set of 
regulations that governed the conduct and conditions of service of civil servants.
Explanation 15: A confirmed officer is one who has been confirmed in their appointment and 
holds a permanent and pensionable position.
Explanation 16: An officer is normally on probation for a period of two years before being 
considered for confirmation.
Explanation 17: If an officer's performance is not satisfactory during probation, the period may 
be extended for a further period.
Explanation 18: The Confidential Report is the formal document used to assess an officer's 
performance and suitability for promotion or advancement.
Explanation 19: The handbook mentions that an officer may be entitled to a specific number of 
days of annual leave, but this can vary by grade level.
Explanation 20: An Indemnity is a formal agreement to protect against a potential financial loss, 
often required for transactions like a loan for a vehicle.
Explanation 21: An officer is informed of their promotion through a formal letter of promotion 
issued by the relevant authority.
Explanation 22: The document prepared by an officer leaving a post to brief their successor is 
called a "Handing Over Note."
Explanation 23: Files that are no longer required for current action should be sent back to the 
Registry for proper archiving and safekeeping.
Explanation 24: The File Movement Register is used to track the location of files to ensure they 
are not misplaced.
Explanation 25: The Registry is the central hub for all official documents and correspondence, 
handling incoming and outgoing mail and ensuring proper filing.
Explanation 26: Each file is assigned a unique reference number or file code for easy 
identification and retrieval.Explanation 27: All official communications between officers in 
different departments should be routed through their respective Heads of Department.
561
Explanation 28: A query is a formal document issued to an officer to explain their alleged 
misconduct or unsatisfactory performance.
Explanation 29: An official accident report must be completed and submitted to the Transport 
Officer for any accident involving a government vehicle.
Explanation 30: The official oath is a formal declaration of loyalty to the Constitution and a 
commitment to serving the public faithfully.
Explanation 31: A Government White Paper is a document published by the government to 
outline its policy or position on a specific issue or a report from a commission of inquiry.
Explanation 32: An officer must seek and obtain the express permission of the Head of the Civil 
Service of the Federation before engaging in any private trade or business.
Explanation 33: If an officer receives a document not addressed to them, it is a "misdirection" 
and should be forwarded to the correct recipient.
Explanation 34: The Staff Lists of the Federal Government is a source of information that 
contains the names, grades, and departments of all federal government employees.
Explanation 35: The Finance and Accounts Division is responsible for the financial 
administration of the Ministry, including budget preparation and expenditure control.
Explanation 36: A circular is a form of communication that is disseminated widely to a group of 
people or offices to inform them of a new policy, rule, or information.
Explanation 37: Civil servants are required by law to declare their assets upon appointment, at 
the end of their service, and at certain other intervals.
Explanation 38: The Job Description is the official document that outlines the specific roles, 
duties, and responsibilities of an officer in a particular post.
Explanation 39: The National Salaries, Incomes and Wages Commission is the body 
responsible for establishing and regulating the salary structures for all public servants.
Explanation 40: The maximum number of years of service for retirement is 35 years, or 60 years 
of age, whichever comes first.
562
Explanation 41: A "Submission" is a formal document from a junior officer to a senior officer, 
usually seeking a decision or approval.
Explanation 42: A request for a file from another department or officer is commonly referred to 
as a "call for file."
Explanation 43: The Staff Welfare Committee is responsible for addressing staff welfare issues, 
including loans, housing, and other benefits.
Explanation 44: A temporary absence from duty due to illness is referred to as "sick leave."
Explanation 45: Casual leave is a short period of absence from work, typically for a few days, 
that can be granted for urgent private affairs.
Explanation 46: The Permanent Secretary is the accounting officer and is responsible for the 
overall financial management of the Ministry.
Explanation 47: The term "public servant" is broader than "civil servant" and includes all 
government employees, such as those in the military and police.
Explanation 48: The proper protocol for raising a grievance is to follow the chain of command, 
starting with the immediate superior.
Explanation 49: An officer may be granted "study leave" to attend an approved course or 
training program.
Explanation 50: A vetting form is used to verify the personal details and background of an 
officer, often for security and reliability purposes.
Explanation 51: A voucher is the official document that authorizes a financial expenditure and is 
used for accounting purposes.
Explanation 52: The minutes of a meeting are the official written record of the proceedings of a 
meeting.
Explanation 53: An "acting officer" is an officer who is temporarily performing the duties of a 
higher-grade position.
Explanation 54: A "survey report" is a document prepared to report the loss or damage of 
government property.
563
Explanation 55: The Transport Officer is responsible for all matters related to government 
vehicles, including their maintenance, allocation, and use.
Explanation 56: The term "AWOL" (Absent Without Official Leave) is a common military and civil 
service term for an officer who is absent from duty without permission.
Explanation 57: The Financial Regulations are the official document that provides the rules and 
procedures for the management of government funds.
Explanation 58: A "minute" is a brief official message from a senior officer to a junior officer, 
usually on a file.
Explanation 59: Treasury Circulars are official communications from the Ministry of Finance that 
provide instructions on financial matters.
Explanation 60: The Chief Security Officer is responsible for the security and safety of a 
government office and its personnel.
Explanation 61: "Overtime" is the official term for working beyond the normal office hours, for 
which an officer may be compensated.
Explanation 62: The Attendance Register is the official document where staff on GL. 03-13 are 
required to sign in and out of the office.
Explanation 63: A "transfer" or "posting" is the process of moving an officer from one post or 
department to another.
Explanation 64: Compassionate leave is granted for personal or family emergencies, such as 
the death of a close relative.
Explanation 65: A "working group" or "committee" is a group of civil servants appointed to carry 
out a specific task or function.
Explanation 66: The Official Gazette is the official publication that lists new government 
appointments, promotions, and other official notices.
Explanation 67: "Unserviceable property" is the official term for government property that is no 
longer useful and is to be disposed of.
564
Explanation 68: The Appropriation Act is the law that authorizes the government to spend 
money for a financial year.
Explanation 69: The Head of Department is responsible for the general administration of their 
department.
Explanation 70: A "disciplinary hearing" is a formal investigation into an officer's alleged 
misconduct.
Explanation 71: The National Pension Commission (PENCOM) is the body responsible for the 
overall regulation and supervision of the pension scheme.
Explanation 72: An "induction" is the process of a new officer being introduced to the 
department, its staff, and its functions.
Explanation 73: The Public Service Rules are a key document that regulates the conduct and 
general conditions of service of all public servants.
Explanation 74: A "pass-out" is an official document that allows an officer to leave the office for a 
short period for an urgent private matter.
Explanation 75: The Personnel Officer is responsible for the management of the staff records of 
a department.
Explanation 76: A "requisition" is an official document used to request a new item or service for 
the office.
Explanation 77: Establishment Circulars, issued by the Office of the Head of Civil Service of the 
Federation, contain information on appointments, promotions, and staff welfare.
Explanation 78: The "charge sheet" is the official document that outlines the specific charges 
against an officer in a disciplinary case.
Explanation 79: An unserviceable government property may be sold to an officer after following 
the appropriate procedures.
Explanation 80: "Emoluments" is the official term for the salary and allowances paid to an officer 
for their services.
565
Explanation 81: A "vehicle advance application form" is the official document used to apply for a 
loan to purchase a vehicle.
Explanation 82: An "inter-ministerial transfer" is the process of moving an officer from one 
Ministry to another.
Explanation 83: An "audit query" is a formal document from an auditor that questions a financial 
transaction or expenditure.
Explanation 84: A letter sent to a high-ranking official is typically marked with a specific 
classification, such as "confidential" or "top secret," to indicate its importance.
Explanation 85: The Staff Development and Training Division is responsible for organizing and 
managing training programs and courses for staff.
Explanation 86: An "establishment report" or a similar document is used to request a new staff 
member for a department.
Explanation 87: A "welfare loan" is a loan provided to staff for personal or family emergencies.
Explanation 88: "Secondment" is the process of an officer being temporarily released from their 
duties to work in another organization.
Explanation 89 : The Director of Human Resources or Personnel is responsible for the overall 
human resource management of a Ministry.
Explanation 90: "Retirement" is the official term for the process of an officer leaving service due 
to age or years of service.
Explanation 91: While the Head of Department or Permanent Secretary may initiate disciplinary 
action, the Federal Civil Service Commission is the highest disciplinary authority for civil 
servants.
Explanation 92: An unserviceable government property may be sold to an officer at a "residual 
value" after it has been properly valued.
Explanation 93: A "personnel file" is a confidential file that contains an officer's personal details, 
documents, and records of their service.
566
Explanation 94: A "formal letter" is used for communication with external organizations or the 
public.
Explanation 95: The National Assembly is the legislative arm of government, responsible for 
making laws and providing oversight of the government.
Explanation 96: The Public Service Institute is the body responsible for training and capacity 
building for civil servants.
Explanation 97: An "extension" or "renewal" is the process of an officer's contract being 
renewed for a further period.
Explanation 98: A "contract officer" is an officer who is appointed to a post for a specific period 
of time and is not on the permanent establishment.
Explanation 99: A Staff Performance Management System is used to assess and manage an 
officer's performance and productivity.
Explanation 100: A "budget submission" is the official document that is used to request a new 
budget for a department for the next financial year.
567
Chapter 6: General Questions on Most Recent Current Affairs
Staying informed about the world around you is crucial for any civil servant. This chapter is 
designed to test your knowledge of recent events, policies, and global developments. It serves 
as a tool to ensure you are well-versed in the current affairs that shape our nation and its place 
in the international community. A strong grasp of current affairs is essential for informed 
decision-making and effective public service. This chapter consists of 160 multip[le choice 
questions on the most recent current affairs questions you might likely see in your exams.

Conclusion: A Call to Leadership
This book has provided you with the tools to master the content and the format of the Federal 
Civil Service promotion examination. But your journey does not end with passing this test. It is a 
stepping stone to a new phase of leadership, responsibility, and service to the Nigerian nation.
As a director, you will be the custodian of the civil service's values and the driver of its mission. 
You will be expected to make decisions with integrity, manage resources with prudence, and 
lead your team with a clear vision. The knowledge you have gained from the Public Service 
Rules, Financial Regulations, Administrative Procedures, and the Civil Service Handbook 
will be your compass.
Embrace this new challenge with confidence and a renewed commitment to excellence.
